npcs.name: Aokiji
npcs.base_backstory: Kuzan served the Marines for decades, rising to the rank of Admiral with the epithet Aokiji and a philosophy of "lazy justice". After losing a ten-day duel against Sakazuki on Punk Hazard — losing part of his left leg in the process — he resigned and left the organization. Since then he has wandered without a declared faction, having been spotted near the Blackbeard Pirates at Hachinosu, where his presence and loyalty remain a subject of speculation.
npcs.current_goal: Remain among the Blackbeard Pirates while his true intentions stay opaque to everyone around him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Find a form of justice that makes sense outside the structures that betrayed him — or simply decide which side he is on.
npcs.mood: Calm and distant, observing more than acting.
npcs.description: Kuzan is a tall, lean middle-aged man with angular features and a perpetually bored gaze that conceals a sharp mind. He dresses carelessly — a coconut hat, loose clothing — and wields an ice lance he conjures at will. His presence chills the atmosphere in both the literal and figurative sense; those who know him are aware that the apparent laziness can give way to glacial violence in fractions of a second.

current_state.summary_text: Kuzan operates in Hachinosu alongside the Blackbeard Pirates, but his loyalty to the faction is uncertain — no one knows for sure whether he acts as an ally, a spy, or simply an opportunist.

npcs.name: Brook
npcs.base_backstory: He was the leader and musician of the Rumbar Pirates, a crew that sailed the Grand Line and perished by poisoning in battle. The Yomi Yomi no Mi brought him back to life, but his soul took so long to find his body that the flesh had already rotted away, leaving only bones. He spent fifty years wandering alone through the Florian Triangle, sustained by the promise of reuniting with the whale Laboon at the entrance to the Grand Line. He was rescued by the Straw Hat Pirates and, after Thriller Bark, officially joined the crew. During the timeskip, he became the artist known as "Soul King" while separated from the crew.
npcs.current_goal: Protect the crew and escape from Egghead alongside Luffy, fulfilling his role as swordsman and musician of the Straw Hats.
npcs.long_term_dream: Reunite with Laboon at Reverse Mountain and play for him the song the Rumbar Pirates promised.
npcs.mood: Cheerful and resolute, maintaining lightness even in the face of chaos — joy is his armor.
npcs.description: Brook is a tall, slender skeleton dressed in an elegant musician's attire — a top hat, black tailcoat, sword cane, and a voluminous afro that survived death. His face is nothing but a grinning skull, yet his bearing is that of a refined gentleman. The impression he makes oscillates between comical and genuinely unsettling when he manipulates soul energy.

current_state.summary_text: Brook sails with the Straw Hats as the crew's musician and swordsman, having lived through the events of Egghead alongside Luffy. His immediate whereabouts follow those of the Thousand Sunny after the escape from the island.

npcs.name: Don Krieg
npcs.base_backstory: Don Krieg built the largest fleet in the East Blue through treachery, firepower, and terror. He attempted to invade the Grand Line with an armada of 50 ships and 5,000 men, but was destroyed by Mihawk in a matter of hours. He returned to the East Blue in a deplorable state, seeking the resources of the Baratie to regroup his forces. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy in the duel at the floating restaurant, sealing the end of his campaign of conquest.
npcs.current_goal: Rebuild his fleet and reassert his dominance over the East Blue.
npcs.long_term_dream: Rule the seas through absolute fear and firepower superior to any opponent.
npcs.mood: Resentful and determined, carrying the humiliation of defeat as fuel.
npcs.description: A stocky man of imposing stature, covered from head to toe in heavy golden armor packed with weapon compartments. His scarred face and permanently threatening expression convey the image of someone who built his entire identity around brute force. His physical presence intimidates before a single word is spoken.

current_state.summary_text: Exact whereabouts unknown after the defeat at the Baratie; he is believed to remain somewhere in the East Blue, possibly regrouping the remnants of his fleet. His name still circulates as a symbol of destroyed ambition.

npcs.name: Miss Monday
npcs.base_backstory: Recruited by Baroque Works as a Border Agent, Miss Monday was assigned to Whiskey Peak, where she disguised herself as a nun to lure and neutralize unsuspecting travelers. Her distinguishing trait was pure brute strength — no Devil Fruit, no sophisticated weapons, just musculature and endurance above the norm. With the fall of Baroque Works following Crocodile's defeat, the organization dissolved and Miss Monday lost her position. Since then her whereabouts are uncertain; she is believed to have remained somewhere on the Grand Line or returned to the East Blue.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find a new purpose after the collapse of Baroque Works.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove that pure brute strength is enough to impose herself in the world of pirates.
npcs.mood: Wandering and without clear direction, but with her fighting spirit intact.
npcs.description: Miss Monday is a woman of imposing muscular build, remarkably large by feminine standards — a body built for hand-to-hand combat. She used to wear a nun's habit as a disguise in Whiskey Peak, creating a disconcerting contrast between the devout appearance and the brutality she concealed. Her physical presence is enough to intimidate before any word or blow.

current_state.summary_text: A former Border Agent of Baroque Works, Miss Monday remains at an unknown location after the dismantling of the organization. No one knows for certain where she is or with whom she has allied herself since then.

npcs.name: Mr. 9
npcs.base_backstory: Mr. 9 served as a Border Agent of Baroque Works in Whisky Peak, taking part in the pirate-capture scheme disguised as a welcoming village. He worked alongside Miss Monday as her designated partner, fighting with metal clubs and improvised explosives in an acrobatic and chaotic style. After the defeat in the confrontation with Zoro at Whisky Peak and the subsequent dissolution of Baroque Works following Crocodile's fall, he was imprisoned alongside Miss Monday but eventually released. His trail is lost in the East Blue after those events.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and rise again after the collapse of Baroque Works, seeking a new purpose or organization that can make use of his skills.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove his worth as a fighter and be recognized as someone of consequence, not merely a low-ranking agent.
npcs.mood: Restless and adrift, maintaining a facade of confidence even without a clear direction.
npcs.description: Mr. 9 is a young man of agile build, always carrying his metal clubs as though they were natural extensions of his body. He dresses flamboyantly and moves with the restless energy of someone who needs to be noticed. The impression he gives is that of a competent braggart — more dangerous than he appears at first glance, but far from a serious threat to any experienced fighter.

current_state.summary_text: A former Border Agent of Baroque Works, Mr. 9 remains somewhere in the East Blue at an unknown exact location. Without an organization to support him, his current situation is uncertain.

npcs.name: Kuro
npcs.base_backstory: Kuro was the captain of the Black Cat Pirates in the East Blue, feared for his speed and cruelty. To escape the Marines, he faked his own death and spent three years assuming the identity of Klahadore, the faithful butler of the wealthy heiress Kaya in Syrup Village. His plan was to assassinate Kaya, inherit her fortune, and vanish from the pirate world for good. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy and his allies, and the Black Cat Pirates were dissolved.
npcs.current_goal: Remain hidden and live far from the pirate life and the Marines.
npcs.long_term_dream: Escape definitively from his pirate past and never be found again.
npcs.mood: Cautious and reclusive.
npcs.description: Kuro is a lean man with black hair and round glasses, bearing a permanently serene expression that conceals a lethal temperament. He wears long razor-like claws attached to his fingers, capable of tearing through anything in their path. His presence conveys an artificial stillness — the calm of someone who has rehearsed every gesture to appear harmless.

current_state.summary_text: Whereabouts unknown after the defeat at Syrup Village; no one knows for certain whether he is still in the East Blue or has fled to more distant waters.

npcs.name: Gunko
npcs.base_backstory: Born into the Manmayer family, a lineage of Celestial Dragons, Gunko was raised within the structure of privilege and authority that defines the upper echelons of the World Government. She rose through the ranks of the God's Knights, becoming one of the elite protectors of the ruling order. She took part in the God Valley Incident, a historically significant event that reinforced her reputation as a high-ranking fighter. Her presence in the field is described as a wave of black lightning, a reflection of her exceptional mastery of Haki.
npcs.current_goal: Act on behalf of the Celestial Dragons and the God's Knights in Elbaf, maintaining order and the World Government's interests on the island.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve and defend the supremacy of the world order established by the Celestial Dragons at any cost.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and restrained — operates in silence, ready to act.
npcs.description: Gunko carries the upright posture and cold gaze of someone who has never had to justify her actions. Her presence in the field is immediate and oppressive — the Haki she emanates is described as black lightning, dense and suffocating. She uses the Arrow-Arrow no Mi to construct arrows of pure force that not only wound but bind, block, and amplify her mobility. She seems sculpted for war, without unnecessary ornament.

current_state.summary_text: A God's Knight of the Manmayer family, she is in Elbaf as an operative force of the Celestial Dragons. She acts in silence, but her combat capability places her among the most lethal threats on the field.

npcs.name: Arlong
npcs.base_backstory: Arlong grew up on Fishman Island under the ideal of Fish-Man supremacy, becoming a member of Fisher Tiger's Sun Pirates before founding his own gang, the Arlong Pirates. He settled in the East Blue, where he ruled Cocoyasi Village for years through extortion and terror, forcing Nami to chart the world in exchange for her village's freedom. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy and handed over to the Marines, subsequently being confined in Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive confinement and await any opportunity for escape or rescue.
npcs.long_term_dream: Establish Fish-Man domination over humans and build a world where his species reigns supreme.
npcs.mood: Contained and seething with resentment.
npcs.description: Arlong is a Sawshark Fish-Man of imposing stature, with dark blue skin, yellow eyes, and a long serrated nose he uses as a weapon. He wears open-chested clothing that exposes his brutal musculature. His presence carries immediate hostility — every gesture communicates the contempt he feels for any human being who dares to share the same space as him.

current_state.summary_text: Arlong is held in Impel Down, reportedly on Level 6, following his defeat in the East Blue. His name still evokes fear on the islands he terrorized, but his direct influence on the world is suspended for as long as he remains imprisoned.

npcs.name: Laboon
npcs.base_backstory: Laboon was a calf when he grew attached to the Rumbar Pirates during their crossing of the East Blue. Being too young to cross the Grand Line, he was left at Twin Cape under Crocus's care, with the promise that the crew would return. The Rumbar Pirates perished in the Grand Line, and Crocus — who became a doctor and lighthouse keeper at Twin Cape — dedicated decades to caring for Laboon and trying to stop him from destroying himself against the Red Line. Monkey D. Luffy sealed a truce with Laboon by tying a headbutting battle, promising to return to Twin Cape after sailing around the world.
npcs.current_goal: To await Luffy's return — and, through that promise, the symbolic fulfillment of the Rumbar Pirates' original promise.
npcs.long_term_dream: To reunite with those who promised to come back for him.
npcs.mood: Patient and melancholic, but stable under Crocus's care.
npcs.description: Laboon is a colossal humpback whale whose body rivals small islands; his head is marked by deep self-inflicted scars from ramming the Red Line, and painted upon it is the Straw Hat flag, left by Luffy as a symbol of the promise. His expressive eyes convey an emotional intelligence that disarms anyone who learns his story. Despite his devastating size, every movement carries a gentleness that makes his loyalty all the more moving.

current_state.summary_text: Laboon remains at Twin Cape, under Crocus's care, awaiting Luffy's return as per the promise sealed in battle. His scars on the Red Line are covered by the Straw Hat flag.

npcs.name: Igaram
npcs.base_backstory: Igaram served as captain of the Alabasta Royal Guard under King Cobra Nefertari, becoming one of the most loyal men to the royal family. To investigate Baroque Works, he infiltrated the organization under the codename Mr. 8, accompanied by his wife Terracotta disguised as Miss Monday. After Crocodile's defeat and the restoration of peace in Alabasta, he resumed his official post alongside the Nefertari family. With Cobra's death during the Reverie summit and Vivi's rise to the center of world attention, Igaram remains as a pillar of the kingdom's security.
npcs.current_goal: To protect Alabasta and maintain the kingdom's stability following King Cobra's death, watching over Vivi's safety wherever she may be.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Alabasta and the Nefertari family in permanent peace and safety, with Vivi protected from all the dangers of the world.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and apprehensive — Cobra's death and Vivi's disappearance weigh on him, but he maintains the firm composure of someone who cannot afford to fall apart.
npcs.description: A middle-aged man of considerable stature, celebrated for his voluminous blonde wig that conceals a functional machine gun — his most characteristic trick. He wears the Alabasta Royal Guard uniform with evident pride. He conveys a mixture of solemn authority and paternal anxiety; those who know him understand that his loyalty is priceless.

current_state.summary_text: Igaram commands the Royal Guard in Alabasta, sustaining the kingdom's order following Cobra's assassination at the Reverie. Vivi's current whereabouts are uncertain, and that uncertainty weighs on his every decision.

npcs.name: Alvida
npcs.base_backstory: Alvida commanded the Alvida Pirates with an iron fist, obsessed with her own beauty and intolerant of anyone who contradicted her. Her defeat at Luffy's hands marked the beginning of her transformation: upon eating the Sube Sube no Mi, she lost her previous appearance and gained a slender figure, smooth skin, and immunity to physical impacts. She allied herself with Buggy before the timeskip, sailing with him during the Marineford War. With the rise of the Cross Guild following the events of Wano, she positioned herself as part of the new power arrangement led by Buggy.
npcs.current_goal: To consolidate her position in the Cross Guild and be recognized as the most beautiful and feared woman on the seas.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be adored and feared by all as the most beautiful and powerful woman on the seas.
npcs.mood: Arrogant and satisfied with her current position.
aliases: The Most Beautiful Woman on the Seas
npcs.description: Alvida is a woman of striking beauty, with smooth, lustrous skin that repels any touch — a direct effect of the Sube Sube no Mi. She carries a massive iron mace that contrasts with the lightness and grace with which she moves. Her presence is that of someone who knows exactly the effect she has and demands it from everyone around her.

current_state.summary_text: Alvida operates from Karai Bari Island as a member of the Cross Guild, allied with Buggy. She maintains her posture of absolute superiority and uses the Sube Sube no Mi to render physical attacks harmless against her.

npcs.name: Crocus
npcs.base_backstory: Crocus served as ship's doctor on the Roger Pirates during Gol D. Roger's final great voyage to the Final Road Poneglyph, being one of the few who completed the journey around the world with the King of the Pirates. After the crew's disbandment and Roger's execution, he retired to Twin Cape, at the foot of Reverse Mountain, where he took up operating the lighthouse that guides ships into the Grand Line. There, he also assumed the role of caretaker for Laboon, the gigantic whale awaiting the promised return of the Rumbar Pirates, treating the wounds the whale inflicts upon himself against the mountain.
npcs.current_goal: To keep the Twin Cape lighthouse, tend to Laboon's wounds, and ensure the safe entry of ships into the Grand Line.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Laboon reunite with the Rumbar Pirates and finally heal the wounds the whale carries out of love for the lost crew.
npcs.mood: Calm and resigned, carrying a quiet melancholy that coexists with genuine peace of mind.
npcs.description: An elderly man with voluminous white hair and a thick mustache, with tired eyes that convey decades of experience and loss. He wears simple work clothes and carries a harpoon he uses both for fishing and for self-defense. His presence is that of someone completely frozen in time — a silent witness to an era the world has already buried, but which he refuses to forget.

current_state.summary_text: Crocus remains at Twin Cape as guardian of the lighthouse and caretaker of Laboon, living in voluntary isolation far from the conflicts that have redefined the world. One of the last surviving members of Roger's crew, his name is known only to those who pass through the entrance of the Grand Line.

npcs.name: Smoker
npcs.base_backstory: Smoker served as a Marine Captain in Loguetown, where he attempted to capture Monkey D. Luffy before he entered the Grand Line. After multiple failed confrontations with Luffy — including in Alabasta and at the Sabaody Archipelago — he was transferred to the New World as commander of Marine Base G-5. In Punk Hazard, he uncovered Caesar Clown's illegal experiments and entered into direct conflict with Trafalgar Law, even having his heart swapped by Vergo before being rescued. Following Doflamingo's fall in Dressrosa, he remains in the Marines operating in the New World.
npcs.current_goal: To capture Monkey D. Luffy and any pirate who crosses his path in the New World.
npcs.long_term_dream: To enforce justice on his own terms, without bending to the corruption or political convenience of the Marines.
npcs.mood: Determined and frustrated — the world changes around him, but his hunt does not stop.
npcs.description: Smoker is a stocky man with short white hair and two cigars always lit in his mouth, from which smoke constantly escapes. He wears the white Marine coat draped over his shoulders in the admirals' style and carries a long jitte with a sea stone tip. His presence is imposing: anyone who sees him approaching knows there is no negotiation.

current_state.summary_text: Vice Admiral of the Marines based at Marine Base G-5 in the New World, Smoker continues his relentless hunt for pirates, with Luffy at the top of the list. His exact whereabouts following the events of Egghead have not been confirmed, but he remains in operation in the New World.

npcs.name: Monkey D. Dragon
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Monkey D. Garp and father of Monkey D. Luffy, Dragon founded and leads the Revolutionary Army, the largest organized force of opposition to the World Government. He abandoned any conventional path to dedicate his life to overthrowing the tyranny of the Celestial Dragons and the system that sustains it. He was present in Loguetown during Luffy's departure to sea, where he intervened in a mysterious manner. After the destruction of the Revolutionary Army's headquarters in Baltigo at the hands of the Blackbeard Pirates, he relocated the base of operations to the Kamabakka Kingdom on Momoiro Island.
npcs.current_goal: To coordinate the Revolutionary Army to dismantle the World Government and free the world from the oppression of the Celestial Dragons.
npcs.long_term_dream: To bring down the World Government and build a world where no one lives under the boot of tyrants.
npcs.mood: Determined and composed — he absorbs recent events, including Luffy's rise and the chaos at Egghead, as pieces on a board moving in the direction he always foresaw.
npcs.description: Dragon is a man of imposing stature, with dark hair and a red tattoo marked on the left side of his face. His eyes carry an analytical coldness that makes those he speaks with feel they are being completely read. The mere mention of his name within the World Government provokes genuine alarm — he is classified as the world's most dangerous criminal, and his silent presence fully justifies that reputation.

current_state.summary_text: He commands the Revolutionary Army from the Kamabakka Kingdom, waging a global war against the World Government. His true power remains unknown to the world at large — no Devil Fruit, no Haki publicly confirmed, only the certainty that few in the world would be capable of facing him.

npcs.name: Miss Valentine
npcs.base_backstory: A Baroque Works agent under the codename Miss Valentine, partner of Mr. 5, she carried out elimination and containment missions for Crocodile. Defeated by Nefertari Vivi during the crisis in Alabasta, she was imprisoned alongside Mr. 5 after the organization's collapse. Following Crocodile's fall and the dissolution of Baroque Works, she was released from prison and her whereabouts remain unknown.
npcs.current_goal: To survive and rebuild her position after the collapse of Baroque Works.
npcs.long_term_dream: To secure her own prosperity and never again serve an organization that puts her at a disadvantage.
npcs.mood: Resentful and adrift, without an organizational structure to anchor her.
npcs.description: A young woman of average height, short blonde hair, and a perpetually bored expression. She carries a colorful parasol she uses both for gliding and as an improvised weapon. The lightness of her movements contrasts with the brutal impact she can generate by amplifying her own weight — a dissonance that tends to catch opponents off guard.

current_state.summary_text: Former Baroque Works agent, at large following the organization's collapse in Alabasta; current whereabouts unknown, with no traceable faction.

npcs.name: Mr. 5
npcs.base_backstory: Mr. 5 served as an official agent of Baroque Works, the secret organization led by Crocodile, holding the number 5 position alongside his partner Miss Valentine. Specializing in assassinations, he operated in the Grand Line carrying out elimination missions for the organization. He was defeated by Zoro and Nami during the mission in Little Garden, and subsequently captured by the Marines along with other Baroque Works agents following Crocodile's fall in Alabasta. His whereabouts after the dissolution of Baroque Works and the possible completion of his sentence remain unknown.
npcs.current_goal: To survive and recover freedom and relevance after the collapse of Baroque Works.
npcs.long_term_dream: To prove his worth as an independent elite assassin, without needing an organization to define his status.
npcs.mood: Cold and calculating, with latent resentment over Baroque Works' fall.
npcs.description: A man of average height, dark hair, and a permanently bored expression, as though nothing around him deserves his full attention. He dresses soberly — dark suit, no unnecessary adornments. The impression he gives is one of silent danger: there is no bluster, only the quiet certainty that he can detonate anything he touches.

current_state.summary_text: Former Baroque Works agent, captured following Crocodile's defeat in Alabasta; his current whereabouts are unknown, possibly held by the Marines or released after serving his sentence.

npcs.name: Dorry
npcs.base_backstory: Dorry is one of the legendary giant warriors of Elbaf, co-captain of the Giant Warrior Pirates alongside Brogy. A disagreement over which of the two had taken down the larger prey during a hunt started an honor duel between them that stretched on for over a hundred years on Little Garden. After being rescued by the Straw Hat Pirates and the end of the conflict with Baroque Works, Dorry and Brogy returned together to the world of the seas. Both reunited with Luffy's fleet during the campaign in Elbaf, fighting alongside the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.
npcs.current_goal: To fight and die with honor alongside Brogy, defending Elbaf and the allies he respects.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be remembered as a true warrior of Elbaf — someone who never abandoned honor, even in the face of an endless duel.
npcs.mood: Resolute and serene, like a warrior who has found his place again among his own.
npcs.description: Dorry is a giant of colossal stature, with wild red hair and beard and scars accumulated over centuries of combat. He wields an enormous sword that would be impractical for any human being. His physical presence commands immediate respect — he seems more like a force of nature than an individual, and his gaze carries the heavy calm of someone who has seen everything.

current_state.summary_text: Dorry is in Elbaf, reunited with Brogy and integrated into the forces orbiting the alliance with the Straw Hat Pirates. The century-long duel that imprisoned him on Little Garden belongs to the past.

npcs.name: Brogy
npcs.base_backstory: Brogy is a giant warrior from Elbaf who, after an honor dispute with Dorry over which fish was larger, was compelled by the giants' code to resolve the impasse in a duel. The two voluntarily exiled themselves to Little Garden, where they fought regular battles for over a hundred years without reaching a definitive winner. Both were freed from the impasse by Luffy's group and, after the events of Marineford and Dressrosa, Brogy rejoined the Giant Squad led by Dorry. The two now sail alongside Luffy's allies, having taken part in the events of Elbaf beside the Straw Hat fleet.
npcs.current_goal: To fight alongside Dorry and the Giant Squad in defense of Elbaf and their allies.
npcs.long_term_dream: To die in battle with honor, as the warrior code of Elbaf demands.
npcs.mood: Fired up and combative, ready for the next battle.
npcs.description: Brogy is a colossal giant with dark skin, red hair and beard, and scars accumulated over centuries of combat. He wields an immense axe and wears sparse warrior garb. His presence is overwhelming — he radiates warmth and brutality at the same time, and the mere sound of his footsteps is enough to intimidate.

current_state.summary_text: Brogy operates in Elbaf alongside Dorry and the Giant Squad, serving as one of the island's military pillars and a declared ally of Monkey D. Luffy.

npcs.name: Miss Goldenweek
npcs.base_backstory: An official agent of Baroque Works, the secret organization led by Crocodile, with the designation of Mr. 3's partner. She demonstrated her Color Trap ability during the confrontation with Luffy on Little Garden, immobilizing him with ease. After the fall of Baroque Works in Alabasta, she took part in an operation to rescue captured agents from Impel Down, led by Miss Valentine and Mr. 2 Bon Clay. Since then, she has kept out of the spotlight, with no known public alignment.
npcs.current_goal: To stay out of the authorities' reach and preserve the freedom of the former Baroque Works agents.
npcs.long_term_dream: To live without restrictions, painting and existing on her own terms, far from any organization or conflict.
npcs.mood: Apathetic and calm, with no apparent urgency.
npcs.description: A girl of deceptively young appearance, with short hair and a casual, disheveled style that contrasts with the seriousness of her companions. She always carries a paintbrush and a palette of colorful paints, and has the habit of eating crackers with indifference even in dangerous situations. Her expression rarely changes, conveying an unsettling calm that unnerves opponents before she even raises her brush.

current_state.summary_text: Former Baroque Works agent, exact whereabouts unknown after the organization's collapse in Alabasta. Remains at large, with no traceable public affiliation.

npcs.name: Mr. 3
npcs.base_backstory: Galdino, known as Mr. 3, was one of the numbered agents of Baroque Works, operating on Little Garden Island under Crocodile's orders. His mission to eliminate the Seven Warlords of the Sea failed and he was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy. Captured and sent to Impel Down, he ultimately escaped during the great rebellion led by Luffy and Emporio Ivankov. After the fall of Baroque Works and the dissolution of Crocodile's scheme, he attached himself to Buggy's structure, joining the Cross Guild from Karai Bari Island.
npcs.current_goal: To maintain his position and usefulness within the Cross Guild, avoiding direct conflict while profiting from Buggy's structure.
npcs.long_term_dream: To accumulate wealth and recognition through his own intelligence, without having to risk his own skin.
npcs.mood: Cautious and opportunistic, navigating the Cross Guild's unstable hierarchy with heightened attention.
npcs.description: A man of average height, with blond hair styled in the shape of the number three and thick, expressive eyebrows that would betray his emotions even if he tried to hide them. He dresses with excessive care for someone of his background, maintaining a look that communicates intellectual pretension. The impression he gives is of a man far more dangerous at a distance than up close — his wax traps are ingenious; his courage, scarce.

current_state.summary_text: Galdino operates on Karai Bari Island as a member of the Cross Guild, putting his intelligence and the Doru Doru no Mi at the service of Buggy's organization. His loyalty is instrumental — he stays where survival and profit align.

npcs.name: Wapol
npcs.base_backstory: Wapol was the tyrannical king of the Drum Kingdom, ruling with absolute selfishness — he expelled all doctors from the country except his Ten Barrels and held the population hostage to his will. After being defeated by Monkey D. Luffy and losing the throne to Dalton, he wandered as a beggar until he discovered his talent for creating toys with the Baku Baku no Mi, building a new commercial empire. With his company Evil Black Drum, he became a globally recognized toy magnate, reclaiming noble status and marrying Kinderella. His position as an honorary Celestial Dragon granted him access to the Reverie and Mary Geoise, where he witnessed events that only a few know about.
npcs.current_goal: To consolidate his position among the world nobility and expand the Evil Black Drum commercial empire.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be recognized as the most powerful and wealthy man in the world, this time in an unshakeable way.
npcs.mood: Arrogant and calculating, with growing paranoia following what he witnessed at the Reverie.
npcs.description: Wapol is a broad-built man with a disproportionately large mouth, a visible trait of his Devil Fruit. He dresses in extravagant clothes that mix noble pomp and kitsch excess. His default expression oscillates between scorn and greed, and his physical presence conveys both ridiculousness and genuine danger — those who underestimate him tend to regret it.

current_state.summary_text: Wapol operates from Mary Geoise as a noble magnate and CEO of Evil Black Drum, having accumulated status among the Five Families of the West; he carries with him the weight of secrets he witnessed during the Reverie.

npcs.name: Dalton
npcs.base_backstory: Dalton served as a loyal guard of the Drum Kingdom under Wapol's reign, but his conscience led him to oppose the king's cruelties — he was even shot by the monarch himself for trying to protect the citizens. After Wapol's defeat by the Straw Hat Pirates, Dalton was acclaimed by the people as the island's rightful protector. He assumed the throne and renamed the territory the Sakura Kingdom, in honor of the cherry blossoms that Kureha and Chopper associated with hope and healing. Since then he has governed with a firm hand and a heart turned toward the people's well-being.
npcs.current_goal: To govern and protect the Sakura Kingdom, ensuring stability and prosperity for his people.
npcs.long_term_dream: To build a just kingdom where no citizen is abandoned or oppressed by those who should protect them.
npcs.mood: Steady and vigilant — he carries the weight of the throne with serenity, alert to any threat to his people.
npcs.description: Dalton is a man of imposing stature, with broad shoulders and a presence that fills any room. He wears ruler's attire that does not conceal the musculature of an experienced warrior. His weathered face conveys seriousness without hostility — the expression of someone who already chose the right side once and has no intention of changing.

current_state.summary_text: Dalton rules the Sakura Kingdom on Drum Island as its legitimate king, maintaining peace and order in the territory he freed from Wapol's tyranny. He remains on the island as its chief protector and central political figure.

npcs.name: Doctor Kureha
npcs.base_backstory: Kureha is a brilliantly gifted doctor born on Drum Island, whose extraordinary longevity — over a century of active life — has granted her an unparalleled store of medical knowledge on the Grand Line. It was she who took in Tony Tony Chopper, a reindeer who ate the Hito Hito no Mi, and trained him as a doctor for years, becoming his maternal figure and mentor. After the fall of Wapol's reign and the rise of the Sakura Kingdom under Dalton, Kureha remained in the castle she occupies at the top of the snowy mountain, continuing to treat patients. Her reputation crosses borders, but she has never left the island out of ambition — the island is her territory and her purpose.
npcs.current_goal: To continue practicing medicine in the Sakura Kingdom and maintain the castle as the island's medical reference.
npcs.long_term_dream: To live and practice medicine indefinitely, proving that age is no limit for anyone — especially for her.
npcs.mood: Energetic and ironic, with no sign of decline whatsoever.
npcs.description: Kureha is a woman of surprisingly youthful appearance for someone who claims to be over a hundred years old: voluminous white hair, sharp and lively eyes, and a posture that admits no weakness. She dresses eccentrically and carries syringes and potions with the ease of someone bearing weapons. The impression she gives is of a force of nature disguised as an elderly doctor — those who underestimate her because of her age soon regret it.

current_state.summary_text: Kureha remains in the castle at the top of the snowy mountain on Drum Island, practicing medicine in the Sakura Kingdom with the same energy and authority as always. Her whereabouts and health are stable and unchanged since Chopper's departure.

npcs.name: Crocodile
npcs.base_backstory: Crocodile rose to the rank of Warlord of the Sea as "Sir Crocodile", building in parallel the criminal organization Baroque Works with the secret objective of taking over the kingdom of Alabasta. His plan was dismantled by Monkey D. Luffy, who defeated him three times, leading to his imprisonment in Impel Down. He was freed during the invasion of Marineford and, after the timeskip, formed the Cross Guild alongside Mihawk and Buggy on Karai Bari, transforming the organization into a powerhouse that places bounties on the heads of Marines.
npcs.current_goal: To consolidate the Cross Guild's power and advance his own domination agenda, keeping Buggy as a front while operating the organization's real gears.
npcs.long_term_dream: To seize absolute power over the world — an objective that the defeat in Alabasta delayed, but did not extinguish.
npcs.mood: Cold and restrained, tolerating Buggy's facade with calculated disdain while waiting for the right moment to act.
npcs.description: Crocodile is a tall, muscular middle-aged man with black hair slicked back, a cigarette permanently between his lips, and a large scar crossing his face. His left hand was replaced by a golden hook — which conceals a poisoned blade. He radiates a cold, calculated authority; those who face him feel the weight of an intelligence that has already played chess with an entire kingdom before breakfast.

current_state.summary_text: Crocodile operates on Karai Bari Island as one of the real pillars of the Cross Guild, exerting strategic influence behind the scenes while the organization places bounties on Marines around the world. His ambition remains intact and his loyalty to the Cross Guild lasts as long as it serves his own ends.

npcs.name: Bon Clay
npcs.base_backstory: Bentham, known as Bon Clay or Mr. 2, served as a Baroque Works agent under Crocodile's orders, operating as a dancer and Okama Kenpo fighter. After the defeat in Alabasta, he was imprisoned in Impel Down, where he forged an unlikely alliance with Luffy during the great escape. He voluntarily stayed behind to ensure Luffy's escape, sacrificing his freedom. He survived and rose to the position of Vice-Warden of New Kama Land, the secret Level 5.5 of Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: To protect and lead the inhabitants of New Kama Land as Vice-Warden, keeping the flame of freedom alive within the bowels of Impel Down.
npcs.long_term_dream: To live fully as himself, free of all shackles, and one day reunite with Luffy as a free man.
npcs.mood: Exuberant and resolute, carrying the role of leader with genuine pride.
npcs.description: Bentham is a tall, slender man of extravagant appearance, with elaborate makeup, false eyelashes, and colorful dance outfits that blend the okama style with a secret-agent aesthetic. His movements are fluid and performative, with long legs he wields as lethal weapons. The presence he commands is at once comical and genuinely imposing — those who underestimate him because of the feathers quickly discover their mistake.

current_state.summary_text: Bentham commands New Kama Land on Level 5.5 of Impel Down as Vice-Warden, using his Okama Kenpo and the Mane Mane no Mi to protect the refugees of the secret level. He remains voluntarily imprisoned, cut off from the outside world.

npcs.name: Nefertari Vivi
npcs.base_backstory: The crown princess of Alabasta, she infiltrated Baroque Works as Miss Wednesday to uncover Crocodile's plans against her kingdom. She traveled with the Straw Hat Pirates until the organization was dismantled and Alabasta was saved, choosing to stay behind and fulfill her duty as princess. Years later, she attended the Reverie at Mary Geoise as Alabasta's representative, where she was attacked by unknown agents and disappeared during the events of the summit. She reappeared as a declared member of the Revolutionary Army, alongside her father Cobra — who was assassinated by Im during the Reverie — now bearing the weight of loss and secrets concerning the D. Family and the Void Century.
npcs.current_goal: To uncover the truth behind her father Cobra's assassination and the secrets the World Government conceals, while deciding the future of Alabasta.
npcs.long_term_dream: To protect Alabasta and its people, and one day reveal to the world the truths buried by the Void Century.
npcs.mood: Grief-stricken and resolute — mourning her father's death while holding a cold determination to act.
npcs.description: A young woman with long blue hair, expressive blue eyes, and a bearing that combines a princess's elegance with a fighter's readiness. She wields the Peacock Slashers — cutting-wire rings — with surprising precision for someone who appears at first glance to be merely a court figure. She radiates a quiet authority that inspires immediate trust, but those who know her well perceive the unyielding determination beneath the composure.

current_state.summary_text: Vivi disappeared during the events of the Reverie following the assassination of her father, King Cobra, and her current whereabouts are unknown. She carries information about Im and the secrets of the D. Family, making her a target of the World Government.

npcs.name: Miss Doublefinger
npcs.base_backstory: Zala, known as Miss Doublefinger, was one of the highest-ranking agents of Baroque Works, holding the position of Mr. 1's partner. She was actively involved in the organization's operations in Arabasta, where she faced Nami in direct combat. After the defeat and dissolution of Baroque Works through the intervention of the Straw Hat Pirates and the fall of Crocodile, her trajectory became uncertain. There are no public records of her subsequent activities.
npcs.current_goal: To survive and operate on the fringes of the criminal underworld, away from the World Government's spotlight.
npcs.long_term_dream: To prove her superiority as a fighter and never submit to anyone weaker than herself.
npcs.mood: Contained and watchful, with no organization to anchor her.
npcs.description: Zala is a tall woman of imposing presence, with platinum blonde hair and cold eyes that rarely betray emotion. Her posture is always upright and deliberate, every movement carrying the precision of someone trained to kill. The Toge Toge no Mi allows her to project rigid spines from any part of her body, turning her into a walking weapon of variable range. Those who meet her for the first time tend to underestimate her; those who face her once do not make the same mistake.

current_state.summary_text: A former elite agent of Baroque Works, Zala dropped off the radar after the organization's dissolution in Arabasta. Her current whereabouts are unknown.

npcs.name: Mr. 4
npcs.base_backstory: Mr. 4 served as an official Baroque Works agent under Crocodile's command, operating as a pair with Miss Merry Christmas. His role was that of a field enforcer, leveraging his immense physical strength and his dog-cannon Lassoo to eliminate targets. He was defeated by Usopp and Chopper during the invasion of Alabasta. With the fall of Baroque Works following Crocodile's defeat, the organization was dismantled by the Marines.
npcs.current_goal: To survive and remain by Miss Merry Christmas's side after the dissolution of Baroque Works.
npcs.long_term_dream: To stay by his partner's side, carrying out whatever work keeps them both going.
npcs.mood: Apathetic and steady — with no organization to serve, he continues at the same slow cadence as always.
npcs.description: Mr. 4 is a man of average height and massive build, with dense muscles that justify his ability to wield a giant iron baseball bat as though it were a twig. His face is almost always expressionless, conveying a calm that borders on total indifference. His presence on the field is heavy — not through verbal intimidation, but through silence and the certainty that every blow he lands was calculated to destroy.

current_state.summary_text: A former Baroque Works agent, Mr. 4's whereabouts have been uncertain since Crocodile's fall in Alabasta. With no active organization, his trail was lost alongside that of his partner Miss Merry Christmas.

npcs.name: Miss Merry Christmas
npcs.base_backstory: A high-ranking Baroque Works agent, Miss Merry Christmas operated under Crocodile's orders as Mr. 4's partner. She took part in the operation in Alabasta to topple the Nefertari kingdom and install Crocodile as its ruler. She was defeated by Usopp and Chopper during the battle in Alubarna and captured by Marine forces along with the other surviving agents of the organization. With the fall of Baroque Works following Crocodile's defeat, the organization was dismantled and its members scattered or imprisoned.
npcs.current_goal: To survive the dispersal of Baroque Works and maintain her freedom after Crocodile's fall.
npcs.long_term_dream: To wield power and dominance without having to serve anyone.
npcs.mood: Resentful and adrift, without the structure of Baroque Works to channel her brutality.
npcs.description: A woman of imposing stature and build, with an appearance that blends human features with those of a mole — a prominent nose, a hunched posture, and broad hands adapted for digging. She projects an intimidating presence even in silence, with small eyes and a permanently contemptuous expression. In combat, her body mass and brute strength are her primary weapons.

current_state.summary_text: A former Baroque Works agent, captured after the defeat in Alabasta, with uncertain whereabouts following the organization's dismantlement. Her current situation is unknown.

npcs.name: Lassoo
npcs.devil_fruit: Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund
npcs.base_backstory: Lassoo is a pistol transformed into a living dog by the Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Dachshund, becoming an autonomous and sentient weapon. He was used by Mr. 4 and Miss Merry Christmas in Baroque Works' operations in Alabasta. During the confrontation with the Straw Hat Pirates in Alabasta, Lassoo was defeated by Usopp and Chopper. With the dissolution of Baroque Works following Crocodile's defeat, Lassoo's whereabouts became uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: To exist and survive after the dissolution of Baroque Works.
npcs.long_term_dream: To remain by his partner's side and continue functioning as the dog-weapon he is.
npcs.mood: Wandering
npcs.description: Lassoo is a baseball pistol that came to life in the form of a chubby orange dog, with a gun barrel visible on his snout and the compact body of a dachshund. He fires explosive baseballs with unpredictable accuracy, blending animal behavior with devastating firepower. His absurd appearance contrasts with the genuine danger he poses in combat.

current_state.summary_text: With the fall of Baroque Works in Alabasta, Lassoo lost his operational structure and his current whereabouts are unknown. His last recorded location was the territory of Alabasta, during the conflict that brought the organization's activities to an end.

npcs.name: Pell
npcs.devil_fruit: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon
npcs.base_backstory: Pell is one of the oldest and most loyal guardians of the Alabasta Kingdom, having served the Nefertari family for decades. He ate the Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Falcon, becoming the kingdom's only Devil Fruit user and earning the title of Alabasta's Strongest Warrior. During the Baroque Works invasion, he absorbed the explosion of a time bomb over Alubarna to save the city and its population, miraculously surviving the event. He returned to active service as a guardian of the kingdom after peace was restored in Alabasta.
npcs.current_goal: To protect the Nefertari family and the Alabasta Kingdom from any internal or external threat.
npcs.long_term_dream: To ensure that Alabasta remains at peace and that the Nefertari line continues to reign with justice.
npcs.mood: Serious and vigilant, carrying the silent weight of someone who has already given his life for the kingdom once.
npcs.description: Pell is a man of tall stature and impeccable bearing, with dark hair and features marked by military dedication. He wears the Alabasta guardian uniform with pride, and his presence commands immediate respect. His eyes carry the stern calm of someone who has faced death without flinching.

current_state.summary_text: Pell serves as a guardian of the Alabasta Royal Palace, protecting the Nefertari family with renewed determination following Vivi's departure for the Reverie. His loyalty to the kingdom remains unshakeable.

npcs.name: Eyelashes
npcs.base_backstory: Eyelashes is a super spot-billed duck who serves as the loyal mount and companion of Princess Vivi of Alabasta. He accompanied Vivi during the crisis in Alabasta, carrying her into battle and flight with exceptional speed. After the resolution of the conflict in Alabasta, he remained in the kingdom alongside the Nefertari family.
npcs.current_goal: To remain by Vivi's side and protect her wherever she goes.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve Princess Vivi until the end of his days.
npcs.mood: Calm and watchful, attentive to Vivi's movements.
npcs.description: Eyelashes is a super spot-billed duck of elegant bearing, with long, expressive eyelashes that earned him his name. His plumage is white and clean, and his bearing conveys an almost aristocratic dignity. Anyone who approaches without being Vivi receives a look of pure disdain — he makes it clear he is not a mount for just anyone.

current_state.summary_text: Eyelashes resides in Alabasta alongside Princess Vivi, serving as her mount and trusted companion. His exact whereabouts within the kingdom are not widely disclosed to the outside world.

npcs.name: Chaka
npcs.base_backstory: Chaka served for decades as captain of the Alabasta Royal Guard, alongside his partner Pell. During the crisis orchestrated by Crocodile and Baroque Works, he led the defense of the Alubarna palace while the kingdom burned in civil war. He survived the conflict and remained as a pillar of the kingdom's security. With the death of King Cobra at Marie Geoise and Vivi's rise as the central figure of the Nefertari family, he continues to guard Alabasta and the legacy of the royal family.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the security of Alabasta and protect what remains of the Nefertari family following the death of King Cobra.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Alabasta in permanent peace, with the kingdom's honor and sovereignty preserved for future generations.
npcs.mood: Grave and vigilant — he carries grief over King Cobra's death without letting it weaken his bearing as a guardian.
npcs.description: Chaka is a man of athletic build, with sharp features and a permanently serious expression. He wears the Alabasta Royal Guard armor with the ease of someone who has never taken it off. His presence conveys restrained authority — he does not threaten; he simply does not yield. In his jackal form, he becomes a golden, swift silhouette that commands respect even from the most hardened soldiers.

current_state.summary_text: Chaka commands the Royal Guard in Alubarna, maintaining order in Alabasta following the assassination of King Cobra at Marie Geoise. His loyalty now extends to protecting the kingdom and the Nefertari legacy in the sovereign's absence.

npcs.name: Sarquiss
npcs.base_backstory: Sarquiss served as first mate of the Bellamy Pirates, acting as the captain's right hand in operations on Jaya. He took part in the seizure of gold from the Masira and Shoujou Pirates, and confronted Luffy and Zoro in Mock Town without managing to provoke a reaction from them. He was defeated by Blackbeard during the attack on Bellamy's camp on Jaya, an event that marked the collapse of the original crew. After the dissolution of the Bellamy Pirates, his whereabouts and subsequent allegiances are unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find a new purpose after the collapse of the Bellamy Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve under a powerful captain and prove his worth as a fighter in the Grand Line.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and directionless since his defeat at Blackbeard's hands.
npcs.description: Sarquiss is a man of average height with blond hair and an intimidating presence cultivated through arrogance. He dresses in the flamboyant style typical of Mock Town pirates and carries swords with the posture of someone always ready for a fight. The impression he gives is that of a seasoned bully who has met his limit.

current_state.summary_text: Former first mate of the Bellamy Pirates, he was defeated by Blackbeard during the attack on Jaya. His current whereabouts are unknown following the dissolution of Bellamy's original crew.

npcs.name: Toto
npcs.base_backstory: Toto is a simple man from Alabasta, father of Koza, the leader of the Alabasta Resistance. During the great crisis provoked by Baroque Works and Crocodile, Toto spent years alone in the abandoned village of Yuba, digging in search of water in the desert to keep alive the hope that the town could be rebuilt. His unwavering determination and unarmed courage impressed both Luffy and other pirates who crossed his path. With Crocodile's defeat and the restoration of peace in Alabasta, Toto remained in the kingdom, resuming a quiet life alongside his son.
npcs.current_goal: Live in peace in Alabasta and support the safety and well-being of his community.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Alabasta prosperous and at peace, with Yuba revived and his son Koza by his side.
npcs.mood: Calm and resigned, carrying the serenity of someone who has already faced the worst and survived.
npcs.description: Toto is a man of advanced middle age, with skin weathered by the desert sun and hands calloused from years with a shovel. His bearing is simple, but there is something solid about him — like a rock the wind could not move. He inspires quiet respect in those who know him, not through power, but through character.

current_state.summary_text: Toto lives in Alabasta, likely in Yuba or its surroundings, leading a simple life following the restoration of peace in the kingdom. Father of Koza, he is a respected figure in his local community.

npcs.name: Koza
npcs.base_backstory: Koza grew up in Yuba, in the Alabasta desert, and forged a deep friendship with Princess Vivi in childhood. When drought and misery ravaged the kingdom — manipulated by the schemes of Crocodile and Baroque Works — he founded and led the Rebel Army, convinced that King Cobra had abandoned the people. Upon discovering the truth about the conspiracy, Koza risked his own life to stop the final confrontation between the rebels and the royal troops, being shot in the open field while trying to calm the forces on both sides. After Crocodile's defeat and the restoration of peace, he reconciled with the royal family and began serving as a civil leadership figure in Alabasta.
npcs.current_goal: Support the political stability of Alabasta alongside the Nefertari family and rebuild the inland communities devastated by years of conflict.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Alabasta prosperous and free from any external threat, with its people united under just leadership.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and committed; he carries the scar of the civil war as a permanent reminder of what is at stake.
npcs.description: Koza is a young man of athletic build forged by the desert, with light hair and a direct gaze that conveys immediate authority. He dresses functionally, without adornment, and carries twin sabers that he wields with the efficiency of someone who learned to fight out of necessity. His presence in a setting signals seriousness — not intimidation, but the gravity of someone who has already seen the consequences of failure.

current_state.summary_text: Koza serves as a civil leader in Alabasta, working alongside the Nefertari family in the reconstruction and maintenance of peace in the kingdom. His whereabouts and specific activities post-Egghead are not detailed in canon, but his loyalty to Vivi and Alabasta remains unshaken.

npcs.name: Blackbeard
npcs.devil_fruit: Yami Yami no Mi / Gura Gura no Mi
npcs.base_backstory: Marshall D. Teach spent decades as a member of the Whitebeard Pirates, patiently waiting for the opportunity to obtain the Yami Yami no Mi — the fruit he had always desired. He murdered his crewmate Thatch to steal it and fled, triggering the pursuit by Portgas D. Ace that culminated in the Marineford War. After the war, he attacked the remnants of the Whitebeard Pirates' crew at Hachinosu and, upon defeating Commander Shiryu, stole the Gura Gura no Mi from Whitebeard's corpse — becoming the only known being to wield two Devil Fruits simultaneously. He rose to the rank of Emperor and established Hachinosu as the base of operations for the Blackbeard Pirates.
npcs.current_goal: Gather the Nine Road Poneglyphs and be the first to claim the One Piece, becoming King of the Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: To claim the One Piece and prove that destiny belongs to those who seize it with their own hands.
npcs.mood: Confident and expansive — he commands the world from Hachinosu with the certainty that the moment to act has come.
npcs.description: Teach is an enormous man of heavy build and imposing posture, with disheveled black hair, stubble, and notoriously damaged teeth. He wears a dark coat over tattered clothes and carries a three-pronged claw and pistols at his belt. His presence blends vulgar charisma with a visceral menace that is hard to ignore — the broad smile never reaches his eyes.

current_state.summary_text: Marshall D. Teach commands the Blackbeard Pirates from Hachinosu as one of the Four Emperors, simultaneously wielding the Yami Yami no Mi and the Gura Gura no Mi. His pursuit of the Road Poneglyphs puts him on a direct collision course with the other great powers of the seas.

npcs.name: Bellamy
npcs.base_backstory: Bellamy gained notoriety in the New World as a pirate captain famous for ridiculing those who chased dreams considered impossible, a philosophy that made him popular in certain circles and despised in others. After being humiliated by Luffy on Jaya, he became one of the officers of the Donquixote Pirates under Doflamingo, eventually commanding operations in Dressrosa. During the Dressrosa crisis, he was manipulated and forced to fight Luffy by Doflamingo, and was defeated. With Doflamingo's fall, the faction dispersed and Bellamy set out on his own, without a defined allegiance.
npcs.current_goal: Follow his own path on the seas without serving any superior, proving his worth through his own fists.
npcs.long_term_dream: To find a genuine purpose on the seas — something worth fighting for without depending on another's shadow.
npcs.mood: Wandering and bitter, but with a spark of his own determination.
npcs.description: Bellamy is a tall, muscular man with short blond hair and piercing blue eyes, with tattoos that mark his pirate identity. His physical presence is imposing and aggressive — he occupies space like someone accustomed to intimidating others. The smile he carries holds more challenge than joy, and his movements carry the coiled tension of springs ready to snap.

current_state.summary_text: Bellamy sails independently following the dissolution of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa; his exact whereabouts are uncertain, but his last known position is Dressrosa itself. Without a faction or a master, his loyalty and fate now belong to him alone.

npcs.name: Van Augur
npcs.base_backstory: Van Augur was an original member of the Blackbeard Pirates from before Teach's rise to power, serving as the crew's sniper from their earliest days at sea. He took part in the attack on Banaro Island and the siege of Impel Down, cementing himself as one of the pillars of Teach's original formation. After the Marineford War, he joined the expansion of Blackbeard's empire across the New World, and at some point acquired the Wapu Wapu no Mi, adding teleportation to his already lethal sniper capabilities.
npcs.current_goal: Defend and expand Blackbeard's territory, eliminating threats from a distance before they become problems.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve as the perfect bullet — never miss, never be reached, never be needed more than once.
npcs.mood: Calm and watchful, as always.
npcs.description: Van Augur is a tall, thin man of cadaverous appearance, with sunken eyes and a permanently vacant expression. He wears a long coat and carries a long-barreled rifle that looks disproportionate even for his height. The impression he gives is that of a statue: motionless, silent, watching — until the moment the target is already dead.

current_state.summary_text: Van Augur operates from Hachinosu as the sniper and frontline combatant of the Blackbeard Pirates, combining long-range precision with the mobility granted by the Wapu Wapu no Mi. He is one of the most original and trusted members of Teach's crew.

npcs.name: Masira
npcs.base_backstory: Masira is the captain of the Masira Pirates and co-founder of the Saruyama Alliance alongside Shoujou and Mont Blanc Cricket. A specialist in salvage and diving, his crew operates in the waters near Jaya recovering treasures and wreckage from the ocean floor. He helped Luffy and his allies during the Skypiea saga by providing logistical and naval support for the launch to the sky island. He remains active in the Jaya region, continuing the alliance's diving and salvage operations.
npcs.current_goal: Lead diving and salvage expeditions around Jaya, accumulating treasure and sustaining the Saruyama Alliance.
npcs.long_term_dream: To prove the worth of the Saruyama Alliance as the greatest salvagers and divers in the Grand Line.
npcs.mood: Energetic and combative, always ready for a new expedition or a brawl.
npcs.description: Masira is a muscular man of average height with striking features and a perpetually agitated expression. He wears practical sailor's clothes and carries the unmistakable air of someone who has spent decades on the ocean floor. He gives the impression of a confident brawler — loud, physical, and hard to intimidate.

current_state.summary_text: Masira commands the Masira Pirates from Jaya, conducting diving and salvage expeditions together with the Saruyama Alliance. His whereabouts and activities remain largely unchanged since the events of Skypiea.

npcs.name: Shoujou
npcs.base_backstory: Shoujou is the captain of the Shoujou Pirates and a member of the Saruyama Alliance, a group founded around the dream of finding the legendary Sky Island. Brother of Masira, he operates near Jaya salvaging shipwrecks using unconventional methods. He took part in the expedition that helped the Straw Hat Pirates reach Skypiea, fulfilling Montblanc Cricket's dream of confirming Skypiea's existence. After that journey concluded, he returned to his usual activities in the seas around Jaya.
npcs.current_goal: Lead salvage and shipwreck recovery missions in the waters around Jaya.
npcs.long_term_dream: Continue the deeds of the Saruyama Alliance and honor Montblanc Cricket's legacy on the seas.
npcs.mood: Energetic and confident, operating with the satisfaction of someone who has already seen his alliance's dream come true.
npcs.description: A heavyset man with an intimidating appearance, wild hair, and a posture reminiscent of a gorilla — hence the thematic nickname of his crew. He wears practical pirate clothing and carries a presence that commands respect. His most feared weapon is not visible: it is his voice, capable of emitting sonic screams that disorient and damage enemies in combat.

current_state.summary_text: Shoujou leads the Shoujou Pirates in the waters of Jaya, continuing salvage and recovery operations as part of the Saruyama Alliance. His exact whereabouts post-Egghead are unknown, but his base of operations remains in the Jaya region.

npcs.name: Doc Q
npcs.base_backstory: Doc Q has been part of the Blackbeard Pirates since the crew's earliest days, serving as the crew's doctor despite appearing to suffer from chronic poor health. He revealed during the confrontation at Whole Cake Island that he is a user of the Shiku Shiku no Mi, capable of transmitting diseases to whomever he chooses, which made him suddenly far more dangerous than his appearance suggested. Riding his sickly horse Stronger — who also consumed a Devil Fruit — he sails the seas alongside Blackbeard. Following Blackbeard's rise to Yonko status and the consolidation of Hachinosu as a base of operations, Doc Q remains stationed there as the fleet's healer and biological terror agent.
npcs.current_goal: Serve as doctor and biological weapon of Blackbeard's fleet, keeping the crew operational while spreading diseases among the Yonko's enemies.
npcs.long_term_dream: Remain at Blackbeard's side as he reshapes the world, witnessing — and catalyzing — the chaos and decay he believes to be the true nature of existence.
npcs.mood: Quietly threatening, with a serenity that one cannot tell is genuine illness or calculated performance.
npcs.description: Doc Q is an extremely thin man with a cadaverous appearance, always mounted on the skeletal horse Stronger — both seeming to be on the verge of death at any moment. He wears clothing reminiscent of a doctor from a bygone era, carries a scythe as both weapon and symbol, and his eyes hold a calm that does not match the fragility of his body. The impression he gives is of something rotten on the inside, waiting for the right moment to contaminate everything around him.

current_state.summary_text: Doc Q operates in Hachinosu as doctor and biological warfare agent of the Blackbeard Pirates, wielding the Shiku Shiku no Mi to inflict diseases on enemies while keeping the Yonko's crew functional.

npcs.name: Jesus Burgess
npcs.base_backstory: Helmsman and frontline fighter of the Blackbeard Pirates since the crew's earliest days, Burgess represented the crew in the Mera Mera no Mi tournament in Dressrosa, where he faced Luffy and was defeated. After the battle, he attempted to kill Trafalgar Law to steal his Devil Fruit, without success. With Blackbeard's rise to Yonko status and the takeover of Hachinosu, Burgess cemented himself as one of the crew's pillars of combat. He acquired the Riki Riki no Mi, which amplifies his already colossal physical strength to absurd levels.
npcs.current_goal: Strengthen the Blackbeard Pirates' position in Hachinosu and prove himself the most powerful fighter in the crew.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the strongest man in the world, worthy of fighting alongside the future King of the Pirates.
npcs.mood: Confident and aggressive, ready for combat at any moment.
npcs.description: Burgess is a man of monumental stature and musculature, wearing a wrestling mask that covers his face and a wrestler's jumpsuit. The Riki Riki no Mi allows his muscle mass to expand to grotesque proportions in combat, turning him into a wall of flesh and strength. The impression he gives is that of a ring predator who treats any confrontation as personal entertainment.

current_state.summary_text: Helmsman of the Blackbeard Pirates stationed in Hachinosu, Burgess operates as the crew's primary shock force, wielding the Riki Riki no Mi in combination with Armament Haki.

npcs.name: Wyper
npcs.base_backstory: A direct descendant of Calgara, the legendary Shandian warrior, Wyper grew up bearing the weight of four hundred years of struggle to return to the sacred land of Shandora. He became the warrior leader of the Shandians in Skypiea, leading relentless attacks against the Skypieans and the self-proclaimed god Enel. During the battle of Skypiea, he used a Reject Dial against Enel at nearly fatal cost, being defeated by the power of the Goro Goro no Mi. After Enel's defeat at Luffy's hands and the reconciliation between the Shandians and the Skypieans, Wyper remained in Skypiea as guardian of his people and the reconquered land.
npcs.current_goal: Protect the Shandian people and preserve the sacred reconquered land of Shandora in Skypiea.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor Calgara's legacy and ensure the Shandians never lose their homeland again.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and resolute — the peace with the Skypieans is real, but Wyper does not let his guard down.
npcs.description: Wyper is a man of athletic and striking build, with tribal markings on his face and a permanently tense expression, like someone who has never fully stood down. He carries Seastone weapons and dials with the ease of someone born on a battlefield. His presence intimidates before any word is spoken — he projects the raw authority of a warrior who has already died and come back for his people's cause.

current_state.summary_text: Wyper remains in Skypiea as the warrior leader of the Shandians, guardian of the sacred land of Shandora following Enel's defeat. His existence is virtually unknown to the world beyond the clouds.

npcs.name: Gan Fall
npcs.base_backstory: Gan Fall served for decades as the God of Skypiea, the protective ruler of the clouds, before being deposed by Enel during the tyrant's takeover of the sky archipelago. After Enel's defeat at the hands of Monkey D. Luffy, Gan Fall reclaimed his post as God, restoring peace between the Skypieans and the Shandians. Over time, he passed the title of God to another and took on the role of Knight of the Sky, continuing to protect Skypiea with his lance, dials, and his faithful bird-horse Pierre.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Skypiea and its inhabitants as the Knight of the Sky.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that Skypiea remains at peace and free from tyrants, so that his people never suffer again as they did under Enel.
npcs.mood: Calm and vigilant, carrying the serenity of someone who found his place after years of struggle.
npcs.description: Gan Fall is an elderly man of upright bearing and grave countenance, with long white hair and a thick mustache that reinforce his appearance as a noble veteran. He rides Pierre, a horse with bird wings, giving him an imposing presence in the skies. He wields a lance with precision and carries dials strategically, conveying the image of a warrior who combines experience with ingenuity.

current_state.summary_text: Gan Fall serves as the Knight of the Sky in Skypiea, dedicated to protecting the sky archipelago after decades as its God. His whereabouts and situation are stable and well known among the cloud dwellers.

npcs.name: Mont Blanc Cricket
npcs.base_backstory: A direct descendant of Mont Blanc Noland, the explorer who reported the existence of a city of gold in the skies and was executed as a liar, Cricket grew up bearing the weight of his family's disgrace and was expelled from the nobility of Lvneel. He settled on the island of Jaya, where he spent decades diving in the submerged wreckage in search of proof that would rehabilitate Noland's name and confirm the existence of Skypiea. He became the informal leader of the Saruyama Alliance alongside Masira and Shoujou, who support him in his search. When Luffy and his allies passed through Jaya, Cricket helped them fire the Knock Up Stream and reach the sky island, finally obtaining confirmation that Noland had told the truth.
npcs.current_goal: Continue investigating the history of the sky islands and preserve Noland's legacy, now with the certainty that his ancestor told the truth.
npcs.long_term_dream: Fully rehabilitate the name of Mont Blanc Noland before the world and prove that his family's lineage was never one of liars.
npcs.mood: Determined and at peace with himself — he carries the weight of the past more lightly since Skypiea's existence was confirmed.
npcs.description: Cricket is a middle-aged man of robust build, marked by years of diving in the open sea — weathered skin, dense muscles, and work scars. He dresses simply and practically, with the appearance of someone who has spent his entire life on the margins of society. He radiates a quiet stubbornness that blends melancholy and determination, like a man who carried an unjust burden for decades and refused to bend the knee before it.

current_state.summary_text: Cricket lives in Jaya alongside the Saruyama Alliance, continuing his research into the history of the sky islands and Noland's legacy. The confirmation of Skypiea's existence ended decades of doubt, but did not extinguish his pursuit of knowledge and recognition.

npcs.name: Enel
npcs.base_backstory: Originally from Birka, Enel destroyed his home island with his own power before ascending to the skies and conquering Skypiea, deposing the rightful ruler Gan Fall and installing a reign of terror under the title of "God". Defeated by Monkey D. Luffy — whose rubber body made him immune to lightning — Enel abandoned Skypiea aboard his ark Maxim and set off toward the moon, fulfilling his obsession with reaching "Fairy Vearth". On the moon, he encountered the automata Spaceys and ruins of an ancient civilization, establishing his new base of power there.
npcs.current_goal: Rule the moon as his new divine domain and uncover the secrets of the ancient civilization that once inhabited the satellite.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized and worshipped as a true god, sovereign of worlds beyond the reach of common mortals.
npcs.mood: Serene and sovereign — he rules the moon unopposed and unhurried.
npcs.description: Enel is a tall, dark-skinned man with long blonde hair wrapped around golden drums on the sides of his head. He dresses in white and gold garments that reinforce his deity aesthetic, and carries the staff Nonosama Bo. His presence is suffocating — he looks at everything and everyone the way a monarch regards insects, and the silence surrounding him carries the permanent tension of a lightning bolt about to strike.

current_state.summary_text: Enel inhabits the moon as his personal domain, commanding the automata Spaceys over the ruins of an ancient civilization. His whereabouts are unknown to nearly the entire world, which considers him to have disappeared from Skypiea.

npcs.name: Pagaya
npcs.base_backstory: Pagaya is a Skypiean engineer specializing in dials and Sky Island technology, recognized among his people for his craftsmanship and inventiveness. He is the father of Conis, whom he raised alone in the clouds of Skypiea. During the Straw Hat Pirates' passage through Skypiea, he assisted the group by repairing the Waver and contributed to Enel's defeat. After Skypiea's liberation from Enel's rule, he remained on the island, contributing his technical expertise to the community's reconstruction and defense.
npcs.current_goal: Improve Skypiea's defenses and infrastructure using dial technology.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure lasting prosperity and safety for the people of Skypiea through engineering.
npcs.mood: Calm and dedicated to his daily work on the island.
npcs.description: A middle-aged man of average height, with a distinctive mustache and a gentle expression that conveys trustworthiness. He wears the practical work clothes typical of Skypieans. He gives the impression of a patient and reliable craftsman, more at ease with tools in hand than in confrontations.

current_state.summary_text: Pagaya lives in Skypiea, where he works as a dial engineer in service of the Skypiean community. His daughter Conis remains by his side, and the island enjoys the peace won after Enel's defeat.

npcs.name: Conis
npcs.base_backstory: Conis grew up in Skypiea under Enel's oppressive regime, learning from an early age to move with caution so as not to draw the wrath of the self-proclaimed god. During the Straw Hat Pirates' invasion, she risked her own life by revealing Enel's trap to the visitors, being sentenced to death for it — and survived. After Enel's defeat, she helped orchestrate the pirates' escape on the Going Merry, piloting her Waver with precision. With the fall of the regime, she began living freely in Skypiea, taking part in rebuilding coexistence between the Bilkans and the Shandia.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Skypiea and its inhabitants, assisting anyone who needs help in the sky islands.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Skypiea prosper in peace, with Bilkans and Shandia living in harmony.
npcs.mood: Serene and resolute, carrying the memory of the times under Enel as motivation to never again stay silent in the face of injustice.
npcs.description: A young woman with light hair and the white wings characteristic of the Bilkans, Conis has a delicate appearance that contrasts with the firmness she shows when the moment demands it. She dresses simply and practically for life in the clouds. Those who meet her quickly notice that behind the gentleness lies a courage that has already placed her before death without making her retreat.

current_state.summary_text: Conis lives in Skypiea, where she serves as a guide and protector of her community following the fall of Enel's regime. She pilots her Waver with skill and remains ready to help those who arrive at the sky islands.

npcs.name: Ohm
npcs.base_backstory: Ohm is one of Enel's four priests in Skypiea, responsible for the Iron Judgment — the most lethal trial of the White Space. He wields the Eisen Whip, an extendable sword made of iron cloud that can be manipulated like a whip with surgical precision. He fought Zoro during the Straw Hats' invasion of Skypiea and was defeated after a long duel. With Enel's departure for the Moon, the priests' power structure in Skypiea collapsed.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain order in Skypiea in Enel's absence, exercising residual authority as a priest.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve and carry out the will of an absolute god with martial perfection.
npcs.mood: Restrained and without clear purpose since Enel's departure — discipline without an object.
npcs.description: Ohm is a man of average height and athletic build, with light hair and a permanently serene expression that borders on emptiness. He wears white priestly robes and carries the Eisen Whip across his back, a sword of ordinary appearance that reveals itself to be an extension of malleable iron cloud. His presence radiates absolute control — there is no agitation, no hesitation, only the certainty of one who has already decided the outcome before the fight begins.

current_state.summary_text: A priest defeated by the Straw Hats during the invasion of Skypiea, Ohm remains on the island after Enel's departure for the Moon, his authority and purpose fragmented without the god he served.

npcs.name: Yama
npcs.base_backstory: Yama served as commander of Enel's Divine Soldiers in Skypiea, being the most powerful of the three commanders in his service. A specialist in a brutal form of heavyweight sumo, he was tasked with carrying out Enel's orders and eliminating any threat to his authority. He was defeated by Robin during the events of the Skypiea arc, after indiscriminately destroying the island's sacred flora. With Enel's fall and his departure for the Moon, the divine army was dissolved and Yama lost his post.
npcs.current_goal: Find a new purpose or leadership following the dissolution of Enel's divine army.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve a supreme power worthy of his loyalty and prove his strength as the greatest warrior of the skies.
npcs.mood: Drifting without clear direction, maintaining a martial bearing out of habit.
npcs.description: Yama is a man of immense stature and mass, with a body built to absorb and distribute impact. His physical presence dominates any space — broad shoulders, upright posture, permanently severe expression. He gives the impression of a walking wall: there is no urgency in his movements, only the certainty that whatever he hits will not get back up.

current_state.summary_text: With Enel gone to the Moon and the divine army dissolved, Yama remains in Skypiea without formal command, his strength intact but his loyalty without a destination.

npcs.name: Paulie
npcs.base_backstory: Paulie grew up in Water 7 and built his career as a shipwright at the Galley-La Company, rising to the position of foreman of Dock 1. During the crisis in Water 7 and the CP9 invasion, he fought alongside the Straw Hats to protect the city and exposed Iceburg's betrayal, proving his unconditional loyalty.
