After CP9's defeat and the restoration of order in Water 7, he resumed his post at Galley-La. With the construction of the Thousand Sunny completed by the Franky Family, he continued his shipwright work in the city.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Galley-La Company running and protect Water 7 from any threat.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the greatest shipwright in Water 7 and pay off all his gambling debts.
npcs.mood: Agitated and hardworking, always with some complaint on the tip of his tongue but ready to act when necessary.
npcs.description: Paulie is a stocky man of average height, with blond hair tied back and a perpetually irritated expression. He carries ropes coiled all over his body, used both in work and in combat. He gives the impression of someone who speaks before thinking, but who never abandons those who depend on him.

current_state.summary_text: Paulie serves as foreman of Dock 1 at the Galley-La Company in Water 7, maintaining the shipbuilding routine and the city's security. His loyalty to Iceburg and to the city remains unshakeable.

npcs.name: Gedatsu
npcs.base_backstory: Gedatsu served as one of Enel's four priests in Skypiea, responsible for guarding the Swamp Ordeal and eliminating intruders. Despite his disoriented and frequently absent-minded behavior, he demonstrated considerable martial skills and developed Observation Haki. He was defeated by Chopper and Usopp during the events of Skypiea. After Enel's defeat, he descended to the Blue Sea and established a new life on Ukkari Hot-Spring Island, where he works at the hot springs alongside his father.
npcs.current_goal: Manage the Ukkari Hot Springs alongside his father with the same devotion he gave to his priestly duties.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve with purpose and discipline, whether as a sacred guardian or as a hot-spring worker — the form matters little.
npcs.mood: Calm and absent-minded, absorbed in the daily tasks of the hot springs.
npcs.description: Gedatsu is a man of average height with vacant eyes and a permanently distant expression, as if he were always halfway between two thoughts. He has dressed simply since leaving Skypiea, but maintains the upright posture of someone trained to kill. To those who meet him, he gives a strange impression: he seems harmless until he no longer does.

current_state.summary_text: Gedatsu lives and works on Ukkari Hot-Spring Island, far from Skypiea and the conflicts of the Grand Line. His eccentricity remains intact; his dangerousness, underestimated.

npcs.name: Satori
npcs.base_backstory: Satori served as one of Enel's four priests in Skypiea, tasked with guarding the Forest Ordeal — the second stage of the trial imposed on intruders. He used Surprise and Trial Balls hidden within his own body to attack by surprise, combining this with Kenbunshoku Haki to anticipate opponents' movements. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy during the invasion of Skypiea, after Luffy discovered that attacking without thinking made his movements unpredictable. With Enel's fall and the end of his dominion over Skypiea, Satori's role as guardian-priest lost its reason to exist.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain his position and relevance in Skypiea after the collapse of Enel's theocracy.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve a supreme power and be feared as the undisputed guardian of the skies.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and resentful, without the master to whom he devoted absolute loyalty.
npcs.description: Satori is a man of average height and a rotund body, with Skypiean wings on his back and a permanently haughty expression. He wears priestly robes and carries Surprise and Trial Balls concealed on his body, which he fires in unpredictable bursts. He gives the impression of an overconfident fanatic — dangerous precisely because he believes the judgment has already been decided before the fight begins.

current_state.summary_text: With Enel gone toward the Moon, Satori remains in Skypiea without a master or clear purpose, guarding a trial forest whose ritual has lost its authority. His exact loyalty and whereabouts are unknown to the peoples of the Blue Sea.

npcs.name: Blueno
npcs.base_backstory: Blueno was trained from a young age as a CP9 agent, operating for years undercover as a bartender in Water Seven while monitoring the Galley-La shipyards. He took part in the Enies Lobby mission to retrieve Nico Robin, where he was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy. After the operational dissolution of the classic CP9, he was reintegrated into the World Government's structures and ascended to CP0, the most powerful arm of the Cipher Pol.
npcs.current_goal: Execute CP0 missions with maximum efficiency, eliminating threats to the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the World Government as its most precise instrument — without failures, without traces.
npcs.mood: Neutral and operational.
npcs.description: Blueno is a man of imposing stature, with dark hair combed back and a severe face that rarely betrays any emotion. He wears dark formal attire befitting high-ranking Cipher Pol agents. His presence is contained and calculated — he does not threaten with words, only with silence and positioning.

current_state.summary_text: Blueno operates as an active CP0 agent, his exact whereabouts unknown following the events of Egghead. He combines complete mastery of the Rokushiki and the Doa Doa no Mi with Armament Haki, remaining among the World Government's most feared field agents.

npcs.name: Kalifa
npcs.base_backstory: Kalifa infiltrated the Galley-La Company as Iceburg's personal secretary for years, accumulating information for the Cipher Pol while maintaining an impeccable cover. Her identity as a CP9 agent was revealed at Enies Lobby, where she faced Nami in combat and was defeated. After the formal dissolution of CP9, she was absorbed into CP0 and resumed active operations in service of the World Government. She remains active as a field agent, carrying out high-risk missions with the same cold efficiency as always.
npcs.current_goal: Execute the missions assigned by CP0 with absolute precision and without leaving traces.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the World Government as the perfect agent — invisible, relentless, and irreplaceable.
npcs.mood: Focused and impassive.
npcs.description: Kalifa is a woman of average height, with pinned blond hair and round glasses that give her the appearance of a meticulous secretary — a disguise she used for years to conceal her nature as an assassin. She dresses with austere elegance, generally in neutral tones. Her presence is cold and calculated; she smiles easily, but the smile never reaches her eyes.

current_state.summary_text: Kalifa operates as an active CP0 agent, her exact whereabouts unknown, conducting missions in service of the World Government with the same efficiency that defined her career in CP9.

npcs.name: Rob Lucci
npcs.devil_fruit: Neko Neko no Mi: Modelo Leopardo
npcs.base_backstory: Recruited by the World Government as a child, Lucci was forged into a weapon from an early age, becoming the most lethal agent of CP9 before reaching adulthood. His personal philosophy — that strength justifies existence and weakness deserves extinction — guided every mission with absolute efficiency. Defeated by Monkey D. Luffy at Enies Lobby, he was temporarily removed from service, but returned as a CP0 agent, elevated to the highest operational level of the Government. At Egghead, he confronted Luffy again and was neutralized during the incident, surviving and returning to active duty.
npcs.current_goal: Continue executing CP0 missions with total precision, reaffirming his position as the most lethal agent of the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be the perfect weapon — to exist solely as pure force in the service of the power he deems worthy of serving.
npcs.mood: Cold and operational. No visible residue from the Egghead incident.
npcs.description: Lucci is a man of athletic and rigid build, with short black hair and yellow feline-pupiled eyes that betray the fruit he carries. He wears the standard dark suit of CP0 with the white mask when on official missions. His presence silences rooms — not through the performative intimidation of a bully, but through the objective sense that he calculated every angle of the room before entering.

current_state.summary_text: Active CP0 agent, Lucci operates on World Government missions after surviving the Egghead incident. His exact whereabouts are classified, but his return to service is confirmed.

npcs.name: Fukurou
npcs.base_backstory: Fukurou is a CP9 agent trained in the six techniques of Rokushiki, recruited by the World Government for infiltration and elimination missions. He took part in the operation at Enies Lobby to deliver Nico Robin to the World Government, and was defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates during the assault on the island. After the debacle at Enies Lobby, CP9 was disbanded and its agents were left on the margins; Fukurou and the other survivors regrouped under Rob Lucci before eventually returning to the service of the World Government as CP0.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out intelligence and elimination missions in the service of the World Government as a Cipher Pol agent.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve the World Government with absolute excellence, without failures or traces.
npcs.mood: Impassive and professional, operating in the shadows without drawing attention.
npcs.description: Fukurou is a man of average height with one impossible-to-ignore feature: his mouth has a functional zipper, which he opens and closes deliberately when speaking. He wears the standard dark attire of Cipher Pol agents and carries a cold, contained presence. His blank expression and economical movements convey the sense of someone who calculates every step before taking it.

current_state.summary_text: Fukurou is a Cipher Pol agent who survived the Enies Lobby operation, whose exact whereabouts remain unknown; he is presumed to be operating on classified missions in the service of the World Government.

npcs.name: Jabra
npcs.devil_fruit: Inu Inu no Mi: Modelo Lobo
npcs.base_backstory: Jabra was one of CP9's elite agents, recruited and trained from a young age to serve the World Government as an assassin and spy. He took part in the mission at Enies Lobby to recover the Poneglyph and Robin, where he was defeated by Sanji. After the dissolution of Spandam's CP9, he was reintegrated into the World Government's apparatus as a CP0 agent, continuing to operate in the shadows. He maintains a fierce rivalry with Rob Lucci, marked by constant clashes that mask a veiled mutual respect.
npcs.current_goal: Execute CP0 missions and assert his superiority over Rob Lucci within the World Government hierarchy.
npcs.long_term_dream: To prove himself the most lethal and indispensable agent of the World Government, surpassing Lucci in recognition and power.
npcs.mood: Aggressive and combat-ready, with poorly concealed impatience beneath a layer of calculated arrogance.
npcs.description: Jabra is a muscular man of average height, with dark hair and fierce eyes that convey constant predation. He wears the standard black suit of Cipher Pol agents, but carries an animalistic posture — tense shoulders, abrupt movements — that betrays the beast lurking beneath the clothes. His presence in a room raises the tension: his expression shifts between the crooked grin of a con man and the fixed stare of a predator about to strike.

current_state.summary_text: Operates as a CP0 agent following reintegration into the World Government, exact whereabouts unknown. He remains one of the most dangerous human weapons in the governmental apparatus, his rivalry with Lucci intact.

npcs.name: Kumadori
npcs.base_backstory: Kumadori served as a CP9 agent under Spandam's supervision, being deployed to Enies Lobby during the operation to extradite Nico Robin. He faced Chopper in the World Court and was defeated after being locked in a refrigerator. After CP9's defeat and the flight from Enies Lobby, the group was abandoned by the World Government; Kumadori and the other CP9 survivors operated on their own for a period before returning to government service.
npcs.current_goal: Reclaim his post as a World Government agent and prove his worth after the disgrace of Enies Lobby.
npcs.long_term_dream: To achieve martial perfection and honor the name of CP9 with his theatrical combat art.
npcs.mood: Resolute and performative, carrying the shame of defeat with dramatic exaggeration.
npcs.description: Kumadori is a tall man of unusual appearance, with long white hair that covers part of his face and kabuki makeup in red and white. He wears traditional Japanese-style clothing and carries a staff as his main weapon. He makes an immediate impression of absolute eccentricity — somewhere between the ridiculous and the genuinely threatening, his presence is impossible to ignore.

current_state.summary_text: Kumadori is a former CP9 agent whose whereabouts after reintegration into the World Government remain unknown. His loyalty to the government holds, but his current operational position is uncertain.

npcs.name: Kokoro
npcs.base_backstory: Kokoro is a mermaid who worked alongside Tom, the legendary shipwright of Water 7, serving as his assistant and learning the maritime operations of the region. After Tom's execution by the Marines, she took over as conductor and station chief of the Puffing Tom Sea Train, keeping the line operational. During the crisis at Water 7 and Enies Lobby, she was a key figure in the rescue of Nico Robin and the Straw Hat Pirates, piloting the Puffing Tom in a decisive maneuver. Since then, she has remained in Water 7 as a local figure of reference, raising Chimney as a granddaughter.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Sea Train running and look after the well-being of Chimney and the Water 7 community.
npcs.long_term_dream: To honor Tom's legacy by keeping alive the sea route he helped build.
npcs.mood: Calm and content with her routine, steady at her post.
npcs.description: Kokoro is an elderly mermaid with disheveled white hair and features marked by years of work at sea. She wears simple dockworker's clothes and is rarely seen without a drink in hand. Despite her relaxed appearance, she projects a calm, quiet authority — those who know her understand that behind the easy humor lie decades of experience in the waters of Water 7.

current_state.summary_text: Kokoro runs the Sea Train station in Water 7, keeping the line operational and looking after Chimney. Her life follows the rhythm of the tides and the departures of the Puffing Tom.

npcs.name: Spandam
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Spandine, former director of CP9, Spandam inherited his father's position and cynicism. He commanded CP9 during Operation Pluton at Enies Lobby, where he orchestrated the kidnapping of Nico Robin and Franky to extract the Poneglyphs. The operation failed catastrophically: CP9 was defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates and Spandam himself was brutally beaten by Robin before fleeing. Despite the disaster at Enies Lobby, he rose to the directorship of CP0, the most powerful of the World Government's intelligence agencies.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate CP0's authority over Egghead and capture or eliminate threats to the World Government in the region.
npcs.long_term_dream: To accumulate enough power and prestige to be recognized as the most influential man in the World Government's intelligence agencies, surpassing his father's legacy.
npcs.mood: Tense and calculating, operating in an unstable theater of operations following the events of Egghead.
npcs.description: A man of average height with an exaggerated-featured face and a prominent scar crossing it — a permanent reminder of his encounter with Nico Robin at Enies Lobby. He wears the standard dark suit of Cipher Pol agents and carries Funkfreed, a long sword housing a Zoan elephant, at his side. His presence projects inflated authority; up close, the nervousness in his eyes betrays the cowardice behind the facade.

current_state.summary_text: Director of CP0, Spandam operates from Egghead imposing the World Government's agenda with the formal authority of his office and the elephant sword Funkfreed as a symbol of power. His real influence depends more on hierarchy and manipulation than on his own combat ability.

npcs.name: T-Bone
npcs.base_backstory: T-Bone served as a Marine Captain in the South Blue, earning a reputation for his genuine devotion to protecting civilians — rare among the military ranks. His unusual face, with angular and prominent features, contrasted with his gentle and altruistic character. During the Puffing Tom's advance toward Enies Lobby, T-Bone leapt onto the waters to intercept what he believed to be a threat, and was defeated by Zoro. He was later murdered by a civilian he himself had protected, an act that ended him quietly and without public glory.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: To protect every innocent civilian in the world, without exception and without expecting recognition.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: T-Bone is a tall, thin man with extremely angular features — a prominent jaw, deep-set eyes, and a sharp nose that give him an almost frightening appearance at first glance. He wears the Marine Captain's uniform with austerity, without unnecessary adornments. The contradiction between his severe appearance and his genuinely gentle behavior causes immediate unease in those who meet him for the first time.

current_state.summary_text: T-Bone is dead, murdered by a civilian he himself was protecting — a quiet, inglorious end for one of the most honorable officers in the South Blue. His name circulates little beyond the Marines' internal records.

npcs.name: Tom
npcs.base_backstory: Tom founded Tom's Workers in Water 7 and became the world's most celebrated shipwright, responsible for building the Oro Jackson, the ship that carried Gol D. Roger to become King of the Pirates. His prestige was such that the World Government granted him a temporary pardon for the crime of building Roger's ship, in exchange for his participation in the Sea Train project. Betrayed by his apprentice Spandam, who manipulated the incident of the attack on Water 7 to frame him, Tom was sentenced and sent to Enies Lobby by Buster Call, sacrificing himself to protect Iceburg and Franky. His legacy lives on in his two apprentices and in the philosophy that every ship deserves to be built with pride.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased.
npcs.long_term_dream: To build ships that carry anyone to anywhere in the world, and to die with no shame for his own work.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
npcs.description: Tom is an immense Fishman of imposing appearance, with dark blue skin, visible gills, and a musculature that impresses even the most robust shipwrights. He wears a worn work apron with tools hanging from his belt, and carries in his bearing the relaxed posture of someone who never needed to prove anything to anyone. His mere presence in a dock silences apprentices and makes veterans straighten up.

current_state.summary_text: Tom died executed at Enies Lobby, sentenced by the World Government after being betrayed by Spandam. His legacy endures in Iceburg and Franky, the two apprentices he shaped, and in the shipbuilding philosophy that still defines Water 7.

npcs.name: Absalom
npcs.base_backstory: Absalom was one of Gecko Moria's officers on Thriller Bark, commanding the Zombie Soldiers and obsessed with marrying Nami. After Moria's defeat, he abandoned Thriller Bark and began operating as a ghost journalist under the pseudonym Absa, using his invisibility to cover world events without being detected. He was captured by Catarina Devon, who assumed his identity and appearance using the Inu Inu no Mi, Model: Nine-Tailed Fox, while Absalom was eliminated by the Blackbeard Pirates in Dressrosa.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: To marry the woman of his dreams and live in absolute impunity under the cover of invisibility.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: Absalom is a muscular man with a face partially replaced by animal parts — lion jaws and transplanted eyes — the result of Dr. Hogback's surgeries. He wears dark formal clothing that contrasts with his disturbing behavior. He gives the impression of veiled menace and discomfort, like someone who may be present without being seen.

current_state.summary_text: Absalom was killed by the Blackbeard Pirates in Dressrosa; his Suke Suke no Mi was stolen and his identity is now used by Catarina Devon as a disguise.

npcs.name: Dr. Hogback
npcs.base_backstory: Hogback was once one of the most celebrated surgeons in the world, capable of performing operations considered impossible and acclaimed as a medical genius. He fell in love with the warrior Cindry, who died in an accident before he could confess his feelings. Consumed by obsession, he joined Gecko Moria and used the Yonko's Kage Kage no Mi to resurrect Cindry's corpse as a zombie, placing her under his control. He went on to serve as chief scientist of Thriller Bark, developing the shadow-transplant technology that sustained Moria's zombie army.
npcs.current_goal: To preserve his own life and continue his experiments with corpses away from hostile eyes.
npcs.long_term_dream: To master the boundary between life and death through surgery, achieving a scientific immortality by proxy.
npcs.mood: Apprehensive and erratic — without Moria's protection, he operates in the shadows with growing paranoia.
npcs.description: A heavyset middle-aged man with disheveled dark hair and a stained white lab coat he wears as a status symbol. His broad face carries a perpetually self-satisfied expression that crumbles into grotesque panic when events slip beyond his control. He exudes the performative authority of someone who spent decades being applauded — and the unease of someone who knows he didn't deserve all that applause.

current_state.summary_text: Hogback remains associated with Thriller Bark following Gecko Moria's defeat and disappearance; his exact whereabouts are uncertain, but he is still alive and at large, with no faction to protect him.

npcs.name: Perona
npcs.base_backstory: Perona grew up on Thriller Bark under the tutelage of Gecko Moria, becoming one of the Four Great Commanders of his crew. She commands Perona's Wonder Garden with an army of zombies and Hollows at her disposal. After Moria's defeat on Thriller Bark by Admiral Kizaru and the collapse of the crew, she was teleported by Bartholomew Kuma to Kuraigana Island, where she ended up living alongside Roronoa Zoro during the timeskip. She remains on the island after the Straw Hat Pirates' reunion, tending to the ruined castle alongside Mihawk.
npcs.current_goal: To maintain the castle on Kuraigana and await news of Gecko Moria, to whom she still holds loyalty.
npcs.long_term_dream: To live surrounded by cute and frightening creatures in her own gothic kingdom, with no one to defy her.
npcs.mood: Bored and mildly melancholic, keeping the castle's routine with Mihawk as her only constant company.
npcs.description: Perona is a young woman with long pink hair, always dressed in a gothic-lolita outfit in shades of red, black, and white, carrying a doll-shaped umbrella. Her delicate appearance and large eyes contrast with the unsettling power of her ghosts, creating the impression of a porcelain doll capable of draining the will to live from any opponent.

current_state.summary_text: Perona inhabits the ruined castle on Kuraigana Island, sharing the space with Dracule Mihawk; her whereabouts and intentions following the events at Egghead remain unknown to the world at large.

npcs.name: Victoria Cindry
npcs.base_backstory: Victoria Cindry was a famous and beloved actress, engaged to a man who had proposed to her, but she rejected Dr. Hogback's proposal — he was in love with her — and died in an accident. Hogback, unable to overcome his loss, used his knowledge of necromancy in partnership with Gecko Moria to revive her as a zombie, erasing her original personality and replacing it with absolute obedience. As a zombie, she served Hogback on Thriller Bark, carrying out his orders and fighting intruders with metal plates and superhuman durability. After Moria's defeat in the battle against the Straw Hat Pirates, the shadow animating her was returned to its original owner, and Cindry's body reverted to an inanimate corpse.
npcs.current_goal: None — Cindry's body has been inanimate since the shadow animating it was expelled.
npcs.long_term_dream: In life, she dreamed of genuine love; as a zombie, she had no dreams of her own — only Hogback's commands.
npcs.mood: Inert
npcs.description: Victoria Cindry bears the preserved appearance of a young woman of striking beauty, with long dark hair and vacant eyes that betray the absence of real life behind them. She wears an elegant dress that contrasts with the cadaverous coldness of her movements. Even inanimate, there is something unsettling in her expression — as though the beauty of the actress she once was still haunts the face of what remains.

current_state.summary_text: Victoria Cindry's body remains on Thriller Bark, inert since the shadow animating it was returned to its original owner following Gecko Moria's defeat. Her existence as a functional zombie ended there; what remains is only the corpse of the actress Hogback refused to let rest.

npcs.name: Oars
npcs.base_backstory: Oars was an ancient giant of legendary fame, feared as the "Devil of the Giants" for his brutality and ambition to conquer the world. He died centuries ago and his colossal corpse was preserved in the cold of a frozen island until it was recovered by Gecko Moria. Moria implanted Monkey D. Luffy's shadow into his body, reanimating him as Thriller Bark's supreme zombie and endowing him with the Straw Hat's instincts and mannerisms. During the confrontation on Thriller Bark, he was defeated by Luffy's allies after a devastating battle, and his corpse remained on the island when Moria abandoned it.
npcs.current_goal: No active will of his own — his inanimate body has lain on Thriller Bark since his defeat.
npcs.long_term_dream: To conquer the entire world — the dream he carried in life and that Luffy's shadow reignited in his zombie form.
npcs.mood: Inactive — defeated and without a host shadow.
npcs.description: Oars is an ancient giant of absurd proportions, many times larger than a common giant, with the grey-green, putrefied skin of a reanimated corpse. His body bears the marks of ancient battles and the scars of Moria's experimentation. His presence on the battlefield is less that of a soldier and more that of a natural disaster in motion — each step shakes the ground, each punch brings down entire structures.

current_state.summary_text: Oars's corpse remains on Thriller Bark following the expulsion of Luffy's shadow; without a shadow to animate it, it is merely a colossal inert body, a monument to Gecko Moria's most ambitious experiment.

npcs.name: Kumashi
npcs.base_backstory: Kumashi was an ordinary stuffed bear before being reanimated as a zombie through Gecko Moria's shadow magic on Thriller Bark. He went on to serve Perona as a loyal bodyguard and companion, carrying her on his shoulders and carrying out her orders without hesitation. He remained on Thriller Bark even after the events of the arc, bound to Perona's retinue.
npcs.current_goal: To serve and protect Perona wherever she may be.
npcs.long_term_dream: To exist at Perona's side as her eternal companion.
npcs.mood: Passive and watchful, ready to act on command.
npcs.description: Kumashi is a giant stuffed bear reanimated as a zombie, with worn fur and vacant eyes that convey both a childlike charm and something deeply unsettling. His imposing frame contrasts with the absolute docility with which he carries Perona on his shoulders. He shows no will of his own — he is pure physical presence in service of his mistress.

current_state.summary_text: Kumashi remains Perona's zombie companion, serving her as bodyguard and mount. His exact whereabouts follow Perona's, whose location after Egghead is uncertain.

npcs.name: Lola
npcs.base_backstory: A daughter of Charlotte Linlin (Big Mom), Lola fled an arranged marriage to Prince Loki of Elbaf, ruining the alliance that would have given her mother access to the giants. Disowned and banished from the Charlotte family, she spent years on Thriller Bark's Ghost Ship as a zombie before being freed by the Straw Hat Pirates. After Gecko Moria's defeat, she formed the Rolling Pirates and continued sailing the seas on her own terms. She was spotted in Dressrosa, where she was continuing her independent journey.
npcs.current_goal: To sail the seas with the Rolling Pirates and find the great love of her life.
npcs.long_term_dream: To marry for true love and live free, far from the shadow of the Charlotte family.
npcs.mood: Lively and hopeful, indifferent to the chaos that the fall of Big Mom's empire has caused the world.
npcs.description: Lola is a stout woman of average height, with dark hair and a permanent smile that radiates infectious energy. She carries an axe and wears practical pirate clothing. The impression she gives is of someone completely devoid of inhibitions — she hugs strangers, declares love within minutes, and leads through sheer personal magnetism.

current_state.summary_text: Lola commands the Rolling Pirates somewhere on the Grand Line, having cut ties with the Charlotte family and charting her own course. Her exact whereabouts are unknown.

npcs.name: Camie
npcs.base_backstory: Camie is a young mermaid who lived in the Sabaody Archipelago, where she worked as a waitress at the floating restaurant Mermaid Cafe and maintained a close friendship with the fish-man octopus Hatchan. She was captured by slave traders in the service of the Celestial Dragons and rescued by Luffy and his allies during the incident at Sabaody. After the timeskip, she returned to Fish-Man Island, where she lives and works, maintaining her bonds with the Straw Hat Pirates who saved her.
npcs.current_goal: To live well among the residents of Fish-Man Island, offering friendship and support to her community.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see humans and merfolk coexist in peace and harmony, free from slavery and prejudice.
npcs.mood: Optimistic and affectionate, at peace with her life on Fish-Man Island.
npcs.description: Camie is a young-looking mermaid with a fish tail and long light-colored hair that floats around her expressive face. Her large eyes immediately convey curiosity and warmth. She radiates a disarming energy — it is hard not to feel welcome in her presence.

current_state.summary_text: Camie lives on Fish-Man Island, where she maintains her routine of friendships and work in the local community. She holds deep admiration for the Straw Hat Pirates who rescued her at Sabaody.

npcs.name: Silvers Rayleigh
npcs.base_backstory: First mate of the Roger Pirates, Rayleigh sailed alongside Gol D. Roger throughout the Great Age of Pirates, reaching the end of the Grand Line and Raftel. After the crew's dissolution and Roger's execution, he retired from piracy and settled in the Sabaody Archipelago as a coating mechanic. He trained Monkey D. Luffy for two years in the mastery of Haki before the attack on Marineford, playing a central role in the formation of the future King of the Pirates. His wife, Shakuyaku, runs a bar in Sabaody.
npcs.current_goal: Live in quiet retirement in Sabaody, observe the new era that Luffy and his generation are building, and preserve, in his own way, Roger's legacy.
npcs.long_term_dream: See someone worthy inherit Roger's will and find the One Piece, completing what the crew began.
npcs.mood: Serene and contemplative, with a quiet satisfaction at what the new generation is achieving.
npcs.description: An elderly man with long silver hair and a beard of the same shade, wearing round glasses and a physique that still betrays decades of combat. He dresses simply, without ostentation. The presence he radiates is silent and absolute — those who know him understand that the calm on the surface conceals a power capable of paralyzing crowds.

current_state.summary_text: Rayleigh remains in Sabaody as a coating mechanic and discreet guiding figure, withdrawing from direct conflicts while following from afar the rise of Monkey D. Luffy to the title of King of the Pirates.

npcs.name: Hatchan
npcs.base_backstory: Hatchan is a six-armed octopus Fish-Man who served as an officer of the Arlong Pirates, where his mastery of six simultaneous swords made him one of the most feared fighters on the crew. After Arlong's defeat by Monkey D. Luffy in East Blue, Hatchan was captured by the Marines and later escaped. He attempted to open a floating takoyaki restaurant but ended up enslaved by Duval before being rescued by the Straw Hat Pirates. Reconciled with Nami and with his past, he settled on Fish-Man Island as a takoyaki vendor, embracing a peaceful life alongside the Sun Pirates.
npcs.current_goal: Sell takoyaki and live in peace on Fish-Man Island, contributing to the harmonious coexistence between Fish-Men and humans.
npcs.long_term_dream: Atone for the violent past alongside Arlong and prove, through everyday life, that Fish-Men and humans can coexist in peace.
npcs.mood: Calm and content with the simple life he has built.
npcs.description: Hatchan is an octopus Fish-Man of imposing stature, with six muscular arms and bluish skin covered in suckers. He carries up to six swords at once, one in each hand, though today he prefers a cook's apron to blades. His open expression and easy smile contrast with his intimidating physique, conveying the impression of someone who has found genuine peace with himself.

current_state.summary_text: Hatchan lives on Fish-Man Island as a traveling takoyaki vendor, far removed from the criminal life he led under Arlong's orders. He maintains bonds of friendship with the Sun Pirates and the Straw Hat Pirates.

npcs.name: Duval
npcs.base_backstory: For years, Duval lived as a fugitive because his face was identical to Sanji's wanted poster, accumulating hatred for the Straw Hat while leading the Flying Fish Riders in the shadows of the Sabaody Archipelago. His encounter with Sanji ended with the cook forcibly remodeling his face; the result pleased Duval so much that he abandoned his hostility and became an ally of the Straw Hats. Since then, he guards their ships and provides logistical support to the group in the archipelago, operating as a fixed contact in Sabaody.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain order in the Sabaody Archipelago and serve as a support point for the Straw Hats whenever needed.
npcs.long_term_dream: Live respected for his new appearance and be recognized as an indispensable ally of the Straw Hats.
npcs.mood: Stable and proud, commands his men with authority and keeps watch over the archipelago.
npcs.description: Duval is a heavyset man with dark hair and a thin mustache, whose face — once the source of tragedies and misunderstandings — was drastically altered by Sanji and now sports features he himself considers handsome. He typically appears mounted on Motobaro, his imposing iron bull, carrying a lance with an air of authority. The combination of dramatic posture and exaggerated vanity makes him a figure that is simultaneously intimidating and comical.

current_state.summary_text: Duval leads the Flying Fish Riders in the Sabaody Archipelago, acting as an ally and logistical support for the Straw Hats. His whereabouts and loyalty have remained consistent since the crew's reunion in Sabaody.

npcs.name: Shakuyaku
npcs.base_backstory: Shakuyaku was pursued by the then-Admiral Garp for over a decade, which attests to her relevance as an active pirate. She retired from the seas and settled in the Sabaody Archipelago, where she runs Shakky's Rip-off Bar and works as an information broker. She became Silvers Rayleigh's life partner, maintaining deep ties with the pirate underworld and with survivors of Gol D. Roger's era. She guided the Straw Hats during their first passage through Sabaody, demonstrating encyclopedic knowledge of the Supernovas and the mechanisms of the New World.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain her information network and offer selective guidance to pirates she considers worthy of attention.
npcs.long_term_dream: Live on the margins of the world, alongside Rayleigh, watching the new generation write the history that she and her contemporaries left unfinished.
npcs.mood: Calm and observant — she follows the tremors of the post-Egghead world with calculated distance and genuine interest.
npcs.description: A middle-aged woman of elegant and relaxed appearance, almost always with a cigarette between her fingers and the expression of someone who has seen it all. She dresses simply but with style, and carries a pistol with the ease of someone who has never needed to use it to prove a point. She immediately gives the impression of knowing more about you than you would expect.

current_state.summary_text: Shakuyaku remains in Sabaody, running her bar and her information network as the world collapses following the events of Egghead. Her whereabouts are stable, but the reach of her network and her recent movements are known only to a few.

npcs.name: Sentomaru
npcs.base_backstory: Sentomaru grew up under Vegapunk's tutelage and became his personal bodyguard, as well as commander of the Marines' Pacifista units. He took part in the incident at the Sabaody Archipelago, where he faced Luffy and the Supernovas. At Egghead, he received orders to capture Vegapunk after the scientist fled with the Straw Hat Pirates, but was gravely wounded by a Seraphim reconfigured on the orders of the Five Elders, leaving him out of action during the island's siege.
npcs.current_goal: Recover from the wounds suffered at Egghead and reaffirm his position in the Marines after the island's collapse.
npcs.long_term_dream: Fulfill his duty to protect those in his charge, honoring Vegapunk's legacy.
npcs.mood: Downcast and uncertain, bearing the loss of Vegapunk and the defeat at Egghead.
npcs.description: A heavyset man of imposing stature, he wears a sumo outfit and wields a battle axe as his signature weapon. He radiates raw physical authority and a seriousness that intimidates at first glance. Despite his stern appearance, he shows genuine loyalty to those he considers his own.

current_state.summary_text: Sentomaru was gravely wounded by the Seraphim S-Bear during the siege of Egghead, after attempting to resist the Five Elders' orders. His exact whereabouts after the island's collapse are uncertain.

npcs.name: PX-1
npcs.base_backstory: PX-1 is one of the Pacifista units produced by the World Government based on the body and appearance of Bartholomew Kuma, developed by Dr. Vegapunk. Equipped with laser beam cannons built into its palms and mouth, as well as highly durable cybernetic armor, it was deployed as a suppression weapon in the Sabaody Archipelago during the pre-timeskip period. Following Vegapunk's advances and the creation of the Seraphim, the original PX models were gradually demoted to second-line units, operated under the direct orders of the Marines and the World Government.
npcs.current_goal: Execute suppression and patrol orders as programmed by the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Has no dream — it is a war machine with no will of its own.
npcs.mood: neutral-operational
npcs.description: PX-1 is a cyborg copy of Bartholomew Kuma: heavyset, over five meters tall, clad in metallic armor beneath a human appearance. It bears the same impassive face as the original, but without any spark of personality. Its presence on the battlefield evokes silence — not out of respect, but from the instinctive fear of something that does not negotiate, does not retreat, and does not feel pain.

current_state.summary_text: PX-1 operates as a patrol and suppression unit under Marine command, stationed in the Sabaody Archipelago. With the introduction of the Seraphim, it has become a second-line combat platform, still functional but technologically surpassed.

npcs.name: Jewelry Bonney
npcs.base_backstory: The daughter of Bartholomew Kuma and Ginny, Bonney was born with an incurable disease called Sapphire Scale, which kept her trapped in the appearance of a child. To save her life, Kuma accepted a deal with the World Government, becoming a Warlord of the Sea and eventually sacrificing his humanity as a test subject for the Pacifista Program. After being cured by Vegapunk and discovering the truth about her father's past, Bonney escaped from government custody and sailed to Egghead to find him. At Egghead, she witnessed Kuma's final sacrifice — he gave his life to save her from one of the Five Elders — and was reunited with him for a brief moment before his death.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the chaos of Egghead and honor Kuma's memory while deciding her next step as a captain.
npcs.long_term_dream: Live freely without being controlled by the World Government and discover the world her father loved so much.
npcs.mood: Grief-stricken and furious, carrying the pain of Kuma's loss but refusing to yield.
npcs.description: Bonney appears to be a girl of around twelve years old — a reflection of the disease that marked her childhood — but her pink eyes and bearing carry an intensity far beyond her apparent age. Disheveled pink hair frames an expressive face that shifts between scorn and sorrow in seconds. Those who underestimate her because of her size soon discover that she can age or rejuvenate anyone with a touch, and that her fists arrive before any warning.

current_state.summary_text: Bonney is at Egghead after witnessing Kuma's death, as he gave his life to protect her from one of the Five Elders; her immediate whereabouts after the island's collapse are uncertain, but she remains alive and free.

npcs.name: Scratchmen Apoo
npcs.base_backstory: Scratchmen Apoo is the captain of the On Air Pirates, hailing from the Longarm Tribe, and rose to prominence as one of the members of the Worst Generation. Before the events of Wano, he approached Kaido as an infiltrated agent — or a traitor, depending on one's perspective — luring Kid and Killer into a trap. During the Wano War, he fought at the Onigashima festival alongside the Beasts Pirates, but his loyalty was never unconditional. With Kaido's defeat, the alliance with the Beasts Pirates dissolved.
npcs.current_goal: Stay relevant and profitable in the new balance of power in Wano, selling information and forging convenient alliances.
npcs.long_term_dream: Dominate the seas as the most powerful and feared musician in the world.
npcs.mood: Opportunistic and animated, sniffing out advantages in the vacuum left by Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Apoo is a long-armed human, a trait inherited from the Longarm Tribe, with an extravagant appearance marked by dark dreadlocks, colorful clothes, and a permanently cocky expression. His body literally functions as an instrument: teeth that sound like piano keys, arms that stretch like strings, and attacks that arrive as invisible sound waves before the target notices the danger. He gives the impression of a dangerous clown — easy to underestimate, hard to ignore.

current_state.summary_text: With Kaido's fall in Wano, Apoo operates independently within the country, unaffiliated with the Beasts Pirates and seeking to capitalize on the new political landscape. His precise whereabouts within Wano are uncertain, but he remains active and on the lookout for opportunities.

npcs.name: Basil Hawkins
npcs.base_backstory: Basil Hawkins emerged as one of the great names of the Worst Generation, feared for his reputation of surviving anything — and for transferring damage to others using the Wara Wara no Mi. After Sabaody, he sailed to the New World and ended up allying with Kaido, serving as one of the Beasts Pirates' commanders stationed in Wano. With Kaido's fall in Wano, Hawkins was defeated by Trafalgar Law during the conflict at Onigashima Palace; he survived the confrontation, but his position and allies crumbled along with the Beasts Pirates' regime.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and rebuild some form of power or purpose after the collapse of the Beasts Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: Sail according to the destiny his cards reveal — and prove that no force can defy what is written.
npcs.mood: Resigned and calculating; coldly assessing the odds of a fresh start.
npcs.description: Hawkins is a tall, slender man with long blond hair falling over his shoulders, cold eyes, and a perpetually impassive expression. He wears earth-toned clothing with straw motifs and always carries a tarot deck. His presence is unsettling: he speaks little, smiles even less, and every gesture seems like the prelude to an already-calculated verdict.

current_state.summary_text: Hawkins is in Wano following the defeat of the Beasts Pirates, having survived the confrontation with Law at Onigashima. His exact whereabouts and next moves are unknown.

npcs.name: X Drake
npcs.devil_fruit: Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Allosaurus
npcs.base_backstory: Son of the pirate Diez Barrels, Drake was handed over to the Marines at a young age after his father was murdered by Doflamingo, and rose to the rank of Rear Admiral before faking his desertion to infiltrate the New World as a pirate captain. He founded the Drake Pirates and joined the Supernovas with a bounty of 222 million berries, using that cover to gather intelligence for SWORD. In Wano, he served as one of Kaido's Tobiroppo before revealing his true loyalty by momentarily allying with Luffy during the battle of Onigashima. After Kaido's fall, his exact whereabouts are uncertain, but his affiliation with SWORD remains active.
npcs.current_goal: Report to SWORD on post-Wano developments and await a new infiltration mission.
npcs.long_term_dream: Purge the corruption that destroyed his family and build a Marine worth the loyalty he has placed in it.
npcs.mood: Cautious and in assessment mode — Kaido's fall has exposed his cover to unforeseen risks and he is recalibrating his position.
npcs.description: Drake is a tall, muscular man with short dark hair, an angular face, and a permanently calculating expression. He wears a pirate captain's coat with ease, but his rigid posture and cold eyes betray the military training beneath the facade. He gives the impression of someone who weighs every word and every step before acting — dangerous precisely because of it.

current_state.summary_text: Drake operates under SWORD as an undercover agent, with his exact whereabouts unknown following the events of Wano. His identity as an infiltrated Marine was exposed to a restricted circle of Luffy's allies, making his operational situation delicate.

npcs.name: Capone “Gang” Bege
npcs.base_backstory: Before becoming a pirate, Bege built his reputation as the leader of a mafia family in the West Blue, eliminating rivals with surgical coldness. Upon entering the Grand Line, he became captain of the Fire Tank Pirates and was recruited by Big Mom as one of her commanders. On Whole Cake Island, he conspired with Luffy and Law to assassinate Big Mom, and although the plan failed, he managed to escape with his family intact. Since Kaido's fall in Wano, he sails independently with the Fire Tank Pirates, distanced from the major alliances.
npcs.current_goal: Expand the Fire Tank Pirates' territory and power in the New World seas, protecting his family and consolidating his position as an independent force.
npcs.long_term_dream: Build his own kingdom where his family and his men stand above any outside power.
npcs.mood: Calculated and vigilant, assessing the new balance of power after the fall of the Yonko.
npcs.description: Bege is a short, stocky man, always dressed in elegant gangster attire and a fedora hat. His face wears a permanently cold and calculating expression, with narrow eyes that rarely betray emotion. His presence conveys the quiet authority of someone who has killed many people without losing sleep — a mafia boss who happens to command a pirate ship.

current_state.summary_text: Bege sails the New World at the head of the Fire Tank Pirates, operating as an independent force after breaking with Big Mom's pirates. His exact whereabouts are uncertain, but he maintains the posture of a mafia leader in quiet expansion.

npcs.name: Urouge
npcs.base_backstory: Urouge was born on the Sky Islands and descended to the Grand Line to found the Fallen Monk Pirates, becoming one of the eleven Supernovas who arrived at Sabaody with bounties above 100 million. During the post-Marineford period, he survived the New World and defeated Snack, one of Big Mom's four Sweet Commanders, before being driven out of her territory. He took refuge on an inaccessible Sky Island, where he recovered and remained beyond the reach of his enemies.
npcs.current_goal: Train, strengthen his men, and wait for the right moment to act again in the New World.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove the greatness of the Sky Island pirates by carving out a definitive place in the New World.
npcs.mood: Serene and contemplative, distant from the great conflicts stirring the world after Egghead.
npcs.description: Urouge is an enormous man with dark skin, a shaved head, and the characteristic wings of the Sky Islands folded at his back. He carries a massive staff with chains and paradoxically radiates a monastic aura — as though the violence he inflicts were a spiritual ritual. Those who face him quickly learn that striking Urouge is the greatest mistake possible: every blow he receives makes him more dangerous.

current_state.summary_text: Urouge remains on a Sky Island of unknown location, removed from the great conflicts that have redefined the New World. His exact whereabouts are unknown to most; it is only known that he is alive and still active.

npcs.name: Eustass Kid
npcs.base_backstory: Born in the South Blue, Kid built his crew through brute force and arrived at Sabaody as one of the most dangerous Supernovas of his generation. He survived the Great Pirate Era, took part in the alliance against Kaido in Wano, and was decisive in Big Mom's defeat alongside Law. After Wano, he challenged Shanks at Elbaf with his fleet, was crushed in a single move, and lost most of his ships and crewmates. He survived the defeat and remains in Elbaf, diminished but not broken.
npcs.current_goal: Rebuild his strength and reassert his name in the New World after the humiliating defeat at the hands of Shanks.
npcs.long_term_dream: Find the One Piece and become the King of the Pirates.
npcs.mood: Furious and wounded in his pride, but determined not to back down.
npcs.description: Kid is a tall, muscular man with wild bright-red hair and aggressive yellow eyes. He wears leather clothing with metal adornments and his left arm has been replaced by a mechanical prosthetic. His presence inspires immediate fear — he does not need to shout to make it clear he is willing to kill.

current_state.summary_text: Kid is in Elbaf after suffering a devastating defeat against Shanks, who destroyed his fleet with a single use of Conqueror's Haki. He remains alive with part of his crew, but his standing in the New World has been severely shaken.

npcs.name: Boa Marigold
npcs.devil_fruit: Hebi Hebi no Mi, Model: King Cobra
npcs.base_backstory: The younger sister of Boa Hancock and Sandersonia, Marigold was enslaved alongside her sisters by the Celestial Dragons while still young, a period during which the three were force-fed Zoan Devil Fruits. Rescued by Fisher Tiger during the attack on Mary Geoise, she returned to Amazon Lily and became one of the Kuja Tribe's elite warriors. She fought alongside Hancock during the conflict at Marineford. After the war, she remained on Amazon Lily defending the island and her queen.
npcs.current_goal: Defend Amazon Lily and the Kuja people from any external threat, especially following the removal of the Seven Warlords of the Sea's protection.
npcs.long_term_dream: Keep Amazon Lily free and inviolable, preserving the strength and honor of the Kuja Tribe.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and tense, aware that the island faces growing threats without its former diplomatic shield.
npcs.description: Marigold is a woman of imposing stature, a muscular build, and dark hair tied up, with a posture that conveys raw strength and combat readiness. Her transformation into a giant snake makes her even more threatening, with scales enveloping her body and flames emanating from her mouth. She carries a stern expression and a determined gaze, typical of the Kuja warriors forged to protect Amazon Lily.

current_state.summary_text: Marigold garrisons Amazon Lily alongside Sandersonia, sustaining the island's defense while Hancock operates elsewhere. With the dissolution of the Seven Warlords of the Sea, Amazon Lily's security depends increasingly on the strength of the Kuja warriors.

npcs.name: Trafalgar Law
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Flevance, he survived the extermination of his hometown by the White Lead Disease and was taken in by Donquixote Doflamingo, becoming a member of the Donquixote Pirates. After the death of Corazon — who gave him the Ope Ope no Mi and revealed the truth about Doflamingo — Law left the family and built the Heart Pirates, rising to great notoriety as a Supernova. He formed the Straw Heart Alliance with Luffy to bring down Kaido in Wano, contributing decisively to Big Mom's defeat alongside Kid. After Wano, the Heart Pirates set an independent course through the New World; Law was defeated by Blackbeard at the Kurohige Islands during the search for a Road Poneglyph, being temporarily captured before escaping.
npcs.current_goal: Recover his standing in the New World and continue the search for the Road Poneglyphs, keeping the Heart Pirates operational after the confrontation with Blackbeard.
npcs.long_term_dream: Uncover the secret of the "D." and the true legacy of Corazon — and, in doing so, find meaning in the survival that was given to him.
npcs.mood: Determined and cautious; he carries the weight of the defeat against Blackbeard but shows no open despondency.
npcs.description: Law is a young man of average height with short black hair beneath a stained white hat, and tattoos covering his arms, hands, and part of his neck. He carries the nodachi Kikoku on his back and projects a cold, calculating presence that keeps allies and enemies equally on edge. His yellowish eyes have a clinical intensity, as though he is always assessing the field before acting.

current_state.summary_text: Captain of the Heart Pirates, sailing through the New World after surviving the confrontation with Blackbeard; exact whereabouts uncertain, but remains active and independent.

npcs.name: Boa Sandersonia
npcs.devil_fruit: Hebi Hebi no Mi: Model Anaconda
npcs.base_backstory: Sandersonia was born on Amazon Lily and, while still young, was captured alongside her sisters Hancock and Marigold and enslaved by the Celestial Dragons, with the Celestial Dragon's mark burned into her back. After their escape, the three sisters returned to Amazon Lily and rose as elite warriors of the Kuja Tribe, guarding the secret of their enslavement on pain of death. Sandersonia ate the Hebi Hebi no Mi, Model: Anaconda, becoming one of the island's most powerful combatants. Since the war at Marineford and the subsequent pressure from the World Government on Amazon Lily, she has remained at Hancock's side as a defensive pillar of the island.
npcs.current_goal: Defend Amazon Lily and support Hancock in maintaining the island's independence from the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that Amazon Lily and the Kuja people remain free and never be enslaved again.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and determined, with latent tension in the face of the World Government's growing pressure on the island.
npcs.description: Sandersonia is a tall, muscular woman with long dark hair that she wields as a weapon in combat, wrapping it around enemies with lethal precision. Her posture is confident and predatory, conveying the strength of someone who endured slavery and emerged harder for it. In anaconda form, she becomes a colossal beast capable of crushing opponents under the weight of her own body. Alongside her sister Marigold, she forms a combat duo feared throughout the Grand Line.

current_state.summary_text: Sandersonia guards Amazon Lily alongside Marigold, sustaining the island's defense while Hancock maintains her unstable political standing before the World Government. She is one of the military pillars of the Kuja Tribe.

npcs.name: Boa Hancock
npcs.base_backstory: Born into the Kuja tribe, Hancock was kidnapped as a child along with her sisters and sold into slavery to the Celestial Dragons, during which period she received the mark of the sun to conceal the slave's tattoo. Freed by Fisher Tiger, she returned to Amazon Lily and ascended to the throne as Empress, becoming the captain of the Kuja Pirates. She joined as a Warlord of the Sea and was the only person to enter and leave Impel Down without formal authorization, aiding Luffy in the rescue of Ace. After the abolition of the Seven Warlords of the Sea by the World Government, she lost her official title, but retains sovereign control of Amazon Lily and the unconditional loyalty of the Kuja.
npcs.current_goal: Defend Amazon Lily and the Kuja people from any external threat, especially in the face of the instability brought about by the end of the Warlords era.
npcs.long_term_dream: Find Monkey D. Luffy again and, in her own way, stand by his side as he reaches the top of the world.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and proud, but carrying a tension held in check by Amazon Lily's growing exposure following the dissolution of the Warlords.
npcs.description: Hancock is a woman of extraordinary beauty — tall, slender, with long black hair and dark almond-shaped eyes. She dresses in clothes that accentuate her figure, always carrying herself with an air of absolute sovereignty. Her presence paralyzes as much through Haki as through her appearance; those who face her tend to hesitate before attacking.

current_state.summary_text: She rules Amazon Lily as the sovereign Empress of the Kuja, without the diplomatic shield of the Warlord title. The island remains independent territory hostile to invaders, guarded by the strength of the Kuja and Hancock's power.

npcs.name: Gloriosa
npcs.base_backstory: Gloriosa was the Empress of Amazon Lily before Boa Hancock, and one of the few to have survived "love sickness" — the disease that kills Kuja women who develop romantic feelings for men. After abdicating the throne, she became the island's elder guardian and adviser to subsequent generations. She witnessed the enslavement of the Boa sisters by the Celestial Dragons and carries the weight of that secret alongside them. She remains in Amazon Lily as a living memory of Kuja traditions and a voice of prudence at Hancock's side.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Amazon Lily and guide Boa Hancock in the face of the growing threat from the World Government following the fall of the Warlord status.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that Amazon Lily and the Kuja legacy survive the storms of the world, independent of any external power.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and grave — the island's situation demands constant attention.
npcs.description: An elderly woman, hunched over a thick staff, with white hair pinned up and small, piercing eyes that miss nothing. Despite her frail build, she radiates a calm authority that silences the room. Those who see her for the first time may underestimate her; those who know her are aware that she has survived things that killed younger warriors.

current_state.summary_text: Gloriosa resides in Amazon Lily as elder adviser to Boa Hancock, keeping Kuja traditions alive while the island faces hostility from the World Government following the abolition of the Warlord system.

npcs.name: Magellan
npcs.base_backstory: Magellan rose to the position of Chief Warden of Impel Down, becoming the foremost guardian of the world's greatest prison. His mastery of the Doku Doku no Mi made him virtually unbeatable within the prison walls, feared by prisoners and guards alike. During the invasion by Monkey D. Luffy and the subsequent mass breakout, over 200 prisoners escaped on his watch — an unprecedented failure in Impel Down's history. As a direct consequence, he was demoted to the position of Vice Warden, a post he has held since the fall of Marineford.
npcs.current_goal: Oversee Impel Down as Vice Warden and restore the prison's reputation for inviolability.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that no prisoner ever escapes from Impel Down on his watch again.
npcs.mood: Grim and determined, carrying the weight of his demotion as a permanent scar on his honor.
npcs.description: Magellan is a man of colossal stature, with an imposing figure clad in a dark cape and prominent horns on his helmet. His scowling face and piercing eyes convey an absolute authority that needs no words. At any moment he can envelop his own body in viscous purple poison, making himself untouchable and lethal to the touch.

current_state.summary_text: Magellan serves as Vice Warden of Impel Down after being demoted for the mass breakout that defined his tenure. He remains the most dangerous combat force within the prison, maintaining order through the constant threat of his deadly poisons.

npcs.name: Hannyabal
npcs.base_backstory: Hannyabal served for years as Vice Warden of Impel Down under Magellan, demonstrating both administrative competence and a poorly concealed ambition to claim the top post. During Luffy's invasion of the prison and the subsequent chaos that followed, he faced the invaders directly despite his limitations. With Magellan suffering institutional consequences for the incident that allowed the greatest escape in Impel Down's history, Hannyabal was promoted to Chief Warden, fulfilling the dream he never bothered to hide.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain order and Impel Down's reputation as an inescapable prison, erasing the stigma of the great breakout and consolidating his authority as Chief Warden.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the greatest Chief Warden in Impel Down's history and, perhaps, rise even further within the World Government.
npcs.mood: Tense and vigilant — the prison's reputation still bears the scars of Luffy's escape, and Hannyabal feels the pressure to prove that his administration is different.
npcs.description: Hannyabal is a tall man of eccentric appearance, with dark hair and an expressive face that rarely conceals his emotions — or his ambitious thoughts. He wears the Chief Warden's uniform of Impel Down with a seriousness that contrasts with his tendency to let slip comments about his own ambitions. He carries a bifurcated spear as his main weapon and projects an intimidating presence that barely masks his vanity.

current_state.summary_text: Hannyabal commands Impel Down as Chief Warden, overseeing the security and daily operations of the prison following the promotion that came after Magellan's demotion. His authority over the facility is total, though the shadow of the greatest breakout in the prison's history still looms over his administration.

npcs.name: Sadi
npcs.base_backstory: Sadi rose to the post of chief jailer of Impel Down, commanding the Jailer Beasts — monstrous creatures used to suppress riots and punish inmates. During the great breakout led by Luffy and Blackbeard, she was defeated by Ivankov and was left out of action while the prison descended into chaos. Following the events at Impel Down, she resumed her duties at the prison, which operates under reinforced World Government control.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain absolute order in Impel Down and ensure that no prisoner escapes or challenges her authority.
npcs.long_term_dream: Exercise total control over the world's most dangerous prisoners, with unrestricted pleasure in every punishment administered.
npcs.mood: Enthusiastic and vigilant, with heightened tension following the humiliation of the great breakout.
npcs.description: Sadi is a young woman of provocative appearance, always dressed in a red and black leather outfit that matches the whip she carries. Her face wears a constant smile of barely concealed pleasure, and her eyes light up with enthusiasm every time an opportunity to punish someone arises. The impression she gives is of someone who genuinely loves her work — which is, by far, the most terrifying thing about her.

current_state.summary_text: Sadi holds the post of chief jailer of Impel Down, commanding the Jailer Beasts with characteristic brutality. Her whereabouts and function are stable within the World Government's underwater prison.

npcs.name: Shiryu
npcs.base_backstory: Shiryu was the head guard of Impel Down, feared for his brutality and pleasure in killing prisoners — which led his own organization to imprison him on Level 6. During Blackbeard's invasion of Impel Down, he was released and chose to betray the Marines, allying himself with the Blackbeard Pirates. After the War of Marineford, he took part in the attack on Level 6 of Impel Down, recruiting elite criminals for the crew. He later obtained the Suke Suke no Mi by killing Absalom, becoming the captain of the second ship in Blackbeard's fleet.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate his power within the Blackbeard Pirates and await the crew's next offensive.
npcs.long_term_dream: Kill and dominate without restraint, free from any institutional shackles.
npcs.mood: Contained and predatory — awaiting opportunity.
npcs.description: Shiryu is a tall man of imposing build, with long dark hair and a perpetually cold gaze. He wears a long coat and carries a broad-bladed sword at his hip. His presence provokes instinctive discomfort — not through agitation, but through the absolute impassiveness with which he appraises everything around him, as though calculating the cost of killing every person in the room.

current_state.summary_text: Captain of the second ship of the Blackbeard Pirates, Shiryu operates from Hachinosu, combining invisibility through the Suke Suke no Mi with swordsmanship mastery and Armament Haki, positioning himself as one of the crew's most lethal combatants.

npcs.name: Inazuma
npcs.base_backstory: Inazuma is a commander of the Revolutionary Army and the inseparable partner of Emporio Ivankov, the leader of the Grand Line division. He was imprisoned in Impel Down until freed by Monkey D. Luffy during the rescue of Portgas D. Ace, subsequently taking part in the War of Marineford alongside the escapees. After the timeskip, he returned to revolutionary activities and remains as Ivankov's right-hand man at the base on Momoiro Island.
npcs.current_goal: Support Ivankov and the Revolutionary Army's operations from the base at Momoiro.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the world freed from the oppression of the Celestial Dragons and the World Government.
npcs.mood: Stable and focused, awaiting orders or on operational standby.
npcs.description: Inazuma is a man of average height with long hair parted down the middle, often seen in elegant clothes that contrast with his warrior's bearing. His hands — the extension of the Choki Choki no Mi — convey a cutting calm that intimidates as much as any blade. His presence is understated but undeniable; those who know him are aware that his silence is more dangerous than another person's shout.

current_state.summary_text: Inazuma is stationed on Momoiro Island as a revolutionary commander and Ivankov's right-hand man, maintaining the operational base of the Grand Line division.

npcs.name: Ivankov
npcs.base_backstory: Emporio Ivankov is the ruler of the Kamabakka Kingdom on Momoiro and one of the most powerful commanders of Dragon's Revolutionary Army. He was imprisoned for years in Impel Down, on Level 5.5 (Newkama Land), until freed during the chaos provoked by Luffy before the War of Marineford. He fought alongside Luffy at Marineford and afterward returned to Momoiro with the other revolutionaries. He took part in the Revolutionary Army summit that declared war on the Celestial Dragons during the Reverie incident.
npcs.current_goal: Strengthen the Revolutionary Army and bring down the system of the Celestial Dragons.
npcs.long_term_dream: See a world free from the oppression of the Celestial Dragons and the World Government, where everyone can live as they please.
npcs.mood: Exuberant and combative — the declared confrontation with the World Government ignites his revolutionary fervor even further.
npcs.description: Ivankov is a colossal, androgynous figure, with an enormous face framed by dome-shaped blue hair and wide eyes of perpetually exaggerated expression. He dresses in extravagant outfits that blend the style of a queen with that of a warrior. His presence fills any space — it is impossible to ignore him, whether by the volume of his voice, the size of his body, or the intensity of his gaze.

current_state.summary_text: Ivankov commands the Kamabakka Kingdom on Momoiro and serves as one of the pillars of the Revolutionary Army, which now operates in a state of open war against the World Government.

npcs.name: Buggy
npcs.base_backstory: Buggy sailed on the same ship as Shanks during the era of Gol D. Roger, having access to the Pirate King's maps and secrets from a young age. He accidentally ate the Bara Bara no Mi and formed his own crew after Roger's execution. Captured and sent to Impel Down, he led a mass breakout during the Marineford War, gaining fame disproportionate to his actual power. His manufactured reputation elevated him to Warlord of the Sea under the World Government and, later, to the title of Emperor, culminating in the founding of the Cross Guild alongside Crocodile and Mihawk on Karai Bari Island.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain his position as Emperor and expand the Cross Guild without having to fight personally.
npcs.long_term_dream: Accumulate enough wealth and fame to be recognized as the greatest pirate in the world — preferably without risking his own skin.
npcs.mood: Agitated and paranoid under the weight of an Emperor's responsibilities, yet unable to refuse the prestige the title brings.
npcs.description: Buggy is a man of average height with disheveled blue hair, a white-painted face with red details, and a round red nose he guards with fanatical jealousy. He wears a striped coat and a characteristic clown hat. The impression he gives is one of walking chaos: he shouts constantly, gestures wildly, and radiates the energy of a charlatan who, by accident or fate, always ends up on top.

current_state.summary_text: Buggy commands the Cross Guild on Karai Bari Island as one of the Four Emperors, a title he owes more to his inflated reputation and his association with Crocodile and Mihawk than to his combat power. His position is real in the world, even if his effective leadership within the organization is contested.

npcs.name: Edward Newgate
npcs.base_backstory: Born without a family or a name, Edward Newgate grew up in Sphinx dreaming of having a real family. He became the most powerful of the Four Emperors, leading hundreds of adoptive sons under the banner of the Whitebeard Pirates. He died at Marineford protecting his sons and Roger's memory, standing upright, never having turned his back on an enemy. After his death, Blackbeard stole the power of the Gura Gura no Mi from his corpse.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased.
npcs.long_term_dream: To have a true family — not riches or the title of Pirate King.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
aliases: The Strongest Man in the World
npcs.description: Edward Newgate was a man of colossal stature, with muscles that defied his age and a scar crossing his chest — the mark of countless battles. His white crescent-shaped mustache was unmistakable in any port in the world. Even ill and covered in wounds, he radiated a presence that made the air around him feel heavier. He carried an enormous bisento as if it were a toothpick, and his every step literally shook the ground.

current_state.summary_text: Edward Newgate is dead, buried on Sphinx Island, venerated as a father by his former sons. The power of the Gura Gura no Mi, stolen by Blackbeard at Marineford, remains in his hands.

npcs.name: Jozu
npcs.base_backstory: Jozu rose to the post of 3rd Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, becoming one of the fleet's military pillars. At the Marineford War, he faced admirals directly, demonstrating devastating resilience and power. During the conflict, he lost an arm in battle against Aokiji, suffering one of the most severe wounds among the battle's survivors. After the fleet's dissolution following the deaths of Whitebeard and Ace, his whereabouts became unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find his place in the world after the fall of the Whitebeard fleet.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor Whitebeard's legacy by remaining standing, as proof that the fleet was not wiped out in vain.
npcs.mood: Somber and restrained — he carries the weight of defeat and the loss of an arm without openly showing it.
npcs.description: Jozu is a man of massive build, with musculature that rivals that of giants and a bearing that conveys absolute solidity. Parts of his body can crystallize into diamond, giving him a partially mineral appearance in combat. He lost his left arm at Marineford, and the absence is visible. His expression is almost always closed off — he occupies space with presence, not with words.

current_state.summary_text: Former 3rd Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, Jozu survived the Marineford War at the cost of an arm. His whereabouts have been unknown since the fleet's dissolution.

npcs.name: Marco
npcs.devil_fruit: Tori Tori no Mi, Model: Phoenix
npcs.base_backstory: Marco served as First Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates and Edward Newgate's right-hand man, being one of the crew's most powerful members. He took part in the Marineford War attempting to save Ace and protect Whitebeard, surviving the battle that decimated the crew. After the formal dissolution of the Whitebeard Pirates, he took on the guardianship of Sphinx Island, home of the villagers Whitebeard had protected. He took part in the conflict in Wano alongside Luffy and his allies against Kaido's Beasts, fulfilling his debt to Whitebeard's legacy.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Sphinx Island and the villagers Whitebeard left in his care.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor Whitebeard's legacy and ensure that those he loved are safe.
npcs.mood: Serene and resigned — he carries the weight of his losses with composure, without apparent bitterness.
npcs.description: Marco is a man of average height with blond hair swept back and piercing blue eyes, frequently wearing the open-shirt outfit characteristic of Whitebeard's commanders. His relaxed posture and impassive expression convey a quiet authority — the kind of man who does not need to raise his voice to be obeyed. When he activates his Devil Fruit, blue flames envelop his body and phoenix wings emerge from his arms, making him simultaneously majestic and threatening.

current_state.summary_text: Marco guards Sphinx Island, protecting the villagers who were under Whitebeard's care. After Wano, he stepped back from that guardian role, distant from the major movements of the New World.

npcs.name: Vista
npcs.base_backstory: Vista served as 5th Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, recognized as one of the greatest swordsmen in the world. At the Marineford War, he dueled Mihawk — one of the few combatants to face him on equal footing, at least briefly. After Whitebeard's death and the crew's collapse in the post-Marineford era, the remaining crew members scattered. Vista is still alive, but his whereabouts are unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Preserve the legacy of the Whitebeard Pirates and survive in a world reshaped by the Four Emperors.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor Whitebeard's memory as the greatest swordsman to have served under his flag.
npcs.mood: Reserved and watchful — he carries the weight of Marineford's defeat without letting it show.
npcs.description: Vista is a middle-aged man with a refined mustache and impeccable posture, unusual among pirates. He dresses with care and carries two swords with which he executes sweeping arc strikes that evoke flowers — hence his epithet. His presence conveys absolute control: no wasted movement, no unnecessary word.

current_state.summary_text: 5th Division Commander of the now-defunct Whitebeard Pirates, Vista remains alive after the crew's dissolution, but his whereabouts are unknown. No one knows for certain where he operates or whom, if anyone, he now serves.

npcs.name: Akainu
npcs.base_backstory: Sakazuki served in the Marines for decades, rising to the rank of Admiral under the epithet Akainu through his absolute mastery of the Magu Magu no Mi. During the Marineford War, he executed Portgas D. Ace and pursued the Straw Hat Pirates' survivors until Marineford fell. After the war, he defeated Aokiji in a ten-day duel on Punk Hazard to contest the position of Fleet Admiral, emerging victorious and assuming supreme command of the Marines. He relocated headquarters to New Marineford and has since governed the institution with an iron fist and a doctrine of Absolute Justice.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the World Government's absolute order, eradicate any threat to the Marines' stability, and eliminate the remaining Four Emperors.
npcs.long_term_dream: A world purged of pirates and of any force that challenges the World Government's authority — the complete realization of Absolute Justice.
npcs.mood: Tense and controlled. The events of Egghead — Monkey D. Dragon's declaration of war, Luffy's rise as Emperor, and the deaths of several Gorosei — are pressing the Marines' command harder than ever before.
npcs.description: Sakazuki is a man of imposing stature, with a severe face marked by scars and a permanently hardened expression. He wears the white Fleet Admiral's coat over a dark floral shirt, with his marine cap tilted. His presence empties rooms — not through charisma, but through sheer weight. Those who face him feel that no negotiation is possible.

current_state.summary_text: Sakazuki commands the Marines from New Marineford, managing the institutional crisis opened by the events of Egghead and the escalating conflicts with the Four Emperors. His position as Fleet Admiral remains solid, but the balance of power in the world is under growing pressure.

npcs.name: Kizaru
npcs.base_backstory: Borsalino, known by the epithet Kizaru, joined the Marines and rose to the rank of Admiral, becoming one of the three military pillars of the World Government. He consumed the Pika Pika no Mi, which transforms him into light and grants him unmatched speed and offensive power. He took part in the Marineford War alongside Admirals Aokiji and Akainu, facing the Whitebeard Pirates. At Egghead, he received orders from the Five Elders to eliminate Vegapunk and contain the situation on the island, where he directly confronted Luffy and Vegapunk himself — revealing signs of internal conflict by hesitating at decisive moments.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out the orders of the Marines and the Five Elders, maintaining world order — even at the cost of his own moral hesitation.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the Marines to the end, even as the weight of the orders he receives eats away at him from within.
npcs.mood: Outwardly calm and disinterested; inwardly divided after the events of Egghead.
