npcs.description: A tall, middle-aged man with dark hair swept back and a characteristic yellow suit. His half-closed eyes convey perpetual boredom, and his slow, languid movements contrast absurdly with the absolute speed he can reach in a fraction of a second. His presence on the battlefield inspires a quiet tension — not for what he says, but for what he can do without warning.

current_state.summary_text: Kizaru remains in his post as Marine Admiral after leading the operation at Egghead, where he carried out the Five Elders' orders with hesitations that did not go unnoticed. His loyalty to the institution remains, but signs of internal conflict mark his current bearing.

npcs.name: Sengoku
npcs.devil_fruit: Hito Hito no Mi: Model Daibutsu
npcs.base_backstory: Sengoku served in the Marines for decades, reaching the highest rank of Fleet Admiral. He was the architect of the Marineford War, mobilizing the World Government's forces against the Whitebeard Pirates to execute Portgas D. Ace. After the war and the post-timeskip events, he requested retirement, ceding the post to Sakazuki. He remains connected to the Marines as an advisory figure and maintains close ties with Tsuru and Garp.
npcs.current_goal: Observe the new balance of power in the world and advise the Marines when called upon, without resuming formal command.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that the Marines survive and maintain some form of justice in the world, even if imperfect.
npcs.mood: Resigned and watchful — he carries the weight of past decisions without deluding himself about the present.
npcs.description: Sengoku is an elderly man, tall and of imposing bearing, with white hair and a long beard. He wears an admiral's cap and is known to carry a baby Den Den Mushi and, notably, a pet goat. Even in retirement, he radiates absolute authority — those who know him understand that behind the relaxed posture lies one of the most dangerous strategists in the world.

current_state.summary_text: Sengoku lives in retirement at New Marineford, removed from formal command but still present as one of the Marines' deepest institutional memories. His whereabouts are stable and his influence, discreet.

npcs.name: Demaro Black
npcs.base_backstory: Demaro Black is an opportunistic pirate who, after Monkey D. Luffy's two-year disappearance, assumed the identity of the Straw Hat Pirates' captain in the Sabaody Archipelago, forming a crew of impostors. Using Luffy's fame, he recruited naive pirates and terrorized local civilians without possessing any real power. His ruse was exposed when the real Luffy and his allies reappeared at Sabaody, and he was incapacitated by Silvers Rayleigh and later arrested by Marine Admirals. He survived capture and his whereabouts after detention are uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find a new opportunity to rise to fame at the expense of another borrowed name.
npcs.long_term_dream: Attain fame and power without having to earn either through his own merit.
npcs.mood: Apprehensive and resentful, aware that his ruse was exposed in a humiliating fashion.
npcs.description: Demaro Black is a heavyset man with short dark hair and a perpetually puffed-up expression of self-confidence. He wears clothes that imitate Luffy's style — straw hat included — but the imitation always looked crude to anyone who knew the original. He gives the impression of a second-rate bully trying to use someone else's shadow to appear larger than he is.

current_state.summary_text: Demaro Black had his false identity exposed at Sabaody and was detained by the Marines; his current whereabouts are unknown, and the impostor crew he led has been disbanded.

npcs.name: Manboshi
npcs.base_backstory: The youngest son of King Poseidon Neptune and brother of Fukaboshi and Ryuboshi, Manboshi grew up on Fish-Man Island as a prince of the Ryugu Kingdom. Together with his brothers, he led the resistance against the occupation by Hody Jones and his pirates during the island's crisis, protecting the kingdom in the king's absence. After Hody's defeat, the three princes resumed their roles as guardians of the kingdom alongside Neptune.
npcs.current_goal: Defend the Ryugu Kingdom and support the royal family in governing Fish-Man Island.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Fish-Man Island in lasting peace, with humans and fish-men living without mutual hatred.
npcs.mood: Energetic and committed to his duty of protecting the kingdom.
npcs.description: Manboshi is a fish-man of athletic build and an ever-animated expression, with lively eyes and a posture that radiates constant energy. He wears royal attire from Fish-Man Island and rarely stands still. He gives the impression of someone equally capable of hugging and crushing with the same intensity.

current_state.summary_text: Manboshi resides in Ryugu Palace as a prince of the Ryugu Kingdom, fulfilling his role alongside his brothers Fukaboshi and Ryuboshi in the defense and administration of Fish-Man Island.

npcs.name: Ryuboshi
npcs.base_backstory: Ryuboshi is the second son of King Neptune and a prince of the Ryugu Kingdom on Fish-Man Island. He grew up alongside his brothers Fukaboshi and Manboshi, sharing with them the role of guardians of the fish-man people. During the invasion by the New Fish-Man Pirates led by Hody Jones, he fought to defend the kingdom alongside his brothers. After Hody's defeat and the Straw Hat Pirates' departure, he resumed his royal duties alongside his family.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Fish-Man Island and maintain the stability of the Ryugu Kingdom alongside his brothers.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the day when fish-men and humans live together in peace and equality, fulfilling Otohime's dream.
npcs.mood: Lively and theatrical, carrying out his royal duties with characteristic enthusiasm.
npcs.description: Ryuboshi is a fish-man of imposing stature, with striking features and an exuberant bearing that betrays his love of spectacle. He carries a harp and dresses in a flamboyant manner befitting his theatrical temperament. His presence fills any room — he seems incapable of entering a space without turning it into a stage. Behind the showmanship lies a dedicated warrior and a prince genuinely committed to his people.

current_state.summary_text: Ryuboshi remains on Fish-Man Island carrying out his duties as a prince of the Ryugu Kingdom, alongside his brothers Fukaboshi and Manboshi. He serves as a guardian of the fish-man people under Neptune's reign.

npcs.name: Caribou
npcs.base_backstory: Caribou is a pirate captain from the Worst Generation who rose to notoriety during the Shichibukai summons at Marineford, where he infiltrated by pretending to be an ally of the Straw Hat Pirates. At the Sabaody Archipelago and on Fish-Man Island, he revealed his treacherous nature by attempting to capture mermaids to sell on the black market. After being defeated by Luffy and left behind on Fish-Man Island, he went through a series of captures and escapes before reaching Wano, where he collected critical information about the powers of the Yonko. After Wano, he disappeared carrying secrets that could shake the balance of power in the New World.
npcs.current_goal: Sell or use the information gathered in Wano about Luffy's and Yamato's powers to gain favor with a major power — possibly Blackbeard or the government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a figure of power in the underworld, enriching himself at the expense of the powerful and the weak alike.
npcs.mood: Anxious and calculating — he carries valuable secrets and knows he is a target of multiple factions.
npcs.description: Caribou is a tall man with a bony face and sunken eyes that convey a permanent unease. He wears a hood and dark clothes that reinforce his appearance as a figure of the shadows. At any moment he can dissolve his own body into dark, viscous mud, making his physical presence as unstable as his loyalty. The impression he gives is that of a predator who prefers ambush to direct confrontation.

current_state.summary_text: Caribou is at large in the New World after escaping from Wano, carrying sensitive information about the powers of Luffy's allies. His exact whereabouts are unknown, but his intention to trade those secrets with some major power makes him an unpredictable variable in the current geopolitical landscape.

npcs.name: Fukaboshi
npcs.base_backstory: The eldest son of King Neptune and the late Queen Otohime, Fukaboshi grew up bearing the weight of his mother's aspirations for coexistence between humans and fish-men. After Otohime's assassination by Hody Jones, Fukaboshi and his brothers carried her grief and mission in silence. During the New Fish-Man Pirates' invasion, he led the defense of the Ryugu Kingdom alongside Ryuboshi and Manboshi, being temporarily defeated by Hody. With Hody's defeat at Luffy's hands, the island was saved and Fukaboshi resumed his role as guardian prince.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Fish-Man Island and advance the dream of peaceful coexistence with humans left behind by his mother, Otohime.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Fish-Man Island rise to the surface and its inhabitants live in peace alongside humans, honoring Otohime's legacy.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and resolute, bearing the kingdom's responsibility with composure.
npcs.description: Fukaboshi is a shark fish-man of imposing stature, with a muscular body and sharp features that convey natural authority. He dresses in attire that combines royal dignity and combat practicality, carrying a sword at his side. His presence is calm and solid — the kind of leader who does not need to raise his voice to be obeyed.

current_state.summary_text: Fukaboshi fulfills his duties as crown prince of the Ryugu Kingdom, guarding Fish-Man Island and maintaining order following the events that shook the kingdom. His whereabouts and situation remain stable on the island, with no movements recorded since then.

npcs.name: Vander Decken IX
npcs.base_backstory: Vander Decken IX is the ninth bearer of the name Vander Decken, a descendant of the legendary pirate who pursued the Mermaid Poseidon centuries ago. He ate the Mato Mato no Mi and spent decades obsessed with marrying Princess Shirahoshi, bombarding Ryugu Castle with colossal projectiles. He allied with Hody Jones to conquer Fish-Man Island, but was defeated by Luffy during the conflict at Ryugu Castle and Gyoncorde Plaza. After his defeat, he was arrested and sent to Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and nurse his obsession with Shirahoshi even behind bars.
npcs.long_term_dream: Make Shirahoshi his wife at any cost — or destroy her if he can never have her.
npcs.mood: Resentful and unstable, alternating between obsessive daydreams and impotent rage.
npcs.description: Vander Decken IX is a fish-man of average height with a sinister appearance, his bulging eyes betraying his mental instability. He wears a distinctive mushroom hat and carries multiple throwing weapons strapped to his body. His presence conveys a disturbing mixture of warped gallantry and latent violence.

current_state.summary_text: Vander Decken IX is serving his sentence in Impel Down following his defeat on Fish-Man Island, where his alliance with Hody Jones and his obsession with Shirahoshi culminated in total failure.

npcs.name: King Neptune
npcs.base_backstory: Neptune is the king of the Ryugu Kingdom, ruler of Fish-Man Island at the bottom of the sea. Widower of Queen Otohime, whose assassination shook the kingdom, he raised his four children — Fukaboshi, Ryuboshi, Manboshi, and Princess Shirahoshi — while maintaining peace between fish-men and humans. During the New Fish-Man Pirates' invasion led by Hody Jones, he was captured and later freed with the help of the Straw Hat Pirates. Following Kaido's fall in Wano and the abolition of fish-man slavery promoted by King Nefertari Cobra at the Levely, the Ryugu Kingdom faces a historic moment of change in its relations with the surface.
npcs.current_goal: Ensure the safety and stability of the Ryugu Kingdom amid the political transformations stirring the world following the Levely and the events at Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: Fulfill Otohime's dream: a world where fish-men and humans live in peace and equality.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and sorrowful — aware that the world is changing too fast and his kingdom must position itself carefully.
npcs.description: Neptune is a fish-man of colossal stature, with koi carp features and a long white beard that reinforces his appearance as a majestic elder. He carries a trident of monumental proportions and wears royal attire that makes his rank unmistakable. His physical presence dominates any space — yet in his eyes there is a quiet sadness that anyone who knows his history recognizes immediately.

current_state.summary_text: Neptune governs the Ryugu Kingdom on Fish-Man Island, maintaining internal order while closely following the political upheavals of the world above — including the collapse of the Yonko structure and the consequences of the Levely on fish-men's rights.

npcs.name: Shirahoshi
npcs.base_backstory: The youngest daughter of King Neptune and the late Queen Otohime, Shirahoshi grew up confined in Ryugu Tower for years, a constant target of Vander Decken IX's threats as he pursued her with guided projectiles. During the crisis on Fish-Man Island, Monkey D. Luffy protected her and, for the first time, she left the tower. On that occasion, she involuntarily manifested her power over the Sea Kings, revealing herself as the incarnation of the Ancient Weapon Poseidon. After the end of the threat posed by Hody Jones and Decken, she began to live with greater freedom within the Ryugu Kingdom, participating in diplomatic events such as the Levely at Mary Geoise.
npcs.current_goal: Honor her mother Otohime's dream and build a world of peaceful coexistence between humans and Fish-Men.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the day when Fish-Men and humans live in full equality, without fear or prejudice.
npcs.mood: Hopeful and still frightened of the outside world, but determined not to retreat.
npcs.description: Shirahoshi is a mermaid of gigantic proportions, with long pink hair and large, expressive eyes that easily fill with tears. Her presence is at once imposing in size and completely disarming in the gentleness she radiates. Those who meet her for the first time rarely associate that tearful, gentle figure with the power capable of moving the Sea Kings.

current_state.summary_text: Shirahoshi remains in the Ryugu Kingdom under the protection of the royal family, silently bearing the weight of being the living incarnation of Poseidon. Her whereabouts and safety are a matter of concern for both Luffy's allies and the World Government.

npcs.name: Hody Jones
npcs.base_backstory: Hody Jones grew up on Fish-Man Island fed by a deep hatred of humans, inherited from previous generations without ever having directly suffered at their hands. He rose to captain of the New Fish-Man Pirates and planned a coup against the Ryugu Kingdom, using Energy Steroids to artificially amplify his physical capabilities. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy during the invasion of Ryugu Palace and imprisoned. The excessive use of the steroids drastically accelerated his aging, leaving him as a debilitated elder before he was transferred to Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Level 6, consumed by the hatred that never left him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Exterminate humans and establish Fish-Men as the dominant race of the world.
npcs.mood: Powerless and prematurely aged, trapped in a body destroyed by the steroids themselves.
npcs.description: Hody is a great white shark Fish-Man of imposing stature and massive musculature — or was, before the steroids took their toll. His body aged decades in just a few years, turning him into a frail-looking elder who carries the shadow of what he once was. His eyes still hold the same irrational hatred, but the physique that sustained him is irreversibly ruined.

current_state.summary_text: Hody Jones is imprisoned in Level 6 of Impel Down, his body destroyed by the premature aging caused by the abuse of Energy Steroids. His threat to the world is nonexistent; only the hatred remains.

npcs.name: Hammond
npcs.base_backstory: Hammond is a Fish-Man officer of the New Fish-Man Pirates, the operational right-hand man of Hody Jones on Fish-Man Island. A fanatic for the hatred of humans preached by Hody, he served as recruiter and enforcer of the captain's orders. He was defeated by Luffy and his allies during the battle at Poseidon Palace and subsequently imprisoned in Impel Down along with the other survivors of the group.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fish-Men dominate humans and inherit the world.
npcs.mood: Defeated and restrained, but with hatred intact.
npcs.description: Hammond is a large Fish-Man with the bulky musculature typical of his race and hard features that convey immediate brutality. He carries a spear as his preferred weapon and commands presence through his size and the intensity of his gaze. To those who see him, he gives the impression of a fanatical soldier, ready for violence without hesitation.

current_state.summary_text: Hammond is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the New Fish-Man Pirates on Fish-Man Island. His faction was dismantled and his captain Hody Jones imprisoned at the same destination.

npcs.name: Ikaros Much
npcs.base_backstory: Ikaros Much is a squid-type Fish-Man and officer of the New Fish-Man Pirates under the command of Hody Jones. He actively participated in the attempted coup on Fish-Man Island, where he used his tentacles and ink jets to fight Luffy's allies. After the defeat of Hody and the group, he was arrested and sent to Impel Down, where he serves his sentence alongside other members of the organization.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fish-Men dominate humans, realizing Hody Jones's ideal.
npcs.mood: Resigned and restrained, with no prospect of imminent freedom.
npcs.description: Ikaros Much is a stocky Fish-Man with squid-like features — long tentacles, large eyes, and dark mottled skin. He carries an intimidating presence even in silence, and his slow movements conceal the brutality with which he attacks. He gives the impression of a soldier who blindly believes in the cause he has embraced.

current_state.summary_text: Imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the New Fish-Man Pirates on Fish-Man Island, Ikaros Much awaits incarcerated with no prospect of release.

npcs.name: Dosun
npcs.base_backstory: Dosun is a hammerhead shark Fish-Man and lieutenant of the New Fish-Man Pirates, a group founded by Hody Jones with the goal of exterminating humans and dominating the world in the name of Fish-Men. He participated in the invasion of Fish-Man Island, where he fought against Luffy's allies during the confrontation at the Royal Palace. After the defeat of Hody Jones and the entire group, he was captured and sent to Impel Down, where he serves his sentence. His combat ability is based on a massive club and a power-fluctuation mechanism tied to his verbal exclamations.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and wait for an opportunity to escape or be rescued.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fish-Men dominate humans and rule the world from the depths of the ocean.
npcs.mood: Downcast and restrained, but with latent resentment intact.
npcs.description: Dosun is a hammerhead shark Fish-Man of imposing stature, with the flattened head characteristic of his species and massive musculature. He carries an enormous club as his main weapon. His expression alternates between explosive fury and deep dejection, reflecting the peculiar mechanism that regulates his strength in combat.

current_state.summary_text: Dosun is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the New Fish-Man Pirates on Fish-Man Island. His whereabouts are known to the Marine authorities, and no rescue attempt has been recorded.

npcs.name: Daruma
npcs.base_backstory: Daruma is a cookiecutter shark-type Fish-Man who served as an officer of the New Fish-Man Pirates under the command of Hody Jones. He actively participated in the attempted coup against the Ryugu Kingdom and the rebellion on Fish-Man Island. After the defeat of Hody Jones at the hands of the Straw Hat Pirates, he was captured along with the other survivors of the group. He has been detained in Impel Down ever since.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and wait for any opportunity to escape or be rescued.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fish-Men dominate humans and avenge the historical hatred he harbors against them.
npcs.mood: Restrained by imprisonment, but internally seething with anger and resentment.
npcs.description: Daruma is a short and stocky Fish-Man with a disproportionately large mouth full of serrated shark teeth that he uses both as a weapon and as a tool. His small eyes and permanently furious expression convey instinctive hostility. The impression he gives is that of a barely contained fighting animal — unpredictable and dangerous even when outnumbered.

current_state.summary_text: Daruma is imprisoned in Impel Down following the failed rebellion of the New Fish-Man Pirates against the Ryugu Kingdom. His whereabouts are confirmed, but his internal situation within the prison is unknown.

npcs.name: Zeo
npcs.base_backstory: Zeo is a chameleon Fish-Man and one of the elite officers of the New Fish-Man Pirates, loyal to Hody Jones and the ideology of hatred toward humans. Specialized in stealth combat, he used his natural camouflage ability to turn invisible and attack by surprise, wielding chains in battle. He participated in the coup against King Neptune and the attempted massacre on Fish-Man Island. After the defeat of Hody Jones at the hands of Monkey D. Luffy and his allies, Zeo was captured and handed over to the authorities, being transferred to Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and wait for any opportunity to escape or be rescued.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fish-Men dominate the seas and humans subjugated.
npcs.mood: Restrained and resentful, masking the humiliation of capture with calculated coldness.
npcs.description: Zeo is a Fish-Man with a chameleon-based appearance, with eyes that move independently and skin capable of mimicking the surrounding environment, rendering him practically invisible. His build is robust, typical of Fish-Men, and he carries chains as a combat weapon. He gives the impression of something that is always present without being seen — even when visible, there is something in his posture that suggests he would rather not be there.

current_state.summary_text: Zeo is detained in Impel Down following the defeat of the New Fish-Man Pirates on Fish-Man Island. His faction was dismantled and his leader Hody Jones equally imprisoned, making any prospect of liberation remote.

npcs.name: Madame Shyarly
npcs.base_backstory: Shyarly is a mermaid of prestige on Fish-Man Island, Arlong's sister, and owner of the Mermaid Café. Her clairvoyance is innate and recognized as genuine — her previous predictions, such as the fall of a king, all came true. During Luffy's visit to the island, she predicted that a man in a straw hat would destroy Fish-Man Island, a vision that caused panic and confrontation. After the battle against Hody Jones and the partial fulfillment of the prophecy, Shyarly shattered her crystal ball and declared she would make no more predictions.
npcs.current_goal: Live in peace without making new predictions, managing the Mermaid Café free from the burden of clairvoyance.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Fish-Man Island prosper without her visions being the driving force of others' fates.
npcs.mood: Quiet and introspective, carrying the ambiguity of a prophecy that was fulfilled in ways she did not fully anticipate.
npcs.description: Shyarly is a mermaid with a white tail and an elegant appearance, with piercing eyes that seem to look beyond her interlocutor. Her presence at the Mermaid Café is serene, but there is a silent weight to her — that of someone who has seen too much and chosen to stop looking. She inspires instinctive respect, not through physical power, but through the authority of someone who has never been wrong.

current_state.summary_text: Shyarly remains on Fish-Man Island, running the Mermaid Café, after having shattered her crystal ball and publicly ended her practice of clairvoyance. Her prophecy about the straw hat and the island is considered fulfilled by the inhabitants, though its exact meaning remains a topic of local debate.

npcs.name: Caesar Clown
npcs.base_backstory: Caesar Clown was one of the World Government's top scientists, working alongside Vegapunk before being expelled for conducting forbidden experiments with weapons of mass destruction. He established himself on Punk Hazard, where he conducted illegal chemical warfare research and experiments on children under the secret patronage of Donquixote Doflamingo. He was captured by the Straw Hat Pirates on Punk Hazard and used as a bargaining chip against Doflamingo, being kept under forced custody. After the events of Whole Cake Island, he collaborated with Germa 66 to escape, and his whereabouts have remained uncertain ever since.
npcs.current_goal: Ensure his own survival and continue chemical warfare research out of reach of the World Government and former allies who hunt him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the greatest scientist in the world, surpassing Vegapunk.
npcs.mood: Anxious and paranoid, moving in the shadows to avoid being captured by any of the major factions.
npcs.description: Caesar is a tall, skeletal man with gray skin and yellow eyes with cross-shaped pupils — a characteristic trait of a Gasu Gasu no Mi user. He wears a worn laboratory coat and carries with him a permanent air of intellectual superiority tainted by visible cowardice. His presence causes immediate discomfort: there is something unstable in his broad gestures and in his smile that is too wide for the situation.

current_state.summary_text: Caesar Clown is a renegade scientist specializing in chemical warfare whose whereabouts are unknown; he was last seen in the company of Germa 66 following the events of Whole Cake Island.

npcs.name: Vergo
npcs.base_backstory: Vergo infiltrated the Marines as Doflamingo's long-standing agent, rising to the rank of Vice Admiral while maintaining absolute loyalty to the Donquixote Pirates. He served as the secret link between the criminal organization and the World Government, operating in Punk Hazard as commander of base G-5. He was defeated by Trafalgar Law in Punk Hazard, who used the power of the Ope Ope no Mi to slice his body along with the entire facility, killing him in the subsequent explosion.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve Doflamingo with absolute loyalty until the end.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
npcs.description: A man of imposing stature, dark hair combed back, and a permanently serious expression. He carries a bamboo staff used as his main weapon, often with food stuck to his face — a detail that absurdly contrasts with his aura of genuine menace. His physical presence radiates authority and contained danger.

current_state.summary_text: Vergo died in Punk Hazard after being defeated by Trafalgar Law, who sliced him along with the entire G-5 facility before its explosion. His double identity as a Donquixote Pirates agent infiltrated within the Marines was exposed during the events of that arc.

npcs.name: Monet
npcs.base_backstory: Monet was recruited by Donquixote Doflamingo along with her sister Sugar as children, becoming a loyal agent of the Donquixote Pirates. She infiltrated Punk Hazard as Caesar Clown's secretary, monitoring his activities on Doflamingo's behalf. During Luffy's invasion of Punk Hazard, she confronted the Straw Hat Pirates and was paralyzed by Zoro, who chose not to kill her — but she ultimately died when Caesar inadvertently destroyed the heart Trafalgar Law had swapped out, which belonged to her.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve Doflamingo and ensure the success of the Donquixote Pirates' plans at any cost.
npcs.mood: N/A
npcs.description: Monet had the appearance of a harpy, with snow wings in place of her arms and bird legs with sharp talons — transformations imposed by Law's surgery at Doflamingo's request. Her yellow eyes and constant smile conveyed an unsettling calm, as if violence were merely part of a routine.

current_state.summary_text: Monet died in Punk Hazard when Caesar Clown accidentally destroyed the heart she carried in her chest — someone else's heart, swapped by Law. Her loyalty to Doflamingo was absolute until the end.

npcs.name: Tashigi
npcs.base_backstory: Tashigi joined the Marines driven by a personal goal: to rescue noble swords from the hands of criminals and return them to worthy destinies. She became Smoker's direct subordinate, following him from the Loguetown Base to base G-5 in the New World. In Punk Hazard, she led G-5 soldiers against Caesar Clown and Doflamingo's experiments, playing a decisive role in protecting the kidnapped children. Following the events of Dressrosa and the subsequent restructuring, she remains a Captain under Smoker's command.
npcs.current_goal: Capture dangerous pirates in the New World and rescue noble swords from the hands of criminals.
npcs.long_term_dream: To collect all great swords in the hands of criminals and return them to worthy purposes.
npcs.mood: Determined and slightly frustrated with the slow pace of progress toward her ideals.
npcs.description: Tashigi is a young woman of average height with short dark hair partially pinned back and thin-framed glasses that rarely stay in place. She always carries the katana Shigure at her hip and wears the Marine Captain's uniform with a firm posture, though her expression frequently betrays a mixture of determination and frustration with herself. The impression she gives is of someone genuinely committed to justice, but still in the process of proving her worth — to herself more than to anyone else.

current_state.summary_text: Tashigi serves as a Marine Captain at base G-5 in the New World, operating under Smoker's command and maintaining active operations against pirates in the region.

npcs.name: Momonosuke
npcs.devil_fruit: Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Seiryu (artificial)
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Kozuki Oden and Toki, Momonosuke was sent to the future by his mother to escape Oden's execution by Orochi and Kaido, arriving twenty years ahead along with his retainers. During that period he accidentally consumed the artificial dragon Zoan Fruit created by Vegapunk, gaining the ability to transform. After Kaido's fall in Wano, he assumed the title of Shogun and sealed the country, declaring Wano's isolation to protect it. His matured transformation into an adult dragon was decisive in the final events of the conflict on Onigashima.
npcs.current_goal: To rule Wano as Shogun and rebuild the country after decades of oppression, maintaining the kingdom's isolation.
npcs.long_term_dream: To open Wano's borders to the world when the country is ready, fulfilling Kozuki Oden's legacy.
npcs.mood: Solemnly determined, bearing the weight of governance with forced maturity and longing for the allies who have departed.
npcs.description: Momonosuke is a young man with long black hair and features that strongly evoke his father, Kozuki Oden. He wears traditional samurai attire befitting a shogun, carrying the austere dignity of the Kozuki clan. He can transform into an enormous pinkish-white dragon, a form he uses to fly and move clouds. His presence inspires devotion in Wano's samurai, even as he visibly bears the weight of someone who grew up under tragedy.

current_state.summary_text: Momonosuke rules Wano as Shogun of the Flower Capital, with the country rebuilding after Kaido's fall. He maintains the kingdom's isolation by his own decree, awaiting the right moment to open Wano to the world.

npcs.name: Yeti Cool Brothers
npcs.base_backstory: Rock and Scotch are two assassins of enormous stature recruited by Caesar Clown to operate in Punk Hazard as enforcers and guardians. They served as Caesar's armed arm, patrolling the island and eliminating threats with precision rifles. They were defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates and their allies during the incident in Punk Hazard. After Caesar's fall and the island's abandonment, their whereabouts are unknown.
npcs.current_goal: To survive and find a new employer after the dissolution of Caesar's operations in Punk Hazard.
npcs.long_term_dream: To continue operating as paid elite assassins — survival and the trade are an end in themselves.
npcs.mood: Neutral and waiting — no active mission, no visible agitation.
npcs.description: Rock and Scotch are two men of colossal stature, covered in snow gear and hoods that conceal almost their entire faces, with long-range rifles always at hand. Their presence evokes the literal coldness of the environment they operate in — there is no exposed anger or cruelty, only calculated efficiency. Seen together, they seem like a single entity split into two bodies.

current_state.summary_text: The Yeti Cool Brothers were defeated during the Punk Hazard incident and their whereabouts following the fall of Caesar Clown's operations on the island are unknown.

npcs.name: Brownbeard
npcs.base_backstory: Brownbeard was a mid-level pirate captain operating in the New World until he was defeated and had his legs destroyed. Caesar Clown saved him with mechanical lizard prosthetics, making him the leader of the Punk Hazard Centaurs and a loyal bodyguard to the scientist. During the Straw Hat Pirates' invasion of Punk Hazard, he was captured and confronted with the truth about Caesar, realizing he had been manipulated. After the events of Punk Hazard, he was taken by the Marines along with the other survivors of the island.
npcs.current_goal: To survive Marine custody and rebuild some sense of purpose after Caesar's betrayal.
npcs.long_term_dream: To reclaim his dignity as a pirate captain and take revenge on Caesar Clown for using him as a pawn.
npcs.mood: Bitter and disoriented, bearing the weight of having been completely deceived by someone he considered his benefactor.
npcs.description: A stocky man with a thick brown beard that gives him his name, mounted on mechanical lizard-leg prosthetics that raise him above most men. Despite his intimidating appearance, his eyes reveal a chronic naivety. He gives the impression of a second-rate tough who believes himself far more important than he actually is.

current_state.summary_text: Brownbeard is in Marine custody following the events of Punk Hazard, where he was captured along with the other survivors of the island. His loyalty to Caesar Clown was shattered upon discovering that the scientist never truly intended to heal him.

npcs.name: Donquixote Doflamingo
npcs.base_backstory: Born a Celestial Dragon, Doflamingo was cast out of Mary Geoise by his father's decision and spent his childhood as a target of commoners' hatred, forging in him a deep resentment against the world. He murdered his own father while still young and, using secrets about the Celestial Dragons as blackmail currency, forced the World Government to grant him a seat among the Seven Warlords of the Sea. Under the codename Joker, he built the underworld's largest weapons trafficking scheme — the SMILE factory in Dressrosa — while ruling the island with the Ito Ito no Mi and terror. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy at the Dressrosa Colosseum and handed over to the Marines.
npcs.current_goal: To survive imprisonment and await any opening that allows him to regain influence over the underworld or the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: To destroy the order of the Celestial Dragons and rule the world in the chaos that would remain — proving that only the strongest deserve to exist.
npcs.mood: Contained and cold; the arrogance remains intact, but the defeat has left a crack he will not admit.
npcs.description: Tall and of imposing bearing, Doflamingo wears a long pink coat draped over his shoulders like a cape and yellow-lensed sunglasses he rarely removes. The wide grin and bared teeth are a constant trademark, conveying genuine pleasure in cruelty. His presence fills any room with a suffocating tension — those who face him feel they are standing before something that respects no human rule.

current_state.summary_text: Doflamingo is imprisoned in Level 6 of Impel Down under maximum surveillance, stripped of his Seven Warlords of the Sea title and control over Dressrosa. His underworld network has been dismantled, but the secrets he carries about the Celestial Dragons keep him alive and politically inconvenient.

npcs.name: Diamante
npcs.base_backstory: Diamante served as one of the three executive officers at the Executive level of the Donquixote Pirates, leading the entertainment sector and managing the Corrida Colosseum in Dressrosa. He was one of the pillars of Doflamingo's operation on the island, responsible for overseeing the fights and maintaining the spectacle that concealed the Devil Fruit trafficking. During the battle of Dressrosa, he faced Rebecca and Kyros, being defeated by the former general. With Doflamingo's fall, he was captured by the Marines along with the other family members.
npcs.current_goal: To survive imprisonment and await any opportunity for escape or rescue.
npcs.long_term_dream: To restore the Donquixote family to power and reclaim his prominent role in the underworld.
npcs.mood: Contained and resentful, masking the humiliation of defeat with forced haughtiness.
npcs.description: Diamante is a man of average height with voluminous reddish-pink hair and extravagant clothing that reinforces his showman persona. His eyes carry a calculated coldness behind the stage smile. His presence evokes the distorted glamour of a master of ceremonies who would never hesitate to send someone to their death amid applause.

current_state.summary_text: Diamante is imprisoned following the Donquixote Pirates' defeat in Dressrosa, with the family dismantled and Doflamingo in Marine custody. His exact whereabouts within the prison system are not widely disclosed.

npcs.name: Pica
npcs.base_backstory: Pica is one of the three Top Officers of the Donquixote Pirates, serving as one of the organization's military pillars. He commands the Spade Army and holds the highest rank in the hierarchy below Doflamingo and the Executives. During the battle of Dressrosa, he merged with the island's very structure and assumed a colossal stone form to crush his opponents. He was defeated by Zoro and, after Doflamingo's fall, captured by the authorities.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment and await any chance to resume service to Doflamingo.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve and protect Donquixote Doflamingo above all else.
npcs.mood: Restrained and fierce, consumed by frustrated loyalty.
npcs.description: Pica is a man of immense stature, with a massive body, square jaw, and an expression permanently laden with hostility. He wears a mask over his face and the characteristic garb of the Donquixote Pirates. The contradiction between his intimidating physique and his shrilly high-pitched voice provokes involuntary reactions in those who encounter him for the first time — reactions he does not tolerate.

current_state.summary_text: Pica is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat and capture of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa. His loyalty to Doflamingo remains unshaken even behind bars.

npcs.name: Trebol
npcs.base_backstory: Trebol found Doflamingo as a child, when the latter was living on the streets as a fallen Celestial Dragon. Together with Vergo, Pica, and Diamante, he became one of the four founding pillars of the Donquixote Pirates, being the one responsible for delivering the Ope Ope no Mi to Doflamingo. For decades he served as the Heart Executive Officer, controlling the production and distribution of SMILE in Dressrosa under Doflamingo's rule. He was defeated by Luffy and Law during the fall of Dressrosa.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment while awaiting any chance to reunite with or serve Doflamingo again.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Doflamingo restored to power and to the throne of the Celestial Dragons.
npcs.mood: Downcast and bitter, but with his devotion to Doflamingo intact.
npcs.description: An extremely obese middle-aged man, coated in a constant layer of sticky mucus that conceals his body and distorts his silhouette. He wears round glasses and a pointed hat that reinforce his eccentric fool appearance. The first impression is one of absurdity, but those who underestimate the viscosity of his mucus Haki pay dearly.

current_state.summary_text: Trebol is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa. His exact whereabouts within the prison are unknown, but his capture is an established fact.

npcs.name: Lao G
npcs.base_backstory: Lao G served the Donquixote Pirates for decades, becoming one of Doflamingo's oldest and most loyal officers. A combatant specialized in martial arts, he developed his own technique that channels internal energy into devastating physical explosions. He fought in Dressrosa during the operation to retake the country, being defeated by Chinjao and his grandson Sai. After Doflamingo's fall and the dismantling of the organization in Dressrosa, he was captured and sent to Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and preserve his dignity as a veteran combatant.
npcs.long_term_dream: To prove that his martial art and his strength do not decay with age, dying as a respected warrior.
npcs.mood: Resigned, but still proud — he refuses to show weakness even while imprisoned.
npcs.description: An elderly man of short stature with a body hardened by decades of martial training. He dresses in the characteristic clothing of Donquixote officers and maintains an upright posture despite his years. Despite the appearance of a frail old man, his eyes carry the intensity of an experienced fighter, and his fists bear the marks of countless battles.

current_state.summary_text: Lao G is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa. He remains alive and in captivity under Marine custody.

npcs.name: Senor Pink
npcs.base_backstory: He was a high-ranking officer of the Donquixote Pirates, serving Doflamingo for decades. He married a woman named Russian, who was unaware of his life as a pirate; when she discovered the truth, she suffered a stroke and fell into a coma. Their infant son, Gimlet, died of illness shortly after. Since then, Senor Pink began wearing baby clothes as a silent tribute to his son and to the wife who never woke up again. He was defeated by Franky during the battle of Dressrosa and captured after Doflamingo's fall.
npcs.current_goal: Serve his sentence in silence, without betraying the memory of Russian and Gimlet.
npcs.long_term_dream: To honor the memory of his wife and son, even if that means carrying the guilt for the rest of his life.
npcs.mood: Resigned and closed off — he endures captivity with the same impassiveness with which he endured everything else.
npcs.description: Senor Pink is a heavyset middle-aged man with broad shoulders and a permanently somber expression. He wears a blue baby romper, bonnet, and pacifier — an outfit that carries a devastating emotional weight beneath the absurdity. He makes a disconcerting impression: simultaneously ridiculous and genuinely threatening, with eyes that reveal a silent pain that has never been resolved.

current_state.summary_text: Senor Pink is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa. He remains silent, showing no intention of resistance or escape.

npcs.name: Sugar
npcs.base_backstory: Sugar ate the Hobi Hobi no Mi as a child, which permanently froze her appearance at 10 years of age, even though she is already 22. She was recruited by Doflamingo as a special officer of the Donquixote Pirates and became a central piece of control over Dressrosa: she transformed citizens and enemies into toys, erasing their existence from everyone's memory. Her operation was dismantled when Usopp made her faint from terror, reverting all transformations and exposing Doflamingo's lies. With Doflamingo's fall in Dressrosa, Sugar was captured by the authorities and sent to Impel Down.
npcs.current_goal: Survive captivity and await any opportunity to escape or be rescued.
npcs.long_term_dream: To regain control over others through her powers, never being dominated or humiliated again.
npcs.mood: Resentful and restrained, carrying silent hatred — especially toward Usopp.
npcs.description: Sugar has the permanent appearance of a 10-year-old child, with short purple hair and large, expressive eyes that rarely show emotion. She wears elaborate clothing befitting her position as a special officer. Despite her small stature and childlike features, the coldness of her gaze and the precision of her gestures betray an adult, calculating mind behind the innocent facade.

current_state.summary_text: Detained in Impel Down following the collapse of Doflamingo's regime in Dressrosa, Sugar remains imprisoned, her powers neutralized by the prison environment and the absence of free targets.

npcs.name: Buffalo
npcs.base_backstory: Buffalo is an officer of the Donquixote Pirates, loyal to Doflamingo and close to Baby 5, with whom he forms a combat duo. He participated in the operation at Punk Hazard alongside Baby 5, sent to eliminate Trafalgar Law on Doflamingo's orders. He was defeated by Franky during the events of Dressrosa and captured by the Marines together with Baby 5. He has been imprisoned in Impel Down ever since.
npcs.current_goal: Survive captivity and await any chance to reunite with Baby 5 or the Donquixote remnants.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve his captain and protect Baby 5 at any cost.
npcs.mood: Resigned, but still fervent when the subject is Baby 5 or Doflamingo.
npcs.description: Buffalo is a man of average height with a round face and prominent cheeks, dark hair, and an always intense expression. His Devil Fruit allows him to spin his entire body like a propeller, and he fights exclusively in the air, using rotation as the basis of all his attacks. He gives the impression of a dedicated brute — dangerous in aerial combat, but without tactical depth beyond that.

current_state.summary_text: Buffalo is imprisoned in Impel Down following Doflamingo's fall in Dressrosa. His situation within the prison and eventual fate remain uncertain.

npcs.name: Gladius
npcs.base_backstory: Gladius served as an officer of the Donquixote Pirates under Doflamingo, leading the Pica block alongside the other officers. During the fall of Dressrosa, he was defeated by Cavendish and his faction was dismantled by Luffy's alliance. After Doflamingo's imprisonment in Impel Down, Gladius was captured and sent to the same prison, where he remains detained.
npcs.current_goal: Survive imprisonment in Impel Down and await any opportunity for escape or rescue.
npcs.long_term_dream: To avenge Doflamingo's fall and restore the Donquixote Pirates.
npcs.mood: Angry and frustrated, held in check by the prison.
npcs.description: Gladius is a man of average height with a peculiarly large head and a wide-brimmed black hat that appears fused to his skull. He wears the dark uniform of Donquixote officers and maintains a permanently tense expression, as if he were about to explode. His presence radiates contained hostility — any carelessness around him feels like a real risk.

current_state.summary_text: Gladius is imprisoned in Impel Down following the defeat of the Donquixote Pirates in Dressrosa. He remains detained with no immediate prospect of freedom.

npcs.name: Baby 5
npcs.base_backstory: Raised on the streets with no one who wanted her, Baby 5 developed an irreversible compulsion to fulfill any request from any person, out of fear of being discarded again. Recruited by Doflamingo while still young, she served as an officer of the Donquixote Pirates for years, being used as an enforcer and field operative on high-risk missions. During Doflamingo's fall in Dressrosa, she allied herself with Luffy's allies and grew close to Sai, heir to the Happo Navy. She married Sai after Dressrosa and left Doflamingo's crew for good, going on to live in the Kano Country alongside her husband.
npcs.current_goal: Live alongside Sai and be of use to the Happo Navy and the Kano Country.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be truly needed by someone who wants her in return — found in Sai and the Happo Navy.
npcs.mood: Stable and dedicated, with flashes of jealousy and emotional hypervigilance.
npcs.description: Baby 5 is a young woman with long black hair and a slender body, frequently seen in clothing that exposes the limbs she transforms into weapons. She carries a simultaneously dangerous and vulnerable presence — her large, expressive eyes betray the emotional need that contrasts with the cannons and blades that sprout from her arms. In combat she is versatile and lethal; outside of it, she is the first to ask if she can help with something.

current_state.summary_text: Baby 5 lives in the Kano Country as Sai's wife and an affiliated member of the Happo Navy, far removed from any loyalty to the Donquixote Pirates. Her compulsion to be useful remains intact, now directed toward her husband and her new family.

npcs.name: Violet
npcs.base_backstory: Daughter of King Riku Doldo III, Violet watched the throne of Dressrosa be usurped by Donquixote Doflamingo and spent years infiltrating his organization under the codename Giolla — in truth, Viola, second princess of the Riku family. She used her clairvoyance powers to spy from within while secretly protecting her father. After Doflamingo's defeat at Luffy's hands, the Riku family retook control of Dressrosa and Violet resumed her role as princess. She has since remained in Dressrosa, helping with the reconstruction of the kingdom and governing alongside her father.
npcs.current_goal: Support the reconstruction of Dressrosa and protect her father and the kingdom.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Dressrosa prosperous and free from any shadow that would oppress it again.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and serene — she carries the weight of the years of infiltration, but finds stability in the home she reclaimed.
npcs.description: Violet is a woman of striking beauty, with large and expressive eyes that seem to see beyond what any person should be able to. She carries herself with the grace of a dancer even off the stage, and every movement holds calculated precision. Her presence conveys calm — and a silent warning that nothing escapes her notice.

current_state.summary_text: Violet serves as princess of Dressrosa alongside King Riku Doldo III, participating in the governance and reconstruction of the kingdom following Doflamingo's fall. Her whereabouts are Dressrosa.

npcs.name: Kyros
npcs.base_backstory: Born into humble circumstances, Kyros became the greatest gladiator in the history of the Dressrosa Colosseum, accumulating 3,000 victories without a single defeat. After being transformed into a toy soldier by Sugar on Donquixote Doflamingo's orders, his existence was erased from everyone's memory — including Rebecca's, his own daughter. During Doflamingo's fall in Dressrosa, he regained his human form and decapitated Doflamingo himself, ending decades of oppression over the kingdom. Since then, he has lived in Dressrosa as a loyal protector of the Riku Royal Family and Rebecca's acknowledged father.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Rebecca and the Riku Royal Family as Dressrosa rebuilds under the reign of Riku Doldo III.
npcs.long_term_dream: Atone for his violent past and ensure that Rebecca lives free from suffering, in a Dressrosa at peace.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and restrained — he carries the peace of someone who has fulfilled his most urgent mission, but never lets his guard down.
npcs.description: A man of imposing stature, his musculature marked by decades of combat and his left leg amputated below the knee, replaced by a functional prosthetic. His face is austere, with scars that map an entire lifetime in the arena. Those who encounter him feel the weight of someone who has killed more times than he can count — and who chose, despite that, to stand by those he protects.

current_state.summary_text: Kyros remains in Dressrosa as protector of the Riku Royal Family, living openly alongside Rebecca for the first time since he was turned into a toy. His legend as the undefeated gladiator is known throughout the kingdom, but he refuses any role of public prominence.

npcs.name: Rebecca
npcs.base_backstory: Daughter of Kyros and granddaughter of King Riku Doldo III, Rebecca grew up without knowing her father's identity, as he lived transformed into a toy soldier by Sugar's curse. After the death of her mother Scarlett, she was raised alone by the "toy soldier" without ever recognizing him as her father. She became a gladiator in the Corrida Colosseum to survive, adopting a purely defensive style to avoid harming her opponents. With Doflamingo's defeat by Luffy and the breaking of Sugar's curse, she was reunited with Kyros and abdicated her life in the arena to return to the palace as the rightful princess of Dressrosa.
npcs.current_goal: Fulfill her role as princess of Dressrosa and support the reconstruction of the kingdom alongside King Riku and her father Kyros.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Dressrosa prosperous and at peace, and live alongside her father without the burden of the past.
npcs.mood: Calm and hopeful, carrying the scars of the difficult years but looking toward the kingdom's future.
npcs.description: Rebecca is a young woman with pink hair and blue eyes, with an athletic build forged through years in the arena. She dresses with the dignity of a princess, far from the gladiator armor she wore in the Colosseum. She carries a presence that is at once delicate and determined — those who knew her in the arena know that behind the gentle smile lies a fighter who never backed down.

current_state.summary_text: Rebecca lives in Dressrosa as a recognized princess of the Riku Family, alongside her father Kyros and her grandfather Riku Doldo III. She left her life as a gladiator behind and dedicates herself to the reconstruction of the kingdom after the years under Doflamingo's rule.

npcs.name: King Riku
npcs.base_backstory: Riku Doldo III governed Dressrosa as a beloved king before being manipulated by Donquixote Doflamingo, who forced him to attack his own subjects and then used the episode to seize the throne. He spent a decade under Doflamingo's rule, kept as a humiliated figure and symbol of the supposed failure of the Riku family. After Doflamingo's defeat at the hands of Luffy and the Straw Hat Pirates, the throne was returned to him and the truth about the coup came to light. He governs Dressrosa once more, dedicated to rebuilding the kingdom and honoring those who suffered during the years of occupation.
npcs.current_goal: Rebuild Dressrosa and restore the people's trust in the Riku family after the years of Doflamingo's rule.
npcs.long_term_dream: Leave Dressrosa prosperous and just enough that the infamy forged by Doflamingo can never be repeated.
npcs.mood: Serene and determined, bearing the responsibility of restoration with dignity.
npcs.description: An elderly man of noble bearing, with white hair and long mustaches that fall over his chest. He wears Dressrosa's royal garments and carries in his eyes the mixture of old guilt and serene determination of one who survived betrayal and public humiliation. His presence radiates gentle authority — the kind of king who rules through trust, not fear.

current_state.summary_text: He governs Dressrosa as its restored king following Doflamingo's fall, leading the reconstruction of the kingdom with the same compassion that once made him an easy target for Doflamingo's manipulation — and that is now his greatest strength.

npcs.name: Cavendish
npcs.base_backstory: Cavendish was once the most famous pirate prince of the New Generation, an idol of the crowds and consumed by murderous jealousy toward the Supernovas who stole his spotlight. He carries a second personality, Hakuba, which awakens in combat and cuts down everything around him at a speed impossible to track. He participated in the Mera Mera no Mi Tournament in Dressrosa, where he allied with Luffy and fought against Doflamingo's forces. After the victory in Dressrosa, he became one of the captains of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, leading his fleet as commander of the First Army.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the prestige of the Beautiful Pirates and honor the blood oath with the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, awaiting Luffy's call.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the most beautiful and powerful pirate on the seas, outshining any rival who dares to steal his spotlight.
npcs.mood: Haughty and restless, torn between the wounded pride of serving another and the genuine loyalty he cannot deny.
npcs.description: Cavendish is a man of outrageous beauty — flowing blond hair, sculpted features, and a smile he himself considers a work of art. He dresses with aristocratic care and carries Durandal at his hip as one who displays a jewel. His presence draws eyes wherever he goes, but those who know Hakuba understand that behind the charm dwells something far more dangerous.

current_state.summary_text: He commands the Beautiful Pirates as captain of the First Army of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, sailing the Grand Line in wait for Monkey D. Luffy's call. His exact whereabouts are uncertain.

npcs.name: Ideo
npcs.base_backstory: Ideo is an elite boxer who participated in the Dressrosa Colosseum Tournament, where he demonstrated explosive strength sufficient to rival high-caliber fighters. After Doflamingo's fall, he joined other former tournament combatants to form the Ideo Pirates, taking on the role of captain. The group subsequently joined the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, swearing loyalty to Monkey D. Luffy following the events of Dressrosa.
npcs.current_goal: Command the Ideo Pirates and strengthen his position as captain within the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove that his fists are the most powerful in the world and lead his companions to glory.
npcs.mood: Determined and focused, ready for combat at any moment.
npcs.description: Ideo is a muscular man of imposing stature, with broad shoulders and developed arms that reflect decades of boxing. His face carries a permanently serious and challenging expression. His presence in any room is physical — he occupies space like someone who expects conflict to come to him.

current_state.summary_text: Ideo commands the Ideo Pirates as captain and serves as a member of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet; his exact whereabouts in the Grand Line are unknown.

npcs.name: Hajrudin
npcs.base_backstory: Hajrudin was born in Elbaf and grew up imbued with the warrior pride of his people. Before joining the Grand Fleet, he participated in the Dressrosa Colosseum Tournament as a gladiator, where he was ultimately defeated by Luffy. After Doflamingo's fall, he swore loyalty to Luffy and founded the New Giant Warrior Pirates as captain, taking on the role of commander of the 5th Army of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.
npcs.current_goal: Strengthen the New Giant Warrior Pirates and consolidate the name of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet across the seas.
npcs.long_term_dream: Revive the legend of the Giant Pirates of Elbaf and elevate the giants to the top of the pirate world.
npcs.mood: Determined and eager for battle, sailing with his crew in search of worthy opponents.
npcs.description: Hajrudin is a giant of colossal stature, with massive musculature, blond hair, and features that evoke the ancestral warriors of Elbaf. He wears partial armor and always carries the upright posture of one who never learned to bow. His presence on the field is enough to intimidate entire platoons before a single punch is thrown.

current_state.summary_text: Captain of the New Giant Warrior Pirates and commander of the 5th Army of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, Hajrudin sails the New World with his crew; his exact whereabouts are uncertain, but his loyalty to Luffy remains unshaken.

npcs.name: Bartolomeo
npcs.base_backstory: Born in the East Blue, Bartolomeo was a feared criminal before witnessing the execution of Portgas D. Ace at Marineford — an event that transformed him into a fanatical devotee of Monkey D. Luffy. He ate the Bari Bari no Mi and became captain of the Barto Club, gaining notoriety in the Corrida Colosseum tournament in Dressrosa. After the events of Dressrosa, he joined the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its vassal captains. Later, his crew was attacked and destroyed by Shanks after they raised the Straw Hat Pirates' flag in a manner considered disrespectful.
npcs.current_goal: Rebuild the Barto Club and continue serving the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, spreading Luffy's glory across the seas.
npcs.long_term_dream: Witness Monkey D. Luffy become King of the Pirates and have been part of that journey.
npcs.mood: Determined and fervent, still shaken by the destruction of his crew by Shanks, but incapable of losing faith in his idol.
npcs.description: Bartolomeo is a young man with spiked green hair, intimidating eyes, and his tongue frequently sticking out — the appearance of a thug that contrasts with the emotional breakdown he suffers in the presence of any Straw Hat. He bears tattoos and wears flashy clothes typical of an East Blue pirate. The impression he gives is of someone dangerous and unpredictable, until he mentions Luffy and transforms into a fan in a trance.

current_state.summary_text: Captain of the Barto Club and vassal of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, Bartolomeo operates on the seas after having his crew devastated by Shanks — exact whereabouts uncertain, but his devotion to Luffy remains unshakeable.

npcs.name: Don Chinjao
npcs.base_backstory: Don Chinjao was one of the most feared pirates in the world, with a drill-shaped head capable of splitting icebergs — and an immense fortune hidden beneath one of them. Garp destroyed his head with a legendary punch, sealing away the fortune and creating a grudge that lasted decades. In the Corrida tournament, Luffy defeated him and inadvertently restored his head to its original shape with a punch, freeing the fortune and dissolving the accumulated hatred. Since then, Chinjao has recognized Luffy as his benefactor and leads the Happo Navy under the banner of Luffy's Pirate Alliance.
npcs.current_goal: Command the Happo Navy and preserve the honor and legacy of the Chinjao clan.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Chinjao name revered by all generations as a symbol of strength and martial honor.
npcs.mood: Serene and proud, with the old grudge extinguished and the fortune recovered.
npcs.description: Chinjao is an elderly man, muscular and imposing, with a distinctive drill-shaped head — the hallmark of his lineage and fighting technique. He wears traditional Kano Country clothing and carries the unshakable bearing of someone who spent decades at the top of the pirate world. His presence commands immediate respect; those who know him understand that behind the old man there still dwells a warrior capable of devastation.

current_state.summary_text: Chinjao commands the Happo Navy in the Kano Country, integrated into Monkey D. Luffy's network of allies. With the grudge against Garp extinguished and the clan's fortune recovered, he dedicates himself to strengthening the Chinjao lineage.

npcs.name: Orlumbus
npcs.base_backstory: Orlumbus built his reputation as an exceptional navigator by sailing the New World at the head of the Yonta Maria Fleet, becoming known as "The Adventurer". He participated in the tournament at the Corrida Colosseum in Dressrosa, where he ended up involved in the battle against Donquixote Doflamingo. After the allies' victory, he swore loyalty to Monkey D. Luffy, joining the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its seven commanders. He leads his fleet autonomously, honoring the pact to answer Luffy's call when necessary.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Yonta Maria Fleet operational and ready to answer Luffy's call, while conducting expeditions across the New World.
npcs.long_term_dream: Explore and chart the most dangerous seas in the world at the head of his fleet, achieving glory as the greatest adventurer admiral of the seas.
npcs.mood: Serene and focused, managing his fleet with disciplined routine while awaiting the Grand Fleet's call.
aliases: Admiral of the Yonta Maria Fleet
npcs.description: Orlumbus is a man of imposing stature, with neatly combed dark hair, a mustache and trimmed beard, always wearing an admiral's coat draped over his shoulders. He carries enormous iron balls as weapons, wielding them with a ease that is disconcerting for someone of his elegance. He conveys the calm authority of an experienced commander, more given to tactical planning than to boasting.

current_state.summary_text: Orlumbus commands the Yonta Maria Fleet as one of the seven leaders of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, operating autonomously across the New World. His exact whereabouts are uncertain, but his loyalty to the pact with Luffy remains firm.

npcs.name: Sai
npcs.base_backstory: Grandson of Don Chinjao and heir to the Happo Navy, Sai grew up mastering Hasshoken, a martial style capable of transmitting devastating vibrations through any point of contact. He participated in the tournament at the Corrida Colosseum in Dressrosa, where he faced elite fighters and ultimately defeated his own grandfather Chinjao in a duel. After Doflamingo's fall, he formally assumed the post of 13th Commander of the Happo Navy and became engaged to Baby 5. Since then, he has led the fleet from the Kano Country, maintaining the alliance with the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate leadership of the Happo Navy and protect the Kano Country.
npcs.long_term_dream: Elevate the Happo Navy to the same level of respect that his grandfather Chinjao earned, preserving the legacy of Hasshoken.
npcs.mood: Firm and focused on the responsibilities of leadership.
npcs.description: Sai is a young man of athletic build, with short dark hair and a habitually serious expression. He wears practical combat attire befitting the Happo Navy tradition. His firm bearing and calculating gaze convey natural authority — those who see him understand without words that he is in command.

current_state.summary_text: Sai commands the Happo Navy as its 13th leader from the Kano Country, alongside Baby 5. He maintains the alliance with the Straw Hat Grand Fleet and watches over the continuity of the Hasshoken style.

npcs.name: Inuarashi
npcs.base_backstory: Inuarashi is one of the two rulers of the Mokomo Dukedom on Zou, holding the title of Duke of Day — governing from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. while Nekomamushi rules the night. A former member of Roger's crew and a close ally of the Kozuki of Wano, he stood alongside Oden during the great voyages of the Pirate Era. After Kaido's fall in Wano, he returned to Zou to resume his duties as sovereign. His longstanding rivalry with Nekomamushi was overcome, and the two resumed governing Zou in harmony.
npcs.current_goal: Rule and protect Zou as Duke of Day, maintaining the security of the Mokomo Dukedom.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve Zou and its people, honoring the legacy of the Kozuki and the alliance forged with the Straw Hats.
npcs.mood: Serene and vigilant, governing with stability restored after the victory in Wano.
npcs.description: Inuarashi is an imposing canine-looking Mink of great stature and light fur, frequently dressed in noble attire that reinforces his position as duke. He carries a long sword and radiates a presence of restrained authority — the kind of warrior who does not need to raise his voice to be obeyed. His Sulong form, activated by the full moon, transforms him into a devastating force of nature.

current_state.summary_text: Rules Zou as Duke of Day alongside Nekomamushi, with the island stable following the conclusion of Wano. He remains one of the most powerful Mink warriors and a steadfast ally of the Straw Hats.

npcs.name: Nekomamushi
npcs.base_backstory: Nekomamushi is one of the two rulers of the Mokomo Dukedom on Zou, holding the title of Duke of Night and leading his tribe from 6 p.m. to 6 a.m. A devoted retainer of Kozuki Oden, he accompanied the daimyo of Wano during his journey with the Roger Pirates and the Whitebeard Pirates, guarding the secret of the Poneglyphs for decades. After Kaido's fall in Wano and the country's liberation, he returned with the other allies, having fulfilled his mission of rallying the samurai and Minks for the decisive battle. He maintains a brotherly rivalry with Inuarashi, his co-ruler and eternal counterpart.
npcs.current_goal: Rule and protect the Mink Tribe on Zou, honoring the legacy of Kozuki Oden.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the King of the Pirates emerge and the Dawn of the World come to pass, in accordance with Oden's will.
npcs.mood: Exuberant and at peace — the war in Wano ended in victory, and his land and people are safe.
npcs.description: Nekomamushi is an imposing feline Mink of great stature, with orange fur and golden eyes that gleam with predatory intensity. He wears elaborate attire befitting his position as duke, but the aspect of a wild warrior never fades — sharp claws always on display and the bearing of someone ready for combat. He gives the simultaneous impression of a party about to begin and a storm about to break.

current_state.summary_text: Rules the Mokomo Dukedom as Duke of Night alongside Inuarashi, with the Mink Tribe stabilized after the victory in Wano. His whereabouts are Zou, where he has resumed his leadership duties.

npcs.name: Wanda
npcs.base_backstory: Wanda is a Mink of the Zou Tribe and a member of the Guardians, the protective force of Zou's forest. When the Beasts Pirates invaded Zou under Jack's command, she fought and survived the massacre, being found wounded by the Straw Hat Pirates. She actively participated in the alliance between the Mink Tribe, the Kozuki clan, and the Straw Hats that culminated in the Wano campaign. After Kaido's defeat in Wano, she returned to Zou alongside her tribe.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the safety of Zou and her tribe after the trauma of the Beasts Pirates' invasion.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that Zou and the Mink Tribe are never subjugated by an outside force again.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and stable — the immediate threat has passed, but her guard does not drop.
npcs.description: Wanda is an athletic Mink with the characteristic ears and tail of her species, typically seen in light combat armor with a sword at her hip. She carries the natural electricity of the Minks — Electro — which crackles in her strikes. She conveys immediate authority: upright posture, calculating gaze, the presence of someone who has seen real battle and survived.

current_state.summary_text: Wanda serves as a guardian on Zou, defending the Mink Tribe after the end of the Wano campaign. Her whereabouts and role within the Guardians remain stable in the Mokomo forest.

npcs.name: Bariete
npcs.base_backstory: Bariete is a Mink who lives on Zou, atop the elephant Zunesha, where she serves as the bell-ringer of the Whale Tree. Her role is to watch over the entrance to the forest and sound the alarm in case of danger, a duty she fulfills with dedication. She was present during the Beasts Pirates' attack on Zou, when Jack devastated the tribe for days. After the Straw Hats and their allies departed, she remained on Zou carrying out her post.
npcs.current_goal: Watch over the Whale Tree and ring the bell at the slightest sign of a threat to Zou.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as the most vigilant guardian in Zou's history, keeping the Mink Tribe safe for her entire life.
npcs.mood: Alert and proud, carrying out her rounds with seriousness.
npcs.description: Bariete is a small-framed Mink with quick movements, short fur, and lively eyes that constantly sweep the horizon. She wears practical clothing suited for climbing and moving through the Whale Tree with ease. Although she is not a fighter, she conveys an alert and committed presence that inspires confidence in the inhabitants of Zou.

current_state.summary_text: Bariete remains at her post at the Whale Tree on Zou, fulfilling the role of Guardian of the Bell and keeping the Mink Tribe informed of any movement around the elephant Zunesha.

npcs.name: Carrot
npcs.base_backstory: Carrot was born on Zou and grew up as a warrior of the Mink Tribe, becoming part of Duke Inuarashi's personal guard. She accompanied the Straw Hat crew and the Wano allies across multiple battle fronts, including Whole Cake Island and the final war on Onigashima, where she activated her Sulong form to fight Big Mom's fleet. After the victory in Wano, she returned to Zou and was put forward as a candidate for the island's leadership, potentially taking on the role of guardian on behalf of the two dukes.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Zou and take on leadership responsibilities for the Mink Tribe.
npcs.long_term_dream: Defend her people and honor the legacy of the Mink warriors who shaped her.
npcs.mood: Determined and serene, carrying the weight of a new responsibility after years of battle.
npcs.description: Carrot is a young rabbit-like Mink with long white hair, large ears, and expressive eyes. Her agile body and sharp claws immediately mark her as a high-level fighter. She radiates a lively and infectious energy that can quickly turn into protective fury when those she cares about are threatened.

current_state.summary_text: Carrot remains on Zou, where she was put forward as a leadership figure for the Mink Tribe following the war on Onigashima. Her position among the island's guardians is consolidated, though the full weight of the role is still being assumed.

npcs.name: Pedro
npcs.base_backstory: Pedro was captain of the Nox Pirates, sailing the seas in search of the Red Poneglyph to help the Mink Tribe fulfill their ancestral oath to the Kozuki Clan. Captured by Big Mom, he lost years of his life as payment and returned to Zou carrying the weight of that sacrifice. He joined the group that infiltrated Whole Cake Island alongside the Straw Hat Pirates to recover Big Mom's Poneglyph. He held off Tamago and then sacrificed himself by detonating explosives against Perospero to ensure his allies' escape.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — died in battle.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see the dawn — the day when the Mink Tribe and their allies would achieve freedom and the world would be transformed by the new era.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
npcs.description: Pedro was an athletic-built jaguar Mink with spotted fur and eyes that conveyed absolute calm even in battle. He carried a long sword and moved with the fluid grace characteristic of Mink warriors, enhanced by Electro. His presence inspired quiet confidence — not through charisma, but through the certainty that he would not back down.

current_state.summary_text: Pedro died on Whole Cake Island by detonating explosives against Perospero, sacrificing himself to ensure the Straw Hat Pirates' escape. His legacy lives on among the Minks and the allies he protected.

npcs.name: Charlotte Katakuri
npcs.base_backstory: The second-oldest son of Charlotte Linlin, Katakuri grew up as the pillar of strength and discipline of the Charlotte Family. He rose to the rank of Sweet Commander with the highest bounty among his siblings, maintaining an undefeated record until his confrontation with Monkey D. Luffy on Whole Cake Island. After a prolonged battle in the Mirro-World, Katakuri was defeated by Luffy and, for the first time, fell on his back — an act he acknowledged as an honorable defeat. With Big Mom's death in Wano and the dissolution of the Emperor's original structure, the fate of the Charlotte Family and Katakuri himself remains open.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the cohesion and power of the Charlotte Family in the face of the void left by Big Mom's death.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be an unshakeable pillar that no enemy can ever bring down — to protect his own without failing.
npcs.mood: Restrained and calculating, bearing the weight of a power structure that has lost its center.
npcs.description: Katakuri is a colossal man of nearly five meters, with dense musculature and spiky gray hair. He wears a scarf that conceals his jagged mouth — a feature he guards ferociously. His presence is oppressive: still, economical in gesture, with eyes that seem to see a second ahead of everything around him.

current_state.summary_text: With Big Mom's death in Wano, Katakuri remains on Whole Cake Island as the most fearsome force of the Charlotte Family, whose future as a pirate organization is uncertain. His exact whereabouts and intentions post-Egghead are unknown to the outside world.

npcs.name: Zunesha
npcs.base_backstory: Zunesha committed a crime millennia ago — the exact nature of which remains unknown — and was cursed by Joy Boy to walk the seas forever, only able to act in combat when given a direct order. Since then, it has carried Zou on its back, serving as home to the Mink Tribe for generations. During Jack's invasion, Zunesha destroyed the Marine's fleet upon receiving an order from Momonosuke, the first person able to communicate with it in centuries. After the events of Wano, Zunesha witnessed the awakening of Momonosuke's Haki and announced the return of Joy Boy — and continues its eternal march across the seas of the New World.
npcs.current_goal: To fulfill its curse by walking the seas eternally, awaiting an order that justifies taking action.
npcs.long_term_dream: To receive forgiveness for the crime it committed and, at last, to rest.
npcs.mood: Serene and resigned, carrying a millennia-old sorrow without visible despair.
npcs.description: Zunesha is an elephant of impossible proportions — its entire body is an island, with forests, rivers, and cities living upon its back. Its legs move beneath the ocean's surface, invisible to those above. When it draws near, the first thing one notices is the dull sound of its footsteps, like distant thunder, before any shape becomes visible on the horizon. Its presence does not inspire ordinary fear: it inspires the sensation that time itself has weight.

current_state.summary_text: Zunesha roams the seas of the New World with Zou on its back, connected to Momonosuke through Observation Haki — the only one who can give it orders. Its curse continues; its pardon, uncertain.

npcs.name: Charlotte Daifuku
npcs.base_backstory: A son of Charlotte Linlin and one of the older children of the Charlotte Family, Daifuku was born as the twin of Charlotte Oven. He serves as Minister of Beans, governing Beans Island in the Whole Cake archipelago. During the Straw Hat Pirates' invasion of Whole Cake Island, he actively fought in defense of the territory and in pursuit of the fugitives, including Sanji and Germa 66. After Big Mom's defeat in Wano and her presumed death in the depths of the Onigashima volcano, the command structure of the Big Mom Pirates collapsed.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain Charlotte territorial control over Whole Cake Island in the face of the power vacuum left by Big Mom's fall.
