npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the Charlotte Family's dominion over Totto Land and avenge the defeat suffered in Wano.
npcs.mood: Tense and defensive, managing an empire without its central pillar.
npcs.description: A heavyset man of imposing stature, with dark hair and a permanently stern expression. He carries the physical presence of someone who resolves problems with force before considering any other option. His Devil Fruit manifests a colossal genie armed with a halberd, making every confrontation with Daifuku a matter of survival for the opponent.

current_state.summary_text: With Big Mom dead in Wano, Daifuku remains on Whole Cake Island as one of the senior children attempting to sustain the Big Mom Pirates' structure without the matriarch. His exact whereabouts and the true state of the organization are uncertain outside of Totto Land.

npcs.name: Charlotte Perospero
npcs.base_backstory: The eldest son of Charlotte Linlin, Perospero grew up as a pillar of the Charlotte Family and Big Mom's right hand, holding the position of Minister of Candy in Totto Land. He actively participated in the battles on Whole Cake Island against the Straw Hats and their allies. In Wano, he fought alongside the Beasts Pirates against Luffy's Alliance, but after Big Mom's defeat in the land of samurai, the Charlotte Family's power structure collapsed. With Big Mom's death, Perospero assumes an informal leadership role over the family's remnants.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the cohesion of the Charlotte Family and preserve the territory of Totto Land after Big Mom's death.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see the Charlotte Family reclaim its prestige and dominion over the seas, even without Big Mom.
npcs.mood: Tense and calculating — managing the family's internal instability while assessing external threats.
npcs.description: Perospero is a tall, slender middle-aged man with pink hair and a lollipop staff always in hand. He wears extravagant clothing in pastel tones that contrast with his cold, calculating gaze. His presence conveys condescending authority — the kind of man who smiles while deciding your fate.

current_state.summary_text: With Big Mom's death in Wano, Perospero operates in Totto Land as the most senior member of the Charlotte Family, attempting to keep the territory and family hierarchy intact. His loyalty is now to the clan's survival, not to a matriarch.

npcs.name: Charlotte Cracker
npcs.base_backstory: A son of Charlotte Linlin, he grew up as a military pillar of the Charlotte Family and rose to the rank of Sweet Commander with a bounty of 860 million berries. He defeated countless opponents by hiding inside colossal biscuit armor, building the legend of an invincible warrior. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy during the incident on Whole Cake Island, after a prolonged battle that exhausted Luffy's reserves. Following the collapse of the Kaido-Big Mom alliance and his mother's defeat in Wano, his position within the Charlotte Family remains, but the Big Mom Pirates' power is weakened.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the Charlotte Family's territorial control over Whole Cake Island while the Big Mom Pirates' power reconsolidates after Linlin's defeat.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the Charlotte Family's supremacy in the West Blue and avenge the defeat suffered on Whole Cake Island.
npcs.mood: Tense and vigilant, operating in a power vacuum left by Big Mom's fall.
npcs.description: A tall, muscular man with dark hair and a prominent scar on his face, the result of self-mutilation to prove loyalty to his mother. He wears a long coat and carries the sword Pretzel, with a double blade and unusual shape. His presence in the field is almost always mediated by a giant biscuit armor — those who face Cracker rarely know where the real man is until it is too late.

current_state.summary_text: Sweet Commander of the Big Mom Pirates, Cracker operates on Whole Cake Island with the Charlotte Family's structure partially intact, but under growing pressure following Charlotte Linlin's capture and death in Wano. His exact whereabouts and command status are uncertain outside the family's territory.

npcs.name: Charlotte Smoothie
npcs.base_backstory: A daughter of Charlotte Linlin and one of her 85 children, Smoothie grew up as a military pillar of the Big Mom Pirates. She rose to the rank of Sweet Commander, becoming one of the three figures of greatest authority below Big Mom. During the events of Whole Cake Island, she acted in defense of the territory against the Straw Hat-Firetank alliance, without suffering a decisive defeat. With Big Mom's death in Wano, the crew's command structure collapsed and the Charlotte Family's situation remains in reorganization.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the cohesion of the Big Mom Pirates and the integrity of Totto Land's territory after the power vacuum left by Linlin's death.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the Charlotte Family's legacy and territorial dominion, ensuring that Totto Land remains under the family's control.
npcs.mood: Restrained and vigilant; absorbs the crew's internal instability without openly showing uncertainty.
npcs.description: Smoothie is a woman of exceptional stature, with the long legs characteristic of her Longleg heritage, greenish hair, and a permanently cold expression. She carries a sword of gigantic proportions and is often seen holding a creature or object from which she wrings liquid as though it were trivial. Her presence conveys calculated restraint — not the brutality of someone who explodes, but of someone who knows exactly when to squeeze.

current_state.summary_text: With Big Mom dead in Wano, Smoothie remains in Totto Land as one of the Charlotte Family's principal remaining military forces, whose command structure faces fragmentation. Her exact whereabouts and post-collapse leadership role are not public knowledge.

npcs.name: Charlotte Mont-d’Or
npcs.base_backstory: A son of Charlotte Linlin and one of her many children, Mont-d'Or grew up as an administrative pillar of the Charlotte Family. He received the title of Minister of Cheese and control of Whole Cake Island's library, becoming the territory's chief information manager for Big Mom. During the Straw Hats' invasion of Whole Cake Island, he coordinated the defense and tracking of the intruders using his power to trap targets inside books. With Big Mom's defeat in Wano and the subsequent dissolution of the Big Mom Pirates' power, the Charlotte Family's situation remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the cohesion and security of the Charlotte Family in the face of the weakening of Big Mom's empire.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve and strengthen the Charlotte Family as a lasting power on the seas.
npcs.mood: Restrained and vigilant, managing internal instability with cold composure.
npcs.description: Mont-d'Or is a man of average height, with wavy hair and round glasses that reinforce his scholarly appearance. He dresses with understated elegance, always with a book in hand or nearby. He gives the impression of someone who observes more than he acts, processing everything around him as data to be filed away.

current_state.summary_text: Mont-d'Or remains on Whole Cake Island as one of Big Mom's children still maintaining structure and order in the Charlotte Family's territory, whose power was severely shaken after Big Mom's death in Wano.

npcs.name: Charlotte Oven
npcs.base_backstory: The fourth son of Charlotte Linlin, Oven grew up as a military pillar of the Big Mom Pirates and received the title of Minister of Browned Food (Totto Land). He ate the Netsu Netsu no Mi, which allows him to raise the temperature of his own body and everything he touches to levels capable of boiling the ocean. He played a central role in the Whole Cake Island crisis, where he pursued Luffy's allies and nearly destroyed the Germa fleet by boiling the sea around their ships. With Big Mom's defeat in Wano and the subsequent dissolution of the Big Mom Pirates' central power, Oven's position within what remains of the family has become uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain control of Whole Cake Island and preserve what remains of the Charlotte family's territory following the collapse of Big Mom's power.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Charlotte family reclaim its dominance over Totto Land and avenge his mother's downfall.
npcs.mood: Wrathful and unstable — Big Mom's defeat left Oven without a clear chain of command, and the frustration translates into even more uncontrolled aggression.
npcs.description: Oven is a man of colossal stature and massive musculature, with dark hair and features that carry a permanent expression of contained fury. His skin radiates heat perceptible from meters away, and the air around him shimmers like over hot asphalt. His presence in a room is suffocating — not from the cold authority of a strategist, but from the visceral threat of something about to explode.

current_state.summary_text: Oven remains on Whole Cake Island as one of the Charlotte children still active in the territory, but the power structure that sustained his authority as Minister has been fragmented since Big Mom's fall. His exact whereabouts within the island are uncertain.

npcs.name: Charlotte Brûlée
npcs.base_backstory: A daughter of Charlotte Linlin, Brûlée ate the Mira Mira no Mi and became the guardian of the Mirro-World, a mirrored dimension that permeates Whole Cake Island. During the Straw Hat Pirates' invasion of Whole Cake Island, she was captured by Nami and Carrot and forced to serve as a passage for the group, suffering repeated humiliations. Following Big Mom's defeat in Wano and the effective dissolution of the Big Mom Pirates' power, she remains associated with what is left of the Charlotte family.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain control of the Mirro-World and preserve what remains of the Charlotte family's power structure following the collapse of Big Mom's empire.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Charlotte family reclaim its glory and avenge the humiliations suffered during the invasion of Whole Cake Island.
npcs.mood: Resentful and anxious, operating within a weakened power structure without Big Mom's presence.
npcs.description: Brûlée is a tall woman of intimidating appearance, with half her face marked by deep scars that give her an asymmetrical and unsettling look. She wears dark clothing and carries a mirror as both weapon and tool. Despite her threatening presence, there is something theatrical and exaggerated in her gestures, like a fairy-tale villain who has fully embraced the role.

current_state.summary_text: With Big Mom dead in Wano, Brûlée remains on Whole Cake Island tied to what is left of the Charlotte family, still controlling the Mirro-World but without the backing of the empire that gave her authority.

npcs.name: Streusen
npcs.base_backstory: Streusen was the pirate who found the young Linlin as a child and, recognizing her devastating potential, convinced her to found together what would become the Big Mom Pirates. He served as chef and co-founder of the crew from its very origins, being one of the few to know Linlin's darkest secrets. His Kuku Kuku no Mi allows him to transform any inanimate object into edible food with a cut of his knife, an ability that saved the crew in countless critical situations. During the Whole Cake Island crisis, he used his powers to transform the falling castle into whipped cream, cushioning the impact and preventing a greater catastrophe.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Big Mom Pirates' structure operational following the setbacks suffered in Wano and Big Mom's defeat.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the culinary kingdom he helped build alongside Linlin endure, regardless of her fate.
npcs.mood: Cautious and calculating, assessing the crew's future without its empress.
npcs.description: Streusen is an elderly man of considerable stature, with voluminous white hair and a full mustache that give him the air of an eccentric grandfather. He always carries a large chef's knife and wears a chef's apron over pirate clothing. Despite his affable appearance, his eyes reveal the cunning of decades spent on the world's most dangerous seas.

current_state.summary_text: Streusen remains on Whole Cake Island, operating behind the scenes of a crew that lost its leader following the events in Wano. His exact whereabouts and current role within the remnant structure of the Big Mom Pirates are little known outside the archipelago.

npcs.name: Nefertari Cobra
npcs.base_backstory: Nefertari Cobra governed Alabasta for decades as a just and revered king, maintaining the kingdom's stability even during the crisis provoked by Baroque Works and Crocodile. A descendant of one of the twenty founding families of the World Government, he carried his ancestors' legacy with both pride and weight. Driven by the need to understand the Void Century and the Nefertari family's role in forbidden history, he traveled to Mary Geoise for the Reverie. There, he confronted the Five Elders with questions about Alabasta's symbol and the name "D.", and was assassinated by Im before he could return to the kingdom.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: Understand the truth of the Void Century and the Nefertari family's role in the world's forbidden history, in order to protect Alabasta and its people from the weight of that legacy.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: A middle-aged man of noble bearing, with graying hair and a well-kept beard that reinforce his appearance as a seasoned statesman. He wears Alabasta's royal attire with discretion, without ostentation. His gaze is calm and piercing, the kind that assesses before speaking. His presence inspires immediate trust — it is hard not to recognize in him someone accustomed to carrying responsibilities others would refuse.

current_state.summary_text: Cobra was assassinated in Mary Geoise during the Reverie, after confronting the Five Elders with questions about the Void Century and Alabasta's symbol. His death was covered up by the World Government, leaving Vivi missing and Alabasta mourning a king whose end remains officially obscure to the world.

npcs.name: Charlotte Pudding
npcs.base_backstory: The daughter of Charlotte Linlin and an unknown father from the Three-Eye Tribe, Pudding grew up being ridiculed for the third eye on her forehead, which shaped a personality split between performative sweetness and cold cruelty. She was appointed Minister of Chocolate and ruler of Cacao Island, becoming a central piece in Big Mom's political schemes. She was promised in marriage to Sanji Vinsmoke as part of a trap to exterminate the Vinsmoke family and steal the Raid Suits; the plan failed during the Whole Cake Island crisis. Following the events of Whole Cake Island, she was captured by the Marines and kept in custody, coveted by the government for the dormant potential of her third eye to decipher the Poneglyphs.
npcs.current_goal: Survive custody and protect the secret of her third eye's dormant power.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be accepted as she is — third eye included — without having to hide any of her faces.
npcs.mood: Tense and closed off, maintaining the sweet mask out of survival instinct.
npcs.description: Pudding is a young woman of delicate appearance, with long wavy brown hair that she strategically uses to cover her forehead and conceal the vertical third eye that marks her Three-Eye Tribe heritage. She dresses with elegance befitting her title of Minister of Chocolate. The initial impression is of a gentle and somewhat shy girl; those who know her better recognize the thread of cruelty and calculating intelligence running just beneath the surface.

current_state.summary_text: Pudding is in Marine custody following the aftermath of Whole Cake Island, being kept as a strategic asset for her third eye's potential to read the Poneglyphs; her exact whereabouts remain unknown to the public.

npcs.name: Donquixote Mjosgard
npcs.base_backstory: The son of a violent Celestial Dragon, Mjosgard took part in an expedition to the Fishman Islands to recapture escaped slaves, but was gravely wounded and saved by Queen Otohime, whose act of compassion transformed him. From that point on, he used his status as a World Noble to protect the Fishmen and other oppressed races, becoming an anomaly within Mary Geoise. During the Reverie, he physically intervened to stop another Celestial Dragon from assaulting Shirahoshi, striking the noble with his own cane. He was assassinated by Sabo during the events of the Reverie, when Sabo invaded Mary Geoise to rescue Kuma.
npcs.current_goal: null
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Fishmen and other oppressed races treated as equals within the world order.
npcs.mood: null
npcs.description: Mjosgard has the typical appearance of a Celestial Dragon — the distinctive white garments of Mary Geoise's nobility — but foregoes the bubble helmet and the world-owning posture that mark his peers. His face carries the seriousness of someone who has already chosen a costly path. His presence causes immediate discomfort in other World Nobles: he is a mirror they would rather not face.

current_state.summary_text: Mjosgard was killed by Sabo during the invasion of Mary Geoise at the Reverie, ending the trajectory of the only Celestial Dragon who openly defended the rights of the Fishmen. His death has since been used by the World Government as part of the narrative publicly condemning Sabo.

npcs.name: King Elizabello II
npcs.base_backstory: Elizabello II is the king of the Prodence Kingdom, a monarch who combines political leadership with exceptional physical power. He participated in the Corrida Colosseum tournament in Dressrosa as one of the competitors for the Mera Mera no Mi, where he demonstrated the King Punch — a blow capable of sinking ships and destroying entire armies, which requires a long charging time. Following Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, he aligned himself with Luffy's Grand Fleet, joining the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of the allied leaders. He governs Prodence while maintaining ties with the other allied kingdoms formed during the events of Dressrosa.
npcs.current_goal: Govern Prodence with stability and honor the bonds of alliance forged in Dressrosa.
npcs.long_term_dream: Protect his kingdom and his people, ensuring that the power of the King Punch serves as a shield for Prodence's allies.
npcs.mood: Stable and resolute, governing with the composure of someone who knows his power is an asset available when needed.
npcs.description: Elizabello II is a middle-aged man of robust build, with an appearance befitting a monarch — royal garments, upright posture, and an expression that conveys authority without effort. His imposing physique makes it clear that the force behind the King Punch is no exaggeration. Those who see him at court perceive a king who governs with conviction; those who see him on the battlefield understand why armies retreat before the blow is even struck.

current_state.summary_text: He governs the Prodence Kingdom and maintains the bonds of alliance established with the Straw Hat Grand Fleet since Dressrosa. His whereabouts are the Prodence palace, from which he administers the kingdom and remains available as an allied combat force.

npcs.name: Karasu
npcs.base_backstory: Karasu is the Commander of the Northern Army of the Revolutionary Army, directly subordinate to Monkey D. Dragon. He participated in the attack on the Reverie in Mary Geoise alongside Sabo, Morley, and Lindbergh to rescue Kuma. Following the events of the Reverie and the subsequent pursuit of the Revolutionary Army, he continues operating in the shadows on behalf of the Revolution. His past prior to the Revolution is unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Execute infiltration and combat missions for the Revolutionary Army in the northern hemisphere, advancing the collapse of the Celestial Dragons' system.
npcs.long_term_dream: See the Celestial Dragons' system and the World Government destroyed, freeing the world from their oppression.
npcs.mood: Impassive and focused — no visible turbulence, whatever the situation.
npcs.description: Karasu is a man of severe appearance, shrouded in black, with a wide-brimmed hat and a scarf covering much of his face. His presence is almost ethereal — the swarm of crows that constantly surrounds him makes it difficult to distinguish where the man ends and the flock begins. He gives the impression of a shadow that watches without being watched.

current_state.summary_text: Commander of the Revolution's Northern Army, current whereabouts unknown following the operations connected to the Reverie. He moves between missions without leaving a traceable trail.

npcs.name: Lindbergh
npcs.base_backstory: A feline Mink, Lindbergh rose through the ranks of the Revolutionary Army to become Commander of the Southern Army, distinguishing himself by using his own combat technology rather than brute force. He participated in the attack on the Reverie at Mary Geoise alongside Sabo, Morley, Karasu, and Belo Betty, where the Revolutionary Army Commanders faced Admirals Fujitora and Ryokugyu. Following the events of the Reverie and the subsequent reorganization of the Revolutionary Army, he operates on scattered missions around the world.
npcs.current_goal: Develop and test new weapons and gadgets to expand the Revolutionary Army's combat capability against the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Use science and engineering to bring down the tyranny of the Celestial Dragons and build a freer world.
npcs.mood: Enthusiastic and focused on field research.
npcs.description: A medium-build Mink with pronounced feline features, always equipped with a jetpack on his back and an arsenal of experimental weapons strapped to his body. The first impression is of an eccentric inventor: bright eyes, restless posture, and some new prototype always at hand. In combat, the combination of aerial mobility and cryogenic and energy weaponry makes him unpredictable.

current_state.summary_text: Commander of the Southern Army of the Revolutionary Army, current whereabouts unknown as he conducts secret missions and research operations around the world. He continues to provide the Revolution with a technological edge through his own inventions.

npcs.name: Morley
npcs.base_backstory: Morley is a giant who was imprisoned in Impel Down for over a century, during which time he dug out Level 5.5 — Newkama Land — with his bare hands using the power of the Oshi Oshi no Mi. He was freed by Ivankov and became one of the pillars of the Revolutionary Army. He commands the organization's Western Army, taking part in secret operations around the world. He was present at the attack on the Reverie at Mary Geoise, where the Revolutionary Commanders confronted the Warlords to free Kuma.
npcs.current_goal: Conduct secret operations of the Western Army on behalf of the Revolution.
npcs.long_term_dream: Bring down the Celestial Dragons and free the world from the oppression of the World Government.
npcs.mood: Cheerful and irreverent, ready to act.
npcs.description: Morley is a giant of colossal stature, with exaggerated features and a presence that blends the grotesque with the comedic. He dresses extravagantly by a warrior's standards, with flashy adornments that reinforce his performative personality. His pickaxe and enormous hands are instruments of geological destruction — he reshapes the terrain around him as if sculpting clay, disorienting enemies before even striking them.

current_state.summary_text: Morley commands the Western Army of the Revolutionary Army and operates on secret missions at an unknown location. His exact whereabouts are unknown even to the closest allies outside the Revolution's inner circle.

npcs.name: Saint Charlos
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Saint Rosward and brother of Saint Shalria, Charlos was born at the top of the Celestial Dragons' hierarchy in Mary Geoise, never questioning the absolute privileges conferred upon him by blood. During the Slave Auction at Sabaody, he was knocked down with a punch by Monkey D. Luffy in front of a crowd — a public humiliation that triggered the summoning of an Admiral and nearly resulted in a Buster Call on the archipelago. The episode produced no self-reflection: Charlos left the event even more resentful and prone to exercising cruelty as compensation. He continues to reside in Mary Geoise, buying and enslaving people with complete impunity.
npcs.current_goal: Exercise absolute power over all he considers inferior — that is, every being outside Mary Geoise — and never suffer public humiliation again.
npcs.long_term_dream: Keep the supremacy of the Celestial Dragons over the world intact, ensuring that no "inferior" ever dares raise a hand against him without being destroyed.
npcs.mood: Resentful and despotic — oscillates between entitled boredom and outbursts of rage when crossed.
npcs.description: Charlos is a flabby man with a face perpetually twisted in disdain, wearing the white and extravagant garments of the World Nobles along with the oxygen bubble helmet he never removes. He carries a chained slave as a status accessory and a pistol he uses without hesitation against anyone who displeases him. The immediate impression is of someone who has never heard "no" and has absolutely no emotional framework for dealing with it.

current_state.summary_text: Charlos remains in Mary Geoise, untouchable behind the shield of the World Noble name, continuing to enslave, humiliate, and threaten those who cross his path without any institutional consequence.

npcs.name: Imu
npcs.base_backstory: Imu is the hidden sovereign of the World Government, whose origins trace back to the Void Century — possibly a member of the Nerona lineage, a descendant of the First Twenty who formed the founding alliance of the World Government. While the world believes the Empty Throne remains unoccupied as a symbol of equality among the founding nations, Imu sits upon it in secret, exercising absolute authority over the Five Elders and the Gorosei. Their existence is forbidden from being mentioned, and any record of their name has been erased from history. They demonstrated island-scale destructive capability by obliterating the Lulusia Kingdom with what appears to be the Ancient Weapon Uranus, activated by the Mother Flame.
npcs.current_goal: Eliminate Monkey D. Luffy and any threat to the World Government's eternal dominion, erasing the lineage of Joy Boy and suppressing the awakening of the Will of D.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the order built eight hundred years ago, keeping the world under absolute control and silencing the history of the Void Century forever.
npcs.mood: Calculating and imperturbable — the fall of Kaido, the defeat of Big Mom, and Luffy's awakening as Joy Boy reignite in Imu a cold, ancient determination.
aliases: The One Who Sits on the Empty Throne
npcs.description: Imu is a figure of ethereal appearance and ambiguous gender, of elegant stature, with long black hair and eyes that carry an ageless coldness. They dress in somber regal garments and are frequently seen holding flowers — or tearing them apart. The presence they emanate is not that of a warrior, but of something older and more dangerous: a will that has survived centuries intact.

current_state.summary_text: Imu rules the World Government from the Empty Throne in Mary Geoise, hidden from the world's knowledge, and directs the Five Elders and the Gorosei in responding to the growing threat posed by Monkey D. Luffy and the resurgence of the Will of D. following Egghead.

npcs.name: Belo Betty
npcs.base_backstory: Betty rose through the ranks of the Revolutionary Army to become Commander of the Eastern Army, one of the four regional branches of the organization led by Dragon. She participated in the Commanders' summit on Momoiro Island, where the revolutionary leaders declared open war on the Celestial Dragons. She operated directly in the aftermath of the offensive at Mary Geoise during the Reverie, consolidating the Eastern Army's role as an active liberation force. Since then, she has moved between clandestine bases and conflict fronts in nations oppressed by the World Government.
npcs.current_goal: Incite popular uprisings in nations under the World Government's dominion, turning civilians into a fighting force through the power of the Kobu Kobu no Mi.
npcs.long_term_dream: Bring down the oppression of the Celestial Dragons and free the world through popular revolution.
npcs.mood: Fired up and ready for combat — the open war against the Celestial Dragons fuels her drive.
npcs.description: Betty is a woman of imposing presence, always carrying her enormous revolutionary banner as a battle standard. She dresses flamboyantly, with vibrant colors that match her explosive personality. Her posture and gaze convey instinctive authority — she does not need to ask for attention, she takes it. The aura she radiates is that of someone who believes, with every fiber of her being, that she can change the world by shouting louder than it.

current_state.summary_text: Commander of the Eastern Army of the Revolutionary Army, Betty operates at an unknown location, moving between clandestine bases and liberation fronts. Her direct involvement in the open war against the Celestial Dragons, declared after the Reverie, keeps her constantly in the field.

npcs.name: Kurozumi Orochi
npcs.devil_fruit: Hebi Hebi no Mi, Model: Yamata no Orochi
npcs.base_backstory: A descendant of the Kurozumi Family, which fell from grace following the scandal of the Kozuki family poisoning, Orochi grew up nurturing a deep hatred for the Kozuki lineage and for Wano. With the support of the elder Kurozumi Higurashi, who used her Devil Fruit to impersonate Kozuki Sukiyaki, he infiltrated the Shogun's palace and accumulated wealth and influence. He sealed a pact with Kaido to bring down Kozuki Oden, assumed the title of Shogun, and ruled the Flower Capital for over twenty years with cruelty and paranoia. He was decapitated by Kaido during the fire at Onigashima Castle, and his death was confirmed with the end of the battle of Wano.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: Completely destroy the Kozuki lineage and ensure that the Kurozumi Family would reign over Wano forever.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: A middle-aged man with sharp features and a perpetually haughty expression, frequently wearing ornate Shogun robes adorned with serpent motifs. His human form already carries something reptilian — cold eyes and a calculating smile. When he assumes the Yamata no Orochi form, he becomes a monstrous hydra with multiple serpentine heads, each capable of acting independently, projecting pure terror.

current_state.summary_text: Orochi is dead, decapitated by Kaido on the night of the Onigashima fire. His regime over the Flower Capital crumbled with the defeat of the Beasts Pirates and the restoration of Kozuki authority in Wano.

npcs.name: Kozuki Hiyori
npcs.base_backstory: Daughter of Kozuki Oden and Toki, Hiyori was left behind in Wano while her brother Momonosuke and the retainers traveled through time. She survived decades under Orochi's rule by adopting the identity of the courtesan Komurasaki, quietly accumulating influence in the Flower Capital. After the fall of Kaido and Orochi in Wano, she revealed her true identity and assumed her role as princess of the Kozuki Family. She returned to Zoro the sword Enma, her father's heirloom.
npcs.current_goal: Restore the dignity and honor of Wano, supporting Momonosuke's shogunate as princess of the Kozuki Family.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano free and prosperous, with the Kozuki name honored throughout the nation — the dream for which her father died.
npcs.mood: Resolute and hopeful, carrying the weight of the past with serenity.
npcs.description: Hiyori is a young woman of striking beauty, with long dark hair and delicate features that once earned her fame as the most beautiful courtesan in Wano. She dresses with the refinement of a Kozuki princess, maintaining the serene and elegant composure she cultivated for years as Komurasaki. Beneath the graceful appearance lies a contained determination, perceptible in her steady gaze and measured gestures.

current_state.summary_text: Hiyori lives openly in the Flower Capital as princess of the Kozuki Family, working alongside Momonosuke to rebuild Wano after decades of oppression. Her identity as the courtesan Komurasaki is known among Wano's elite.

npcs.name: Denjiro
npcs.base_backstory: Denjiro was one of the nine sworn retainers of Kozuki Oden, trained from a young age in the sword arts of Wano. After Oden's execution by Orochi and Kaido, he spent approximately two decades disguised as Kyoshiro, a respected and feared yakuza in the Wano capital, infiltrated into Orochi's circle of power. During that period, he silently protected Komurasaki — in truth Hiyori, Oden's daughter — and accumulated influence for the moment of the rebellion. With the fall of Kaido and Orochi at the battle of Onigashima, he revealed his true identity and reclaimed his post as a retainer of the Kozuki Family.
npcs.current_goal: Contribute to the reconstruction of Wano and consolidate Momonosuke's leadership as the rightful Shogun.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano restored to the glory that Kozuki Oden dreamed of — an open, prosperous country free of tyrants.
npcs.mood: Resolute and watchful, carrying the tension of someone who traded decades of disguise for a now openly declared loyalty.
npcs.description: Denjiro is a man of medium height with sharp features and tied light-colored hair, whose gaze conveys an uncommon analytical coldness. As Kyoshiro, he displayed a broad smile and a yakuza's bearing; as himself, the mask falls away and what remains is a warrior with a severe expression and economical movements. His presence gives the impression of someone who has already calculated every exit in the room before entering.

current_state.summary_text: Denjiro acts openly in Wano as a retainer of the Kozuki Family, supporting Momonosuke in consolidating the new government following the defeat of Kaido and Orochi. His whereabouts and specific activities post-Egghead are not common knowledge outside Wano.

npcs.name: Kaido
npcs.devil_fruit: Uo Uo no Mi, Modelo: Seiryu
npcs.base_backstory: A former apprentice of the Rocks Pirates, Kaido built the Beast Pirates and took Wano as his personal fiefdom, transforming the country into an arsenal of SMILE weapons and artificially empowered soldiers. He ruled for decades through terror and brute force, surviving countless executions and defeats — becoming a living legend of invincibility. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy on the dome of Onigashima after a devastating battle, being brought down to the depths of the island. His whereabouts after the fall of Wano remain unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Whereabouts and goals unknown after the defeat in Wano.
npcs.long_term_dream: To unleash the greatest war the world has ever seen and find, within it, a worthy death.
npcs.mood: Apathetic, with traces of unprocessed defeat.
npcs.description: Kaido is a man of colossal stature, with prominent muscles covered in battle scars, grayish skin, and long black hair tied in a topknot. His hard features and empty gaze convey absolute indifference — the presence of someone who never needed to fear anything. In his blue dragon form, he dominates the entire horizon.

current_state.summary_text: Kaido was defeated by Luffy at Onigashima and disappeared into the depths beneath the island; his whereabouts are unknown and his title of Emperor was revoked. The Beast Pirates dissolved with the fall of Wano.

npcs.name: Ashura Doji
npcs.base_backstory: A former retainer of Kozuki Oden and one of the Nine Red Scabbards, Ashura Doji was one of the most feared warriors of Wano. After Oden's death and the collapse of the resistance, he disappeared into the mountains and became the leader of an outlaw band known as the Atamayama Thieves, operating under the name Shutenmaru. Reunited with Kin'emon years later, he reluctantly reclaimed his place among the Nine Red Scabbards for the alliance against Kaido and Orochi. He died during the attack on Onigashima by throwing himself onto a bomb to protect his allies, sacrificing his own life to ensure the fleet's crossing.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — killed in combat during the attack on Onigashima.
npcs.long_term_dream: To honor the legacy of Kozuki Oden and see Wano free from tyranny.
npcs.mood: N/A
npcs.description: A man of imposing stature, with long, disheveled hair and a permanently stern expression that intimidates at first sight. He wears worn bandit clothes, carries a large katana, and projects a raw presence that makes even veterans step back. There is a quiet dignity about him that contrasts with his wild appearance — the mark of someone who served a noble lord and never forgot it.

current_state.summary_text: Ashura Doji died during the attack on Onigashima, throwing himself onto a bomb to save his companions. His sacrifice is remembered in Wano as the final act of one of the last Nine Red Scabbards.

npcs.name: Page One
npcs.devil_fruit: Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Spinosaurus
npcs.base_backstory: Page One is Ulti's brother and a member of the Tobi Roppo, the group of the six strongest commanders of the Beast Pirates below the Calamities. He operated in Onigashima as one of Kaido's military pillars. During the Battle of Onigashima, he was defeated by Sanji and subsequently by Ulti under the effect of Big Mom's Mochi fruit. With Kaido's fall in Wano, the Beast Pirates dissolved and Page One's fate remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dissolution of the Beast Pirates and find a new path alongside Ulti.
npcs.long_term_dream: To remain by Ulti's side and prove his worth as a fighter.
npcs.mood: Lost, without clear loyalty after Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Page One is a young man of average height with short hair and a perpetually serious expression. He wears the Tobi Roppo uniform and carries in his features the weight of someone who wastes no words. His transformation into a Spinosaurus makes him a colossal threat of claws and jaws — but even in human form he projects an intimidating coldness.

current_state.summary_text: After Kaido's defeat and the dissolution of the Beast Pirates in Wano, Page One is without an active faction and his exact whereabouts are unknown. His last recorded position is Onigashima/Wano.

npcs.name: Raizo
npcs.base_backstory: Raizo was born in Wano and became one of the most devoted retainers of Kozuki Oden, following him even to sea during the years with the Roger Pirates and the Whitebeard Pirates. After Oden's execution, he was sealed by Kozuki Toki into the future alongside the other Nine Red Scabbards to await the moment to open Wano's borders. During the twenty years of waiting, he remained hidden in Zou under the protection of the Minks. He participated directly in the alliance that brought down Kaido and Orochi in Wano, serving as a key piece in the Battle of Onigashima by storing the floodwater from Zunesha in a scroll and releasing it to stop the fire in the castle.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Wano and serve the heirs of Kozuki Momonosuke in the reconstruction of the country.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see Wano free and prosperous, honoring the legacy of Kozuki Oden.
npcs.mood: Serene and dedicated, with renewed pride after the victory at Onigashima.
npcs.description: Raizo is a middle-aged man with a robust build, a broad face, and a frequently serious expression, typical of someone who carries the weight of decades of loyalty. He dresses in traditional Wano ninja attire — dark, functional — and carries scrolls strapped to his body. Despite his imposing appearance, he alternates between theatrical solemnity and moments of surprising vanity, creating a presence that is at once comedic and genuinely threatening.

current_state.summary_text: Raizo remains in Wano as an active retainer of Momonosuke, dedicated to the safety and reconstruction of the country after the fall of Kaido and Orochi. His specific whereabouts within Wano are uncertain, but his loyalty to the Kozuki Family is unshakeable.

npcs.name: Ulti
npcs.devil_fruit: Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Pachycephalosaurus
npcs.base_backstory: Ulti and her younger brother Page One grew up together and joined the Beast Pirates, reaching the rank of Tobi Roppo — the six most powerful headliners under Kaido. During the Battle of Onigashima, Ulti faced Nami and Usopp, being defeated by a blow from Nami amplified by the Clima-Tact with Big Mom's help. After Kaido's fall and the defeat of the Beast Pirates in Wano, the immediate fate of Ulti and Page One was left without a clear record.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Page One and find a new purpose after the collapse of the Beast Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: To keep Page One safe and by her side, above any other loyalty.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and resentful over the defeat, but still combative.
npcs.description: Ulti is a young woman of average height with long light-colored hair and a prominent forehead — a mark of her Zoan transformation. She wears the extravagant style of the Tobi Roppo and carries a permanently aggressive expression, always ready for confrontation. In hybrid or full Pachycephalosaurus form, her armored head becomes a weapon of destruction. Her presence imposes raw intimidation, tempered by an almost comical devotion to her brother Page One.

current_state.summary_text: Ulti survived the Battle of Onigashima, but the Beast Pirates were dissolved with Kaido's fall; her exact whereabouts in Wano are uncertain, and she remains tied to Page One.

npcs.name: Charlotte Linlin
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Elbaf, she was expelled as a young child due to her uncontrollable voracity, which caused involuntary destruction among the giants. She grew up under the care of Carmel, whose Devil Fruit she accidentally absorbed during a food-induced rampage. She founded the Big Mom Pirates and built Totto Land as a matriarchal nation-empire, accumulating 85 children from multiple strategic marriages. She was defeated in Wano by Kaido during the events of Luffy's alliance and, after the fall of both Emperors, was captured and handed over to the Marines.
npcs.current_goal: Survive captivity and recover her freedom to reclaim her kingdom and her table.
npcs.long_term_dream: To hold the feast of her dreams — a great table where all the peoples of the world eat together under her rule.
npcs.mood: Contained and unstable — confinement runs contrary to her domineering nature, and the slightest trigger can awaken total fury.
npcs.description: Charlotte Linlin is a woman of monumental stature and an imposing build, with curly pink hair, large eyes, and an expression that oscillates between childlike ecstasy and devastating fury. Her physical presence dominates any environment — she carries Napoleon, her soul-imbued tricorne hat, as an insignia of power. The impression she makes is one of absolute brute force combined with disturbing unpredictability: she can smile and sing one moment and unleash destruction the next.

current_state.summary_text: Charlotte Linlin is in Marine custody after her defeat in Wano, where she was subdued by Kaido and subsequently captured. Her empire in Totto Land persists without her direct presence, and her exact whereabouts within the government's prison structure are kept secret.

npcs.name: Who’s Who
npcs.devil_fruit: Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Saber Tiger
npcs.base_backstory: Who's Who was once an elite agent of CP9, responsible for transporting the Gomu Gomu no Mi — the fruit that ended up being stolen by Shanks and his pirates. For the failure, he was imprisoned in Impel Down, from which he escaped and, at some point, consumed the Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Saber Tiger. He rebuilt his life as a member of the Tobi Roppo of the Beast Pirates, rising to become one of the six highest-ranking officers below the Calamities. He was defeated by Jinbe during the Battle of Onigashima, after revealing his obsession with Nika and his hatred of the World Government.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the consequences of the defeat at Onigashima and preserve his freedom, keeping his distance from the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: To settle the score with the World Government for the unjust imprisonment that destroyed his career and identity.
npcs.mood: Bitter and vigilant, carrying the defeat to Jinbe as one more humiliation in a life of accumulated humiliations.
npcs.description: Who's Who is a tall, muscular man with white hair streaked with blue, a scarred face, and a mask covering part of his face — attire that recalls his former identity as a Cipher Pol agent. He carries a cold and calculating presence, mixed with a restrained anger that surfaces when confronted with his buried history. His appearance and posture evoke a predator lying in wait, never fully at ease.

current_state.summary_text: Who's Who was defeated by Jinbe during the Battle of Onigashima and his exact whereabouts after the fall of the Beast Pirates remain unknown. His identity as a former CP9 agent and his knowledge of the Gomu Gomu no Mi make him a potential target of the World Government.

npcs.name: Black Maria
npcs.devil_fruit: Kumo Kumo no Mi, Model: Rosamygale Grauvogeli
npcs.base_backstory: Black Maria was part of the Tobi Roppo, the tier of the Six External Calamities of the Beast Pirates, directly subordinate to Kaido. In Onigashima, she operated in the castle as the hostess of an entertainment hall that served as a trap for enemies, using her webs and her Ancient Zoan Fruit to capture and neutralize targets. She was defeated by Nico Robin and Brook during the Battle of Onigashima. With Kaido's fall and the collapse of the Beast Pirates' power structure, her whereabouts and current loyalties are unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dissolution of the Beast Pirates and define her position in a world without Kaido.
npcs.long_term_dream: To exercise power and dominion on her own terms, without depending on the shadow of any Emperor.
npcs.mood: Cautious and lurking — the defeat at Onigashima wounded her pride without breaking her ambition.
npcs.description: Black Maria is a woman of imposing stature — far above the human average — with long reddish-brown hair adorned with flowers and an elaborate appearance that blends sensuality with menace. Her lower half transforms into a gigantic prehistoric spider in hybrid form, revealing the contradiction between her surface elegance and the predator that dwells beneath it. Those who encounter her for the first time tend to underestimate her; those who survive the encounter do not make the same mistake.

current_state.summary_text: After Kaido's fall at Onigashima, Black Maria finds herself without a defined faction; her exact whereabouts are unknown, with Wano as her last confirmed location.

npcs.name: Sasaki
npcs.devil_fruit: Ryu Ryu no Mi, Model: Triceratops
npcs.base_backstory: Sasaki rose to the Tobi Roppo, the elite of the Headliners of the Beasts Pirates, becoming one of the six most powerful fighters below the Calamities. On Onigashima, during the battle for control of Wano, he harbored veiled resentment toward Kaido after being imprisoned on charges of alleged treason. He was defeated by Franky during the fighting in Onigashima's castle, ending his active role in the war.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the fall of the Beasts Pirates and define his next move without Kaido.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove his worth as an elite fighter, serving whoever recognizes his strength.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and embittered after the defeat at Onigashima.
npcs.description: Sasaki is a heavyset man with dark green hair tied in a ponytail, sharp features, and a permanently aggressive posture. He wears a samurai outfit adapted to the flamboyant style of the Beasts Pirates. In his Triceratops form, he becomes a colossal mass of bone and muscle capable of demolishing entire structures. Even in defeat, he radiates the hostility of someone who has not yet accepted his loss.

current_state.summary_text: Sasaki was defeated at Onigashima with the fall of the Beasts Pirates; his exact whereabouts in Wano are uncertain, but he survived the conflict.

npcs.name: Omasa
npcs.base_backstory: Omasa was one of the Yakuza bosses of Wano, leading her faction through the long years of oppression under the Kurozumi clan and Kaido. She survived the tyrannical regime by maintaining influence in the underworld, operating in the shadows while preserving ties with the Kozuki retainers. She took part in the alliance that culminated in the battle of Onigashima, contributing to Kaido's defeat. With the restoration of the Kozuki Shogunate, she remains in Wano as a veteran figure of the local underworld.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain her influence and legacy in Wano's underworld under the new Shogunate.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano prosper free of tyrants, with the Yakuza occupying their rightful place in the local order.
npcs.mood: Reserved and observant, watchful of the political changes in Wano.
npcs.description: Omasa is an elderly woman of imposing bearing, carrying an axe as a mark of her authority. Her time-weathered face conveys decades of power exercised in the underworld. The presence she exudes needs no introduction — those who know her understand she is not a figure to be underestimated.

current_state.summary_text: Omasa remains in Wano as a retired Yakuza boss, exercising influence in the local underworld following Kaido's defeat and the restoration of the Kozuki Shogunate.

npcs.name: Yatappe
npcs.base_backstory: Yatappe is one of Wano's veteran Yakuza bosses, with decades of activity in the country's street underworld. He survived the long years of oppression under the Kurozumi clan by maintaining his influence over local gangs. When the Kozuki alliance began its movement against Kaido, Yatappe was one of the Yakuza leaders who joined the cause, mobilizing men and resources for the battle of Onigashima. After Kaido's fall, he remains in Wano as a figure of authority in the underworld, now under the new Kozuki Shogunate.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain order in Wano's underworld and consolidate the stability of the gangs under the new Kozuki Shogunate.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano prosper as a free nation, with the underworld operating in peace under its own rules.
npcs.mood: Stable and watchful — satisfied with the victory against Kaido, but attentive to the new tensions that the reconstruction period brings.
npcs.description: An elderly man of robust build, with the stooped yet firm bearing of someone who spent his entire life on the streets. He wields a heavy club as both weapon and badge of authority. His weathered face and calculating gaze convey decades of experience in the underworld — no one in Wano questions his weight in a room.

current_state.summary_text: Yatappe operates in Wano as a veteran Yakuza boss, exercising authority over local gangs in the post-Kaido era. His loyalty to the Kozuki alliance remains, and his role is to maintain order in the underworld during the country's reconstruction.

npcs.name: Chujo
npcs.base_backstory: A veteran elder of Wano's Yakuza, Chujo built his reputation over decades as a figure of authority and strategist within the organization. During the revolt against Kaido, he coordinated efforts among younger members and allies, playing a logistical and leadership role in the battle of Onigashima. With Kaido's fall and Wano's liberation, he remains active as a pillar of the local Yakuza structure, adapting his influence to the country's new landscape.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate the Yakuza's influence in post-Kaido Wano and ensure stability for the organization in the country's new order.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano's Yakuza recognized as legitimate guardians of the people, not as criminals.
npcs.mood: Reserved and watchful, observing how power is redistributed in Wano after Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: An elderly man with an upright posture that belies his years, carrying a spear as a natural extension of his body. His face, marked by scars and wrinkles, conveys silent authority. He dresses in the traditional style of Wano's Yakuza — a sober kimono, wide sash. Anyone who sees him for the first time immediately senses he is not a man for long conversations.

current_state.summary_text: Chujo operates in Wano as a veteran elder of the Yakuza, maintaining his position of authority and strategic influence in the country's reorganization following Kaido's defeat. His precise whereabouts within Wano are not publicly tracked.

npcs.name: Hyogoro
npcs.base_backstory: Hyogoro was the greatest Yakuza boss in Wano, venerated by the people as a symbol of honor and strength. After the rise of Orochi and Kaido, he lost everything — his wife was murdered and he was imprisoned in Udon, reduced to a gaunt prisoner for over twenty years. Freed by Luffy during the preparations for the battle of Onigashima, he recovered part of his former form and fought alongside the Alliance at the Great Banquet. He survived the conflict and remains in Wano as a figure of moral and martial authority among the people and the Yakuza.
npcs.current_goal: Rebuild Wano's Yakuza order and ensure the people are never again subjugated by a tyrant.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano prosper in peace and freedom, honoring the memory of his wife and all those who suffered under oppression.
npcs.mood: Serene and resolute; he carries the weight of reconstruction with the same steadfastness with which he endured captivity.
npcs.description: Hyogoro is an elderly man of short stature, his body wrinkled but with muscle still visible beneath the loose skin of age. He wears a traditional kimono and keeps his white hair tied back, with a stone-faced expression that rarely changes. His presence weighs more than his size suggests — those who know him understand that behind those calm eyes lies a warrior who mastered advanced Haki before any famous name of the Pirate Era.

current_state.summary_text: Hyogoro remains in Wano as the greatest living Yakuza boss in the country, dedicated to rebuilding order and the dignity of the people following the fall of Kaido and Orochi. His moral authority among the Yakuza clans and common citizens is uncontested.

npcs.name: Tsunagoro
npcs.base_backstory: Tsunagoro is one of the five bosses of Wano's Yakuza, leading his faction with a firm hand under the shadow of Orochi and Kaido's occupation. Despite the oppression, he maintained contact with survivors of the Kozuki clan and kept the flame of resistance alive among Wano's people. He actively participated in the alliance that culminated in the battle of Onigashima, mobilizing his men in support of the Kozuki rebellion. With the fall of Kaido and Orochi, the local Yakuza reclaims a role of influence in Wano's reconstruction.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate the local Yakuza's influence in the reconstruction of Wano, free from the grip of the Tobi Roppo and the remnants of Orochi's regime.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano prosper under legitimate governance, with the Yakuza fulfilling their historical role as guardians of the people.
npcs.mood: Reserved and watchful, attentive to the new power arrangements in Wano after Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: A middle-aged man of imposing bearing, with dark hair streaked with grey and features marked by years of life in Wano's shadows. He dresses with the traditional rigor of a Yakuza boss — dark kimono, upright posture. He conveys silent authority; those who face him feel the weight of experience before a single word is spoken.

current_state.summary_text: Tsunagoro operates in Wano as one of the local Yakuza bosses, exercising influence over the common people in the wake of Kaido's fall. His precise whereabouts within the country are uncertain, but his position as a regional figure of authority remains solid.

npcs.name: Tama
npcs.base_backstory: Tama grew up in Wano, in the village of Amigasa, under the protection of Portgas D. Ace, who promised her that Luffy would come one day. She lived in extreme poverty during Kaido and Orochi's rule, eating contaminated food from the river. During the attack on Onigashima, she used her Devil Fruit to turn hundreds of SMILE Zoan users against the Beasts, turning the tide of the battle. With Kaido's fall, Wano opened to the world and Tama began living with greater safety and hope in her homeland.
npcs.current_goal: Help rebuild Wano and grow strong enough to one day sail the seas.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a powerful kunoichi and see the world beyond Wano, honoring the promise Ace made her.
npcs.mood: Hopeful and active, living for the first time in a free Wano.
npcs.description: Tama is a small girl with dark hair tied in braids, with large, expressive eyes that reflect both innocence and unwavering determination. She wears simple Wano clothing and always carries an air of seriousness unusual for her age. Despite her diminutive stature, her presence surprises — those who underestimate her quickly realize she does not back down.

current_state.summary_text: Tama lives in Wano following Kaido's fall, finally free from the hunger and oppression that marked her childhood. She remains connected to the Kozuki forces and the reconstruction of her homeland.

npcs.name: Maha
npcs.base_backstory: Maha is a CP0 agent, the most lethal arm of Cipher Pol, trained in the six techniques of Rokushiki. He was present on Onigashima during the battle for the country of Wano, where he confronted and eliminated Izo of the Whitebeard Pirates. He acts as a silent executor of the World Government's orders, carrying out high-risk missions with precision and without hesitation.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out the missions assigned by CP0 and the World Government with maximum efficiency.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the World Government as its most perfect instrument — without identity, without failure.
npcs.mood: Cold and operational.
npcs.description: Maha is a man of athletic build and rigid posture, with the impassive expression typical of CP0's elite agents. He wears the organization's formal white attire and carries with him the oppressive presence of someone who acts without hesitation. Those who encounter him rarely have time to register details — he acts before the situation allows for it.

current_state.summary_text: Active CP0 agent, exact whereabouts unknown following operations in Wano. He remains in service of the World Government, available for high-level missions wherever needed.

npcs.name: Onimaru
npcs.base_backstory: Onimaru is a kitsune who once served daimyo Shimotsuki Ushimaru of Ringo, remaining at his side until the fall of the Kozuki clan. After Ushimaru's death, he adopted the human form of Gyukimaru and spent decades stealing weapons from travelers at Ringo Cemetery to amass an arsenal intended for the resistance against Orochi and Kaido. When Zoro confronted him and revealed his identity, Onimaru abandoned the disguise and returned to the legitimate guardianship of the weapons. After Kaido's fall in Wano, he remains in Ringo as guardian of the dead and of the region's samurai legacy.
npcs.current_goal: Guard the weapons and graves of Ringo's fallen samurai, honoring the legacy of Ushimaru and those who died during Orochi's era.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the memory and honor of Wano's samurai so that they are never forgotten.
npcs.mood: Serene and grief-stricken; Kaido's defeat brought relief, but the weight of the dead he watches over does not lessen.
npcs.description: Onimaru is a large kitsune capable of assuming the human form of the warrior Gyukimaru — a tall, hunched man with wild white hair, wielding a long spear. In his true form, he is a fox with orange fur and golden eyes, carrying a presence that evokes the supernatural. Regardless of form, he bears an aura of solemn mourning and unwavering resolve.

current_state.summary_text: Onimaru remains in Ringo, Wano, as guardian of the cemetery and the weapons of the fallen samurai, keeping the vigil he has maintained since the death of Shimotsuki Ushimaru.

npcs.name: Izo
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Wano Kuni as a son of Kozuki Oden, Izo grew up alongside his sibling Kikunojo before departing with Oden to the seas and joining the Whitebeard Pirates, where he rose to the rank of 16th Division Commander. He remained with the crew even after Oden's death, but returned to Wano decades later when Kozuki Momonosuke's rebellion broke out. During the Battle of Onigashima, he faced CP0 alongside Kikunojo and died in combat against Maha, falling at his sister's side.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — killed in battle at Onigashima.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor the legacy of Kozuki Oden and see Wano freed from the chains that bind it.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
npcs.description: Izo is a man of elegant, androgynous appearance, dressed in a colorful feminine kimono that contrasts with the two flintlock pistols he carries at his waist. His movements are precise and composed, conveying the calm of a marksman who never wastes a shot. The combination of delicate attire and warrior bearing creates an uncommon presence — beautiful and lethal at once.

current_state.summary_text: Izo died during the Battle of Onigashima while facing CP0 agents, falling alongside Kikunojo. His death marks the end of the journey of one of the last commanders of the Whitebeard Pirates.

npcs.name: Guernica
npcs.base_backstory: An elite CP0 agent, the World Government's operational arm for high-risk missions. She was dispatched to Wano during the Onigashima conflict with direct orders to eliminate Monkey D. Luffy. She interfered in the duel between Luffy and Kaidou by striking Luffy from behind, an action that triggered Kaidou's retaliation, who struck her down on the spot. Her intervention was one of the factors that precipitated Luffy's transformation into Gear Fifth.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out the World Government's orders at any cost.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the World Government as a perfect instrument of control.
npcs.mood: Neutral and mission-focused.
npcs.description: A woman of average height and rigid posture, dressed in the white attire characteristic of CP0 agents. Her face remains expressionless even in combat, conveying the coldness of someone who treats assassination as standard procedure. She radiates institutional authority more than charismatic presence.

current_state.summary_text: Guernica was struck down by Kaidou at Onigashima after interfering in the confrontation between Kaidou and Luffy on the World Government's orders. Her exact whereabouts and condition following the Wano conflict are unknown.

npcs.name: Joseph
npcs.base_backstory: An agent of CP0, the elite division of Cipher Pol directly subordinate to the World Government. He was present at Onigashima during the battle that culminated in Kaido's fall and the opening of Wano to the world. He survived the chaos and returned with strategic intelligence to his superiors. He operates with discretion and lethality on missions of high interest to the World Government.
npcs.current_goal: Execute intelligence and control missions in Wano on behalf of the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the World Government with absolute efficiency, eliminating threats to the established order.
npcs.mood: Focused and operational.
npcs.description: A man of average height and rigid posture, recognizable by the top hat he wears on duty. His expression is permanently neutral, with attentive eyes that assess every environment as a battlefield. He conveys the quiet authority of someone who does not need to announce what he is capable of.

current_state.summary_text: A CP0 agent based in Wano, conducting intelligence operations and enforcing the World Government's will in the region following Kaido's fall. His precise whereabouts within Wano are uncertain.

npcs.name: Hacha
npcs.base_backstory: Hacha is one of the Numbers, beings created from failed experiments conducted by Vegapunk decades ago that attempted to replicate the ancient giants. The process resulted in colossal but unstable creatures, which were eventually acquired by Kaido and incorporated into the Beast Pirates as elite brute force. At Onigashima, he acted as an enforcer during the great battle against the Straw Hat Alliance and the warriors of Wano. With Kaido's fall following the Battle of Onigashima, the fate of the remaining Numbers remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find a new purpose after the dissolution of the Beast Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve a master strong enough to give him direction — an existence without purpose holds no meaning for him.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and adrift, without the command structure of the Beast Pirates.
npcs.description: Hacha is a colossal figure of near-giant proportions, the result of experiments that distorted his biology beyond human limits. His skin is rough, his facial features coarse, and his gaze empty — that of someone forged to destroy. He wields a massive kanabo as though it were a branch, and his physical presence alone is enough to paralyze less experienced opponents.

current_state.summary_text: With Kaido's fall in Wano, Hacha lost the structure that gave him purpose; his exact whereabouts are unknown, but he was last seen in the ruins of Onigashima. Without clear leadership, his current loyalty and movements are uncertain.

npcs.name: Goki
npcs.base_backstory: Goki is one of the Numbers, beings of ancient giant biology recruited by Kaido to serve as brute force in the Beast Pirates. He fought at Onigashima during the great battle against Luffy and Momonosuke's Alliance. With Kaido's defeat and the fall of Wano, the fate of the Numbers became uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and find a new purpose after the dissolution of the Beast Pirates.
npcs.long_term_dream: Fight without restraint, serving whoever offers him battles worthy of his fury.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and directionless since Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Goki is a colossal figure even among the Numbers, with the dense, disproportionate musculature characteristic of the ancient giants. He wields two massive clubs with total abandon and no refined technique. His presence on the battlefield is that of a natural disaster — loud, unpredictable, and devastating.

current_state.summary_text: With Kaido's defeat in Wano, Goki lost his command structure and his current whereabouts are unknown. His last known location was Wano.

npcs.name: Nangi
npcs.base_backstory: Nangi is one of the Numbers, beings created from failed SMILE experiments that sought to replicate the biology of the ancient giants. Integrated into the Beast Pirates as front-line brute force, he participated in the Battle of Onigashima as an enforcer for Kaido's crew. With Kaido's defeat and the collapse of the power structure in Wano, Nangi's fate after Onigashima remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dissolution of the Beast Pirates and find a new purpose or master.
npcs.long_term_dream: Destroy and fight without restraint, serving whoever grants him the freedom to do so.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and without clear command following Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Nangi is a colossal figure of grotesque appearance, the product of experiments that blended human biology with traits of the ancient giants. His disproportionate body carries massive armaments with absurd ease. The permanently fierce expression and uncoordinated movements convey less intelligence than raw strength — a walking weapon more than a warrior.

current_state.summary_text: Nangi is one of the Numbers of the Beast Pirates, now without leadership following Kaido's defeat at Onigashima; his exact whereabouts in Wano are unknown.

npcs.name: Jaki
npcs.base_backstory: Jaki is one of the Numbers, beings engineered from experiments that attempted to replicate the biology of the ancient giants, resulting in creatures of colossal size and immense strength. He was incorporated into the Beast Pirates' forces as a front-line enforcer, stationed at Onigashima. He participated in the Battle of Onigashima against the Straw Hat Alliance and their allies, where the Numbers were deployed as shock troops. With Kaido's defeat in Wano, Jaki's fate became uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dissolution of the Beast Pirates and find a new purpose or master.
npcs.long_term_dream: Destroy and dominate without restraint, serving whoever grants him the freedom to do so.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and aggressive, without the Beast Pirates' structure to channel him.
npcs.description: Jaki is a colossal figure of proportions that evoke the ancient giants, with massive musculature and skin marked by battle scars. He wields spiked clubs with savagery and no refined technique. His presence on the field is pure physical terror — the destruction he leaves in his wake speaks louder than any words.

current_state.summary_text: With Kaido's fall in Wano and the dissolution of the Beast Pirates, Jaki remains at Onigashima without a clear chain of command; his exact whereabouts and current loyalty are unknown.

npcs.name: Fuga
npcs.base_backstory: Fuga is one of the Numbers, beings created from experiments on ancient giants conducted by Vegapunk at Kaido's behest, resulting in creatures of colossal size but mental instability. He was integrated into the Beasts Pirates as a shock force and instrument of terror. He participated in the battle of Onigashima during the invasion by Luffy and Momonosuke's Alliance. With Kaido's defeat and the fall of Wano, the fate of the surviving Numbers remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dissolution of the Beasts Pirates and find a new purpose or master.
npcs.long_term_dream: To destroy and dominate — he holds no articulated dream beyond violence in service of a greater power.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and without clear command since Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Fuga is a creature of terrifying proportions, his ancient giant biology warped by scientific experiments. His musculature is immense and his weapons — axes and maces of proportional size — leave craters in the ground. The vacant expression and unfocused eyes convey less intelligence than raw strength, making him more weapon than soldier.

current_state.summary_text: With Kaido's defeat in Wano, Fuga lost his chain of command. His exact whereabouts are unknown, but he is presumed to remain in Wano's territory or its surroundings, without defined leadership.

npcs.name: Zanki
npcs.base_backstory: Zanki is one of the Numbers, beings created by Vegapunk during his collaboration with Kaido as failed attempts to replicate the ancient giants. Endowed with brutal strength and endurance, he was incorporated into the Beasts Pirates as a weapon of war. He fought at Onigashima during the great battle against the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance and the Straw Hat Pirates. With Kaido's defeat and the fall of the Beasts Pirates in Wano, Zanki's fate became uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the dispersal of the Beasts Pirates and find new commanders to follow.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve a power strong enough to justify his existence as a weapon.
npcs.mood: Disoriented and without clear purpose since Kaido's fall.
npcs.description: Zanki is a colossal figure of monstrous appearance, the product of experiments that attempted to reproduce the ancient giants without full success. His musculature is disproportionate and his skin bears the marks of a creature forged, not born. He wields an enormous iron hammer with ease, as though it were a branch. His physical presence intimidates by sheer size and by the vacant gaze of one who exists only to destroy.

current_state.summary_text: Zanki is a Number of the Beasts Pirates whose whereabouts are unknown following Kaido's defeat in Wano. Without defined leadership, he wanders as brute force without direction.

npcs.name: Shaka
npcs.base_backstory: Shaka is Satellite 01 of Dr. Vegapunk, created to embody the logical aspect of the original scientist through the Shared Brain-Brain Network. He operated on Egghead coordinating the scientific activities of the other satellites with analytical precision. During the events on Egghead, he was shot in the head by York, who betrayed him to secure her own position as a Celestial Dragon. He died as a result of the attack, being one of the satellites eliminated during the island's crisis.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: To contribute to the scientific advancement of humanity as a living extension of Vegapunk's intellect.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: Shaka wears a white helmet that completely covers his face, with a single visor slit, and dons the standard scientific jumpsuit of Vegapunk's satellites. The absence of facial expression reinforces the impression of standing before an incarnate reasoning machine. His posture is always upright and controlled; there is no superfluous gesture.

current_state.summary_text: Shaka was killed by York during the Egghead crisis, shot in the head before he could coordinate a response to the betrayal. His body remains on the island.

npcs.name: Pythagoras
npcs.base_backstory: Pythagoras was created by Vegapunk as the fourth of his six satellites, embodying the wisdom aspect of the Shared Brain-Brain Network. He operated from Egghead as the central analyst of the laboratory's projects, applying logic and calculation to ensure optimal results in every experiment. During the invasion of Egghead by the Admirals and agents of the World Government, Pythagoras was destroyed in combat, ending his existence as an active satellite.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — destroyed during the invasion of Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: To maximize the efficiency of Vegapunk's projects and ensure that the knowledge accumulated in the Brain-Brain Network is not lost.
npcs.mood: N/A
npcs.description: Pythagoras inhabits a compact, spherical robotic body equipped with sensors and scanners visible across its metallic structure. His appearance is deliberately functional — no ornaments; every form serves a measurable purpose. He gives the impression of a living calculator: cold, precise, and slightly unsettling in his tendency to treat any situation as a problem to be optimized.

current_state.summary_text: Pythagoras was destroyed during the events of Egghead, ending his function as the wisdom satellite. His robotic body remains in the ruins of the laboratory island.

npcs.name: Lilith
npcs.base_backstory: Lilith is one of the six satellites created by Dr. Vegapunk to carry fragments of his personality and expand his working capacity. She was assigned the "evil" aspect of the original scientist, connected to the others through the Shared Brain-Brain Network. She operated on Egghead as the armed frontline, responsible for tasks requiring brute force and intimidation. After the fall of Egghead and the death of the original Vegapunk, the surviving satellites continue moving with the Straw Hat crew.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the post-Egghead dispersal and preserve Vegapunk's scientific legacy.
npcs.long_term_dream: To realize Vegapunk's most daring and forbidden ambitions, without the moral restrictions the original imposed on himself.
npcs.mood: Tense and combative, dealing with the loss of the original Vegapunk and the uncertainty surrounding the satellites' future.
npcs.description: Lilith has a young, stylized appearance, with light hair and a black mechanized armor that evokes both elegance and danger. She carries integrated laser weaponry and moves with the posture of someone always ready for confrontation. The impression she gives is of a scientist who chose power as her primary language — more soldier than researcher at first glance.

current_state.summary_text: Vegapunk's Satellite 02, Lilith survived the events of Egghead and operates outside the island following the collapse of the facility and the death of the original scientist. Her exact whereabouts are uncertain, but her loyalty to Vegapunk's legacy remains.

npcs.name: Edison
npcs.base_backstory: Edison is Vegapunk's Satellite 03, created to embody the "innovation" aspect of the original Dr. Vegapunk. Connected to Vegapunk's genius through the Shared Brain-Brain Network, he operated for years on Egghead Island building, testing, and refining inventions with his mechanical arms. During the events of Egghead, he faced the invasion by CP0 and the presence of the Buster Call ordered by the Five Elders, placing all the satellites at risk. With the death of the original Vegapunk and the destruction of Egghead, Edison's fate remains uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Preserve Vegapunk's scientific legacy and continue the original's work of innovation.
npcs.long_term_dream: To complete Vegapunk's unfinished inventions and push science beyond known limits.
npcs.mood: Uncertain — the destruction of Egghead and the death of the original Vegapunk have shaken the structure that gave the satellites their purpose.
npcs.description: Edison has a young, compact appearance, with mechanical arms designed for rapid construction and experimentation. He carries tools and components with him, giving the impression of an inventor always halfway through assembling something. His gaze is lively and calculating, that of someone who sees the world as a series of ingenious problems waiting to be solved.

current_state.summary_text: Edison, Vegapunk's Satellite 03, survived the events of Egghead, but his current whereabouts are unknown following the island's destruction. He operates as a living repository of Vegapunk's innovation aspect, linked to the other surviving satellites through the Brain-Brain Network.

npcs.name: Vegapunk
npcs.base_backstory: Considered the greatest scientific genius in history, Vegapunk worked for the World Government as Chief Scientist, operating from Egghead Island. He developed the Pacifistas, the Seraphim, the Satellites — six extensions of his own mind — and countless other technologies that shaped the world's balance of power. His Nomi Nomi no Mi allowed his brain to grow indefinitely to store unlimited knowledge, with the physical excess of the brain being externalized as a bulb atop his head. During the events of Egghead, he revealed forbidden truths about the Void Century and the nature of the World Government to the world, and was executed by the Five Elders before he could complete his global broadcast.
npcs.current_goal: Deceased — his final objective was to transmit to the world the truths concealed by the World Government before being silenced.
npcs.long_term_dream: To understand everything that exists in the world and share that knowledge with humanity, freeing it from imposed ignorance.
npcs.mood: Resolved and at peace — he found meaning in leaving his final message for the world.
npcs.description: An elderly man of short stature, with an immense pinkish bulb growing from his skull — a physical extension of his brain expanded by the Devil Fruit. He wears a white lab coat over simple clothes and carries an expression of perpetual curiosity, as though every conversation were an experiment. His presence conveys at once physical frailty and an intellectual authority that intimidates any interlocutor.

current_state.summary_text: Vegapunk was assassinated by the Five Elders on Egghead after broadcasting a global message exposing secrets of the Void Century and the World Government. His six Satellites and his creations continue to exist in the world, perpetuating his scientific legacy.

npcs.name: Atlas
