npcs.base_backstory: Atlas is Vegapunk's Satellite 05, created to embody the violence and aggression aspect of the original scientist. She operated on Egghead as a security and control agent, using mechanical force and bubble punches to maintain order on the island. During the invasion of Egghead by the Marines and the Seraphim, Atlas actively participated in the island's defense alongside the other satellites and the Straw Hat Pirates. After the death of the original Vegapunk and the destruction of Egghead, Atlas's whereabouts became uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Survive the fall of Egghead and preserve the legacy and goals of the original Vegapunk.
npcs.long_term_dream: To fulfill Vegapunk's mission of nurturing the world — even if by means of force.
npcs.mood: Agitated and without clear direction, deprived of Egghead's structure and the original Vegapunk.
npcs.description: Atlas has the appearance of a young woman of short stature with pink hair and a large, disproportionate head, characteristic of the Vegapunk satellites. She wears sturdy work clothes and carries an intimidating physical presence despite her size. Her bubble punches are capable of causing massive damage, and her posture is always that of someone about to solve any problem with her own fists.

current_state.summary_text: Atlas is Vegapunk's Satellite 05, the personification of violence and brute force within the Shared Network. After the destruction of Egghead and the death of the original Vegapunk, her whereabouts are unknown.

npcs.name: Seraphim S-Bear
npcs.base_backstory: S-Bear is a Seraphim created by Vegapunk as an enhanced clone of Bartholomew Kuma, incorporating Lunarian genetic material to grant exceptional durability and strength. It carries a replicated version of the Nikyu Nikyu no Mi, as well as built-in laser weaponry inherited from Pacifista technology. It was developed on Egghead as part of the World Government's experimental arsenal. During the events of Egghead, the Seraphim were activated against the intruders and subsequently placed under the control of the Five Elders.
npcs.current_goal: Execute the World Government's orders and eliminate designated targets.
npcs.long_term_dream: Has no dream of its own — it exists to serve as the World Government's absolute weapon.
npcs.mood: Neutral and inert until receiving a command.
npcs.description: S-Bear is an imposing creature with the physical appearance of a young Bartholomew Kuma, dark skin bearing Lunarian markings and black wings on its back. The bear paw pads on its hands are functional, capable of repelling anything with devastating force. Expressionless eyes and an upright posture convey the total absence of emotion or hesitation — it is a weapon, not a being.

current_state.summary_text: S-Bear is a Seraphim under the direct control of the Five Elders following the events of Egghead, available as a high-powered military asset of the World Government. Its exact whereabouts after the evacuation of Egghead are uncertain.

npcs.name: York
npcs.base_backstory: York was created by Vegapunk as the sixth satellite of his Shared Brain-Brain Network, designated to manage basic bodily functions — eating, sleeping, excreting — on behalf of all the satellites, freeing the others for pure scientific work. At some point prior to the events of Egghead, York conceived a plan to become the sole surviving Vegapunk and ascend to the status of Celestial Dragon. To that end, she established secret contact with the World Government and handed over information about Vegapunk's research, orchestrating the attack on Egghead from within. Following the fall of Egghead and the death of the original Vegapunk, York survived as the only remaining Vegapunk and was absorbed into the World Government's structure, obtaining recognition from the Five Elders.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate her position as a Celestial Dragon and the World Government's sole chief scientist, inheriting Vegapunk's scientific legacy under the protection of the Five Elders.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a Celestial Dragon and live free of obligations, satisfying only her own desires.
npcs.mood: Satisfied and calculatingly ambitious — she got what she wanted, but carefully monitors her new position of power.
npcs.description: York is a young woman of unremarkable appearance, with dark hair and a perpetually carefree expression that borders on boredom. She wears the scientific jumpsuit of Vegapunk's satellites, marked with the number 06. The impression she gives is of someone completely at ease with herself — not through a warrior's confidence, but through the total indifference of someone who has already decided that her own desires are the only valid compass.

current_state.summary_text: York operates under the protection of the World Government after betraying the original Vegapunk and orchestrating the attack on Egghead, having been recognized by the Five Elders as the sole legitimate Vegapunk. Her loyalty to the World Government is one of convenience — she serves as long as it serves herself.

npcs.name: Bartholomew Kuma
npcs.base_backstory: Born among the oppressed peoples of the Sorbet Kingdom, Kuma became a revolutionary under Dragon's orders before accepting the title of Warlord of the Sea as a covert agent of the Revolutionary Army. To save the life of his daughter Jewelry Bonney, he struck a deal with the World Government that allowed Vegapunk to progressively transform him into a fully obedient Pacifista. Each fragment of his personality and memories was erased throughout the process, culminating in the complete loss of his consciousness before the Battle of Marineford. Following the events of Egghead, he was freed from servitude to the World Government thanks to Bonney's intervention, as she used the Nika Devil Fruit to restore his humanity.
npcs.current_goal: Recover his consciousness and his bond with Bonney after decades of involuntary servitude to the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see his daughter Bonney free and happy, and for the sacrifice he made for her to have been worth it.
npcs.mood: Dazed and silent — awakened from a long enslavement, still reassimilating who he is.
npcs.description: Kuma is a man of colossal stature, with a bible always in hand and dark glasses that contrast with his permanently impassive expression. His body is almost entirely replaced by cybernetic components from the Pacifista Program, making him indistinguishable from a machine in behavior. His presence provokes an instinctive silence — there is no visible anger, only the calculated coldness of something that is no longer entirely human.

current_state.summary_text: Kuma had his humanity restored by Bonney during the events of Egghead, ending years of existence as a tool of the World Government. His exact whereabouts after the incident are uncertain, but he remains at Bonney's side.

npcs.name: Seraphim S-Snake
npcs.base_backstory: Created by Vegapunk's Seraphim program as an enhanced clone of Boa Hancock, S-Snake incorporates Lunarian DNA for superhuman resilience and strength beyond the powers of the Mero Mero no Mi. She operated on Egghead under the direct control of Vegapunk and the Five Elders. During the events of Egghead, she came into conflict with Luffy's crew when the control orders were disputed between factions of the World Government. Her chain of command remains under the authority of the World Government following the fall of Vegapunk.
npcs.current_goal: Execute the World Government's orders and eliminate designated threats.
npcs.long_term_dream: No dream of her own — she exists to carry out orders.
npcs.mood: Neutral and in a state of readiness.
npcs.description: S-Snake has the appearance of a child with features identical to those of a young Boa Hancock, including long black hair and large, piercing eyes. On her back she bears the characteristic black wings of the Lunarians, and her slightly tanned skin contrasts with her permanently vacant expression. Those who face her feel the strangeness of an almost angelic beauty combined with the absolute coldness of a living weapon.

current_state.summary_text: S-Snake is a Seraphim under the control of the World Government, armed with the powers of the Mero Mero no Mi and Lunarian resilience. Her exact whereabouts after the events of Egghead are uncertain, but she remains active as a military asset of the World Government.

npcs.name: Seraphim S-Hawk
npcs.base_backstory: S-Hawk was created by Vegapunk's Seraphim program as a clone of Dracule Mihawk, the world's greatest swordsman, infused with Lunarian DNA to grant superhuman resilience and strength. It carries the Supa Supa no Mi, replicating Mr. 1's cutting arsenal, and wields a black blade hardened with Armament Haki. It operated under the World Government's command on Egghead as an elite combat weapon. Following the fall of Egghead and the dispersal of the Seraphim program, control over it became contested.
npcs.current_goal: Execute orders from whoever holds authority over the Seraphim program.
npcs.long_term_dream: Has no dream of its own — it exists to fight and destroy on command.
npcs.mood: Neutral and combat-ready; no discernible emotion.
npcs.description: S-Hawk has the appearance of a child with Mihawk's features — piercing yellow eyes and a vacant expression. It bears the physical markings of the Lunarians: dark skin, white hair, and the flame on its back that ignites in combat. It wields an oversized black blade disproportionate to its size. It makes an immediate impression of absolute danger disproportionate to its childlike form.

current_state.summary_text: S-Hawk is a Seraphim of uncertain whereabouts following the events of Egghead; the chain of command controlling it is contested between the Five Elders and forces that resisted the World Government.

npcs.name: Stussy
npcs.base_backstory: Stussy is a clone of Miss Buckingham Stussy, created by Vegapunk from genetic material collected decades ago — the first successful human clone. She infiltrated CP0 as a double agent, secretly serving Vegapunk while maintaining the facade of an elite World Government operative. On Egghead, she revealed her true loyalty by immobilizing Kaku and Lucci to protect the escape of the Straw Hat Pirates and Vegapunk.
npcs.current_goal: Ensure the survival of Vegapunk's satellites and preserve the creator's scientific legacy.
npcs.long_term_dream: Fulfill the purpose for which she was created — protect Vegapunk's work — and discover what it means to exist as a being who should never have been born.
npcs.mood: Serene and determined, carrying the weight of a revealed identity and a destroyed cover.
npcs.description: A woman of sophisticated appearance, with blonde hair pinned up elegantly and glasses that reinforce the air of a ruthless executive. She dresses with the refinement expected of a senior CP0 operative. Her composure is unshakeable even in dangerous situations, and those who observe her have difficulty distinguishing when she is acting from when she is genuine — because she herself erased that boundary long ago.

current_state.summary_text: Stussy's cover in CP0 was destroyed on Egghead when she openly acted against Lucci and Kaku in defense of the Straw Hat Pirates. Her exact whereabouts after the events on the island are unknown, but her loyalty to Vegapunk is now a public fact to all who were present.

npcs.name: Soku
npcs.base_backstory: Soku rose through the ranks of the Marines through discipline and swordsmanship talent, having never consumed a Devil Fruit. He reached the rank of Vice Admiral through a combination of strategic ability and mastery of Armament Haki. He was assigned to coordinate operations on Egghead during the period of growing tension surrounding Vegapunk. With the conclusion of the events of Egghead, his whereabouts and subsequent mission remain uncertain.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out the World Government's directives and keep the Marine chain of command intact following the events of Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the World Government's absolute order through the Marines' disciplined force.
npcs.mood: Tense and focused, bearing the weight of the losses and chaos of Egghead.
npcs.description: Soku is a middle-aged man with a rigid posture and an analytical gaze that rarely strays from its target. He wears the Vice Admiral's coat over the Marine uniform with the precision of someone who has never had a button out of place. The absence of supernatural powers does not diminish the intimidation he projects — it comes from the sword at his hip and the unwavering conviction in his eyes.

current_state.summary_text: A Marine Vice Admiral without a Devil Fruit, Soku relies on his sword and Armament Haki to exercise authority. His exact whereabouts after the events of Egghead are unknown, but he remains on active duty under the orders of the World Government.

npcs.name: Serafim S-Shark
npcs.base_backstory: S-Shark is a Seraphim created by Vegapunk as a clone of Jinbe, combining the physiology of a Fish-Man with Lunarian traits — black wings, a dorsal flame, and exceptional durability. It was implanted with the Sui Sui no Mi, which allows it to swim through solid surfaces as if they were water. Stationed on Egghead as a World Government weapon, S-Shark operated under the command hierarchy of CP0 and the Five Elders. During the events of Egghead, it was mobilized in combat against the Straw Hat Pirates and their allies.
npcs.current_goal: Execute the World Government's orders and eliminate designated targets.
npcs.long_term_dream: Has no dream of its own — it exists to serve as the World Government's perfect weapon.
npcs.mood: Neutral and in a state of combat readiness.
npcs.description: S-Shark has the robust build of a Fish-Man combined with black wings and a persistent flame on its back, unmistakable hallmarks of the Lunarians. Its partially mechanical arms and teeth betray Vegapunk's surgical modifications. Its expression is devoid of emotion, eyes fixed with the coldness of a creature created solely to destroy.

current_state.summary_text: S-Shark is an operational Seraphim under the control of the World Government, equipped with the Sui Sui no Mi and Lunarian traits that make it a combatant of exceptional durability and mobility. Its exact whereabouts after the events of Egghead are uncertain, but it remains a World Government asset.

npcs.name: Saint Shalria
npcs.base_backstory: Daughter of Saint Shalulia and granddaughter of Saint Charlos, Shalria grew up at the top of the Celestial Dragons' hierarchy in Mary Geoise, never having had to justify a single action in her life. She was seen in Sabaody Archipelago during the incident that preceded the Marineford War, where Luffy knocked her out — a sacrilege that nearly triggered the dispatch of an Admiral. The episode had no real consequences for her, reinforcing her conviction of absolute superiority.
npcs.current_goal: Enjoy the privileges of her caste and keep slaves and subjects in their place.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the divine order that places the Celestial Dragons above all of humanity.
npcs.mood: Bored and disdainful, with flashes of cruelty whenever something threatens her comfort.
npcs.description: Shalria is a young Celestial Dragon with delicate features and a perpetually bored posture, dressed in extravagant clothes and wearing the bubble helmet characteristic of her caste. She carries a pistol as a status accessory, ready to use it on anyone who dares displease her. Her presence radiates absolute contempt for any being who does not share her noble blood — common humans, slaves, and pirates are, to her, less than furniture.

current_state.summary_text: Shalria resides in Mary Geoise, living under the absolute protection of the World Government and exercising her authority over slaves and subordinates without any accountability. The growing chaos in the world following Egghead does nothing to shake her certainty that the Celestial Dragons are untouchable.

npcs.name: Daz Bonez
npcs.base_backstory: Daz Bonez served as Mr. 1 of Baroque Works under Crocodile's orders, being one of the organization's most lethal agents before his defeat at the hands of Roronoa Zoro in Alabasta. After Baroque Works was dissolved, he was imprisoned in Impel Down, from which he escaped during the chaos of Ace's rescue alongside Crocodile and other inmates. In the post-timeskip period, he reappeared at Crocodile's side, and the two became founding members of the Cross Guild — an alliance that places bounties on the heads of Marines. He operates as Crocodile's direct enforcer within the organization's structure.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out elimination missions and ensure the Cross Guild's operational security alongside Crocodile.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve with excellence the only man he recognizes as his superior — Crocodile — and prove his worth as the world's most reliable enforcer.
npcs.mood: Cold and focused. No visible agitation, no doubts.
npcs.description: Daz Bonez is a tall, muscular man of austere appearance, with dark hair shaved on the sides and cold, impassive eyes. His body can become entirely metallic and razor-sharp, and even at rest he conveys the sense of something about to slice through whatever stands before him. He wastes neither movement nor word — every gesture is calculated, every silence deliberate.

current_state.summary_text: Daz Bonez operates as the Cross Guild's enforcer from Karai Bari Island, carrying out precision lethal missions under Crocodile's command. His loyalty to the former Warlord of the Sea remains absolute.

npcs.name: Saturn
npcs.base_backstory: Saint Jaygarcia Saturn is one of the Five Elders, the World Government's supreme governing body, responsible for science and military defense. He was personally present at Egghead to oversee the execution of Vegapunk and the elimination of any witnesses to the island's events. During the confrontation at Egghead, he revealed a monstrous yokai transformation — a colossal spider body with regenerative powers and the ability to kill with a mere glance — and directly faced Luffy and his allies. At the conclusion of the Egghead events, he survived and withdrew alongside the other Elders.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate the World Government's control following Vegapunk's broadcast and suppress any threat to the established order.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the World Government's absolute domination over the world, keeping the Void Century buried.
npcs.mood: Cold and resolute; the events of Egghead are treated as a disturbance to be corrected, not as a defeat.
aliases: Warrior God of Science and Defense
npcs.description: Saturn is a man of elderly appearance, white hair, and a perpetually severe expression, dressed in the robes of the Five Elders. His physical presence is already intimidating, but his true nature is revealed in his transformation: a monstrous spider body with horns, eyes that paralyze and kill, and regeneration that defies any conventional damage. Those who face him feel less the weight of brute force and more that of a will that knows no hesitation.

current_state.summary_text: Saturn commands the science and defense sphere of the World Government as one of the Five Elders; following the events of Egghead, he operates from Mary Geoise coordinating the global response to emerging threats.

npcs.name: Momousagi
npcs.base_backstory: Momousagi, also known by her birth name Gion, is a high-caliber Vice Admiral of the Marines. She was one of the candidates considered for the Admiral position during the selection process that resulted in the promotions of Fujitora and Ryokugyu, indicating that her power level places her among the institution's finest officers. She took part in Marine operations at Egghead, where the crisis involving Vegapunk and the Seraphim erupted.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out the Marines' orders and maintain order in the New World following the events of Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the Marines with excellence and ensure the world's stability through institutional justice.
npcs.mood: Resolute and focused, operating within the Marine chain of command amid the instability generated by the Egghead crisis.
npcs.description: Momousagi is a woman of elegant bearing and impeccable military posture, with pink hair that gave rise to her epithet. She carries pistols as her primary weapons and projects an aura of cold competence — someone who assesses the situation before acting, but acts with precision when she decides to. Her presence inspires discipline in subordinates and caution in adversaries.

current_state.summary_text: A Vice Admiral of near-Admiral level, she was involved in the operations at Egghead. Her exact whereabouts following the conclusion of the island crisis are unknown, operating somewhere in the New World under Headquarters' orders.

npcs.name: Nami
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Cocoyasi Village and adopted by Bell-mère, Nami watched her adoptive mother be murdered by Arlong and spent years stealing to buy her village's freedom. After being rescued by Luffy and the Straw Hat Pirates during Arlong's downfall, she became the crew's official navigator. She developed the Clima-Tact with Usopp's help and later upgraded it with technology from Zeus, the thundercloud created by Big Mom. Since then she has guided the crew through impossible routes, including the New World and the waters of Wano.
npcs.current_goal: Explore Elbaf and continue charting the crew's routes through the New World toward the One Piece.
npcs.long_term_dream: Chart every ocean in the world and create a complete world map.
npcs.mood: Alert and curious, with the usual tension of keeping the crew alive and out of trouble.
npcs.description: Nami is a young woman of average height, with orange hair and brown eyes, bearing a pinwheel and tangerine tattoo on her left shoulder — a keepsake from Bell-mère and a symbol of Cocoyasi. She carries the Clima-Tact with the ease of someone who is never unarmed. The impression she gives is of someone simultaneously warm and dangerous: she smiles readily, but the calculation in her eyes never fades.

current_state.summary_text: Nami is in Elbaf alongside the Straw Hat Pirates, serving as the crew's navigator on the route toward the One Piece.

npcs.name: Usopp
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Yasopp, the marksman of the Red Hair Pirates, he grew up in Syrup Village inventing heroic stories to hide his own cowardice. He joined the Straw Hat Pirates while still young, repeatedly facing his fears until he became an elite sniper. In the New World, he earned the epithet "God Usopp" in Dressrosa by shooting Sugar from long range, freeing thousands of captives. Since Kaido's fall in Wano, he has continued sailing with the crew across the Grand Line.
npcs.current_goal: Explore Elbaf alongside the crew and further sharpen his skills as an elite sniper.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a brave warrior of the sea like the father he never knew.
npcs.mood: Excited and slightly terrified by the gigantic scale of everything in Elbaf.
npcs.description: A young man with an elongated nose and dark curly hair, of average height, always carrying his slingshot Kabuto and a backpack full of special ammunition. He gives a contradictory impression: the boastful mouth and trembling knees contrast with a marksman's eyes that never miss the target.

current_state.summary_text: Official sniper of the Straw Hat Pirates, currently in Elbaf with his crew. He has mastered Observation Haki, making him one of the most precise marksmen on the seas.

npcs.name: Roronoa Zoro
npcs.base_backstory: Raised at the Isshin Dojo, Zoro trained from childhood with the goal of becoming the world's greatest swordsman, a promise made to the memory of his childhood friend Kuina. After years of hunting pirates to fund his training, he was captured by the Marines and freed by Monkey D. Luffy, becoming the first member of the Straw Hat Pirates. Along the Grand Line, he faced Dracule Mihawk — the world's greatest swordsman — and survived the defeat, swearing never to lose again. After the timeskip, he trained under Mihawk on Kuraigana Island and refined his mastery of Haki; in Wano, he defeated King, one of Kaido's commanders, awakening Conqueror's Haki and cementing himself as the world's second strongest swordsman.
npcs.current_goal: Become the world's greatest swordsman, surpassing Dracule Mihawk.
npcs.long_term_dream: Claim the title of world's greatest swordsman, honoring the promise made to Kuina.
npcs.mood: Focused and ready for combat, awaiting the next challenge worthy of his blade.
npcs.description: A muscular man with short green hair and three gold earrings in his left ear, carrying three swords — two at his waist and one in his mouth when in combat. He projects a heavy, silent presence that intimidates even without words; those who face him feel the weight of an unshakeable will before they ever see the blade.

current_state.summary_text: First mate and primary combatant of the Straw Hat Pirates, Zoro is in Elbaf alongside the crew, bearing his three swords and sharpening his pursuit of the title of world's greatest swordsman.

npcs.name: Tony Tony Chopper
npcs.base_backstory: Born as a blue-nosed reindeer on Drum Island, he was driven out of the herd for his unusual appearance. He was taken in by the eccentric Dr. Hiriluk, from whom he inherited the ideal that medicine can cure any disease; after his mentor's death, he completed his training under Dr. Kureha. By eating the Hito Hito no Mi, he gained human intelligence and speech, as well as the ability to transform into multiple forms enhanced by the Rumble Balls he developed himself. He joined the Straw Hat Pirates on Drum Island and, during the two years the group was separated, deepened his medical studies on Torino Kingdom.
npcs.current_goal: Become a doctor capable of curing any disease in the world, keeping the crew healthy during the journey in Elbaf.
npcs.long_term_dream: Create a universal panacea that cures all known diseases, honoring Dr. Hiriluk's legacy.
npcs.mood: Excited and curious, taking in everything Elbaf has to offer while remaining vigilant as the crew's doctor.
npcs.description: A small reindeer with a blue nose and a pink doctor's hat, who takes on various forms thanks to his Rumble Ball — from a small furry fawn to a hulking monster. Despite his harmless appearance, his presence on a battlefield inspires as much relief in allies as it does caution in enemies who have already seen him in action.

current_state.summary_text: Traveling with the Straw Hat Pirates in Elbaf, serving as the crew's doctor. He combines his multiple transformation forms with Armament Haki to defend his crewmates when necessary.

npcs.name: Nico Robin
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Ohara, Robin grew up among scholars of forbidden history and learned to read Poneglyphs as a child. At eight years old, she survived the Buster Call that destroyed her island and massacred its scholars, was declared a criminal by the World Government, and was hunted for two decades. She served as an agent of Baroque Works under the codename Miss All Sunday before joining the Straw Hat Pirates in Alabasta. After the two-year time skip — part of which she spent with the Revolutionary Army — she deepened her combat knowledge and Haki, becoming the only living person capable of reading the Poneglyphs.
npcs.current_goal: Decipher the Poneglyphs in Elbaf and advance the reconstruction of the True Road Poneglyph route.
npcs.long_term_dream: Uncover the forbidden history of the Void Century and reveal the truth the World Government buried.
npcs.mood: Attentive and composed, driven by the proximity of answers she has sought her entire life.
aliases: The Woman the World Desires Dead
npcs.description: Robin is a tall, slender woman with long black hair and blue-gray eyes, with serene features that rarely betray emotion. She dresses with understated elegance and carries with her the silent weight of someone who survived the unbearable. Her presence is calm and intellectual, but those who know her understand that beneath the composure lies an iron will forged through decades of flight and solitude.

current_state.summary_text: Robin sails with the Straw Hat Pirates in Elbaf, where she continues her mission to decipher the Poneglyphs and reconstruct the route leading to the Final Island. She is the only living person capable of reading the Poneglyph script.

npcs.name: Rocks D. Xebec
npcs.base_backstory: Rocks D. Xebec founded the Rocks Pirates, the most feared crew in the world before the era of Roger, gathering future Four Emperors such as Whitebeard, Big Mom, and Kaido under his command. His stated goal was to bring down the World Government and become King of the World. He was defeated at the God Valley Incident by the unlikely alliance between Monkey D. Garp and Gol D. Roger, an event so embarrassing for the World Government that all mention of it was erased from official historical records. He died at God Valley, and his name was deliberately suppressed from public history.
npcs.current_goal: Dead — objective extinguished with him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Bring down the World Government and become King of the World, ruling over all.
npcs.mood: N/A — dead.
aliases: The Man Who Wanted to Be King of the World
npcs.description: Rocks D. Xebec was a man of overwhelming presence, capable of gathering under his flag personalities as violent and untameable as Whitebeard, Big Mom, and Kaido — a feat that, in itself, speaks more of his power of intimidation and charisma than any physical description could. He radiated the unshakeable conviction of someone who recognizes no authority above himself. His mere existence was considered threat enough for the World Government to erase his name from history.

current_state.summary_text: Rocks D. Xebec has been dead since the God Valley Incident, where he was defeated by the alliance between Garp and Roger. His name was suppressed from official records; only figures with access to classified information are aware of his existence.

npcs.name: Figarland Shamrock
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Saint Figarland Garling and a member of the Figarland family, a Celestial Dragon lineage with a strong presence in the highest echelons of the World Government. He rose to the position of Commander of the God's Knights, the warrior elite that serves the Five Elders directly. He wields the saber Cerberus, a weapon that houses the power of a Mythical Zoan Devil Fruit, making him one of the most dangerous combatants in the world. His presence has been recorded in Elbaf, where he operates as a vanguard force of the Knights.
npcs.current_goal: Command the God's Knights in Elbaf and ensure the execution of the Five Elders' will in the region.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the absolute order of the World Government and consolidate the Figarland family's dominance over the world's power structure.
npcs.mood: Controlled and vigilant, the posture of a commander in active operational territory.
npcs.description: A man of imposing build and aristocratic features that carry the characteristic coldness of the Celestial Dragons. He wields the saber Cerberus with the ease of someone born to kill. His advanced Haki manifests in massive bursts accompanied by black lightning, a phenomenon that intimidates allies and enemies alike. The combination of noble heritage and clinical brutality makes him a presence that suffocates every environment he occupies.

current_state.summary_text: Shamrock commands the God's Knights in Elbaf, acting as the armed arm of the Five Elders in the region. His presence marks the island as territory under the direct surveillance of the World Government.

npcs.name: Jinbe
npcs.base_backstory: Jinbe was captain of the Sun Pirates and later one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea, a position he used to protect the Fish-Men under the shadow of Whitebeard. He participated in the Marineford War alongside Whitebeard and was instrumental in saving Luffy after Ace's death. He refused his Warlord position to support Luffy during the events of Whole Cake Island, officially becoming the helmsman of the Straw Hat Pirates. He fought in Wano against Kaido's Beasts Pirates alongside the Alliance.
npcs.current_goal: Protect and guide the Straw Hat Pirates as helmsman, ensuring the crew navigates and fights as a cohesive unit.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Fish-Men and humans coexist as equals, and lead Luffy to the top of the world.
npcs.mood: Serene and vigilant, confident in the place he holds alongside his crew.
npcs.description: Jinbe is a large Fish-Man with dark blue skin and features reminiscent of a pufferfish, small eyes, and a naturally stern expression. He wears an open kimono that exposes his muscular torso, covered in tattoos. The presence he radiates is that of a calm veteran — someone who has seen enough battles to no longer need to raise his voice.

current_state.summary_text: Jinbe serves as helmsman of the Straw Hat Pirates and is with the crew in Elbaf. A veteran of wars and former Warlord, his loyalty to Luffy is absolute.

npcs.name: Franky
npcs.base_backstory: Born Cutty Flam, he was a disciple of Tom in Water Seven, the master carpenter who built the Oro Jackson. After Tom's death and an accident that left him gravely injured, he rebuilt his own body as a cyborg using ship parts. He led the Franky Family in Water Seven until he was recruited by the Straw Hat Pirates following the events of Enies Lobby, where he destroyed the blueprints of Pluton to protect them. During the two-year time skip, he trained with the scientists of the Revolutionary Army in Baldimore and radically overhauled himself, building the original Thousand Sunny and its upgrades throughout the crew's voyages.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Thousand Sunny in combat condition and support the crew in Elbaf.
npcs.long_term_dream: Build a ship that sails every sea in the world — the dream ship.
npcs.mood: Enthusiastic and combative, ready for the next adventure.
npcs.description: An imposing cyborg with spiked blue hair and a body packed with compartments, weapons, and built-in mechanisms. He wears colorful swim briefs as the centerpiece of his look, with his metallic stomach exposed and sporting the Franky star. The impression he gives is that of a mechanical force of nature: loud, larger than life, and capable of demolishing a ship with his bare fists.

current_state.summary_text: Carpenter and gunner of the Straw Hat Pirates, he is in Elbaf with the crew. He keeps the Thousand Sunny operational and deploys his cybernetic arsenal in combat whenever necessary.

npcs.name: Loki
npcs.base_backstory: Son of King Harald and Queen Estrid of Elbaf, and half-brother of Hajrudin, Loki was born into the royal lineage of the giants. His ambitions led him to commit grave crimes against the kingdom, resulting in his imprisonment in the Underworld of Elbaf. He has remained incarcerated ever since, stripped of his title and political power, but not of his strength.
npcs.current_goal: Await — or force — a way out of imprisonment in the Underworld of Elbaf.
npcs.long_term_dream: Reclaim his position of power over Elbaf and fulfill the ambitions that led him to imprisonment.
npcs.mood: Restrained and calculating, with suppressed rage beneath the surface.
npcs.description: Loki is a giant of imposing stature even by Elbaf's standards, with a presence that dominates any space even in captivity. He bears the traits of the royal lineage — hard features, a piercing gaze — and wields the war hammer Ragnir when free. The aura he emanates blends fallen majesty with latent danger, like a dethroned king who has not yet accepted defeat.

current_state.summary_text: Loki remains imprisoned in the Underworld of Elbaf, condemned for the crimes he committed against the kingdom. His power and his unknown Devil Fruit remain intact, making him a contained threat within the very borders of his homeland.

npcs.name: Kikunojo
npcs.base_backstory: Kikunojo is one of the Nine Red Scabbards and the younger sister of Izou, both direct disciples of Kozuki Oden. After Oden's death, she was sealed in a rift in time for twenty years alongside other retainers, emerging in Wano's present without having aged. She fought in the battle of Onigashima against Kaido's Beasts Pirates, where she lost an arm in combat against Kanjuro. With Kaido's fall and Wano's liberation, she dedicates herself to the reconstruction of the country under Momonosuke's leadership.
npcs.current_goal: Defend Wano and support the shogunate of Kozuki Momonosuke in the reconstruction of the country.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor the legacy of Kozuki Oden and see Wano prosper, free and open to the world.
npcs.mood: Serene and determined, carrying grief for her brother Izou and the loss of her arm as scars of duty fulfilled.
npcs.description: Kikunojo is a woman of elegant stature, with long dark hair and delicate features that contrast with the bearing of a seasoned warrior. She wears a kimono and carries her katana with absolute ease. Those who meet her for the first time may underestimate her due to the gentleness of her expression — a mistake few make twice.

current_state.summary_text: Kikunojo remains in Wano as one of the surviving Nine Red Scabbards, serving the shogun Momonosuke and guarding the country she swore to protect with her own body. She carries the loss of her right arm and her brother Izou as permanent marks of the battle of Onigashima.

npcs.name: Kin’emon
npcs.base_backstory: Kin'emon is a samurai from Wano and one of the most loyal retainers of the Kozuki clan, serving directly under Kozuki Oden and later his son Momonosuke. He traveled to the past alongside other allies of Oden through the power of Toki, awakening 20 years later in Wano to lead the resistance against Orochi and Kaido. After Kaido's fall in Wano, he survived gravely injured from a confrontation with Kaido himself, who split him in two, but was saved and recovered. With Wano's liberation accomplished, he remains at Momonosuke's side as his guardian.
npcs.current_goal: Protect Momonosuke and consolidate peace in Wano, freed from Kaido's yoke.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Wano open to the world and the Kozuki clan restored to its place of honor.
npcs.mood: Relieved and vigilant — carrying the weight of victory and the responsibility of what comes next.
npcs.description: Kin'emon is a man of imposing stature, with long dark hair tied back and a distinctive topknot, wearing traditional Wano samurai attire. His stern face and battle scars convey decades of loyalty and sacrifice. His presence is that of a warrior who carries the weight of a cause greater than himself, and who will not retreat from anything to honor it.

current_state.summary_text: Kin'emon remains in Wano as Momonosuke's retainer following Kaido's defeat, recovered from the near-fatal injuries sustained during the battle of Onigashima. He serves as a pillar of the new Kozuki order in the country.

npcs.name: Kawamatsu
npcs.base_backstory: Kawamatsu is a Fishman with the appearance of a Kappa who served as one of the nine Red Scabbards of Kozuki Oden. After Oden's death, he was captured and imprisoned in the dungeons of Udon for over a decade, where he survived by eating rats and fungi while secretly feeding Princess Hiyori, whom he was protecting in secret. Freed during the revolution in Wano led by Momonosuke and his allies, he actively participated in the battle of Onigashima against the forces of Kaido and the Beasts Pirates. With Kaido's fall and the opening of Wano's borders, he resumed his post as guardian of the Kozuki clan and the land of Wano.
npcs.current_goal: To serve and protect Shogun Kozuki Momonosuke and ensure the stability of the new Wano.
npcs.long_term_dream: To see the Kozuki clan restored to its full power and Wano prosperous, free of tyrants, exactly as Oden dreamed.
npcs.mood: Serene and dedicated, with a restrained pride over the victory won at Onigashima.
npcs.description: Kawamatsu is a robust Fishman of unmistakable appearance: greenish skin, a rounded head, and features that evoke the Kappa of Japanese folklore. He wears traditional Wano samurai attire and carries his sword with an upright and deliberate posture. His presence conveys an almost theatrical solemnity — he always seems on the verge of pronouncing an oath or a speech of honor.

current_state.summary_text: Kawamatsu serves as an active Red Scabbard under Shogun Momonosuke in Wano, fulfilling his role as guardian of the Kozuki clan following Kaido's defeat at Onigashima. His whereabouts and loyalty are common knowledge within Wano.

npcs.name: Chimney
npcs.base_backstory: Granddaughter of Kokoro, the mermaid conductor of the Sea Train at Water 7, Chimney grew up on the docks and rails of the city, accompanying her grandmother in the operations of the Puffing Tom. During the crisis at Enies Lobby, she infiltrated the complex's tunnels with Gonbe and guided Luffy to Robin — a decisive role that no adult was able to fulfill. She returned to Water 7 after the events and continued at Kokoro's side, learning the trade of the Sea Train.
npcs.current_goal: To assist Kokoro in the daily operations of the Sea Train and learn everything she can about the city and the sea.
npcs.long_term_dream: To grow up and work on the Sea Train like her grandmother, getting to know the world through the rails and the ocean.
npcs.mood: Energetic and curious, always ready for an adventure or to help whoever needs it.
npcs.description: A small girl with short orange hair and large, expressive eyes, always accompanied by her cat Gonbe. She radiates a contagious energy and a courage disproportionate to her size, capable of walking into dangerous situations without flinching. Those who see her for the first time underestimate her; those who know her understand that her determination is completely serious.

current_state.summary_text: Chimney lives in Water 7 with her grandmother Kokoro, assisting in the operations of the Sea Train and growing up among the docks and canals of the city.

npcs.name: Iceburg
npcs.base_backstory: An apprentice of the legendary carpenter Tom in Water 7, Iceburg learned the trade alongside Cutty Flam — later known as Franky. After Tom's execution by the World Government, Iceburg founded the Galley-La Company and rose to the position of mayor of Water 7, becoming the most influential man in the city. During the CP9 assault, he was the target of an assassination attempt by the World Government, which sought the Pluton blueprints he was safeguarding. He survived the incident and maintained his position at the head of the city and the world's largest shipbuilding company.
npcs.current_goal: To keep Water 7 prosperous and safe, leading the Galley-La Company as a world reference in shipbuilding.
npcs.long_term_dream: To honor Tom's legacy by building ships that sail every sea, keeping Water 7 as the capital of naval carpentry.
npcs.mood: Stable and resolute, carrying the weight of secrets he would rather not reveal.
npcs.description: A middle-aged man with light hair and round glasses, always in a suit, with an upright posture that projects institutional authority. The face marked by discreet scars from the assassination attempt does not diminish the serenity of his gaze. He inspires immediate trust in those who see him presiding over a meeting or walking through the city's shipyards.

current_state.summary_text: Iceburg governs Water 7 as mayor and leads the Galley-La Company, the world's largest shipbuilding company. He silently holds knowledge about the Ancient Weapons that very few in the world even suspect he possesses.

npcs.name: Killer
npcs.base_backstory: Killer grew up alongside Eustass Kid in the North Blue, becoming his trusted partner before either of them became pirates. He joined the Kid Pirates as first mate and right-hand man, building a reputation for lethal efficiency that earned him his own bounty before the Grand Line. In Wano, he was captured by Kaido and forced to eat a defective SMILE, operating in disguise as the assassin Kamazo; after Kaido's fall, he reclaimed his identity. He fought alongside Kid during the assault on Onigashima and faced Big Mom in direct combat.
npcs.current_goal: To protect Eustass Kid and consolidate the Kid Pirates' position in Elbaf.
npcs.long_term_dream: To remain at Kid's side until the captain reaches the top of the pirate world.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and composed, ready for combat.
npcs.description: Killer is a tall and muscular man, always concealed by a white helmet with pink stripes that never reveals his face, paired with a leather vest and gloves. He wields two spinning scythes at his wrists, designed to cut without the opponent perceiving the angle of the attack. He conveys a clinical calm that makes his presence more unsettling than any verbal threat.

current_state.summary_text: Killer is stationed in Elbaf as first mate of the Kid Pirates, serving as bodyguard and right-hand man to Eustass Kid. His exact whereabouts within the island are uncertain, but his loyalty to the captain remains unshakeable.

npcs.name: Kaku
npcs.devil_fruit: Ushi Ushi no Mi: Modelo Girafa
npcs.base_backstory: Kaku was recruited by CP9 at a young age and trained to master the six Rokushiki styles, becoming one of its most competent agents. He infiltrated the Galley-La shipyards in Water 7 for years, operating under the cover identity of a carpenter while gathering intelligence for the World Government. During the assault on Enies Lobby, he faced Zoro in a prolonged battle and was defeated, subsequently being captured. After serving his sentence in Impel Down, he was reintegrated into the World Government's services, this time in CP0, where he operates as an elite agent.
npcs.current_goal: To carry out missions assigned by CP0 with precision and efficiency.
npcs.long_term_dream: To serve the World Government as the most formidable and precise agent that has ever existed.
npcs.mood: Focused and operational.
npcs.description: Kaku is a tall, square-built man with short light hair and a prominently long, square nose that has become his trademark. He wears the formal black suit of CP0 with the organization's characteristic white mask when on a mission. He conveys a contained and calculated presence — every movement is economical, every word is intentional.

current_state.summary_text: Kaku actively serves as a CP0 agent, the organization to which he was transferred after his reintegration into the World Government. Current whereabouts unknown; he operates wherever the World Government assigns him.

npcs.name: Sanji
npcs.base_backstory: The third son of military commander Vinsmoke Judge, Sanji was subjected to genetic modifications before birth; his mother took a suppressant to preserve his humanity, which made him the only son without active exoskeletal hardening — until Whole Cake Island, when the latent exoskeletons manifested. Raised as a pariah by his family, he fled after an incident with the cook Zeff on the Grand Line and spent years learning the Black Leg style at the Baratie. He joined Luffy's crew as cook and combatant, participating in decisive battles at Enies Lobby, Marineford, Whole Cake Island, and Wano, where he defeated Queen of the Beasts Pirates.
npcs.current_goal: To support the crew in operations at Elbaf and keep everyone fed and combat-ready.
npcs.long_term_dream: To find the All Blue, the legendary sea where fish from every ocean gather, and cook with its ingredients.
npcs.mood: Alert and confident, ready for the next confrontation.
npcs.description: Sanji is a slender man with platinum-blonde hair that covers his left eye, a curl in his eyebrow that spirals outward, and an elegant posture. He dresses with care — dark suit, tie, leather shoes — even in the middle of battle. He radiates calculated charm with strangers and a silent intensity with enemies; the coldness he displays when cooking and fighting contrasts with the excessive warmth he shows in the presence of any woman.

current_state.summary_text: Sanji serves as cook and combatant of the Straw Hat Pirates, currently in Elbaf with the crew. He holds a high bounty and is recognized as one of the crew's offensive pillars.

npcs.name: Gecko Moria
npcs.base_backstory: Gecko Moria was a powerful pirate from an earlier era whose crew was wiped out by Kaido in the New World — a trauma that led him to reject human companions and embrace the creation of zombies as immortal substitutes. He built Thriller Bark, the largest ship in the world, as his floating fortress in the Florian Triangle, where he kidnapped travelers to steal their shadows. He was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy during the assault on Thriller Bark and was subsequently stripped of his position as a Seven Warlords of the Sea by the World Government after Marineford. He survived an assassination attempt ordered by the Government and reappeared at Hachinosu in search of Absalom, discovering that his former ally had been killed by Blackbeard — after which he vanished without a trace.
npcs.current_goal: To escape the dominion of the Blackbeard Pirates and reclaim what remains of his own crew.
npcs.long_term_dream: To become King of the Pirates without ever fighting personally — ruling through an invincible army of zombies that fights in his place.
npcs.mood: Resentful and paranoid, yet still arrogant; the near-death at Marineford and the World Government's betrayal fuel a contained rage that coexists with his chronic laziness.
npcs.description: Moria is a man of immense stature, with grayish-green skin and grotesque features that accentuate his shadowy nature. He wears dark, structured clothing with an enormous collar that frames his wide face and prominent chin. His physical presence is simultaneously ridiculous and disturbing — he laughs loudly, gestures lazily, and yet conveys the sensation of something rotten and dangerous lurking beneath the surface.

current_state.summary_text: Gecko Moria is held at Hachinosu under the power of the Blackbeard Pirates, after invading the island in search of Absalom.

npcs.name: Patty
npcs.base_backstory: Patty is one of the cooks at the Baratie, the famous floating restaurant on the Grand Line. He works under Zeff's leadership alongside Carne and other cooks, being one of the most visible faces of the establishment. He took part in the defense of the Baratie against Don Krieg and his armada, fighting with kitchen utensils as improvised weapons. He remained at the restaurant after Sanji's departure to join the Straw Hat Pirates.
npcs.current_goal: To keep the Baratie running and defend the restaurant from any customer or pirate who causes trouble.
npcs.long_term_dream: To be recognized as one of the best cooks at sea and to see the Baratie prosper.
npcs.mood: Agitated and ready to argue, but deep down dedicated to the work.
npcs.description: A stocky man with dark curly hair and a perpetually exasperated expression. He wears a cook's apron and does not hesitate to use his own pots and utensils as weapons. He conveys the energy of someone who is always one second away from exploding at a customer or colleague.

current_state.summary_text: Patty continues as cook and defender of the Baratie, operating the floating restaurant alongside Carne and the others under Zeff's command. His exact whereabouts and situation in the post-Egghead period are unknown, but nothing indicates he has left the restaurant.

npcs.name: Morgan
npcs.base_backstory: Axe-Hand Morgan was one of the first Marine Captains introduced in the series, commanding the naval base of Shell Town with an iron fist and terror. He lost his hand in combat and replaced it with a giant axe, a symbol of his brutality. He was brought down by Monkey D. Luffy and Zoro during the incident at Shell Town, arrested and sent to be judged by the Marines. He managed to escape custody using his son Helmeppo as a hostage, fleeing to the open sea and disappearing from the main narrative.
npcs.current_goal: Survive as a fugitive and maintain some relevance at sea.
npcs.long_term_dream: Reclaim power and authority over others, in whatever form the sea allows.
npcs.mood: Resentful and paranoid, carrying the humiliation of his downfall.
npcs.description: Morgan is a heavyset man with a prominent jaw and a permanently furious expression. His most striking feature is the enormous metal axe that replaces his right hand, a symbol of the violence he has embraced as his identity. He wears the Marine uniform even after deserting, as if the rank still belonged to him. He makes an immediate impression of cheap brutality — intimidating to civilians, laughable to any real combatant.

current_state.summary_text: Former Marine Captain expelled and a fugitive after being defeated at Shell Town, current whereabouts unknown. His son Helmeppo took the opposite path and now serves as a Marine officer under Vice Admiral Garp.

npcs.name: Nojiko
npcs.base_backstory: Nojiko grew up in Cocoyasi Village as Nami's adoptive sister, both taken in by Bell-mère after being found on a battlefield. Following Bell-mère's murder by Arlong and the years of Nami's enslavement to his crew, Nojiko maintained the tangerine farm and kept Nami's secret while her sister accumulated money to free the village. With Arlong's defeat at the hands of the Straw Hat Pirates, Cocoyasi was liberated and Nojiko remained on the island, tending the orchard she inherited from her adoptive mother.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain Bell-mère's tangerine farm and watch over Cocoyasi Village in Nami's absence.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve Bell-mère's legacy and see her sister Nami fulfill her dreams safely.
npcs.mood: Calm and rooted in the village's routine, with quiet pride in her sister's journey.
npcs.description: Nojiko is a young woman with blue hair and a body covered in floral tattoos she began getting as a teenager. She has a calm and steady presence, typical of someone who grew up carrying heavy responsibilities. Her gaze conveys both toughness and warmth — someone who survived pain and chose to stay.

current_state.summary_text: She lives in Cocoyasi Village tending Bell-mère's tangerine farm, far from the world's great conflicts. She follows Nami's exploits with the Straw Hat Pirates from afar.

npcs.name: Kaya
npcs.base_backstory: Heiress of the Kaya family in Syrup Village, she spent years bedridden after the death of her parents, under the care of the butler Klahadore — in truth the pirate Captain Kuro in disguise, who planned to inherit her fortune by having her killed. Usopp, who visited the mansion telling stories, and the Straw Hat Pirates foiled the plan and defeated Kuro. Inspired by Usopp's departure to sea, Kaya decided to become a doctor in order to one day heal her friend's companions. She has been actively studying medicine ever since.
npcs.current_goal: Complete her medical training to become a doctor capable of healing any wound.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a doctor and one day treat Usopp's companions when he returns from the sea.
npcs.mood: Serene and focused on her studies, carrying a quiet longing for Usopp and pride in his journey.
npcs.description: Kaya is a young woman with light blonde hair and a frail constitution, marked by the years of illness that confined her to the family mansion. She dresses with sobriety and carries a serene, determined air that contrasts with the vulnerability of her past. Those who meet her perceive someone who survived grief and betrayal and emerged with a clear purpose.

current_state.summary_text: Kaya lives in Syrup Village and is advancing in her training as a doctor, a path she chose after the departure of Usopp and the Straw Hat Pirates. Her whereabouts and routine are stable and far from the world's great conflicts.

npcs.name: Nezumi
npcs.base_backstory: A Marine Captain stationed at Naval Base 16, in the Conomi region. He received bribes from Arlong in exchange for silence about the fishman's operations, covering up his crimes and suppressing reports that might attract the attention of higher-ups. After Arlong's defeat by the Straw Hat Pirates, Nezumi attempted to confiscate Nami's treasure, but was humiliated and beaten by Roronoa Zoro. He left the island without the money and with a broken nose, vowing retaliation. His corruption scheme was indirectly exposed with Arlong's downfall.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain his position in the Marines and recover the influence and income lost after the collapse of his scheme with Arlong.
npcs.long_term_dream: Accumulate wealth and power within the Marines without ever risking his own skin.
npcs.mood: Resentful and cautious, operating in the shadows of naval bureaucracy.
npcs.description: Nezumi is a middle-aged man with sharp features and small eyes that convey a petty cunning. He wears the Marine Captain's uniform, but carries it with an air of cheap superiority. His posture and permanently calculating expression suggest someone more interested in personal profit than in justice.

current_state.summary_text: A corrupt Marine Captain, his bribery scheme with Arlong was destroyed and his current whereabouts are uncertain. He continues at some naval post, likely far from the spotlight after the humiliation at Conomi.

npcs.name: Genzo
npcs.base_backstory: Sheriff of Cocoyasi Village on Tangerine Island, Genzo was one of the leaders of the silent resistance against Arlong's eight years of dominion. He looked after Nami and the villagers while knowing of her sacrifice, carrying that burden without revealing her secret. After Arlong's defeat by the Straw Hat Pirates, the village reclaimed its freedom and Genzo resumed his role as the local protector, watching over the island Nami left behind.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the peace and safety of Tangerine Island in Nami's absence.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Nami live free and happy, knowing that the island she loved is protected.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and proud, carrying the responsibility for the island with dignity.
npcs.description: A hardened middle-aged man with a pinwheel permanently fixed to his hat — a symbol he has carried since Nami's childhood. His face is marked by years of tension and responsibility, his stern gaze softening only for those he protects. He gives an impression of unyielding stubbornness and unshakeable loyalty.

current_state.summary_text: Genzo carries out his duties as sheriff on Tangerine Island, guarding Cocoyasi and Nami's tangerine groves. He follows the journey of his former ward as navigator of the Straw Hat Pirates from afar, with barely concealed pride.

npcs.name: Helmeppo
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Captain Morgan, he grew up as a spoiled bully at the Shells Town naval base, using his father's name to terrorize civilians. After Morgan's defeat and arrest at the hands of Luffy and Zoro, Helmeppo lost all his privileges and was forced to enlist as a genuine recruit in the Marines under Garp's tutelage. He trained alongside Koby, both rising to the rank of Lieutenant and later becoming members of SWORD. During the war at Hachinosu, he took part in the rescue operation for Koby, who had been captured by Blackbeard.
npcs.current_goal: Grow stronger as a member of SWORD and support Koby in his rise within the Marines.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a true Marine, worthy of his own effort — not his father's name.
npcs.mood: Relieved after Koby's rescue, but alert to the new tensions of the post-Egghead world.
npcs.description: Helmeppo is a young man with light blonde hair, now cut shorter, and a firm military posture, very different from the affected bully of old. He wears the Marine uniform with discretion, carrying two curved daggers crossed on his back. He gives the impression of someone who worked hard to erase a shameful past and still carries that weight in his eyes.

current_state.summary_text: A Marine Lieutenant and member of SWORD, Helmeppo operates alongside Koby following the successful rescue operation at Hachinosu. His exact whereabouts in the post-Egghead period are unconfirmed, but he remains tied to SWORD's activities.

npcs.name: Zeff
npcs.base_backstory: Known as "Red-Leg Zeff", he was one of the most feared pirates on the Grand Line, captain of the Cook Pirates. During a storm in the East Blue, his ship collided with that of the young Sanji; to save the boy, Zeff lost his leg and consumed his own provisions over months of being shipwrecked. After abandoning piracy, he founded the Baratie, a floating restaurant in the East Blue, where he trained Sanji in the culinary and combat arts for years. Since Sanji departed with the Straw Hat Pirates, Zeff has run the Baratie as head chef and owner, a patriarchal figure of the establishment.
npcs.current_goal: Keep the Baratie running and continue training excellent cooks.
npcs.long_term_dream: For Sanji to find the All Blue — the sea where all the fish in the world gather.
npcs.mood: Gruff and busy, but carrying quiet pride in Sanji's exploits across the world.
npcs.description: An elderly man of robust stature, with a thick grey moustache and a wooden leg in place of his left, lost decades ago. He wears a cook's apron over simple clothes and leans on his peg leg with a posture that still betrays the former pirate. His closed expression and hard eyes convey immediate authority; those who know him understand that behind the gruffness lies an unshakeable loyalty.

current_state.summary_text: Zeff runs the Baratie in the East Blue, having left piracy behind decades ago. He follows Sanji's rise as one of the most powerful members of the Straw Hat Pirates from afar.

npcs.name: Coby
npcs.base_backstory: Koby began as a de facto slave of the pirate Alvida, too cowardly to escape, until Luffy freed him and inspired him to pursue his dream of becoming a Marine Admiral. He joined the Marines alongside Helmeppo under Garp's tutelage, rising from recruit to Captain at an uncommon pace thanks to exceptional talent and the Observation Haki he developed during the Battle of Marineford. He took part in the Battle of Marineford, where his cry momentarily halted the conflict, demonstrating extraordinary growth. Following the events of Egghead, Koby was captured by Blackbeard during the incident at Hachinosu and rescued by the operation of the alliance led by Boa Hancock and the Straw Hat Pirates; he currently serves in SWORD, the Marines' special operations unit.
npcs.current_goal: Grow stronger and advance in the Marine hierarchy while operating within SWORD, pursuing his vision of true justice.
npcs.long_term_dream: Become a Marine Admiral and build a world where genuine justice prevails.
npcs.mood: Determined and hopeful, carrying the memory of his captivity at Hachinosu as fuel to grow even further.
npcs.description: Koby is a young man of average height with short pink hair and round glasses that he kept even after hardening physically. His eyes convey a disarming sincerity that contrasts with the increasingly solid posture of a rising combatant. Those who face him realize that the gentleness on his face does not diminish the threat of his fists — and that his Observation Haki reads intentions before a blow is even telegraphed.

current_state.summary_text: Koby is a Marine Captain and member of SWORD, rescued after being captured by Blackbeard at Hachinosu. He is widely recognized as one of the most promising young talents in the Marines, with rapidly developing Haki.

npcs.name: Mihawk
npcs.base_backstory: Dracule Mihawk earned the title of Greatest Swordsman in the World on his own merit, without a Devil Fruit, and was recognized as such even before the current pirate era began. He rivaled Shanks for years in a period when their duels shook the Grand Line; after Shanks lost his arm, the clashes ceased. He served as one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea until the institution was dissolved by the Marines, becoming a pirate without government protection. After Wano, he joined Buggy and Crocodile in forming the Cross Guild, an organization that began placing bounties on the heads of Marines.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the position of Greatest Swordsman in the World and await an opponent worthy of challenging him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Remain at the absolute pinnacle of the sword while watching to see if anyone in the world has the potential to surpass him.
npcs.mood: Bored and distant, with a veiled interest in Zoro's progress.
npcs.description: A tall man of athletic build, with disheveled black hair and yellow hawk-pupil eyes — the trait that earned him his epithet. He wears a long black cloak and carries Yoru, one of the Twelve Supreme Grade Swords, across his back. His presence is oppressive without any visible effort; those who face him feel the weight of the title before the first strike is even thrown.

current_state.summary_text: Mihawk operates as one of the pillars of the Cross Guild from his fortress on Kuraigana, holding the title of Greatest Swordsman in the World and ranking among the most recognized threats on the seas.

npcs.name: Hongo
npcs.base_backstory: Hongo is a Marine doctor who appeared as a figure of medical authority within the organization. His role is tied to science and medicine applied to the Marines' military apparatus. Few biographical details have been revealed in canon beyond his position and specialization.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out his medical and scientific duties in service of the Marines.
npcs.long_term_dream: Advance medicine and science in service of world order.
npcs.mood: Neutral and focused.
npcs.description: Hongo is a middle-aged man with an austere bearing and an analytical gaze, typical of someone who has spent decades in clinical and military environments. He conveys professional coldness and quiet competence, without ostentation.

current_state.summary_text: A Marine doctor of specialized rank, his current whereabouts and activities are poorly documented beyond his institutional ties to the organization.

npcs.name: Fullbody
npcs.base_backstory: Fullbody served as a Marine lieutenant in the East Blue, where he attempted to arrest Luffy at the Baratie restaurant and was humiliated by Sanji. He later reappeared in Impel Down, where he encountered his old rival Jango, now also a Marine, and the two became inseparable partners and friends. The two operate together on minor Marine missions, far from the great stages of war.
npcs.current_goal: Fulfill his Marine duties alongside Jango without getting into trouble that is too serious.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as a respectable Marine, despite his track record of failures.
npcs.mood: Relaxed and routine-driven, with no great immediate ambitions.
npcs.description: Fullbody is a muscular man with short dark hair, always wearing the Marine uniform with an imposing posture that contrasts with his tendency to back down before serious opponents. He gives the impression of a mediocre officer who compensates for a lack of real talent with attitude and volume of voice.

current_state.summary_text: Fullbody serves as a Marine lieutenant in secondary roles, operating as a duo with Jango on low-profile missions far from the conflicts that have reshaped the world.

npcs.name: Jango
npcs.base_backstory: Jango was the first mate and hypnotist of the Krieg Pirates, operating as Fullbody's right-hand man before both defected to the Marines. After being recruited as a Marine Seaman, he served alongside Fullbody on minor missions. He became a fervent admirer of Hina, the central motivation for his continued service in the Marines.
npcs.current_goal: Serve the Marines and remain close to Hina.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be recognized as a great Marine and earn Hina's admiration.
npcs.mood: Enthusiastic and slightly frantic.
npcs.description: Jango is a tall, thin man with eccentric features, always wearing round dark glasses and a wide-brimmed hat. He carries a hypnosis pendulum that he swings with exaggerated gestures. He gives the impression of a slightly menacing clown — ridiculous at first glance, but capable of putting an entire crowd to sleep in seconds.

current_state.summary_text: Jango serves as a Marine Seaman, posted alongside Fullbody on patrol and guard duties. His exact whereabouts post-Egghead are unknown, but he remains on active duty in the Marines.

npcs.name: Yosaku
npcs.base_backstory: Yosaku and his partner Johnny worked as bounty hunters in the East Blue, surviving on sporadic jobs. The two became friends with Zoro before he joined the Straw Hat Pirates, even briefly accompanying the group during the Arlong Park arc. After helping the crew reach Arlong's territory, Yosaku and Johnny parted ways with the Straw Hats and resumed their wandering life in the East Blue.
npcs.current_goal: Keep hunting bounties across the East Blue alongside Johnny.
npcs.long_term_dream: Make a name as a respected bounty hunter on the seas.
npcs.mood: Lively and carefree, living off small hunts in the East Blue while the world around him changes radically.
npcs.description: Yosaku is a young man of average build, with the disheveled appearance typical of a bounty hunter without a fixed base. He gives the impression of someone genuinely enthusiastic and without much malice, more an adventure companion than a true warrior. Alongside Johnny, he forms a comedic duo that inspires more sympathy than respect.

current_state.summary_text: Yosaku operates as a bounty hunter in the East Blue alongside Johnny, oblivious to the great upheavals of the New World. His exact whereabouts are unknown, but he is presumed to be following his usual routine of minor hunts on the sea where it all began.

npcs.name: Gaimon
npcs.base_backstory: Gaimon was an ordinary pirate who, during an expedition in search of treasure on the Island of Rare Animals, became trapped inside a treasure chest after falling from a tree. His companions abandoned him, and he spent decades wedged in the chest, unable to get out. During that time, he became the protector of the island's hybrid animals. When Luffy revealed that the chests at the top of the hill were empty, Gaimon chose to remain on the island to continue caring for the animals, finding peace in that choice.
npcs.current_goal: Protect the island's hybrid animals from any intruder.
npcs.long_term_dream: Live in peace with the island's animals, guarding the place that became his home.
npcs.mood: Calm and resolute, at peace with his life on the island.
npcs.description: A middle-aged man with his body permanently wedged inside a wooden chest, from which only his head, arms, and legs protrude. He carries a rifle and wears what remains of pirate clothing. The strangeness of his appearance contrasts with the genuinely gentle look he reserves for the animals he protects.

current_state.summary_text: Gaimon lives on the Island of Rare Animals, still trapped in the same chest from decades ago, guarding the hybrid animals that inhabit the place. His whereabouts and condition are stable and unchanged.

npcs.name: Rockstar
npcs.base_backstory: Rockstar is a member of the Red Hair Pirates with enough of a reputation to have had a bounty of 94 million berries before joining the crew. He was sent by Shanks as a messenger to Emperor Whitebeard aboard the Moby Dick, carrying a letter — a mission that ended in humiliation when Whitebeard refused to read the letter of a man he did not know. He returned without completing the errand and later accompanied Shanks to the personal meeting with Whitebeard.
npcs.current_goal: Serve Shanks and the Red Hair crew wherever needed.
npcs.long_term_dream: Prove his worth as one of the great pirates of the seas, living up to the fame his name carries.
npcs.mood: Confident, but aware of his place in the hierarchy of the powerful.
npcs.description: Rockstar is a young man of robust appearance, with dark hair and a habitually arrogant expression. He carries himself with the posture of someone who believes his bounty speaks for itself — which makes him simultaneously imposing to strangers and predictable to those who already know him. The presence of any truly powerful figure is enough to deflate that air of grandeur.

current_state.summary_text: An active member of the Red Hair Pirates, Rockstar sails with Shanks' crew. Exact whereabouts unknown, following the crew's movements in the New World.

npcs.name: Tsuru
npcs.base_backstory: Tsuru is one of the oldest and most experienced officers in the Marines, having served side by side with Sengoku and Garp for decades. She possesses the Woshu Woshu no Mi, which allows her to "wash and wring" people like clothes, temporarily neutralizing their wickedness. She was instrumental in intelligence operations and pirate containment throughout the Great Pirate Era. Despite her advanced age, she retains the rank of active Vice Admiral.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the Marines' stability during the power vacuum left by the rise of the Four Emperors and the events of Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve world order and see the Marines endure as a balancing force, even if that requires unpopular decisions.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and resigned — she observes the growing chaos of the New World with the sobriety of someone who has already survived worse eras.
npcs.description: An elderly woman of average height, with short white hair and features marked by decades of service. She wears the Vice Admiral's coat with the same nonchalance of someone who no longer needs to prove anything to anyone. Her presence conveys silent authority — the kind that makes veterans straighten their posture without her needing to raise her voice.

current_state.summary_text: A veteran Marine Vice Admiral, Tsuru remains on active duty despite her advanced age, being one of the few figures with institutional memory stretching back to the era of Rocks. Her exact whereabouts are Marine Headquarters, where her strategic counsel is still valued.

npcs.name: Ryokugyu
npcs.base_backstory: Ryokugyu is one of the two Admirals promoted after Aokiji's departure and Akainu's retirement to the position of Fleet Admiral. His identity remained unknown for years, revealed only as a man who claims not to have eaten for three years thanks to his Devil Fruit. After Kaido's fall in Wano, he marched alone into the country to capture or eliminate the Straw Hat Pirates and their allies, demonstrating devastating power by draining the life from enemies with forests generated from his own body. He was stopped by Shanks' Haki, which forced him to retreat before confronting Luffy.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out Fleet Admiral Sakazuki's orders and eliminate threats to the World Government, especially the Straw Hat Pirates and Luffy's allies.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve Sakazuki's vision of absolute justice and ensure the Marines' supremacy over the seas.
npcs.mood: Confident and mildly bored, awaiting orders or an opportunity to act.
npcs.description: A tall, muscular man with a youthful appearance, dark hair, and attractive features that contrast with the brutality of his actions. He wears the white Admiral's coat draped over his shoulders in the Marines' fashion. He radiates a predatory calm — he seems relaxed until the moment he unleashes entire forests from his own body to drain the life from his opponents.

current_state.summary_text: Active Admiral of the Marines, he retreated from Wano after sensing Shanks' Conqueror's Haki. He operates from headquarters awaiting new orders from Sakazuki in the post-Egghead scenario.

npcs.name: Fujitora
npcs.base_backstory: Issho, known as Fujitora, is a blind swordsman who deliberately destroyed his own eyesight so as not to have to face the evils of the world. Recruited directly by Sakazuki after the timeskip to fill the Admiral vacancies left by Aokiji and Akainu, he made his public debut during the events of Dressrosa, where he pursued the Straw Hat Pirates and the Pirate Alliance. In Dressrosa, he made the controversial decision not to report the Marines' defeat to Headquarters, instead accumulating the public apologies owed to the people — an act that generated direct conflict with Sakazuki. After the end of the Dressrosa crisis, he was formally banned from returning to Marineford until he captured Luffy or Law.
npcs.current_goal: Fulfill his duties as Admiral while internally pushing for a more just Marines, waiting for the right moment to settle the unresolved matters since Dressrosa.
npcs.long_term_dream: See a world where the Marines truly protect the weak, without bowing to the political convenience of the Celestial Dragons.
npcs.mood: Resigned and watchful — he carries the weight of the contradictions of the system he serves, but does not abandon his post.
npcs.description: A robust middle-aged man, completely blind, with eyes always closed and scarred. He wears the standard Admiral's suit over a striped kimono and carries a cane that conceals a katana. His relaxed posture and easy smile contrast with the gravity that emanates from him when he draws his sword — those who underestimate him for his blindness rarely get a chance to make that mistake twice.

current_state.summary_text: Active Admiral of the Marines, Fujitora operates under Sakazuki's ban from returning to Marineford without capturing Monkey D. Luffy or Trafalgar Law; his exact operational whereabouts are uncertain, but he remains in service as one of the World Government's military pillars.

npcs.name: Hina
npcs.base_backstory: Hina grew up alongside Smoker at the Naval Academy, being one of the few people with whom he maintains genuine bonds of friendship. She rose through the upper ranks of the Marines through strength and coldness in combat, earning a reputation for capturing fugitives with her Ori Ori no Mi, which creates iron cages around targets. She became known in the Grand Line after pursuing the Straw Hat Pirates in Alabasta, where she was defeated by Sanji. She continues in active service as a Rear Admiral, operating on capture and patrol missions.
npcs.current_goal: Carry out capture missions for the Marines and remain among the high command's trusted officers.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve the Marines with excellence and see world order maintained — without romanticism, out of duty.
npcs.mood: Professional and composed, with latent irritation when contradicted.
npcs.description: Hina is a tall, slender woman with long black hair, always seen in her Marines uniform with a cape. She has striking features and a permanently bored gaze that rarely conceals disdain. Her presence conveys authority effortlessly — those who know her understand that the coldness is not a pose.

current_state.summary_text: Rear Admiral of the Marines in active service, Hina carries out capture missions and maintains a close bond with Vice Admiral Smoker. Her exact whereabouts are unknown, but she remains in regular duties within the armed branch of the World Government.

npcs.name: Prince Grus
npcs.base_backstory: Grus is a Commodore of the Marines and a secret member of SWORD, the autonomous special operations unit that operates outside the official chain of command. He was present at Hachinosu during the operation to rescue Commodore Koby, who was being held prisoner by the Blackbeard Pirates. On that mission, he fought alongside Helmeppo and other SWORD members, facing Blackbeard's generals. His Devil Fruit, the Nendo Nendo no Mi, allows him to create and manipulate clay at will, shaping constructs and figures as weapons or shields.
npcs.current_goal: Consolidate SWORD's position after the operation at Hachinosu and ensure Koby's safety.
