npcs.long_term_dream: Protect the world from within the Marines, operating in the shadows where official rules cannot reach.
npcs.mood: Vigilant and composed, carrying the weight of a mission the World Government does not acknowledge.
npcs.description: Grus is a middle-aged man of average height, with an austere appearance and a permanently serious expression. He wears the Commodore's Marines uniform with rigor, without unnecessary adornments. His presence conveys calculated discipline — not the brutality of a brute fighter, but the precision of someone who plans every move before executing it.

current_state.summary_text: Commodore and SWORD member, Grus took part in the operation at Hachinosu to rescue Koby from the Blackbeard Pirates. His exact whereabouts after the island's events remain uncertain, but he continues active within SWORD's clandestine structure.

npcs.name: Mars
npcs.devil_fruit: Uo Uo no Mi, Model: Uta
npcs.base_backstory: Mars is one of the Five Elders, the supreme rulers of the World Government below Imu. Like the other Gorosei, he possesses a monstrous transformation form tied to a mythical Devil Fruit. During the events of Egghead, he personally descended to the island to eliminate Vegapunk and suppress any leak of sensitive information to the world. He actively participated in the siege and destruction of Egghead alongside the other Gorosei.
npcs.current_goal: Suppress all information leaked by Vegapunk and eliminate threats to the World Government's sovereignty.
npcs.long_term_dream: Maintain the World Government's absolute order and protect the secrets of the Void Century under Imu's command.
npcs.mood: Cold and determined, with no apparent emotional disturbance.
npcs.description: Mars is a man of imposing elderly appearance, with white hair and severe features that radiate absolute authority. In his transformed form, he takes the shape of a winged creature of monstrous proportions. His presence drains the air of any lightness — those who face him feel the weight of a force that does not negotiate.

current_state.summary_text: Mars operates as one of the five pillars of the World Government's supreme power, having personally participated in the siege of Egghead. His exact whereabouts after the island's events are unknown, but his loyalty to Imu and his role as supreme enforcer remain intact.

npcs.name: Kujaku
npcs.base_backstory: Granddaughter of the legendary Vice Admiral Tsuru and a descendant of the Figarland family, Kujaku joined the Marines and rose to the rank of Vice Admiral. She ate the Muchi Muchi no Mi, which allows her to transform any object into a whip. Recruited into SWORD — Koby's black-ops unit — she formally renounces the Marines' protection in order to operate without institutional restrictions. She took part in the operations at Hachinosu during the rescue of Koby, facing Blackbeard's pirates.
npcs.current_goal: Operate as an active SWORD member, acting outside the Marines' official chain of command to pursue objectives the institution cannot openly sanction.
npcs.long_term_dream: Honor her family's legacy and prove that it is possible to do the right thing even within a corrupted system.
npcs.mood: Confident and irreverent, with constant combative energy.
npcs.description: Kujaku is a young Vice Admiral with pink hair and an always animated expression, who carries a whip as her main weapon — transformed and amplified by her Devil Fruit. Her presence is at once easygoing and intimidating: she smiles easily, but the smile never erases the sharp competence in her eyes. Granddaughter of Tsuru and a descendant of the Figarland family, she carries a heavy pedigree that she seems to deliberately ignore.

current_state.summary_text: Vice Admiral and active SWORD member, Kujaku operates outside the Marines' official structure on unsanctioned missions. Her exact whereabouts are unknown to the public, kept secret by the group's clandestine nature.

npcs.name: Ju Peter
npcs.devil_fruit: Sandworm (Nushi no Mi, Model: Sandworm — unnamed, manifests as a colossal burrowing worm)
npcs.base_backstory: Ju Peter is one of the Five Elders, the World Government's supreme council that acts as an intermediary between Imu and the world. He participated in the attack on Egghead alongside the other Elders, personally descending to the island to eliminate Vegapunk and the Straw Hat Pirates. He revealed himself capable of transforming into a colossal sandworm-like creature, using that form to attack allies and enemies indiscriminately during the siege.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the World Government's order and eradicate any threat to Imu's authority.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the Void Century and the World Government's eternal dominion over the world.
npcs.mood: Cold and determined; the resistance at Egghead did not shake his composure.
npcs.description: Ju Peter is a man of elderly appearance, with severe features and a rigid posture that convey absolute authority. He wears the Elders' formal attire and carries with him an oppressive presence that precedes any word. In his transformed form, he becomes a creature of catastrophic proportions, capable of swallowing entire ships.

current_state.summary_text: Ju Peter is one of the Five Elders and was present at the siege of Egghead, where he revealed his monstrous colossal creature form. He operates from Mary Geoise as one of the pillars of the World Government's supreme power.

npcs.name: Warcury
npcs.base_backstory: Warcury is one of the five Gorosei, the supreme civilian rulers of the World Government, who have wielded their power for decades from the shadows of Mary Geoise. He revealed himself capable of transforming into a colossal bestial creature with fangs and an impenetrable hide, demonstrating combat power far beyond what would be expected of an elder. At Egghead, he personally descended to the island alongside the other Gorosei to eliminate Vegapunk and suppress the information that threatened the World Government, directly confronting the Straw Hat Pirates.
npcs.current_goal: Suppress any threat to the World Government's authority and eliminate all who hold forbidden knowledge about the Blank Century.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the World Government's absolute order and Imu's supremacy over the world forever.
npcs.mood: Cold and determined — he treats every obstacle as a matter to be eradicated, without urgency and without hesitation.
npcs.description: An elder of robust and severe appearance, with white hair and long mustaches that give him the air of a human boar. He wears the traditional Gorosei robes and carries in his features a calm that intimidates more than any shout. His bestial form is a colossal creature of fangs and impenetrable hide, evoking a beast-god of ancient ages.

current_state.summary_text: Warcury is one of the Five Elders, supreme rulers of the World Government, with a bestial transformation power confirmed in direct combat at Egghead. He remains in a position of supreme command, serving Imu's will.

npcs.name: Nusjuro
npcs.devil_fruit: Uma Fruta do Diabo do tipo Zoan Mítico — forma de uma entidade sobrenatural japonesa (Bakotsu no Mi, modelo Gyuki, conforme indicado pelo canon visual)
npcs.base_backstory: Nusjuro is one of the Five Elders, the supreme council that governs the World Government in the name of Imu. He wields one of the most feared swords in the world and displayed Zoan transformation ability during the events of Egghead, where the Five Elders personally descended to the island to eliminate Vegapunk and contain the information leak. He participated in the Egghead massacre alongside the other Elders, demonstrating catastrophic-level power.
npcs.current_goal: Eliminate threats to the World Government's order and protect the secrets of the Void Century.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the absolute order of the World Government and Imu's dominion over the world.
npcs.mood: Serene and predatory — the calm of one who has never doubted his own supremacy.
npcs.description: Nusjuro is a man of elderly appearance, imposing stature, and sharp features that convey centuries of unshakeable authority. He wears the formal robes of the Five Elders and carries a long-bladed sword, his presence radiating the silent menace of something that need not hurry to kill. His Zoan transformation reveals a monstrous form of supernatural origin, making him even more terrifying in combat.

current_state.summary_text: Nusjuro serves as one of the five supreme rulers of the World Government, based in Mary Geoise; following the events of Egghead, where he fought personally, he resumes his functions of control and suppression of threats to the established order.

npcs.name: Figarland Garling
npcs.base_backstory: Figarland Garling is the Supreme Commander of the Holy Knights, the military elite that protects Mary Geoise and serves the World Government directly. In the past, he was the champion of God Valley, the event that marked the massacre of Rocks D. Xebec and the destruction of the island — an episode still shrouded in official silence. His family belongs to the Figarland lineage, connected to the Celestial Dragons, which grants him a position of the highest prestige within the World Government hierarchy. He commands the Holy Knights with absolute authority and is considered one of the most powerful men in the world.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the supreme order of Mary Geoise and eliminate any threat to the World Government's authority.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the absolute domination of the World Government over the world, keeping the Holy Knights as an uncontested force.
npcs.mood: Calculating and distant, exercising power with the naturalness of one who has never seen it challenged.
aliases: Supreme Commander of the Holy Knights
npcs.description: Garling is a middle-aged man of imposing stature, light hair, and a perpetually severe expression, wearing the ceremonial armor of the Holy Knights. His physical presence intimidates before any word is spoken — he carries the aura of someone who has already won every battle that mattered. His cold eyes and rigid bearing convey not impulsive cruelty, but the absolute conviction of one who believes himself to be the executive arm of the world's order.

current_state.summary_text: He commands the Holy Knights in Mary Geoise, positioned as one of the World Government's greatest military forces during the period of escalating tensions following the events of Egghead. His connection to the Figarland lineage and his role in the God Valley incident remain among the world's most closely guarded secrets.

npcs.name: Gol D. Roger
npcs.base_backstory: Born in Loguetown, East Blue, Gol D. Roger rose from an ordinary pirate to claim the title of King of the Pirates, being the only one to complete the Grand Line and reach Laugh Tale. He formed the Roger Pirates crew and obtained the One Piece, discovering the True History of the world inscribed on the Poneglyphs. Terminally ill, he turned himself in voluntarily to the Marines and was publicly executed in Loguetown approximately 24 years ago, igniting the Great Pirate Era with his final words.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased
npcs.long_term_dream: Find the One Piece, uncover the True History of the world, and leave everything for whoever came after.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased
npcs.description: A man of imposing stature, wavy black hair, and a distinctive mustache. He carried a presence that silenced crowds and inflamed hearts at the same time. Even shackled on the scaffold, he radiated the authority of someone who was never truly defeated.

current_state.summary_text: Gol D. Roger is dead, publicly executed in Loguetown. His legend endures as the catalyst of the Great Pirate Era, and the whereabouts of the One Piece he discovered remain the greatest mystery of the seas.

npcs.name: Scopper Gaban
npcs.base_backstory: Scopper Gaban was one of Gol D. Roger's closest officers, serving as a right-hand man alongside Rayleigh throughout the entire Golden Age of piracy. He participated in the final voyage to Laugh Tale, being one of the few to know the truth of the world and the meaning of the One Piece. After the Roger Pirates disbanded, he disappeared from public life. His whereabouts remained unknown for decades, until he was spotted on Lodestar Island during the Egghead saga.
npcs.current_goal: Wait and observe the new generation's advance toward Laugh Tale, ready to act when the moment comes.
npcs.long_term_dream: See Roger's will fulfilled by the next generation — that someone reaches the end of the Grand Line and frees the world.
npcs.mood: Calm and watchful; he carries the weight of a secret that few in the world know.
npcs.description: A robust middle-aged man with dark hair and an imposing physical presence that betrays decades of combat. He wields twin axes as his signature weapons. He conveys the serenity of one who no longer has anything to prove — only something to protect.

current_state.summary_text: One of the last living members of the Roger Pirates, he is on Lodestar Island — the last island before Laugh Tale — and his exact whereabouts and intentions are known only to very few.

npcs.name: Kozuki Oden
npcs.base_backstory: Son of Wano's Shogun Kozuki Sukiyaki, Oden was exiled while still young due to his indomitable nature and acts that defied the country's order. He became the daimyo of Kuri after subduing the most violent region of Wano, and later sailed with the Whitebeard Pirates and subsequently with the Roger Pirates, reaching Laugh Tale and learning the truth of the world. Upon returning to Wano, he found the country dominated by Orochi and Kaido; he was captured after an ambush and publicly executed by being boiled in oil in Wano, in the year 1502, at the age of 39.
npcs.current_goal: N/A — deceased.
npcs.long_term_dream: Open Wano's borders to the world and lead his people into an era of freedom.
npcs.mood: N/A — deceased.
npcs.description: Oden was a man of imposing stature and powerful build, with long dark hair and a presence that dominated any environment. He carried two great sabers — Ame no Habakiri and Enma — and habitually wore his kimono open to the waist, displaying the scars of a lifetime of battles. His expression alternated between a broad, carefree smile and a look of absolute determination that made adversaries step back.

current_state.summary_text: Kozuki Oden is dead, executed by Shogun Orochi and Kaido in Wano decades before the present. His legacy, however, drives the Nine Red Scabbards and sustains the restoration of the Kozuki clan under Momonosuke.

npcs.name: Toki
npcs.base_backstory: Born over eight hundred years ago, Toki repeatedly used the power of the Toki Toki no Mi to leap into the future, unable to return to the past. She arrived in the era of Kozuki Oden already ill, was rescued by him, and became his wife and the mother of Momonosuke and Hiyori. When Orochi and Kaido destroyed Oden Castle, Toki sent Momonosuke and four of the Nine Red Scabbards twenty years forward in time with a prophecy about the opening of Wano's borders. She died in the fire of Oden Castle in Wano, refusing to use her fruit to escape.
npcs.current_goal: None — she died fulfilling her purpose.
npcs.long_term_dream: Ensure that Oden's children lived to open Wano's borders and fulfill the legacy of the Kozuki clan.
npcs.mood: Absolute peace.
npcs.description: Toki was a woman of singular beauty, with long dark hair and serene features that conveyed a calm beyond time — literally, as she was born centuries before her own husband. She dressed with the refinement of Wano's nobility and maintained an unshakeable composure even in the face of death. Those who saw her for the last time, standing in flames at Oden Castle, described an almost supernatural figure, smiling as she pronounced her prophecy.

current_state.summary_text: Toki died in the fire of Oden Castle twenty years ago, after sending Momonosuke and allies into the future with her prophecy. Her legacy persists in Momonosuke's reign in Wano, whose borders have finally been opened.

npcs.name: Buena Festa
npcs.devil_fruit: Memo Memo no Mi (Fes Fes no Mi — fruta de festival/festejos, canônica)
npcs.base_backstory: Buena Festa, known as "The Master", is a renowned gambler and manipulator who operates in the shadows of the criminal world. He orchestrated the Ship Racing Championship at Donald Moderate's Festival, using the event as bait to lure powerful pirates and unleash a greater chaos. His objective was to free Admiral Byrnndi World from Impel Down, manipulating all parties involved to achieve his ends. After the plan's failure with World's defeat, his whereabouts became unknown.
npcs.current_goal: Survive and plot new schemes from the shadows, away from the eyes of the Marines and the pirates who know him.
npcs.long_term_dream: Move the world like a great game board, proving that any situation can be manipulated by one who knows the right pieces.
npcs.mood: Calculating and composed — he absorbed the failure of the plan with World and awaits the next opportunity.
npcs.description: Buena Festa is a middle-aged man of elegant and eccentric appearance, with long hair and an extravagant party outfit that contrasts with his cold, analytical gaze. He gives the impression of someone who is never genuinely present in the room — he is always three moves ahead, observing. His presence is at once alluring and unsettling, like that of a host who knows exactly what is going to happen to each guest.

current_state.summary_text: Whereabouts unknown following the collapse of the plan to free Byrnndi World from Impel Down. He operates in the shadows as an independent agent, with no traceable faction.

npcs.name: Morgans
npcs.base_backstory: Morgans is the self-proclaimed "King of News" and president of the World Economic Journal, the world's largest media conglomerate. He uses his position to publish headlines that shake governments, markets, and the balance of power among the great powers. During the events of Dressrosa and Big Mom, he demonstrated the ability to negotiate and manipulate information according to his own sense of "good news". Following Egghead, he published Vegapunk's revelations to the world, directly challenging the World Government and fleeing from agents who attempted to suppress the broadcast.
npcs.current_goal: Publish the world's biggest news without being captured or silenced by the World Government.
npcs.long_term_dream: Be the greatest and most influential journalist in history, ensuring that no great truth remains buried.
npcs.mood: Euphoric and defiant — the Egghead revelations are the biggest headline of his career.
npcs.description: Morgans is a man of striking avian appearance: tall, covered in white feathers, with a prominent beak and expressive eyes that light up at any scandal. He dresses in an elegant suit and top hat, projecting the image of a refined magnate. His presence is imposing and boisterous; he dominates any room with genuine enthusiasm and a mental camera always running, evaluating everything by its journalistic weight.

current_state.summary_text: President of the World Economic Journal, Morgans published Vegapunk's broadcast and fled from the World Government's pursuit, operating from an unknown location. He continues to circulate the world's biggest news from some point beyond the immediate reach of the authorities.

npcs.name: Weevil
npcs.base_backstory: Edward Weevil presents himself to the world as the illegitimate son of Edward Newgate, Whitebeard, although the truthfulness of that claim is widely disputed. Raised under the manipulative tutelage of his mother, Miss Bakkin, he began hunting the allies and former crewmates of Whitebeard in search of the inheritance she claims is owed to him. His brute strength is immense, having single-handedly destroyed dozens of pirate groups affiliated with Whitebeard. He was recruited as one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea before the abolition of the system, and was later captured by the Marines during the war that followed the decline of the Yonko.
npcs.current_goal: Await or escape captivity; his mother Miss Bakkin remains free and may be plotting his release.
npcs.long_term_dream: Claim Whitebeard's inheritance and prove to the world that he is the legitimate son of the legendary pirate.
npcs.mood: Confused and restrained — without his mother's voice to guide him, he acts on pure instinct.
npcs.description: Weevil is a man of colossal stature and absurd musculature, his body covered in scars and improvised bandages that he applies carelessly himself. His broad, somewhat vacant face conveys an animal strength without conscious malice — he looks like an enormous child who learned only to destroy. His mere presence intimidates by sheer size, but those who see him up close notice that his eyes follow instructions, not intentions of his own.

current_state.summary_text: Weevil is imprisoned by the Marines, captured by Vice Admiral Ryokugyu prior to the events of Egghead. His exact whereabouts within the prison system are uncertain, but he is not moving freely.

npcs.name: Bakkin
npcs.base_backstory: Bakkin, whose full name is Buckingham Stussy, was a member of the Rocks Pirates, the most feared crew of the era before Roger, led by Rocks D. Xebec. She is the biological mother of Edward Weevil, a son she claims to have had with Edward Newgate, Whitebeard, although the truthfulness of that paternity is widely questioned. After the dissolution of the Rocks, she lived on the fringes of the world, using Weevil as an instrument to claim Whitebeard's inheritance and territories. With Weevil's capture by the Marines, she began operating behind the scenes in search of her son and the spoils she considers her right.
npcs.current_goal: Rescue Edward Weevil from Marine custody and claim Whitebeard's inheritance.
npcs.long_term_dream: Seize all the wealth and territories that Edward Newgate left behind, using Weevil as a tool to do so.
npcs.mood: Calculating and furious over Weevil's capture, seeking an opening to act.
aliases: Self-Proclaimed Strongest Glutton in the New World
npcs.description: Bakkin is an elderly woman of flamboyant appearance and flashy attire that evokes a deliberately exaggerated sensuality. Her face, heavy with makeup, and her arrogant posture convey the image of someone who once held real power and still believes she does. Her presence carries the shadow of the Rocks — a name few dare to speak — which lends her an aura of danger that is difficult to ignore.

current_state.summary_text: Bakkin remains at large in the New World, her exact whereabouts unknown, following the capture of her son Weevil by Admiral Fujitora and Ryokugyu. She maneuvers behind the scenes in search of means to recover him and advance on Whitebeard's inheritance.

npcs.name: Carne
npcs.base_backstory: Carne is one of the cooks at the floating restaurant Baratie, working alongside Patty as the establishment's cooking duo. He was present during Don Krieg's attack on the Baratie and the passage of Luffy and Sanji through the restaurant. He remained at the Baratie after those events, continuing his trade under Zeff's supervision.
npcs.current_goal: Cook and keep the Baratie running day to day.
npcs.long_term_dream: Serve good food at the best restaurant on the sea.
npcs.mood: Routine, slightly anxious when business picks up.
npcs.description: A man of average height and stocky build, with his cook's apron always stained from work. Alongside Patty, he forms the most visible cooking duo at the Baratie — the two of them together give the impression of a loud, proud, and slightly chaotic kitchen.

current_state.summary_text: Carne works at the Baratie as a cook, Patty's kitchen partner. His whereabouts and routine remain the same as always: inside the floating restaurant's kitchen.

npcs.name: Doll
npcs.base_backstory: Doll is a Vice Admiral of the Marines who rose to the upper ranks of the institution in the post-timeskip period. She served as Koby's superior during the time he was stationed at G-14, exercising direct authority over the base. She demonstrated a strict and demanding posture toward subordinates, though she harbors a veiled affection for Koby. The history of her battles and promotions prior to the current era remains without recorded details.
npcs.current_goal: Maintain the operational order of G-14 and oversee the base's activities in the context of the geopolitical chaos following Egghead.
npcs.long_term_dream: Preserve the integrity of the Marines as a genuine force of justice, even in the face of pressure from the World Government.
npcs.mood: Tense and vigilant, with the global power structure in partial collapse following the events of Egghead.
npcs.description: Doll is a woman of imposing stature and impeccable military bearing, with short hair and a habitually severe expression. She wears the white Vice Admiral's coat over the standard Marine uniform. She conveys immediate authority to anyone who enters her field of vision — the kind of officer who does not need to raise her voice to be obeyed.

current_state.summary_text: Vice Admiral stationed at G-14, where she exercises direct command over the base. She maintains a posture of strict discipline while monitoring the developments of the geopolitical crisis triggered by the events of Egghead.

npcs.name: Johnny
npcs.base_backstory: Johnny and Yosaku operate as a bounty hunter duo in the East Blue, having crossed paths with Zoro before he joined the Straw Hat Pirates. The two briefly accompanied the crew on the route to the Grand Line, taking part in the arrival at the Baratie and the battle against Arlong at Cocoyasi Village. After those events, they returned to the bounty hunter life in the East Blue.
npcs.current_goal: Continue the bounty hunter life in the East Blue alongside Yosaku.
npcs.long_term_dream: Make a name as a bounty hunter and live adventures alongside Yosaku.
npcs.mood: Lively and confident day to day, ready to flee when things get serious.
npcs.description: Johnny is a young man of average height wearing a bandana and simple traveler's clothes. He carries a sword and wears a perpetually enthusiastic expression. The impression he gives is of someone brave in speech and hesitant in practice — his courage exists, but has very visible limits.

current_state.summary_text: Johnny operates as a bounty hunter in the East Blue as a duo with Yosaku. His exact whereabouts are unknown, but he continues the hunting routine he has always had.

factions.name: Uzuki Family
factions.description: A noble family of Wano that historically governed the Udon region as a daimyo house under the authority of the Kozuki Family. Their power was suppressed during the Orochi-Kaido regime.

current_state.summary_text: The Uzuki Family lost dominion over Udon with the takeover of Wano by Kurozumi Orochi and Kaido. Since Kaido's fall in Wano, the country is reclaiming its sovereignty, but the family's restored status as a daimyo house is not confirmed by canon.

factions.name: Amatsuki Family
factions.description: A noble family of Wano historically aligned with the Kozuki Family, holding the title of daimyo and responsible for the administrative oversight of regions of the country under that order.

current_state.summary_text: The Amatsuki Family was a daimyo house allied with the Kozuki Family in Wano. Since Kaido's fall in Wano, the country is reclaiming its sovereignty, but the family's current status as an active institution is not confirmed by canon.

factions.name: Fugetsu Family
factions.description: A samurai family of Wano originating from the Kibi region, historically holding the title of daimyo and aligned with the Kozuki Family, with a role in regional governance and defense under that order.

current_state.summary_text: The Fugetsu Family was a daimyo house from the Kibi region, allied with the Kozuki Family in Wano. Since Kaido's fall in Wano, the country is reclaiming its sovereignty, but the family's current status as an active institution is not confirmed by canon.

factions.name: Foxy Pirates
factions.description: Pirate crew specialized in deception tactics and in conducting the Davy Back Fight, a competition through which they contest members and flags from other crews. They operate with an extravagant style and rely more on cunning and the rules of the game than on direct combat power.

current_state.summary_text: The Foxy Pirates are an active crew on the seas, known for practicing the Davy Back Fight as their primary instrument of expansion and conflict. There are no canonical post-Egghead events that alter their status.

factions.name: Giant Squad
factions.description: Elite military unit of the Marines composed of giant warriors. It acts as a shock force in critical operations, with combat capability far superior to that of ordinary soldiers.

current_state.summary_text: The Giant Squad remains an elite unit of the Marines. There are no canonical post-Egghead events that alter its institutional status.

factions.name: The Admirals
factions.description: The top-ranking position of the Marines, immediately below Fleet Admiral. The Admirals are the organization's principal military enforcers, with global authority recognized by nations and feared by pirates. Each Admiral holds enough power to influence the balance of forces in the world.

current_state.summary_text: The Admiral rank remains one of the highest in the Marines' hierarchy, below only the Fleet Admiral. After Egghead, the Admiral roster underwent changes — Kizaru participated in the siege of Egghead and the status of at least one Admiral may be under institutional review, but the rank itself remains active and central to the Marines' structure.

factions.name: The Vice Admirals
factions.description: Intermediate leadership rank of the Marines, immediately below Admiral. Vice Admirals command large groups of sailors, lead field operations, and represent the executive backbone of the Marines in high-complexity missions.

current_state.summary_text: The Vice Admiral rank remains active as an intermediate leadership tier of the Marines, responsible for commanding large contingents and executing high-risk missions. There are no canonical post-Egghead events that alter the institutional status of the rank.

factions.name: Jaygarcia Family
factions.description: Lineage tied to the Gorosei, the supreme governing body of the World Government. The family exerts direct influence over the decisions that shape the global political order, operating from the top of the established power's civilian hierarchy.

current_state.summary_text: The Jaygarcia Family is part of the Five Elders (Gorosei), the ruling council of the World Government. Following the events of Egghead, the World Government faces a legitimacy crisis, but the Gorosei retains formal authority over its structures.

factions.name: Topman Family
factions.description: Lineage tied to the Gorosei, the supreme deliberative body of the World Government. The family has participated in global governance for generations, being one of the institutional pillars that sustain the current order.

current_state.summary_text: The Topman Family is part of the Gorosei, the supreme council of the World Government. The Gorosei remains the formal authority of the world, even as its legitimacy faces growing pressure following Egghead.

factions.name: Shepherd Family
factions.description: Lineage tied to the Gorosei and, by extension, to the apparatus of the World Government. The family holds a position of structural influence in maintaining the global political balance, acting predominantly through institutional and diplomatic channels.

current_state.summary_text: The Shepherd Family is part of the Gorosei, the council that governs the World Government. The institutional role of the Gorosei remains active, although the crisis triggered at Egghead has exposed deep fractures in the power structure.

factions.name: Manmayer Family
factions.description: Lineage tied to the Gorosei and the World Government. The family operates within the global power structure with a discreet institutional profile, exerting influence through less visible channels within the same governing council.

current_state.summary_text: The Manmayer Family is part of the Gorosei, the supreme council of the World Government. Compared to the other Gorosei families, its public presence is less prominent, with a more discreet role within the same apparatus of power.

factions.name: Nerona Family
factions.description: A lineage tied to the Gorosei and, possibly, to even deeper layers of World Government power. The family is associated with the custody of or knowledge about historical secrets that the World Government actively conceals from the world.

current_state.summary_text: The Nerona Family is integrated into the Gorosei, the supreme council of the World Government. There are canonical indications that members of this lineage have a connection to Imu and to access to historical knowledge suppressed by the World Government itself.

factions.name: Furukawa Family
factions.description: A noble lineage from Wano, rooted in traditions of honor and craftsmanship. The family acts as a pillar of social stability within the country, historically serving local power structures with constant loyalty and influence, without overt military prominence.

current_state.summary_text: The Furukawa Family is a noble lineage from Wano. With the fall of Kaido and the end of the Kurozumi shogunate, Wano is undergoing a political transition, and traditional families such as the Furukawa occupy a relevant position in the reconfiguration of the country's internal order.

factions.name: Kurozumi Family
factions.description: A lineage from Wano historically marked by its rise to power through betrayal and manipulation. The family ruled Wano under Orochi's shogunate in alliance with Kaido, but was deposed with the fall of that regime. Its legacy is one of distrust and resentment within Wano.

current_state.summary_text: The Kurozumi Family, whose power in Wano was built on deceit and manipulation, has been dismantled as a ruling force since the fall of Orochi and the defeat of Kaido in Wano. The name carries a deep stigma in the country's collective memory.

factions.name: Charlotte Family
factions.description: An extensive clan built around the former Empress Big Mom, who ruled Totto Land and the territory of Whole Cake Island. The family combined blood ties and marriage with political alliances and territorial control, functioning as the backbone of Big Mom's pirate empire.

current_state.summary_text: The Charlotte Family, the clan of the former Empress Big Mom, lost its central pillar with Big Mom's defeat in Wano. The clan's political standing as an organized pirate power is weakened, although its numerous members remain scattered across the world.

factions.name: Bonney Pirates
factions.description: A pirate crew originating from the South Blue, known for audacity and for having been classified as a significant threat by the Marines. It orbits around its captain and the personal and political consequences tied to her lineage.

current_state.summary_text: The crew operates under the leadership of Jewelry Bonney, whose personal motivations were profoundly altered after the events of Egghead by the revelation about her father Kuma and the direct confrontation with the World Government. The crew's operational status as a cohesive unit after Egghead is not fully established by canon.

factions.name: Gyro Pirates
factions.description: A pirate crew originating from the South Blue, led by Gyro. Known for unconventional methods and adaptability. Their narrative relevance is concentrated in the period prior to entering the Grand Line.

current_state.summary_text: A crew of peripheral presence in canon, with no developments recorded after their appearance in the Sabaody Archipelago arc. Their current state is not established by the work.

factions.name: Shoujou Pirates
factions.description: A pirate crew led by Shoujou, characterized by agile and coordinated combat. Known for combining a laid-back spirit with efficiency in battle.

current_state.summary_text: A crew of limited relevance in canon, associated with the Skypiea arc. No developments are recorded for the post-Egghead period.

factions.name: Beautiful Pirates
factions.description: A pirate crew led by Cavendish, known for discipline, elegance in combat, and tactical efficiency. Part of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its crews, combining loyalty to the alliance with its own identity.

current_state.summary_text: The Beautiful Pirates are part of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its seven constituent crews, maintaining operational autonomy while remaining bound by the oath of support to Luffy sworn after Dressrosa.

factions.name: Straw Hat Grand Fleet
factions.description: A voluntary alliance of seven pirate crews formed after the events of Dressrosa, bound by oath to Monkey D. Luffy. It does not operate as a permanent unified fleet — each crew maintains autonomy — but constitutes a considerable collective force, associated with the name and influence of Emperor Luffy.

current_state.summary_text: The Straw Hat Grand Fleet remains active as an alliance of seven independent crews united by the oath made after Dressrosa. Each crew operates autonomously, converging in mutual support when necessary. With Luffy now recognized as an Emperor of the Sea, the fleet indirectly forms part of one of the greatest pirate power structures in the world.

factions.name: Whitebeard Pirates Family
factions.description: A pirate brotherhood forged under the leadership of Edward Newgate (Whitebeard), united by loyalty and symbolic adoption, not by blood ties. It represented one of the greatest pirate power structures in the world before Marineford. With Newgate's death and the subsequent events, the original organization was dissolved, surviving mainly as an identity and memory among its former members.

current_state.summary_text: With the death of Edward Newgate at Marineford and the subsequent dissolution of the crew's original structure, the Whitebeard Pirates family exists as a legacy and shared identity among survivors, without the hierarchy and territory that once defined it. Some former members went their own independent ways; the faction as an organized force has been dissolved since Marineford.

factions.name: Rimoshifu Family
factions.description: A Celestial Dragon lineage with a seat among the Holy Knights, the World Government's military elite revealed during the events of Egghead. Symbolically associated with the Kirin. Occupies a position of influence within the celestial hierarchy.

current_state.summary_text: A noble family belonging to the Celestial Dragons' structure, with active representation in the Holy Knights — an order revealed at Egghead as the World Government's direct arm of force. Its institutional position is therefore at the center of World Government power in the post-Egghead period.

factions.name: Bavette Family
factions.description: A Celestial Dragon lineage with a documented history of participation in hunting competitions held in the God Valley. Details about its structure, influence, and activities in the current canonical present are not established by the work.

current_state.summary_text: A family belonging to the Celestial Dragons' nobility. Its current institutional role is not detailed by canon beyond historical records of participation in hunting events hosted by the celestial elite.

factions.name: Donquixote Pirates
factions.description: A defunct pirate crew, once organized into hierarchical families and responsible for the clandestine control of Dressrosa and the trafficking of artificial Devil Fruits (SAD/SMILE). It operated as the armed and political arm of its captain, who maintained a dual identity as a Warlord of the Sea and underworld agent.

current_state.summary_text: The Donquixote Pirates have been dissolved as an operational faction since Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa. Their captain is serving a life sentence in Impel Down, and the organizational structure — divided into families — was dismantled by the Marines and Luffy's allies.

factions.name: SWORD
factions.description: A clandestine unit internal to the Marines, composed of officers who formally renounce institutional protection in order to conduct high-risk missions outside official channels. Its existence is not publicly acknowledged by the World Government.

current_state.summary_text: SWORD is a Marine special operations unit that remains active after Egghead. Its agents operate without official cover, which allows them to act in conflict zones without formally implicating the World Government.

factions.name: Special Science Group
factions.description: An elite scientific division subordinate to the World Government, responsible for the development of weapons, military technology, and cutting-edge experiments that sustain the Marines' strategic advantage. It operated centrally from Egghead under the direction of the government's chief scientist.

current_state.summary_text: The Special Science Group had its structure severely compromised following the events of Egghead, with the death of Dr. Vegapunk and the partial destruction of the laboratory island. The operational future of the World Government's scientific division remains uncertain in the current canon.

factions.name: Neo Marines
factions.description: A private military force of non-canon origin (film), composed of dissident ex-Marines who operate outside the World Government with their own agenda of exterminating the age of pirates. Their status within the main canonical continuity is not established.

current_state.summary_text: The Neo Marines are a non-main-canon faction, introduced in One Piece Film: Z, with no confirmed status in the main canonical timeline after Egghead.

factions.name: Heroes' Association
factions.description: Organization with no verifiable counterpart in the One Piece canon. Origin and canonical legitimacy cannot be determined based on available official materials.

current_state.summary_text: The Heroes' Association has no basis in the established One Piece canon. There is no record of this organization in the main work or in recognized official materials.

factions.name: Germa 66
factions.description: The Vinsmoke family's itinerant military kingdom, with no fixed geographic territory, operating as a mercenary and scientific combat force. It is a nominal member of the Levely organization and recognized as a kingdom by the World Government, despite its predominantly military and experimental nature.

current_state.summary_text: Germa 66 remains operational as a military kingdom without fixed territory following Whole Cake Island. The faction participated in the events of Whole Cake Island in a circumstantial alliance with the Straw Hat Pirates, and its post-Wano/Egghead status has not been detailed in the canon.

factions.name: Wano Yakuza
factions.description: Network of traditional criminal clans rooted in the Land of Wano, with influence over local trade, territories, and hierarchies. During Kaido's occupation, part of the leadership was persecuted and imprisoned; after the fall of the regime, the faction is integrated into the social and political fabric of a rebuilding Wano.

current_state.summary_text: The Wano Yakuza played an active role in the alliance that brought down Kaido and Orochi in Wano. With the opening of the country's borders following Kaido's defeat, the faction operates in a Wano undergoing institutional reconstruction, with its internal political role still being defined in the canon.

factions.name: Cross Guild
factions.description: The Cross Guild is a mercenary and bounty-hunting organization composed of high-caliber notorious pirates. It operates as an intermediary force between piracy and institutional power, having been recognized by the World Government as one of the Great Naval Powers. It combines political influence, military might, and economic maneuvering to maintain a presence across the seas.

current_state.summary_text: The Cross Guild operates as a large-scale mercenary organization, recognized by the World Government as one of the world's supplementary naval forces. Following the events of Egghead, its institutional position remains established, though the internal stability of its leadership is uncertain given the history of tensions among its founders.

factions.name: Alvida Pirates
factions.description: The Alvida Pirates are a mid-sized pirate crew known for opportunistic attacks and a flashy style. They operate with speed and cunning against smaller targets, sustaining a reputation for resilience disproportionate to their size.

current_state.summary_text: The Alvida Pirates exist as a crew of limited standing; their independent operational relevance is marginal, given that their captain operates predominantly within the orbit of other factions.

factions.name: Black Cat Pirates
factions.description: The Black Cat Pirates were a pirate crew specializing in stealth, swift attacks, and deceptive tactics. They were led by a calculating strategist and operated with uncommon discipline for pirates, prioritizing planning over brute force. The crew was effectively dissolved with the disappearance of their captain.

current_state.summary_text: The Black Cat Pirates are a crew disbanded in practice: their captain Kuro abandoned the pirate life and vanished from the scene, and the crew was scattered during the events at Syrup Village.

factions.name: Barto Club
factions.description: The Barto Club is a pirate crew of theatrical style and fanatical disposition, known for bold exploits and a strong sense of internal camaraderie. Since Dressrosa, it has served as a member of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet, which grants it political and symbolic alignment with that alliance.

current_state.summary_text: The Barto Club is part of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its subordinate crews, a bond established following the events of Dressrosa.

factions.name: Krieg Pirates
factions.description: The Krieg Pirates were one of the largest pirate crews in the East Blue, ruled through terror and overwhelming numbers. Known for their captain's brutality and heavy armaments, they operated through systematic intimidation. The crew was dismantled as an organized force following the defeat of their leadership at the Baratie.

current_state.summary_text: The Krieg Pirates were a massive crew destroyed as a cohesive force during the events at the Baratie restaurant; their captain was defeated and the crew scattered, with no canonical record of reconstitution.

factions.name: Masira Pirates
factions.description: The Masira Pirates are a crew specialized in salvage operations and recovery of sunken ships, operating near Jaya Island. They are part of the Saruyama Alliance, a cooperative structure that brings together crews from the region with shared historical and operational ties.

current_state.summary_text: The Masira Pirates are part of the Saruyama Alliance and operate in the Jaya region as a maritime salvage crew; there is no canonical record of any significant change in their status after Egghead.

factions.name: Whitebeard Pirates
factions.description: The Whitebeard Pirates were one of the most powerful crews of the pirate era, structured as a family of divisions led by high-ranking commanders. They acted as protectors of allied territories and were considered one of the Four Emperors. The crew was disbanded with the fall of their captain at Marineford and the subsequent collapse of their bases, becoming a historical faction.

current_state.summary_text: The Whitebeard Pirates were dissolved as a unified crew following the death of their captain in the Battle of Marineford and Blackbeard's subsequent attack on the Whitebeard Islands; the remaining members scattered or reorganized into independent groups.

factions.name: Caribou Pirates
factions.description: The Caribou Pirates are an opportunistic, mid-sized pirate crew known for deceptive tactics and a willingness to exploit any situation for personal gain. Their captain possesses a Logia-type Devil Fruit and has a history of infiltrating high-risk conflict zones.

current_state.summary_text: The Caribou Pirates are a crew with a low institutional profile; their captain was involved in the events of Wano and demonstrated opportunistic ambitions tied to knowledge of the Poneglyphs, but the current status and cohesion of the crew are not clearly established in the post-Egghead canon.

factions.name: Obscura Collective
factions.description: A non-canon organization described as specializing in the storage and concealment of illegal goods under a strict hierarchy. It has no established counterpart in the official One Piece universe.

current_state.summary_text: The Obscura Collective is a non-canon organization — there is no record of this faction in the work of One Piece. Its institutional status cannot be determined by the canon.

factions.name: Akuma Alliance
factions.description: A non-canon pirate organization described as based in the New World with a structured leadership. It has no established counterpart in the official One Piece universe.

current_state.summary_text: The Akuma Alliance is a non-canon organization — there is no record of this faction in the work of One Piece. Its institutional status cannot be determined by the canon.

factions.name: Shandia
factions.description: The native people of Skypiea, originally from the terrestrial island of Jaya before its ascent to the skies. The Shandia are a warrior tribe with a strong oral and spiritual tradition, historically in conflict with the Skypieans over dominion of the sacred island of Shandora. Following the resolution of the conflict in Skypiea, they came to coexist with the island's other inhabitants.

current_state.summary_text: The Shandia inhabit Skypiea as the native people of the floating island, having ended centuries of war against the Skypieans following the events of the Skypiea Arc. The tribe maintains its warrior and cultural identity, now living in coexistence with the other inhabitants of Skypiea.

factions.name: Mink Tribe
factions.description: A race of animal humanoids that inhabits Zou, a city built on the back of the giant elephant Zunesha. The Minks are natural fighters with an innate ability in Electro and the capacity to activate the Sulong form under a full moon. They maintain ancestral bonds of loyalty to the Kozuki Family of Wano and operate under a leadership structure divided between the day and night kingdoms.

current_state.summary_text: The Mink Tribe inhabits Zou, the island on Zunesha's back, and maintains an active alliance with the Kozuki Family since the fall of Kaido in Wano. The tribe preserves its dual governance structure and its combat capabilities, including the Sulong transformation.

factions.name: Kozuki Retainers
factions.description: A corps of samurai who serve the Kozuki Family, the historical ruling clan of Wano. The retainers are high-caliber warriors bound by an oath of loyalty to their lord. After decades of resistance against Kaido's occupation, the faction served as the backbone of the alliance that restored the Kozuki clan to power in Wano.

current_state.summary_text: The Kozuki Retainers fulfilled their central oath with the restoration of the Kozuki clan to power in Wano following the fall of Kaido. The faction, as a clandestine resistance force, concluded its cycle of struggle; the surviving samurai now serve the restored shogunate of Wano.

factions.name: Tontatta Tribe
factions.description: A small-statured people inhabiting the island of Green Bit, adjacent to Dressrosa. The Tontatta possess physical strength disproportionate to their size, high speed, and combat ability. They historically maintained ties with the royal family of Dressrosa and were subjected to exploitation during Doflamingo's rule, a condition that ended after his downfall.

current_state.summary_text: The Tontatta Tribe inhabits Green Bit and maintains historical ties with the kingdom of Dressrosa. Following Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, the tribe operates openly as an ally of the kingdom, having brought an end to the period of slavery and exploitation to which it was subjected.

factions.name: Kuja Tribe
factions.description: A female warrior society inhabiting Amazon Lily, in the Calm Belt. The Kuja are skilled fighters with an innate mastery of Haki, organized around an empress. The tribe maintained institutional protection through the Shichibukai system, which was dissolved by the World Government, altering its formal relationship with the world's power structures.

current_state.summary_text: The Kuja Tribe inhabits Amazon Lily and held the Warlord status that protected their territory, a status abolished by the World Government prior to the events of Egghead. With the dissolution of the Seven Warlords of the Sea system, the island finds itself in an institutionally vulnerable position before the Marines, though its combat capability remains intact.

factions.name: Red-Hair Pirates
factions.description: An imperial-level pirate crew recognized both for its combat prowess and its capacity to arbitrate conflicts between factions. It operates in the New World with the authority of a Four Emperor and maintains strategic relations with various forces around the world.

current_state.summary_text: The Red-Hair Pirates remain one of the most influential crews in the New World, led by one of the Four Emperors. Their balanced stance between military force and diplomacy keeps them as a stabilizing factor in the power struggles across the great seas.

factions.name: Monkey D. Family
factions.description: A lineage marked by unwavering conviction and rupture with the established order. Its members occupy leadership positions in ideologically opposed factions — the Marines, the Revolutionary Army, and piracy — making the family an involuntary axis of the world's contradictions.

current_state.summary_text: The Monkey D. family is spread across the world's greatest opposing forces: the Marines, the Revolutionary Army, and piracy — with high-ranking members in each of these institutions. Following Egghead, the name Monkey D. sits at the center of the world's greatest political tensions.

factions.name: Gol Family
factions.description: The lineage of Pirate King Gol D. Roger, extinct in its known direct line following the execution of Portgas D. Ace at Marineford. Carrying this blood is equivalent to being a priority target of the World Government, regardless of any individual action.

current_state.summary_text: The Gol Family is a direct descendant of the only man to claim the title of Pirate King. The blood of this lineage is treated by the World Government as an existential threat, and those who carry it live under constant institutional persecution.

factions.name: Portgas Family
factions.description: A lineage built on secrecy and sacrifice, created to protect the heir to Roger's blood under a different name. Historically associated with the concealment of identity as a survival strategy against the World Government's persecution.

current_state.summary_text: The Portgas Family is known primarily through Portgas D. Ace, Roger's son, who died at Marineford defending Monkey D. Luffy. The lineage, kept secret for years to protect its members from the World Government's persecution, has no known living representatives in the current canon.

factions.name: Vinsmoke Family
factions.description: A royal house without a kingdom, which commands Germa 66 — an elite military force based on genetic modification and advanced combat technology. It operates as an itinerant mercenary army, with no fixed loyalty to any world power.

current_state.summary_text: The Vinsmoke Family commands Germa 66, a paramilitary and scientific organization without fixed territory, which operates as a mercenary force throughout the world. Following the events of Whole Cake Island, the surviving Vinsmokes maintain Germa's operations, albeit with reduced political influence.

factions.name: Kozuki Family
factions.description: The shogunal lineage of Wano and guardian of the knowledge of the Poneglyphs. Historically persecuted and suppressed by outside forces, the clan is associated with resistance, prophecy, and the uncovering of the world's forbidden history. Since Kaido's defeat in Wano, it once again occupies a position of legitimate authority in the country.

current_state.summary_text: The Kozuki Family was restored to power in Wano following the defeat of Kaido and the fall of Orochi's regime. The clan reclaims its position as Wano's legitimate shogunal lineage, also carrying the legacy of the Poneglyphs and the forbidden knowledge of the Void Century.

factions.name: Donquixote Family
factions.description: A former Celestial Dragon family that abdicated its noble status and built a criminal empire in the world's underworld. Operated for decades as an intermediary in arms trafficking and Devil Fruit trading. Dissolved as an organization after the defeat at Dressrosa, with its leadership imprisoned in Impel Down.

current_state.summary_text: The Donquixote Family is dissolved as an active force: its central figure, Donquixote Doflamingo, has been imprisoned in Impel Down since the events of Dressrosa. The family's legacy is marked by the fall of the Celestial Dragons into infamy and by decades of criminal operation in the underworld.

factions.name: Nefertari Family
factions.description: The ruling royal house of Alabasta and a founding member of the World Government that, unlike the other founders, chose to remain among its people instead of becoming Celestial Dragons. This historic choice, combined with evidence of ties to the forbidden history of the Void Century, places the lineage under direct scrutiny from the most hidden forces of world power.

current_state.summary_text: The Nefertari Family rules the Kingdom of Alabasta and is one of the few founding royal houses of the World Government that remained in the world instead of ascending to Pangaea. After Egghead, the Nefertari name returned to worldwide attention: Im-sama demonstrated direct interest in the lineage, signaling that the clan carries secrets tied to the forbidden history of the world.

factions.name: Neptune Family
factions.description: The ruling royal house of the Ryugu Kingdom, on Fishman Island. Holds political authority over the underwater nation and maintains historical ties to the Sea Kings and to the legends of the Void Century. Represents the link between the surface world and the depths of the ocean.

current_state.summary_text: The Neptune Family rules the Ryugu Kingdom, the underwater nation of Fishman Island, and maintains an active alliance with the Straw Hat Pirates since the events of Fishman Island. The kingdom remains under structural tension regarding its place in the world following the abolition of fish-man slavery proclaimed by Monkey D. Luffy.

factions.name: Riku Family
factions.description: The restored royal house of Dressrosa, deposed by Donquixote Doflamingo and reinstated after his fall. Governs the kingdom with a stance centered on compassion and justice, serving as a symbol of legitimacy and institutional resilience in the region.

current_state.summary_text: The Riku Family was restored to the throne of Dressrosa after the defeat of Donquixote Doflamingo by the Straw Hat Pirates and their allies. The kingdom operates under its reinstated legitimate leadership, rebuilding from the period of occupation and conflict.

factions.name: Shimotsuki Family
factions.description: A samurai lineage originating from Wano, whose branches have spread across the seas. Carries a generational tradition of swordsmanship and a code of honor. Its heritage is tied more to cultural identity and the art of the sword than to a unified political structure.

current_state.summary_text: The Shimotsuki Family is present both in Wano and beyond — one branch established itself in Syrup Village (East Blue) generations ago, while the main lineage remains tied to the history of Wano. There is no canonical indication of a centralized active political role in the post-Egghead period.

factions.name: Franky Family
factions.description: A group of shipwrights and street fighters based in Water Seven, united by loyalty and shared purpose rather than blood ties. Operates as a community of craft and combat, with an identity forged around shipbuilding and resistance.

current_state.summary_text: The Franky Family, a group of shipwrights and fighters from Water Seven, remains active in the city following its founder's departure with the Straw Hat Pirates. Its community structure continues to operate in Water Seven without a central political role in the post-Egghead period.

factions.name: Dadan Family
factions.description: A group of bandits led by Curly Dadan, based in the mountains of Dawn Island. Served as an adoptive family for figures tied to Garp's lineage. Its cohesion is based on informal loyalty and shared survival, with no formal institutional structure.

current_state.summary_text: The Dadan Family is a group of bandits operating in the Foosha Village region of Dawn Island, East Blue. Without a relevant political role in the post-Egghead period, it remains a peripheral faction with emotional ties to Garp's lineage.

factions.name: Chinjao Family
factions.description: A lineage of warriors and leaders of the Happo Navy, a faction of fighters from the East. Recognized for its martial tradition and clan pride. Leadership passed to the next generation after Dressrosa, keeping the Happo Navy as an allied force within the Straw Hat Grand Fleet.

current_state.summary_text: The Chinjao Family, a lineage of martial artists of the Happo Navy, has played an active role since the Mera Mera no Mi tournament at Dressrosa. Grandson Sai assumed leadership of the Happo Navy after the events of Dressrosa; there are no central canonical developments for the faction in the post-Egghead period.

factions.name: Accino Family
factions.description: A bounty hunter family introduced in a non-canon anime arc, specializing in tracking and capturing targets across the seas. Their existence and relevance are not confirmed by the manga canon.

current_state.summary_text: The Accino Family is a group of bounty hunters that appeared in the Navalhada Fria arc (anime, non-canon). As non-canon material, their institutional status in the post-Egghead period is not established by the manga.

factions.name: Vegapunk's Creations
factions.description: A collection of inventions, weapons, and artificial organisms developed by the scientist Vegapunk throughout his career. They range from combat robots and cyborgs to artificial Devil Fruits and other cutting-edge technologies. A large portion of these creations operates in service of the World Government and the Marines, functioning as instruments of power and control across the seas.

current_state.summary_text: Vegapunk's technological legacy remains operational and distributed between the World Government and the Marines, although its creator was assassinated by the Five Elders during the events of Egghead. The fate and control of some of these creations is uncertain following the collapse of the research structure on Egghead.

factions.name: Pacifista Program
factions.description: A World Government armament program that mass-produces humanoid combat cyborgs. The units are designed to operate in large numbers, suppressing pirates and imposing the World Government's order. They represent the militarization of Vegapunk's biotechnology on an industrial scale.

current_state.summary_text: Pacifista units continue to serve in the Marines as large-scale suppression weapons, although the program has been partially superseded in strategic priority by the Seraphim, the more advanced generation developed by Vegapunk.

factions.name: Seraphim
factions.description: The most advanced generation of humanoid weapons created by Vegapunk, modeled after historically powerful pirates and endowed with abilities derived from Devil Fruits. Each unit combines genetic engineering, cybernetics, and supernatural powers, making them the most lethal military assets ever developed by the World Government's armament program.

current_state.summary_text: The Seraphim are high-value strategic weapons under the control of the World Government. During the events of Egghead, Seraphim units were directly involved in the confrontations on the island, and their operational control remains in the hands of the Five Elders following Vegapunk's death.

factions.name: Celestial Dragons
factions.description: A hereditary noble class that occupies the top of the World Government's social hierarchy, descended from the founders of the Twenty Allied Nations. They reside in Mary Geoise, enjoy absolute immunity and privileges, and exert influence over the Marines and global institutions. They are widely feared and resented by peoples across the world due to their history of abuses and the power structure that protects them.

current_state.summary_text: The Celestial Dragons maintain their position as an untouchable ruling class at the top of the World Government's hierarchy, residing in Mary Geoise. The events of Egghead — including Vegapunk's public revelation about the world's forbidden history — represent a structural threat to the legitimacy of the system that sustains them, the consequences of which are still unfolding.

factions.name: Five Elders
factions.description: A council of five figures that holds the highest position in the World Government, above the Marines, Cipher Pol, and even the Celestial Dragons in the exercise of power. They oversee global policy, control sensitive information, and make decisions that shape the world order. They possess supernatural combat capabilities and operate with virtually unrestricted authority.

current_state.summary_text: The Five Elders exercise supreme control over the World Government and revealed, during the events of Egghead, combat capabilities and Devil Fruits previously unknown to the public. Following Egghead, their posture has become even more aggressive, with at least one of the Elders deployed for direct external operations.

factions.name: Space Pirates
factions.description: A group of alien beings originating from the moon, characterized by a fox-like appearance. They operate as lunar miners, extracting resources from the satellite, and have a pirate crew structure with their own hierarchy. Their existence demonstrates that intelligent life forms inhabit or once inhabited the moon in the One Piece universe.

current_state.summary_text: The Space Pirates are a group established in the Sky Island arc (Skypiea/Moon), with no subsequent canonical development. There is no record of activity or change in status following the events of Egghead.

factions.name: Roshio Pirates
factions.description: A small pirate crew identified by a jolly roger featuring the silhouette of a hanged man on a red background. They operated on the Grand Line route with a recorded bounty of 42,000,000 berries on their captain. The crew lost their captain in Jaya, with no subsequent institutional development recorded in the canon.

current_state.summary_text: The Roshio Pirates are a minor crew whose captain was defeated and killed by Bellamy in the Jaya archipelago. The crew has no canonical record of activity following that event.

factions.name: On Air Pirates
factions.description: The On Air Pirates are a pirate crew whose combat style exploits the power of sound and rhythm, combining deception and musical coordination to destabilize opponents. Originally led by Scratchmen Apoo, the crew had its operational structure destroyed with the collapse of the Beasts Pirates in Wano, and its status as a cohesive group is uncertain.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was effectively dissolved after Kaido's fall in Wano: its members were mostly Calamities and Tobi Roppo of the Beasts Pirates, structures that ceased to exist with the Emperor's defeat. Scratchmen Apoo remains active as an independent pirate in the New World, with no consolidated crew recorded by canon.

factions.name: Hawkins Pirates
factions.description: The Hawkins Pirates are a pirate crew led by Basil Hawkins, known for their calculating stance and use of strategies based on fate-reading and battlefield manipulation. The crew operated in the New World until it was absorbed into Kaido's power structure in Wano, an event that marked its end as an independent group.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was effectively disbanded during the events of Wano: Basil Hawkins operated subordinate to Kaido and was defeated in the conflict, ending any cohesion the group had. There is no canonical record of the crew being reconstituted after Wano.

factions.name: Nox Pirates
factions.description: The Nox Pirates are a small pirate crew formed by members of the Mink Tribe, founded with the purpose of protecting the Mink people and uncovering the world's secrets. The crew is marked by the sacrifice of its original captain at Whole Cake Island and has no confirmed operational existence after the events of Wano.

current_state.summary_text: The crew lost its founding captain, Pedro, who sacrificed himself at Whole Cake Island. After Wano, Pekoms's whereabouts are uncertain; Carrot remained in Zou as ruler of the Minks. The crew as an active structure has no confirmed existence in post-Wano canon.

factions.name: Drake Pirates
factions.description: The Drake Pirates are a pirate crew that functioned as a front for undercover operations, led by X Drake, a former Marine Vice Admiral and SWORD agent. The crew combines military discipline with undercover activity, and its identity as a genuine pirate group is secondary to its captain's intelligence mission.

current_state.summary_text: The crew operated as cover for X Drake's undercover mission as a SWORD agent, a secret Marine unit. After Wano, Drake survived and his ties to SWORD were exposed; the current status of the crew as an independent pirate entity is not confirmed by canon.

factions.name: Gecko Pirates
factions.description: The Gecko Pirates are a pirate crew led by Gecko Moria, infamous for the use of terror tactics and the exploitation of shadow powers to create armies of undead. The crew had its base, Thriller Bark, destroyed, and has not existed as a cohesive group since the events prior to Marineford.

current_state.summary_text: Gecko Moria survived the events of Marineford and subsequently sought revenge against Big Mom, appearing at Whole Cake Island. The original Thriller Bark crew was destroyed before Marineford; Moria operates independently and his current group has no confirmed structure in post-Egghead canon.

factions.name: Fallen Monk Pirates
factions.description: The Fallen Monk Pirates are a pirate crew led by Urouge, one of the Supernovas known as the Worst Generation. The crew operates independently in the New World, with a reputation built on tactical awareness and the ability to survive confrontations with superior forces.

current_state.summary_text: The Fallen Monk Pirates are led by Urouge, one of the Worst Generation Supernovas. Urouge remains active in the New World and is one of the few Supernovas without a confirmed affiliation to an Emperor. His crew maintains an independent existence, albeit with a low-profile presence in current canon.

factions.name: Giant Warrior Pirates
factions.description: The Giant Warrior Pirates are a crew composed of giants, led by Hajrudin, which is part of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of its constituent fleets. The crew combines exceptional brute strength with battle coordination, and their loyalty has been formally tied to Monkey D. Luffy's alliance since Dressrosa.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was formed after the dissolution of Doflamingo's army and joined the Straw Hat Grand Fleet as one of the seven allied fleets, a position it has held since Dressrosa.

factions.name: Golden Lion Pirates
factions.description: The Golden Lion Pirates were a pirate crew led by Shiki, one of the great pirates of Roger's era, known for their might and aggression. The crew ceased to exist as an active force after Shiki's capture by the Marines, becoming a historical faction with no operational presence in the current canon.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was disbanded with Shiki's defeat and imprisonment by the Roger Pirates and the Marines decades ago. The events of the film Strong World are of disputed canonicity; institutionally, the crew has no active existence in the main canon.

factions.name: Blackbeard Pirates
factions.description: The Blackbeard Pirates are a high-risk crew led by Marshall D. Teach, notorious for their calculated brutality and willingness to betray allies in pursuit of power gains. Organized into squads captained by elite fighters, the crew distinguishes itself by accumulating Devil Fruits deliberately and by acting with tactical unpredictability. They constitute one of the greatest threats to the balance of power in the New World.

current_state.summary_text: After Egghead, the Blackbeard Pirates consolidate themselves as one of the most threatening forces on the seas, with their captain holding two Devil Fruits and actively targeting the title of King of the Pirates. The crew operates aggressively and expansionistically, attacking strategic targets to accumulate power.

factions.name: Heart Pirates
factions.description: The Heart Pirates are a compact and disciplined crew specializing in tactical combat and precision operations, a reflection of the medical-strategic leadership style of their captain Trafalgar D. Water Law. Known for their mobility, coordination, and advanced use of the Ope Ope no Mi, they served as a decisive allied force in the Dressrosa and Wano campaigns. Their operational continuity following Law's defeat remains undefined in canon.

current_state.summary_text: After the events of Egghead, the Heart Pirates face institutional uncertainty: their captain, Law, was defeated by Blackbeard in the North Sea, and the current state of the crew as an operational unit is not confirmed in canon.

factions.name: Kid Pirates
factions.description: The Kid Pirates were an aggressive and ambitious crew from the New World, known for their offensive brutality and the determination of their captain Eustass Kid to rival the greatest powers on the seas. Following the catastrophic defeat at Elbaf against the Red Hair Pirates, the crew ceased to operate as a relevant force.

current_state.summary_text: The Kid Pirates were severely dismantled during the events of Elbaf, where Eustass Kid was defeated by Shanks and his crew destroyed. The current state of the faction as an organized force is one of functional collapse.

factions.name: Fire Tank Pirates
factions.description: The Fire Tank Pirates are a singular pirate crew whose ship is the body of their captain, Capone "Gang" Bege, a walking fortress capable of housing men and weaponry. Combining organized crime tactics with naval combat capability, the crew is known for cold planning and internal loyalty. They acted as tactical allies during the operation against Big Mom on Whole Cake Island.

current_state.summary_text: The Fire Tank Pirates maintain active operations under the command of Capone Bege, with no post-Wano canonical events that significantly alter their institutional status.

factions.name: Sun Pirates
factions.description: The Sun Pirates are a crew composed mostly of Fish-Men, originally founded on the ideal of freeing slaves and fighting oppression against the races of the deep. Heirs to the legacy of Fisher Tiger, the crew bears the symbol of the Sun — originally created to cover up the Celestial Dragon slavery marks. They represent a stance of resistance and self-determination within the underwater world.

current_state.summary_text: The Sun Pirates remain active as a Fish-Man faction committed to the freedom of their people, but operate without their most recent captain, Jinbe, who joined the Straw Hat Pirates as a permanent member.

factions.name: Buggy's Delivery
factions.description: Buggy's Delivery was a mercenary organization led by Buggy the Clown that offered military services for payment. The organization was replaced by the Cross Guild, a larger-scale structure with greater systemic impact that operates by placing bounties on Marine agents. As an independent faction, Buggy's Delivery no longer exists in the post-Wano era.

current_state.summary_text: Buggy's Delivery was dissolved and replaced by the Cross Guild, an organization founded after Wano that places bounties on Marines — including high-ranking officers — and elevated Buggy to the status of Emperor, even though his effective leadership is disputed internally.

factions.name: Straw Hats
factions.description: The Straw Hat Pirates are a pirate crew led by Monkey D. Luffy, whose stated goal is to find the One Piece and become King of the Pirates. Each member carries a personal dream they pursue alongside the collective journey. Known for defending the weak, challenging established powers — including the Seven Warlords of the Sea, the Four Emperors, and the World Government — and forging lasting alliances, the crew is considered a destabilizing force of the world's status quo. Luffy currently holds the title of Emperor.

current_state.summary_text: After Egghead, the Straw Hat Pirates consolidate themselves as one of the central forces of the era, with their captain recognized worldwide as one of the Four Emperors. The crew departed Egghead under massive Marine pressure and continues on an active course toward the final stretch of the Grand Line.

factions.name: Worst Generation
factions.description: The Worst Generation is a classification by the Marines and the media for the group of rookie pirates with exceptionally high bounties who emerged in the same generation, including the eleven Supernovas plus Blackbeard. It does not constitute a formal organization nor does it have a unified command structure — it is a label that groups individuals whose collective impact shook the global balance of power during the post-Summit War era.

current_state.summary_text: The Worst Generation as a cohesive group has lost cohesion after Egghead: several of its members have been defeated, killed, or absorbed into other power structures, and the concept operates more as a historical classification than as an active faction.

factions.name: WE News
factions.description: The World Economy News Paper (WE News) is the primary globally circulated newspaper across the seas, responsible for reporting events involving pirates, governments, and world-scale occurrences. It operates with relative editorial independence from the World Government and reaches the majority of the seas through the News Coo delivery service.

current_state.summary_text: The World Economy News Paper maintains global operation as the seas' primary press outlet, distributed by the News Coo. Following Egghead, the newspaper covered the events of the incident and the World Government's declaration of war against the Straw Hat Pirates, consolidating its position as an independent source of information — though still subject to pressure from the World Government.

factions.name: Nine Red Scabbards
factions.description: The Nine Red Scabbards are the group of elite samurai sworn to the Kozuki family.
