Formed to resist the occupation of Wano and restore the Kozuki clan to power, they served as the backbone of the alliance that brought down Kaido. The faction is defined by absolute loyalty to their lord and by Wano's warrior tradition, having suffered losses and an internal betrayal during the campaign that culminated in the Battle of Onigashima.

current_state.summary_text: The Nine Red Scabbards fulfilled their central objective with the defeat of Kaido and the reopening of Wano to the world. The group, already reduced by casualties and betrayals during the Wano campaign, no longer operates as a unified combat force — its remaining members follow separate paths inside and outside the country.

factions.name: NEO MADS
factions.description: NEO MADS is a scientific organization founded as the successor to the MADS group, with the stated objective of surpassing the achievements of Dr. Vegapunk. It uses the military resources of Germa 66 to support its research and operates outside the official structures of the World Government.

current_state.summary_text: NEO MADS shows no confirmed presence or activity in canon following the events of Egghead. Its current operational status is uncertain; the organization did not participate in the events of the arc and there is no confirmation that it is active or dissolved.

factions.name: Thriller Bark Victims' Association
factions.description: A non-canon construct with no established basis in the work. The Thriller Bark Pirates were the crew commanded by Gecko Moria, operating from the island-ship Thriller Bark and making use of a zombie army created through supernatural powers. The faction was effectively dismantled following the events of the Thriller Bark Archipelago.

current_state.summary_text: The Thriller Bark Victims' Association is not a canon faction established in the work — it is a descriptive construct with no basis in the manga or anime. The ship Thriller Bark and its crew operated under Gecko Moria, whose whereabouts following the events of the Marineford War and Dressrosa remain uncertain; Moria was reported as having been recruited by Blackbeard, but without solid confirmation of his current status.

factions.name: New Fish-Man Pirates
factions.description: The New Fish-Man Pirates were a crew formed by Fish-Men driven by extreme hatred of humans, with the goal of dominating Fish-Man Island and subjugating humanity. The faction was defeated and dissolved following the confrontation on Fish-Man Island, with its leadership incapacitated.

current_state.summary_text: The New Fish-Man Pirates were defeated on Fish-Man Island and ceased to operate as a faction. Their leaders, affected by the abuse of energy steroids, were imprisoned and physically regressed to a childlike state, ending any capacity for leadership or threat.

factions.name: God's Knights
factions.description: The God's Knights, also called the Holy Knights, are the World Government's elite force headquartered at the Holy Land of Mary Geoise. They hold authority superior to that of the Celestial Dragons and have the power to execute World Nobles when necessary. They are recognized for exceptional combat capability and act as direct enforcers of the World Government's will at the highest spheres of power.

current_state.summary_text: The God's Knights remain operational as the World Government's elite force, headquartered at Mary Geoise. Following Egghead, with the World Government in an open war posture against the Straw Hat Pirates and amid a growing legitimacy crisis, the Holy Knights emerge as one of the primary instruments of coercive power within the world structure.

factions.name: Fake Straw Hat Crew
factions.description: A defunct pirate band that operated by impersonating the Straw Hat Pirates in order to usurp their reputation, recruit followers, and exert influence across the seas under a false identity. It was disbanded at the Sabaody Archipelago following a confrontation with the original crew.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was dismantled after a direct confrontation with the true Straw Hat Pirates at the Sabaody Archipelago, ending its identity-usurpation operation. There is no canon record of any activity following the event.

factions.name: Guardians of Zou
factions.description: A warrior corps of the Mink Tribe charged with the defense of Zou, an island situated atop the colossal elephant Zunesha. It operates as the territory's permanent security force, with deep knowledge of the terrain and a duty to protect the Mink population from external threats.

current_state.summary_text: The Guardians of Zou remain active as the military force of the Mink Tribe, responsible for the defense of the island atop the elephant Zunesha. The faction's stance toward the outside world remains one of watchful isolation, though alliances with the Straw Hat Pirates and the Wano Alliance have been established.

factions.name: Flying Pirates
factions.description: Extinct pirate crew that operated in the depths near Fish-Man Island, attacking vessels and terrorizing the local population from underwater. It was disbanded during the conflicts involving the island.

current_state.summary_text: The crew was defeated and disbanded during the events at Fish-Man Island, ending its role as a threat to the waters near the territory. There is no canonical record of any subsequent reorganization.

factions.name: King Harald's Family
factions.description: Royal lineage that governed Elbaf for generations, exercising leadership over the giant people on the basis of strength and tradition. Since the death of King Harald, it no longer formally holds power, but maintains a weighty reputation in giant society.

current_state.summary_text: The family no longer holds the government of Elbaf following the death of King Harald, but preserves prestige and historical recognition among the giants. Its current institutional role in Elbaf's power structure has not been established by canon.

factions.name: Figarland Family
factions.description: A noble lineage of global reach, the Figarland Family maintains deep ties with the power structures of the World Government. Its heritage carries both immense privileges and dangerous secrets, and its name resonates in the highest spheres of the established world order.

current_state.summary_text: The Figarland Family is a noble lineage with connections to the highest echelons of world power, including the Five Elders. Its influence spans the seas and its ties to the World Government make it one of the most powerful families of the current era.

factions.name: Saruyama Alliance
factions.description: An alliance between pirate crews structured around mutual loyalty and collective strength. Its members cooperate in combat and dangerous situations, prioritizing teamwork over individual action.

current_state.summary_text: The Saruyama Alliance is a pact between pirate crews from the East Blue focused on mutual cooperation in combat and protection. Its current operational relevance is uncertain — the group's whereabouts and activity are unknown.

factions.name: Five Families of the West
factions.description: A group of five powerful pirate families that dominate territories and sea routes in the West Blue. They operate both in cooperation and in competition with one another, sustaining their power through accumulated wealth, military strength, and influence over the local seas.

current_state.summary_text: The Five Families of the West are pirate consortiums that exercise territorial and economic control over parts of the West Blue. Their current state following the great upheavals in world pirate power — including the fall of the Emperors — is uncertain.

factions.name: Great Helmet Pirates
factions.description: A pirate crew recognized for its internal cohesion and combat capability. It sails the seas in search of challenges, with a strong culture of mutual protection among its members and unconditional loyalty to the captain.

current_state.summary_text: The Great Helmet Pirates are a crew of loyal fighters, united around their captain. Details about their current location and activities are unknown.

factions.name: WCI 31
factions.description: A body of high-level chefs that operated in the kitchens of Whole Cake Island under Big Mom's empire. Organized into specialized kitchens each led by a head chef, the group was responsible for the elaborate culinary production that sustained the culture and rituals of the Totto Land territory.

current_state.summary_text: WCI 31 was the elite chef corps of the Whole Cake Island kitchens, operating under Big Mom's rule. With Big Mom's defeat in Wano and the dissolution of her empire, the group's institutional status is disbanded — what remains of its culinary structures and members is unknown.

factions.name: Boa Family
factions.description: A powerful pirate lineage whose internal code places loyalty and family honor above all else. It favors strategic approaches over direct confrontation and responds to any affront to its name with immediate retaliation. It is feared both for its intelligence and its martial capability.

current_state.summary_text: The Boa Family is a lineage known for its strategic cunning and combat capability, feared across the seas for its readiness to retaliate against threats to the family name swiftly and decisively. Its current state and whereabouts are unknown.

factions.name: Davy Family
factions.description: A legendary lineage among the bearers of the Will of the D., with roots tied to the mysterious Davy D. Jones — a cursed figure associated with the depths of the ocean. The family carries a deep connection to the sea and its oldest secrets, and its descendants tend to emerge as characters of exceptional weight in the history of the world.

current_state.summary_text: The Davy Family is a legendary lineage bearing the Will of the D., with ties to the mysterious Davy D. Jones and to historical figures of enormous significance such as Rocks D. Xebec. It does not constitute an active, organized faction — it is a scattered lineage whose members arise throughout history as forces of great impact.

factions.name: Big Mom Pirates
factions.description:
The Big Mom Pirates are a massive and relentless crew that rules through a mixture of fear, power, and family loyalty. Led by their unpredictable and terrifying captain, they dominate the seas through sheer force and an unbreakable sense of kinship among their members. Although their actions can be chaotic and violent, the crew thrives on family bonds, making them a dangerous and united force.

Captain:

Big Mom (Charlotte Linlin)

Members:

Charlotte Katakuri

Charlotte Smoothie

Charlotte Cracker

Charlotte Snack

Charlotte Oven

Charlotte Perospero

Charlotte Daifuku

Charlotte Mont-d'Or

Charlotte Compote

Charlotte Brûlée

Charlotte Galette

Charlotte Amande

Charlotte Praline

Charlotte Custard

Charlotte Pudding

current_state.summary_text: Big Mom fell in Wano (Kid and Law); the crew remains active in Totto Land, without their captain.

factions.name: Beasts Pirates
factions.description:
The Beasts Pirates are a terrifying and relentless crew led by a captain feared for his overwhelming power. They rule through force, intimidation, and loyalty to their leader, frequently recruiting powerful fighters and Zoan-type Devil Fruit users to expand their power. The crew is infamous for its brutality and ambition, making them one of the most dangerous forces feared by allies and enemies alike.


Captain:

Kaido

Members:

King

Queen

Jack

Who's Who

Sasaki

Ulti

Page One

X Drake

Apoo

Black Maria

Page Two

Kama

Holdem

Onimaru

Gyukimaru

current_state.summary_text: Defeated in Wano; Kaido fell, remnants scattered or captured.

factions.name: Thriller Bark Pirates
factions.description:
The Thriller Bark Pirates are a frightening and mysterious crew that uses shadows and strange powers to terrify their enemies. They are led by a captain who loves to play tricks and collect strong fighters. Despite their frightening appearance, they are cunning and dangerous on the seas.

Captain:

Gecko Moria

Notable Members:

Hogback

Oars

Absalom

Perona

current_state.summary_text: Dissolved; Moria reappeared at Hachinosu and is captured by the Blackbeard Pirates.

factions.name: Rocks Pirates
factions.description:
The Rocks Pirates were a legendary and feared crew that caused chaos across the seas. They were known for their power, ambition, and willingness to do anything to dominate the world. Many strong and dangerous pirates were part of this crew, making them one of the most notorious in history.

Captain:

Rocks D. Xebec

Notable Members:

Whitebeard (Edward Newgate)

Big Mom (Charlotte Linlin)

Kaido

Shiki

Captain John

Whitebeard's Crewmates

current_state.summary_text: Historical — disbanded at God Valley, decades ago.

factions.name: Roger Pirates
factions.description:
The Roger Pirates were a legendary crew known for their adventures and for discovering the final island, Laugh Tale. They were brave, strong, and full of daring spirit, inspiring fear and respect across the seas. Their captain led them with wisdom and strength, leaving a lasting legacy in the history of pirates.

Captain:

Gol D. Roger

Notable Members:

Silvers Rayleigh

Scopper Gaban

Shanks

Buggy

current_state.summary_text: Historical — disbanded before Roger's execution at Loguetown.

factions.name: Spade Pirates
factions.description:
The Spade Pirates were a strong and courageous crew known for their loyalty and combat skills. They sailed the seas in search of adventure and freedom, facing powerful enemies when necessary. Their captain led them with courage, earning respect from allies and enemies alike. The Spade Pirates were a strong and courageous crew known for their loyalty and combat skills. They sailed the seas in search of adventure and freedom, facing powerful enemies when necessary. Their captain led them with courage, earning respect from allies and enemies alike.

Captain:

Portgas D. Ace

Notable Members:

Izo

Jozu (affiliated later)

Other trusted crew members

current_state.summary_text: Historical — absorbed into the Whitebeard Pirates; Ace is dead.

factions.name: Baroque Works
factions.description:
Baroque Works is a secret criminal organization that seeks to gain power and wealth through illegal means. They operate in the shadows, using spies, assassins, and ingenious schemes to achieve their goals. The group is organized in pairs and follows a strict hierarchy, making them a dangerous force in the world of pirates and politics.

Members:

Crocodile (Mr. 0)

Nico Robin (Miss All Sunday)

Mr. 1 (Daz Bones)

Mr. 2 Bon Clay (Bentham)

Mr. 3 (Galdino)

Mr. 4

Mr. 5

Ms. Doublefinger

Ms. Goldenweek

Ms. Merry Christmas

current_state.summary_text: Dismantled after Alabasta; Crocodile now leads the Cross Guild alongside Mihawk and Buggy.

factions.name: Four Emperors
factions.description: After all the crimes you committed during your years as a pirate, and after facing countless powerful figures with your mighty and formidable crew, you have finally been recognized as one of the Four Emperors. Feared across all seas, the Marines dread your name. You have also allied yourself with other powerful figures, yet remain under the watch of the World Government.

current_state.summary_text: Current lineup post-Wano: Shanks, Blackbeard, Monkey D. Luffy, and Buggy. Kaido and Big Mom fell at Wano.

factions.name: Seven Warlords of the Sea
factions.description:
The Seven Warlords of the Sea are pirates of great renown chosen by the World Government to act as powerful hunters, backed by the World Government, of other pirates. Each Warlord receives a frozen bounty, official protection, and the freedom to rule their own territory, so long as they answer the World Government's call, and their mere presence can shift the balance of a battlefield. Although they hold an official title, the Warlords remain pirates at heart, following their own ambitions, commanding secret armies, and building hidden empires away from the Government's eyes.

Members:

Dracule Mihawk

Bartholomew Kuma

Crocodile

Donquixote Doflamingo

Boa Hancock

Jinbe

Gecko Moria

current_state.summary_text: System ABOLISHED by the World Government at the Reverie; former Warlords lost their immunity and are hunted by the Marines (Seraphim created to replace them).

items.name: Rapier
items.description: A thin sword used for thrusting. Fast, but not very strong.

current_state.summary_text: Generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Wooden Staff
items.description: A long rod. Simple, light, and good for defense.

current_state.summary_text: Generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Iron Spear
items.description: A long weapon with a sharp tip. Good for keeping distance.

current_state.summary_text: Generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Pitchfork
items.description: An agricultural tool used as a spear. Clumsy, but it works in a fight.

current_state.summary_text: Generic object of agricultural use and occasional use in combat; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Machete
items.description: A heavy blade. Cuts through plants and enemies alike.

current_state.summary_text: Generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Battle Axe
items.description: A large axe. Strong, but slow and heavy.

current_state.summary_text: Generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Throwing Knives
items.description: Small knives made for throwing. Weak, but fast and stealthy.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Wooden Club
items.description: A simple piece of wood. Cheap and easy to use.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Short Bow
items.description: A small bow. Not very powerful, but good for hunting.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Spiked Brass Knuckles
items.description: Metal over the knuckles. Makes punches stronger and more painful.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece; no unique specimen or canonical bearer is established by the work.

items.name: Murakumogiri
items.description: Murakumogiri is a legendary and massive naginata. Its wide, curved blade allows for powerful sweeping attacks capable of striking multiple enemies at once. The weapon has become an instrument of unparalleled power and precision, able to clash with other legendary blades.

current_state.summary_text: Murakumogiri is Edward Newgate's (Whitebeard's) naginata; he died at Marineford. After his death, the weapon's current whereabouts are not established by canon — its situation remains uncertain.

items.name: Punishers
items.description: The Punishers are a pair of gauntlets fitted with curved, sickle-shaped blades mounted on axles. They can be spun rapidly, generating shockwaves at the speed of sound that deal internal damage to opponents. These weapons are versatile, capable of both offensive and defensive maneuvers.

current_state.summary_text: The Punishers do not correspond to a named weapon established in One Piece canon; the current bearer and whereabouts are unknown, or the weapon is of non-canonical origin.

items.name: Tsuji Shibai
items.description: Tsuji Shibai resembles a giant ink brush, but functions as a katana. It allows the user to create drawings and illusions to confuse opponents while delivering fast and unpredictable strikes. Its creativity and versatility make it deadly in skilled hands.

current_state.summary_text: Tsuji Shibai does not correspond to a named weapon established in One Piece canon; its bearer and whereabouts are not determined by the work.

items.name: Warabide Sword
items.description: The Warabide Sword is a special long sword with a blade made of straw. It can be extended and shaped in combat, offering great versatility. Considered a Meito, it is a high-quality blade known for its unique design and adaptability.

current_state.summary_text: The Warabide Sword (Warabide-to) is wielded by Kozuki Oden in canonical flashbacks; after his death, the weapon has no current bearer established by canon — whereabouts uncertain.

items.name: Cutelo Ace
items.description: Forged in the flames of a forgotten island and tempered by time, the Cutelo Ace is a legendary saber once wielded by the Pirate King himself. Its golden hilt is wrapped in aged crimson cloth, and its slightly curved blade shines with a splendor that seems to vibrate with power.

current_state.summary_text: The Cutelo Ace does not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; no bearer or whereabouts are determined by the work.

items.name: Peacock Cutters
items.description: The Peacock Cutters are unique weapons consisting of small sharp jewels attached to wires. Resembling the eye-spots on a peacock's tail feathers, they are designed for precise and rapid attacks. The user wraps one end of the wire around a finger and swings the jewel to create a spinning, cutting disc. This allows for quick and graceful strikes that can entangle or cut opponents with remarkable precision.

current_state.summary_text: The Peacock Cutters do not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; bearer and whereabouts are undetermined.

items.name: Kirisame
items.description: Kirisame is a curved blade shaped like a shark's dorsal fin, optimized for underwater combat. Its design allows for quick and lethal strikes, making it effective in aquatic environments. The blade's unique curvature helps cut through water resistance, allowing the user to move and attack with enhanced speed and efficiency below the surface.

current_state.summary_text: Kirisame does not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; its bearer and whereabouts are not determined by the work.

items.name: Brutamontes Axe
items.description: The Brutamontes Axe is a massive and heavy battle axe, renowned for its raw power and devastating impact. Its impressive size and weight make it formidable in combat, capable of delivering crushing blows that can break through defenses and incapacitate opponents. The axe's design emphasizes brute strength, allowing the user to cleave through obstacles and enemies with overwhelming force.

current_state.summary_text: The Brutamontes Axe does not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; bearer and whereabouts are undetermined.

items.name: Yakuza Kasen
items.description: Yakuza Kasen is a high-quality shikomizue, crafted with great skill. Combining elegance with lethal precision, it features a balanced blade perfect for quick and deadly strikes. The sword's design allows it to function well both as a support cane and as a concealed weapon for combat.

current_state.summary_text: Yakuza Kasen does not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; bearer and whereabouts are undetermined.

items.name: Kosho
items.description: Kosho is a finely crafted katana, ranked among the 50 Skillful Grade swords. Designed for fast and precise attacks, it emphasizes balance, cutting power, and reliability in combat. The sword's craftsmanship ensures durability and sharpness, allowing the user to execute swift strikes with confidence and effectiveness.

current_state.summary_text: Kosho does not correspond to an established named weapon in the One Piece canon; bearer and whereabouts are undetermined.

items.name: Terry Sword
items.description: The Terry Sword is a sword of unique design that combines strength and tradition. Its construction allows for powerful strikes while maintaining balance and agility. The sword's design reflects a blend of durability and elegance, making it suited for both offensive and defensive maneuvers in combat.

current_state.summary_text: The Terry Sword does not correspond to any established named weapon in the One Piece canon; its bearer or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Soto Muso
items.description: Soto Muso is a large and simple sword, consisting only of a blade, hilt, and scabbard, with no hand guard. The hilt features dark diamond patterns, emphasizing practicality over ornamentation. Its minimalist design allows for unimpeded movement and quick strikes, focusing on the user's skill and precision rather than the weapon's adornments.

current_state.summary_text: Soto Muso does not correspond to any established named weapon in the One Piece canon; its bearer or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Shusui
items.description: Shusui is a legendary black blade ranked as one of the 21 Great Grade swords. Renowned for its durability and cutting power, it is a symbol of tradition and mastery in swordsmanship. The sword's dark hue and craftsmanship make it a prized possession, reflecting the rich history and skill of its makers.

current_state.summary_text: Shusui, a Great Grade black blade, was returned by Roronoa Zoro to Ryuma's grave in Wano during the events of the Wano Country arc; since then, the sword has remained in Wano as a national treasure, and Zoro took up Enma in its place.

items.name: Shirauo
items.description: Shirauo is a sleek and narrow sword designed for quick, fluid strikes. Its aerodynamic form allows for precise attacks and agile handling. The sword's design emphasizes speed and agility, enabling the user to execute swift maneuvers and outpace opponents in combat.

current_state.summary_text: Shirauo does not correspond to any named weapon established in One Piece canon; its wielder or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Raiu
items.description: Raiu is a sword named thunderstrike, built to deliver fast and powerful blows. Its design emphasizes speed and impact, making it well suited for aggressive combat. The sword's construction allows for rapid strikes that can overwhelm opponents, reflecting the force and unpredictability of a storm.

current_state.summary_text: Raiu does not correspond to any named weapon established in One Piece canon; its wielder or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Nakiri Swords
items.description: Nakiri Swords are twin blades crafted for quick, coordinated strikes. Their design allows for seamless combos and precise attacks in close-quarters combat. The swords' balanced construction ensures fluid movement, enabling the user to execute rapid strike sequences with precision and efficiency.

current_state.summary_text: Nakiri Swords do not correspond to any named weapon established in One Piece canon; their wielder or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Sorcery Clima-Tact
items.description: A further evolution of the weather staff that combines Weatheria technology with a compact design and immense versatility. It can extend and contract at will, generate a variety of weather phenomena, and even perform utility tasks beyond combat. This version transforms the staff into a true magic wand of climate manipulation.

current_state.summary_text: The Sorcery Clima-Tact is Nami's current weapon, obtained with knowledge from Weatheria; Nami wields it as her primary weapon in the post-Egghead era.

items.name: Cerberus Sword
items.description: A brutal blade that was animated by a Mythical Zoan Devil Fruit to take the form of a three-headed dog. The sword's spine splits into three sharp fangs near the guard, giving each strike a rending and tearing effect. When the user channels their fighting spirit into it, the heads roar with a burst of energy, creating shockwaves that resemble a monster charging forward. This weapon is feared because each blow feels like an attack from multiple blades at once.

current_state.summary_text: The Cerberus Sword does not correspond to any named weapon established in One Piece canon; its wielder or whereabouts cannot be determined based on canonical sources.

items.name: Magnet-Magnet Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate and control magnetic forces to attract or repel metal at will. The user is able to draw in metal weapons — swords, guns, cannons, even metal parts of ships — to disarm opponents or form armor, weapons, and mechanical limbs from nearby metal scrap. They can also repel metal projectiles or metal-containing attacks back at attackers, turning enemy weapons against them.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Magnetic Manipulation — The user can generate and control magnetic fields to attract or repel metal objects.

Metal Attraction — The user pulls weapons or metal objects toward themselves or enemies, disarming or pinning them.

Metal Repulsion — The user can push metal objects with great force, using them as projectiles or to create barriers.

Magnetic Levitation — The user manipulates magnetic fields to levitate themselves or others for movement or evasive maneuvers.

Magnetic Shield — The user creates a protective barrier by repelling metal attacks or projectiles.

Magnetized Punch — The user enhances their punches by magnetizing their fists, increasing impact and causing magnetic disruption.

Metal Binding — The user can magnetically restrain opponents by controlling handcuffs, chains, or metal weapons attached to them.

current_state.summary_text: The Magnet-Magnet Fruit was wielded by Eustass Kid. Following his defeat in Wano and subsequent defeat at the hands of Shanks in the North Sea, Kid is out of action; the current whereabouts of the fruit are not confirmed by canon.

items.name: Straw-Straw Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create and control straw at will, forming bundles, threads, or masses of straw from nothing, or drawing straw out of surrounding surfaces. The user can shape the straw into weapons, shields, or even dolls and puppets. They can send straw threads out as whips or bind enemies, create straw constructs to trap or restrain enemies, or even cover themselves in straw armor.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Straw Creation — The user can generate and manipulate straw from their body to use in various ways.

Straw Binding — The user can entangle enemies with tightly wound straw to immobilize or restrict movement.

Straw Whip — Using straw threads as flexible whips for long-range attacks.

Straw Shield — The user can weave straw into a shield to block or deflect attacks.

Straw Clone — Creating straw duplicates to confuse or distract opponents.

Straw Spikes — Forming sharp straw spikes to pierce or stab enemies.

Straw Armor — The user can coat themselves with dense straw for limited protection against physical attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Straw-Straw Fruit does not correspond to any established fruit in One Piece canon; its wielder and whereabouts are unknown.

items.name: Ton-Ton Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to drastically increase their body weight at will, turning them into a Super-Heavy Human. With it, the user can come crashing down with unbearable force, crushing enemies or breaking the ground beneath them by becoming so heavy they strike like a meteor. Their weight becomes a weapon — even a simple fall or stomp carries the force of tons. But the power has drawbacks — if they miss their target, the full weight hits the ground, injuring themselves or wasting the attack.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Body Enlargement — The user can enlarge any part or all of their body to an enormous size, increasing their strength and reach.

Ton-Ton Crush — A powerful punch delivered with the enlarged fist, capable of causing massive damage.

Ton-Ton Stomp — Using enlarged feet to stomp the ground, creating shockwaves that can repel opponents.

Ton-Ton Grab — Enlarged hands allow the user to grab and crush enemies or objects with tremendous force.

Ton-Ton Throw — The user can hurl large objects or enemies with increased power due to their enlarged size.

Size Manipulation — The user can selectively enlarge or shrink parts of their body for both offense and defense.

current_state.summary_text: The Ton-Ton Fruit was wielded by Machvise, an officer of the Donquixote Pirates. With Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, the current whereabouts of Machvise and the fruit are unconfirmed by canon.

items.name: Swim-Swim Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to swim not through water, but through solid matter — ground, walls, earth — as if rock or soil were liquid. With this power, the user becomes a Free-Swimming Human, capable of diving into the ground, traveling under walls, emerging from unexpected places, infiltrating or escaping from fortresses, ambushing enemies from below or escaping undetected, and even gliding upward through terrain to reach elevated positions with momentum.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Water Creation — The user can create and control water, manipulating it at will for attacks or defense.

Water Manipulation — The user can shape water into various forms, such as jets, waves, or weapons.

Water Clones — The ability to create clones made of water to confuse and attack opponents.

Hydro Whip — Forming water into whip-like weapons for long-range attacks.

Water Barrier — Creating a shield of water to block enemy attacks.

Water Healing — Using the properties of water to heal wounds and recover stamina.

Tsunami Wave — Generating a massive wave of water to sweep away multiple enemies.

current_state.summary_text: The Swim-Swim Fruit was wielded by Senor Pink, an officer of the Donquixote Pirates. With Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, the current whereabouts of Senor Pink and the fruit are unconfirmed by canon.

items.name: Spin-Spin Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to spin or rotate any part of their body, including limbs, clothing, and even hair, at superhuman speeds, turning them into a Rotation Human. With this spinning power, they can turn limbs into rotating weapons or propellers, deliver strikes like blades, spin to create powerful gusts or shockwaves, or even spin to fly or move with incredible speed across the ground or through the air.

Rotating Attack — The user can spin their whole body or parts of it at high speed to create destructive attacks capable of cutting through objects.

Gyroscope Spin — A focused rotational movement that can generate powerful shockwaves to repel enemies.

Rotating Defense — Spinning rapidly to deflect incoming attacks or reduce damage.

Whirlwind Cut — Spinning while using sharp objects or limbs to cut multiple targets around the user.

Cyclone Throw — Using the spinning motion to grab and hurl enemies with increased force and range.

Spinning Charge — The user rushes forward while spinning rapidly, knocking down anyone in their path.

current_state.summary_text: The Spin-Spin Fruit does not correspond to any established fruit in the One Piece canon; its wielder and whereabouts are unknown.

items.name: Pop-Pop Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to make any inorganic object they touch, or even parts of their own body, swell until they rupture and violently explode, turning them into a Rupture Human. With this power, the user can make metal weapons, walls, machines, or other solid items expand and shatter, demolishing defenses or creating chaos with sudden explosions.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Met Punc — The user inflates their helmet and causes it to explode, launching shrapnel toward the target.

Jirai Punc — The user implants swelling points on a surface; if touched, these points will explode.

Punc Bala — The user inflates bullets, causing them to float like balloons; these inflated bullets detonate on contact.

Punc Rock Fest — The user inflates the ground around them and then causes it to explode, dealing massive destruction to the surrounding area.

Catapult Punc — The user fires several inflated bullets at the enemy using a firing mechanism installed in their arms.

Bracchium — The user inflates their wrist devices, causing them to swell and explode.

Punc Hair — The user inflates their hair, turning each strand into sharp needles, which they then fire at their opponents.

Fashion Punc — The user swells up, transforming into a giant balloon-like appearance. If attacked in any way while in this form, their body causes a massive explosion.

Punc Rock Super Arena — A stronger version of Punc Rock Fest; the user causes an area to swell to great proportions before detonating it.

current_state.summary_text: The Pop-Pop Fruit was wielded by Gladius, an officer of the Donquixote Pirates. With Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, the current whereabouts of Gladius and the fruit are unconfirmed by canon.

items.name: Hobby-Hobby Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform any living person or animal they touch into a toy, and when they do, everyone else instantly forgets that person ever existed — memories of them vanish as if erased from history. The user can reshape victims into dolls, stuffed animals, toy soldiers, or any toy form of their choosing, stripping them of their powers, weapons, and normal physical strength. Once transformed, the user can bind the toy to a contract so that it must obey all orders, making them loyal soldiers or servants for life.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Contract: After transforming a target into a toy, the user can impose specific rules the toy must follow, such as "you will not harm humans" or "you will obey all orders given to you."

Little Black Bears: The user rapidly moves their hands to touch multiple targets at once, transforming them into black stuffed bears.

Nutcracker Doll: The user transforms victims into giant nutcracker dolls composed of several people, possessing tremendous durability and biting power.

current_state.summary_text: The Hobby-Hobby Fruit was wielded by Sugar, an officer of the Donquixote Pirates. With Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa, the current whereabouts of Sugar and the fruit are unconfirmed by canon.

items.name: Ripple-Ripple Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user and anything they touch to ripple, flutter, or wave as if made of fabric: solid metal, wood, or weapons become flexible and ripple like a flag at the user's will. The user can bend, flatten, or fold rigid objects, turning swords into whips, metal shields into undulating barriers, or even reshaping terrain into flexible, rippling surfaces. Metals and materials still retain their original strength and sharpness, but their form becomes fluid, granting the user enormous versatility in combat and creativity.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Wave Pulse: The user emits waves of energy that disturb the internal organs of living beings, causing intense pain and incapacitation without inflicting external injuries.

Healing Touch: The user can direct wave energy to accelerate the healing process of themselves or others.

Wave Projection: The user can send wave energy through surfaces and objects to affect targets at a distance.

current_state.summary_text: The Ripple-Ripple Fruit does not correspond to any established fruit in the One Piece canon; its wielder and whereabouts are unknown.

items.name: Stick-Stick Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate and control rods or rigid stick-like objects at will, sprouting them from their body or materializing them from nothing. The user can form rods into weapons such as spears, clubs, staffs, projectiles, throwing sticks or darts, barricades or barriers to block attacks, or even support structures like bridges, ladders, or scaffolding on demand.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Rod Extension: The user can extend their limbs or body parts like rods, increasing reach for attacks and grabs.

Rod Hardening: The user can harden parts of their body to increase durability and offensive power.

Rod Projectile: The user can launch hardened rods as projectiles to strike enemies at a distance.

Rod Whip: The user can manipulate elongated rods like whips for versatile attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Stick-Stick Fruit does not correspond to any established fruit in the One Piece canon; its wielder and whereabouts are unknown.

items.name: Sew-Sew Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to sew or unsew anything, living or not, as if stitching fabric. The user can pin enemies to walls or the ground by sewing them in place, immobilizing them, stitch together objects of any material — even metal or stone — reattach broken limbs or torn items, mend wounds or repair broken equipment, or undo the stitching to restore the original state.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Needle Threading: The user can generate and control threads or needles from their body to sew objects or wounds.

Thread Binding: The user can use threads to tie down or restrain opponents with precision.

Thread Repair: The user can fix torn or damaged materials, including clothing and flesh.

Thread Manipulation: The user can control threads remotely for attacks or to manipulate objects.

Seam Cut: Using sharp threads as blades to cut through enemies or obstacles.

current_state.summary_text: The Sew-Sew Fruit is not an established canon fruit in the One Piece series; its current wielder is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Whip-Whip Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to command anything they strike with a whip, living or not, implanting obedience or control in the target, bending weapons, objects, or even the terrain to their will. The user becomes a master manipulator, whipping a building and making its walls move, cracking a stone floor and making it crawl, or striking metal to force it to take shapes and forms under their command.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Whip Strike – The user extends their hair or limbs into whip shapes and attacks enemies with high speed and flexibility.

Whip Barrage – Releases a rapid flurry of whip attacks in multiple directions.

Trap Whip – A whip attack intended to capture or restrain opponents by coiling around them.

Whip Spin – Spins the whips over a wide area to repel or deflect incoming attacks.

Whip Grab – Uses a whip to grab surfaces or enemies to pull them closer or move across terrain.

current_state.summary_text: The Whip-Whip Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Chain-Chain Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to sprout and control chains from their body at will; arms, legs, even fingertips can extend like metal chains controlled by the user's will. The user can attack with chains like whips or maces, swing or grapple across terrain, bind or restrain enemies, form chain armor, or use the chains as piercing or blunt weapons.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Chain Bind – The user conjures chains to tie or restrain targets, effectively immobilizing them.

Chain Strike – Invokes large, heavy chains to attack enemies with crushing force.

Chain Prison – Encloses enemies within a cage-shaped chain formation, imprisoning them.

Hook Shot – Fires a hook-tipped chain to pull objects or enemies toward the user.

Chain Shield – Forms a wall of spinning chains to block or deflect incoming attacks.

Chain Launch – Uses chains to launch the user across distances or to grapple onto structures.

current_state.summary_text: The Chain-Chain Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Voice-Voice Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to manipulate sound and voices: they can perfectly mimic or steal any voice they have heard, human, animal, even mechanical, and then speak using that voice as if it were their own. The user can disguise themselves, impersonate others, deceive enemies by issuing orders in someone else's voice, or sow confusion and chaos by mimicking sounds from afar.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Voice Amplification – The user amplifies their voice to deafening levels, disorienting enemies or shattering objects.

Echo Blast – Releases a concentrated burst of sonic energy from their voice to repel or damage targets.

Imitation Call – Mimics any voice the user has heard, perfect for deception tactics, infiltration, or confusion.

Soundwave Push – Emits a powerful sonic wave to repel multiple enemies at once.

Sonic Whisper – Projects the user's voice directly into someone's ear at a distance, allowing secret communication or psychological attacks.

Vocal Trap – Manipulates sounds in an area to create false footsteps, voices, or noises to deceive or lure enemies.

current_state.summary_text: The Voice-Voice Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Molten-Molten Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create, control, or transform parts of their body and nearby materials into molten metal or melted rock. With this power, the user can melt metal or earth at will and reshape molten matter into weapons, armor, molten projectiles, or flows of lava and liquid metal to attack, burn, or flow over obstacles.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Molten Fist – The user launches a massive, fast punch made entirely of molten material, capable of incinerating nearly everything it touches.

Molten Eruption – Ejects molten magma from the body in bursts, creating deadly shockwaves and lava splatter.

Molten Rain – Sends high-temperature molten projectiles raining down over a wide area, devastating everything below.

Molten Current – The user releases a continuous river of molten substance from their limbs, reshaping the battlefield with lava.

Lava Body – Passively allows the user's body to become molten, melting weapons and burning attackers on contact.

Molten Meteor – Forms and hurls a massive, concentrated sphere of molten material at high speed, causing a large explosion on impact.

Molten Wall – Creates a wall of lava to block attacks or trap enemies.

Volcanic Surge – A wave of lava launched through the ground to sweep away enemies and terrain.

Molten Strike – A short-range superheated punch or kick with molten power, piercing through defenses.

current_state.summary_text: The Molten-Molten Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Meld-Meld Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to merge or blend into any surface or object they touch, including stone, metal, ground, wood, walls — essentially any inanimate material — and while merged, they can move through it as if the material were liquid, travel inside it, reshape it from within, or emerge anywhere within that connected material. The user can hide inside walls or floors, pass through barriers, travel undetected through soil or solid structures, ambush enemies by emerging behind them, or reshape the merged material.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Merge – The user can blend their body into solid surfaces such as walls, floors, or even ships, allowing for stealth or surprise attacks.

Phase Slide – While partially merged, the user can move through a solid material like a liquid, allowing travel through buildings or terrain undetected.

Trap Merge – The user can leave part of their body inside a surface and then suddenly emerge to grab or attack an unsuspecting target.

Surface Lock – Merges part of an enemy's body into a solid object to immobilize or pin them temporarily.

Emergency Merge – Used defensively, the user quickly sinks into the floor or wall to evade attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Meld-Meld Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Jacket-Jacket Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a wearable jacket; when someone puts on that jacket, the user becomes a skin-jacket and gains control over the wearer's body and physical abilities. The user can let a stronger or larger ally wear them, borrowing that person's strength, size, and capabilities, transforming into a living suit of flesh, fabric, or flesh-fabric. While worn, the user can move and speak, and control the wearer's every action, turning even a timid or weak person into a powerful fighter under their direct command.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Human Jacket Fusion – The user transforms into a living jacket and leaps onto another person. Once worn, the user takes control of the host's body and movements while enhancing their physical power, depending on the host's strength.

Combat Dominance – While merged, the user can perform complex combat techniques using the host's body with expert precision, often improving their fighting efficiency.

Forced Control – If the host is unwilling, the user can still override their will, though this may lead to resistance or unstable control.

Relay Switch – The user can detach from one host and immediately leap onto another, allowing smooth transitions between fighters during battle or infiltration.

current_state.summary_text: The Jacket-Jacket Fruit (Fuku Fuku no Mi) is held by Nefertari Cobra in canon — however, the description presented here diverges from the established canon power. The canon bearer of the Fuku Fuku no Mi is Nefertari Cobra, but the fruit described here, with the power to transform into a jacket and control the host, does not correspond to any known canon fruit; current bearer uncertain.

items.name: Love-Love Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to turn to stone anyone who feels lust, love, or deep attraction toward them, or even petrify objects on contact, effectively freezing targets in place as stone statues. The user is capable of firing heart-shaped beams, kisses, or other charm-based attacks that exploit desire or passion to immobilize or neutralize enemies; even weapons, metal, or inanimate objects struck by the right move can be turned to stone.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Love-Love Beam – The user forms a heart with their hands and fires a heart-shaped beam. Anyone who harbors feelings of lust for the user and is struck by this beam will be turned to stone. Petrification can be avoided if the target is unaffected by lust or is distracted by other emotions such as fear or pain.

Awakening – The user blows a kiss toward a person or persons previously turned to stone by the Love-Love Beam. The kiss produces small hearts that envelop them, restoring them to their normal state.

Pistol Kiss – The user blows a kiss, creating a heart-shaped projectile that can be aimed at a victim using their index finger. This attack can be combined with Armament Haki to increase its power.

Slave Arrow – The user breathes deeply and blows a large heart. They then pull the heart back with one hand and, with a snap like a bubble bursting, hundreds of arrows are fired in a barrage toward opponents. These arrows can petrify anyone and anything they hit, including non-living objects such as cannonballs.

Femur Perfume – The user performs a handstand and spins to deliver a series of kicks. Anything struck by these kicks, including inanimate objects and living beings, instantly turns to stone and shatters from the force of the attack. Unlike other techniques, only the immediate area around the point of contact turns to stone.

current_state.summary_text: The Love-Love Fruit (Mero Mero no Mi) is held by Boa Hancock, empress of Amazon Lily. Following the events of Egghead, Hancock remains the leader of the Kuja and the confirmed bearer of the fruit.

items.name: Smelt-Smelt Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to become a living furnace; they can consume metal ore or metal objects, melt them inside their body, and then reshape the molten metal into weapons, armor, or tools at will, forming swords, shields, heavy weapons, or even constructions from smelted ore. The user can draw molten metal from inside their body to attack, coat themselves in molten armor, pour molten metal over enemies to burn or encase them, or quickly forge metal equipment for attack, defense, or utility.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Molten Weapon Forge – The user consumes ore and melts it inside their body, then shapes it into weapons such as swords, axes, or spears, which can be drawn from their abdomen.

Molten Current – The user releases molten metal from their body, creating currents that can melt structures or incapacitate opponents with intense heat.

Metal Armor Coating – The user coats their body in solidified metal, increasing durability and enabling offensive and defensive maneuvers.

Molten Projectile Launch – The user fires molten metal projectiles from their body, which can solidify on impact or remain molten to cause additional damage.

Ore Consumption – By consuming large quantities of ore, the user increases their size and strength, becoming more formidable in battle.

Heat Emission – The user emits intense heat from their body, capable of melting nearby objects and repelling close-range attacks.

Steam Propulsion – By releasing pressurized steam from their body, the user can propel themselves into the air or move rapidly across the battlefield.

current_state.summary_text: The Smelt-Smelt Fruit is not an established canon fruit in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Gigantify-Gigantify Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to dramatically increase the size of their body at will, transforming into an imposing giant whose reach, strength, and mass become enormous. As the user transforms into a Giant, they can stomp or punch with enough force to crush the ground, break walls, uproot trees, or sweep enemies aside like ragdolls.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Gigantify Transformation — The user can transform parts or all of their body to a gigantic size, enormously increasing their physical strength and reach.

Colossal Punch — Using their giant limbs, the user delivers powerful punches capable of causing massive destruction.

Giant Stomp — The user stomps the ground with overwhelming force, creating shockwaves that can repel enemies and damage the terrain.

Extended Reach Strike — The user stretches their giant arms or legs to attack distant targets with increased power.

Gigantic Defense — Their enlarged body can act as a shield to protect allies or block attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Gigantify-Gigantify Fruit is not a canon fruit established in One Piece; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in the official canon.

items.name: Dagger
items.description: A small knife. Easy to conceal, quick in close-quarters combat.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon widely used in the world of One Piece by pirates, Marines, and civilians. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer.

items.name: War Hammer
items.description: A large hammer. Can crush, but is very heavy.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer.

items.name: Spiked Club
items.description: A wooden club with nails. More lethal than plain wood.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer.

items.name: Morning Star
items.description: A spiked ball on a rod. Heavy and dangerous.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer.

items.name: Hand Axe
items.description: A hand axe, perfectly balanced for close-quarters combat, can be an incredibly lethal weapon. When wielded with skill and precision, it has the power to bring down even the most formidable opponents.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer.

items.name: Cat Claws
items.description: A pair of black fur gloves fitted with full-length katana blades at the tip of each finger. They are used for swift and lethal attacks.

current_state.summary_text: A generic weapon present in the world of One Piece. No unique, named specimen with an established canon bearer; gloves with blades are associated with combat styles of characters such as Kaku (giraffe claws, not exactly this type), but no specific bearer of this generic item is traceable through canon.

items.name: Mud-Mud Fruit
items.description: Transforms the user's body into liquid mud, allowing them to slide through crevices, bind enemies, and launch heavy earth-based attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Mud-Mud Fruit is not an established fruit in the One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or the fruit may not exist as an official canon item.

items.name: Sandai Kitetsu
items.description: A cursed katana with a thirst for blood. Sharp and deadly, but dangerous even to the one who wields it. A blade that defies fate with every strike.

current_state.summary_text: The Sandai Kitetsu is with Roronoa Zoro, who has carried it as one of his three swords since the events in Loguetown.

items.name: Wado Ichimonji
items.description: An immaculate white katana forged by a master. Revered as one of the 21 Great Grade swords, it represents unwavering will and legacy. Fast, precise, and unyielding.

current_state.summary_text: The Wado Ichimonji is with Roronoa Zoro, who wields it in honor of Kuina's legacy since the beginning of his journey as a pirate.

items.name: Enma
items.description: A legendary sword that involuntarily draws out its wielder's Haki. It demands great control, but offers immense cutting power capable of leaving marks on even the most powerful foes.

current_state.summary_text: Enma is with Roronoa Zoro, who received it from Kozuki Hiyori in Wano and has wielded it as one of his three swords since Kaido's defeat in Wano.

items.name: Kabuto
items.description: A long-range slingshot-like weapon enhanced with Dials and Pop Greens. Capable of firing explosive plants, projectiles, and unique ammunition with immense precision.

current_state.summary_text: Kabuto is with Usopp, who has used it as his primary weapon since its creation and improvement throughout the saga.

items.name: Rumble Ball Transformation
items.description: Transforms the user's body into different monstrous or hybrid forms, each with unique combat styles such as horns, claws, or brute strength. A powerhouse of shape-shifting.

current_state.summary_text: The Rumble Ball is used by Tony Tony Chopper to boost and expand the transformations of his Hito Hito no Mi; Chopper remains with the Straw Hat Pirates.

items.name: Soul-Based Sword
items.description: A blade concealed within a cane. Combined with soul manipulation, it enables icy cuts, sound wave-based attacks, and freezing soul energy strikes.

current_state.summary_text: The sword hidden within the cane is the signature weapon of Brook, violinist of the Straw Hat Pirates, who wields it in combination with the powers of his Yomi Yomi no Mi.

items.name: Gryphon
items.description: An elegant saber with a red hilt and a slightly curved blade, Gryphon is a notable sword renowned for its unparalleled sharpness and strength. The blade can cut through almost anything with ease and is even more powerful with Haki mastery.

current_state.summary_text: Gryphon is the saber of Shanks, the Emperor, who wields it as his primary weapon.

items.name: Jitte
items.description: A large, heavy jutte with a thick metal shaft and a hook near the handle. This weapon is specially designed to combat Devil Fruit users, as the hook contains Seastone, which nullifies Devil Fruit powers on contact.

current_state.summary_text: The Seastone Jitte is the signature weapon of Momonga or, more notably, of Smoker — however, the most iconic Seastone Jitte in canon belongs to Smoker, who uses it as a Marine Vice Admiral.

items.name: Hollow-Hollow Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create spectral ghosts known as Hollows, under their control. The user can summon a swarm of these ghosts at will, shaping them into anything from small and sneaky ghosts to larger and more powerful specters. The ghosts float, pass through walls and solid matter, and cannot be harmed by physical attacks.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.
Moves:

Invisible Cloak – The user makes their body or parts of it invisible to evade attacks or go unnoticed by enemies.

Invisible Weapons – The user can make weapons or items they are holding invisible, confusing opponents.

Invisible Touch – The user can extend invisibility to others by touching them.

Silent Approach – Combining invisibility with stealth tactics to move silently and unseen.

current_state.summary_text: The Hollow-Hollow Fruit is carried by Perona (Ghost Princess), who has used it since her introduction at Thriller Bark. Her current whereabouts after Egghead are not explicitly reconfirmed by recent canon, but Perona remains its known bearer.

items.name: Clear-Clear Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to become invisible at will and make invisible anything they touch, including clothes, weapons, or small objects. The user can walk unnoticed through crowds, slip past guards, eavesdrop on conversations, infiltrate enemy ships, or set up ambushes without being detected. Invisible weapons and items can attack or move unseen, and small equipment or even boats can disappear to aid in stealthy travel or escapes.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.
Moves:

Transparent Form – The user makes their body completely clear and translucent, making detection extremely difficult.

Light Refraction – The user bends light around themselves to create illusions or blind enemies.

Intangible Defense – The user shifts to a state where physical attacks pass through harmlessly.

Clear Strike – The user condenses their transparent form to deliver a focused, powerful attack with high speed and precision.

current_state.summary_text: The Clear-Clear Fruit (Suke Suke no Mi) is carried by Shiryu of the Rain, one of the ten Titanic Captains of the Blackbeard Pirates, who obtained the fruit after killing Absalom.

items.name: Biscuit-Biscuit Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to summon and shape living biscuits at will; with a palm or even a gesture they create biscuit material from nothing, molding it into whatever they need, from weapons and shields to humanoid soldiers of solid biscuit that fight, protect, or carry the user. The user becomes a Biscuit Human, capable of generating nearly unlimited biscuit constructs, covering themselves in armor made of hardened biscuits, building walls or weapons, or sending legions of biscuit golems to overwhelm enemies.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.
Moves:

Biscuit Creation — The user can create and manipulate biscuit-like structures, crafting weapons, barriers, or projectiles from biscuit material.

Biscuit Armor — The user can harden their body or parts of it with biscuit material for enhanced defense or strength.

Biscuit Constructs — The user can create biscuit clones or constructs to assist in battle or for utility purposes.

current_state.summary_text: The Biscuit-Biscuit Fruit is carried by Charlotte Cracker, Sweet Commander of the Big Mom Pirates. Following Big Mom's defeat at Wano, Cracker's current status and that of the fruit are not explicitly reconfirmed by canon — he remains its known bearer.

items.name: Butter-Butter Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create and control butter at will, shaping it into sticky, viscous butter that can trap or capture anything — limbs, weapons, even entire bodies. The user becomes a Butter Human, capable of spreading butter on the ground to make opponents slip, flooding an area with adhesive butter to immobilize enemies, or wrapping enemies in thick walls of butter to trap or restrain them.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Butterfly — The user creates and manipulates a giant butterfly made of butter, which can be used to trap or attack enemies.

Butter Ray — The user fires concentrated beams of butter to immobilize or damage opponents.

Butter Clone — The user creates clones made of butter to confuse or overwhelm enemies.

Butter Trap — The user spreads slippery butter over the ground or surfaces to make enemies lose their balance.

current_state.summary_text: The Butter-Butter Fruit does not correspond to an established canonical fruit in One Piece. Its current bearer is unknown or the fruit does not exist in the canon of the work.

items.name: Wringer-Wringer Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to drain all liquid from anything — living beings, objects, even the air inside a fruit or a container — simply by squeezing or wringing as if wringing out a wet cloth. The user becomes a Dehydration Human, capable of drawing moisture from opponents to dehydrate them, weaken them, or even leave them as withered husks; they can also drain liquids from weapons, objects, or terrain.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Wringer Wrap — The user tightly wraps opponents with elastic wringer-like bands, immobilizing them.

Wringer Crush — The user compresses and wrings objects or enemies with powerful twisting force.

Wringer Whip — The user extends wringer-like tentacles to whip or grab opponents at a distance.

Wringer Spin — The user spins rapidly to create a twisting vortex, damaging anything caught inside.

current_state.summary_text: The Wringer-Wringer Fruit does not correspond to an established canonical fruit in One Piece. Its current bearer is unknown or the fruit does not exist in the canon of the work.

items.name: Memory-Memory Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to access a person's mind and manipulate memories — they can visualize, steal, erase, or alter them at will, making someone forget who they are, what they know, or believe in events that never happened. The user becomes a Memory Human, capable of rewriting reality in the minds of others: erasing past traumas, fabricating false histories, eliminating secrets, or implanting lies so convincing that the victim believes them as truth.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Memory Retrieval — The user can extract and relive memories from themselves or others, accessing detailed past experiences.

Memory Erasure — The user can erase specific memories from a person's mind, causing amnesia or confusion.

Memory Transfer — The user can transfer memories between individuals, instantly sharing knowledge or secrets.

Memory Manipulation — The user can alter or distort memories to change a person's perception of events.

current_state.summary_text: The Memory-Memory Fruit does not correspond to an established canonical fruit in One Piece. Its current bearer is unknown or the fruit does not exist in the canon of the work.

items.name: Cook-Cook Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform ordinary objects into edible food at will. The user becomes a Food Human, with the power to transform logs, stones, metal, and any non-living object into meals, baked goods, or other forms of nourishment. With this ability, the user can feed themselves or allies indefinitely, create food from nothing when resources are scarce, or transform dangerous objects into harmless nutrition.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Kitchen Knife Slash — The user creates sharp slashing attacks resembling kitchen knives made from their body or energy to cut opponents.

Food Creation — The user can instantly create edible food from their body or surroundings to feed themselves or allies.

Flavor Enhancement — The user can enhance the taste and nutritional value of food, boosting allies' stamina and morale.

Grease Slide — The user produces slippery grease from their body to slide rapidly or cause opponents to lose their balance.

current_state.summary_text: The Cook-Cook Fruit does not correspond to an established canonical fruit in One Piece. Its current bearer is unknown or the fruit does not exist in the canon of the work.

items.name: Calm-Calm Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to silence all sounds around them: they can create a zone of absolute silence or cancel any noise they or others make, rendering footsteps, voices, explosions, and even gunshots completely soundless. As a Silence Human, the user can pass undetected through enemy territory, speak in private, eavesdrop on conversations, or launch surprise attacks without warning.

You must remember to inform them of their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of their moves.

Moves:

Calm Calm Field — The user emits a calming aura that suppresses emotions and aggression in the vicinity, helping to pacify enemies or defuse situations.

Calm Air Manipulation — The user can control and shape air currents to create gentle breezes or powerful gusts, using them for both attack and defense.

Calm Vacuum — The user can create vacuum zones by manipulating air pressure, temporarily immobilizing or suffocating opponents.

Calm Shock Wave — The user releases a burst of concentrated calm energy in a shock wave that disrupts enemy attacks or defenses.

current_state.summary_text: The Calm-Calm Fruit (Nagi Nagi no Mi) was carried by Donquixote Rosinante (Corazon), who died. After his death, the fruit was reborn somewhere in the world; its current bearer is not established by canon.

items.name: Brush-Brush Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate ink from their body and use a brush or any painting tool, even hair, to draw images that instantly become real, three-dimensional objects once painted. The user becomes a Brush-Human, capable of creating weapons, armor, tools, creatures, food, or nearly anything they can imagine — ink swords that fight, ink creatures that serve as allies, ink ships to sail the seas, or ink food to eat. What you draw becomes real, and your creations carry the same physical properties as the real thing.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves,

Moves:

Brush Brush Sweep — The user creates large brushstrokes that can strike enemies or clear obstacles with powerful force.

Brush Brush Wall — The user forms a protective wall made of bristles to block attacks.

Brush Brush Bind — The user extends bristles to entangle or immobilize opponents.

Brush Brush Cut — The user shapes bristles into sharp blades for cuts in close-quarters combat.

current_state.summary_text: The Brush-Brush Fruit does not correspond to an established canon fruit in One Piece. Its current bearer is unknown, or the fruit does not exist in the canon of the work.

items.name: Barrier-Barrier Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate and shape invisible or translucent barriers at will — walls, domes, spheres, or even staircases — that can block, deflect, or trap anything: bullets, blades, punches, and even the most ferocious attacks. The user can summon a protective shield in front of them for defense, seal off an area to trap enemies, erect staircase-shaped barriers to climb or reach elevated terrain, or launch a barrier forward as an enormous force-filled projectile to crush enemies.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves,

Moves:

Barrier Wall — Creates a large solid wall of barrier energy to block attacks or protect allies.

Barrier Dome — Forms a spherical barrier around the user or a target, providing complete defense.

Barrier Shield — Generates a portable, handheld barrier that deflects or absorbs incoming attacks.

Barrier Burst — Releases a powerful burst of barrier energy to repel or damage nearby enemies.

Barrier Thorn — Projects sharp spikes made of barrier energy to pierce or attack opponents.

Barrier Net — Creates a barrier-based net to trap or restrain enemies.

Barrier Flight — The user can create barrier platforms or wings to fly or glide through the air.

Barrier Reflection — Deflects energy attacks or projectiles, bouncing them back at the attacker.

Barrier Prison — Completely envelops an enemy within barrier energy, fully restricting movement.

Barrier Blade — Forms a sword or bladed weapon made of hard barrier energy for close-quarters combat.

Barrier Stairs — Creates solid staircases made of barrier energy, allowing the user or allies to reach higher ground or escape quickly.

current_state.summary_text: The Barrier-Barrier Fruit (Bari Bari no Mi) is held by Bartolomeo, leader of the Barto Club and ally of the Straw Hat Pirates. After Egghead, Bartolomeo remains its bearer as established by canon.

items.name: Slow-Slow Fruit
items.description: Emits Noroma Photons that drastically slow the movements of anything they touch for 30 seconds.

current_state.summary_text: The Slow-Slow Fruit (Noro Noro no Mi) belongs to Foxy the Silver Fox, captain of the Foxy Pirates. There is no canonical indication that Foxy has lost the fruit after Egghead.

items.name: Wheel-Wheel Fruit
items.description: Allows the user to grow wheels on their body and use them both for movement and for powerful spinning attacks.

current_state.summary_text: The Wheel-Wheel Fruit (Guru Guru no Mi) belongs to Buffalo, a member of the Donquixote Pirates. There is no canonical indication that Buffalo has lost the fruit after the events of Dressrosa or Egghead.

items.name: Art-Art Fruit
items.description: Allows the user to transform others and objects into abstract works of art, altering their physical appearance and function.

current_state.summary_text: There is no Devil Fruit called the 'Art-Art Fruit' with this description in the One Piece canon. The current bearer is unknown — canon does not establish this fruit as existing or attributed to any character.

items.name: String-String Fruit
items.description: Generates thin but powerful strings from the user's body, capable of cutting, manipulating people, or even stitching organs.

current_state.summary_text: The String-String Fruit (Ito Ito no Mi) belonged to Donquixote Doflamingo. After Doflamingo's defeat in Dressrosa and his imprisonment in Impel Down, the current whereabouts of the fruit — and whether it has already reborn in a new copy — is not established by canon.

items.name: Whisper-Whisper Fruit
items.description: Grants the user the ability to communicate secretly with animals and possibly inanimate objects.

current_state.summary_text: There is no Devil Fruit called the 'Whisper-Whisper Fruit' with this description in the One Piece canon. The current bearer is unknown — canon does not establish this fruit as existing or attributed to any character.

items.name: Spores-Spores Fruit
items.description: Generates harmful spores that can cause enemies to hallucinate, fall ill, or become paralyzed.

current_state.summary_text: There is no Devil Fruit called the 'Spores-Spores Fruit' with this description in the One Piece canon. The current bearer is unknown — canon does not establish this fruit as existing or attributed to any character.

items.name: Amplify-Amplify Fruit
items.description: Grants the ability to amplify the size, speed, or strength of anything the user interacts with.

current_state.summary_text: There is no Devil Fruit called the 'Amplify-Amplify' with this description in One Piece canon. The current owner is unknown — canon does not establish this fruit as existing or attributed to any character.

items.name: Transmit-Transmit Fruit
items.description: Transforms the user into a living Den Den Mushi, allowing them to retransmit and intercept all communications.

current_state.summary_text: There is no Devil Fruit called the 'Transmit-Transmit' with this description in One Piece canon. The current owner is unknown — canon does not establish this fruit as existing or attributed to any character.

items.name: Leopard-Leopard Fruit
