items.description: Grants transformation into a leopard and a leopard-human hybrid, increasing speed, strength, and stealth in combat.

current_state.summary_text: The Leopard-Leopard Fruit (Neko Neko no Mi, Model: Leopard) belongs to Rob Lucci, agent of CP0. Lucci remains active as a World Government operative in the post-Egghead period.

items.name: Fish-Fish Fruit
items.description: Allows transformation into a fish and a fish-human hybrid, granting swimming speed and underwater breathing.

current_state.summary_text: There are multiple versions of the Fish-Fish Fruit (Sakana Sakana no Mi) in canon. The most relevant model is the Model: Rochen (stingray), held by Jinbe — current helmsman of the Straw Hat Pirates, active after Egghead.

items.name: Clima-Tact
items.description: A slender, extendable staff imbued with advanced weather-manipulation technology. This weapon has the ability to summon and control weather phenomena such as lightning, wind, and rain in battle. Its lightweight design allows for quick strikes, while its hidden compartments release devastating electrical and weather-based attacks that throw enemies off balance.

current_state.summary_text: The Clima-Tact is wielded by Nami, navigator of the Straw Hat Pirates, and remains her primary weapon in combat following the events of Egghead.

items.name: Kiribachi
items.description: A massive, jagged serrated sword that embodies a brutal and savage fighting style. The Kiribachi's serrated blade can tear through wood, flesh, and bone with ease, and its heavy weight allows the wielder to deliver powerful, crushing blows.

current_state.summary_text: The Kiribachi is the weapon associated with Killer of the Kid Pirates; its exact whereabouts following the dissolution of the alliance at Wano are not precisely established by canon, but it remains linked to its wielder.

items.name: Kashu
items.description: A large, heavy cutlass-type sword that emphasizes brute strength and overwhelming power over finesse. Kashu's broad blade can cut through resistant materials and opponents alike, making it a favored weapon for those who fight with raw strength.

current_state.summary_text: The Kashu is one of the swords Zoro carried briefly; it was discarded before the current events and its present whereabouts are not established by canon.

items.name: Kikoku
items.description: An imposing nodachi characterized by its exceptional length and weight. Kikoku's heavy blade is capable of delivering devastating cuts, often used to intimidate and overpower enemies. It is handled with specialized skill to maximize reach and cutting power.

current_state.summary_text: Kikoku is Trafalgar Law's nodachi; following the events of Wano and Law's defeat by Blackbeard, the exact whereabouts of the sword are not confirmed by canon — Law was captured by Blackbeard and the weapon's situation remains uncertain.

items.name: Shigure
items.description: Known as the "Rain Cutter", Shigure is a finely crafted katana, famous for its elegance and sharpness. It is said that its blade glides through the air like a gentle rain, allowing for swift and precise strikes. It is prized by those who combine artistic technique with lethal efficiency.

current_state.summary_text: Shigure was Tashigi's katana, a Marine officer; its current status in Tashigi's hands is not explicitly reconfirmed after Wano, but there is no canonical indication that the weapon has changed bearers.

items.name: Hiru
items.description: A sword of unique design with a curved blade optimized for swift cuts and close-quarters combat. The balanced construction of the Hiru allows its user to perform quick, fluid attacks, making it a favorite among agile swordsmen.

current_state.summary_text: Hiru is a sword whose canonical identity and bearer have not been clearly established in the work; its current whereabouts are unknown.

items.name: Germa 66 Raid Canister
items.description: A specialized high-tech device used by the Germa 66 military force, these canisters are equipped with offensive and defensive armaments. They can deploy traps, emit energy blasts, or release smoke screens to disorient opponents. Designed for versatility and tactical advantage, the Raid Canisters enhance Germa 66's combat efficiency.

current_state.summary_text: The Raid Canisters are standard equipment of Germa 66, used by the Vinsmokes; following the events of Whole Cake Island and Germa's subsequent operations, they remain in the possession of the Vinsmoke military organization.

items.name: Dream-Dream Fruit
items.description:
Grants the user the ability to enter and manipulate the dreams of others, allowing them to influence thoughts and actions within the dream world. This power can be used for both combat and psychological manipulation.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Dream Walk — Allows the user to enter and control another person's dream.

Nightmare Grip — Induces a nightmare state in opponents, causing fear and paralysis.

Dream Forge — Creates tangible objects within the dream that can interact with the real world.

Sleep Wave — Emits a wave that induces deep sleep in all who hear or see it.

current_state.summary_text: The Dream-Dream Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established by One Piece canon; its bearer and existence are not recognized by the work — it is an item with no confirmed canonical basis.

items.name: Cutlass
items.description: A short pirate sword. Easy to handle on ships, good for quick fights.

current_state.summary_text: The cutlass is a generic weapon common among pirates in the world of One Piece, with no unique specimen or defined canonical bearer; it functions as standard armament for ordinary sailors and pirates.

items.name: Sickle-Sickle Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to grow long, sharp nails like blades that can cut through the air as if they were sickles. With this power, the user can attack directly with sharp nails in close-quarters combat or swing to generate cutting air blades that slice through flesh, metal, or even stone at a distance. They can release bursts or whirlwinds of cutting wind to attack groups of enemies or pierce heavy defenses.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Sickle Blade Creation — The user can generate sharp, sickle-shaped blades from their body or weapons.

Sickle Slash — A swift, sweeping attack using the sickle blades to cut through enemies and objects with precision.

Sickle Spin — The user spins rapidly, wielding sickle blades in both hands to strike multiple opponents around them.

Sickle Launch — The user can hurl sickle blades as long-range weapons that return like boomerangs.

Twin Sickle Combo — A rapid combination attack using two sickles for close-quarters combat, mixing slashes and thrusts.

current_state.summary_text: The Sickle-Sickle Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Sleep-Sleep Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to put other living beings to sleep at will, by touch, voice, or a subconscious trigger, instantly sending targets into slumber and rendering them unconscious. The user can pacify enemies without violence, infiltrate guarded locations by silently dropping guards, or disable large groups simply by putting them to sleep. The user can also induce targets into a deep sleep, causing them to dream, fall into illusions, or remain defenseless for extended periods, while the user remains free to act.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Sleep Induction — The user can put targets to sleep by touching them or making eye contact, rendering them temporarily unconscious.

Sleep Field — The user creates an area where anyone who enters instantly falls asleep.

Sleep Manipulation — The user can control the depth and duration of sleep, from light naps to deep, prolonged slumber.

Sleep Projection — The user can send sleep-inducing energy or waves toward targets at a distance.

Sleep Touch — Physical contact causes immediate drowsiness or sleep in opponents.

current_state.summary_text: The Sleep-Sleep Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Spore-Spore Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create, control, and transform into spores and fungi at will, turning them into a Spore Human. With this power, the user can release spore clouds to poison, paralyze, or put enemies to sleep, infecting the air or surfaces and turning entire areas into danger zones.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Spore Release — The user can release spore clouds that can have various effects, including intoxicating or putting enemies to sleep.

Spore Manipulation — The user controls the density, spread, and effects of the spores in the air.

Spore Healing — The user can emit spores that accelerate healing for themselves or allies.

Spore Poisoning — Some spores can act as toxins, weakening or paralyzing opponents over time.

Spore Form — The user can transform parts of their body into spores, allowing them to pass through small spaces or evade attacks.

Spore Infestation — The user can infect an area or enemy with spores that deal continuous damage or disrupt functions.

current_state.summary_text: The Spore-Spore Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Echo-Echo Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to control sound waves and echoes: they can amplify, distort, recreate, or project any existing sound, voice, noises, ambient sounds, transforming them into tools or weapons. The user can release deafening sonic blasts to shatter objects or repel enemies, distort sounds to confuse or deceive enemies, create illusions of footsteps or voices to mislead people, or silently mimic someone's voice to impersonate them.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Echo Blast — The user emits powerful sound waves that can repel or stun enemies.

Echo Shield — The user creates a shield of reverberating sound waves that can deflect attacks.

Sonic Blast — A concentrated burst of sonic energy capable of causing explosions and severe damage.

Sound Manipulation — The user can manipulate sound waves to confuse enemies or disrupt communications.

Echo Step — The user uses sonic vibrations to increase their speed and agility.

Resonance Strike — The user channels sonic vibrations into physical attacks, increasing their impact.

current_state.summary_text: The Echo-Echo Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Heat-Heat Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to superheat their body and anything they touch, turning flesh, weapons, or even water into scalding heat. The user can burn enemies with a simple touch, melt metal weapons or armor, heat their own fists or blades to pierce through defenses, or even boil the surrounding water or the sea itself to hinder ships or aquatic enemies.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Hot Sea Inferno — The user heats their hands and submerges them in water, causing the surrounding water to boil, damaging enemies and affecting the environment.

Hot Sea Torpedo — The user creates underwater heat beams that travel like torpedoes to attack opponents at range.

Hot Wind Fist — The user heats their forearm to deliver a powerful scorching punch.

Hot Match Face — The user uses their entire heated body to deliver strong, blazing physical attacks.

Heat Ram — The user heats their head and charges at enemies, using their heated body to inflict damage.

current_state.summary_text: The Heat-Heat Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Glare-Glare Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to see through anything — skin, clothing, walls, even the minds and memories of others — and perceive hidden truths that others conceal. The user can expose lying enemies, read memories, uncover secrets, and detect invisible traps or hidden enemies. They can also project or transfer memories to others, allowing them to plant false memories or share their own without spoken words.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

* Blinding Flash — The user emits an intense flash of light from their body to temporarily blind opponents.

* Glare Beam — Fires a focused beam of dazzling light that can disorient or damage targets.

* Reflective Shield — Creates a barrier of refracted light that can deflect attacks.

* Mirage Creation — Manipulates light to create illusions that confuse enemies.

* Flash Step — Uses bursts of light to move at high speed, appearing to teleport over short distances.

current_state.summary_text: The Glare-Glare Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Color-Color Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to paint or coat people, objects, or surfaces with color at will, changing their appearance so they blend into the environment, disguise themselves or others, or even mimic a completely different appearance. The user can spray or coat ships, walls, clothing, or bodies to make them nearly invisible or disguised, perfect for stealth, infiltration, surprise attacks, or escapes. They can also recolor enemies or objects to sow confusion, change armor to look like stone, weapons to look like harmless tools, or create illusions by altering the colors of the environment.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Camouflage — The user sprays paint on a target, blending it with the environment to become nearly invisible.

Copy Paint — The user paints a target's face to mimic the appearance of another person, though the disguise is often crude and easily detected.

Skittles — The user fires small rainbow-colored projectiles at opponents, which can have various effects depending on the color.

Bifrost — The user transforms into a rainbow and teleports to a different location, reappearing in a burst of color.


Sprinkles — After using Bifröst, the user releases a barrage of rainbow-colored projectiles from the sky.

current_state.summary_text: The Color-Color Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Book-Book Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to manipulate books at will — they can control any nearby book, make books fly or be used as platforms, and more dangerously, trap living beings inside books. The user can open a book over a target and imprison them, turning them into part of the book's world or illustration, effectively locking them in an alternate story prison where they do not age.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Book World — The user hovers an open book over a target, trapping them inside a visual illusion of the book's world. The illusion ends when the book is closed.

Book Prison — The user physically traps a target inside a book, turning them into an illustration that does not age. The target can be freed by destroying the book, though this may cause harm to them.

Telekinetic Control — The user can manipulate books telekinetically, using them as supports or to interact with objects.

Long-Distance Communication — The user connects multiple Den Den Mushi to a book, allowing them to communicate with several people simultaneously.

Imprisonment Nails — The user uses large, seemingly unbreakable nails to pin books shut, securing targets within.

Illusion Manipulation — The user can create illusions within the books, distracting or confusing enemies by altering their perception of reality.

Story Creation — The user can create detailed stories within the books, potentially influencing the behavior of those trapped inside.

Bookmark Portal — The user places a special bookmark on a page, allowing them to open and close the book at will, controlling access to the trapped world.


Book-Based Flight — The user uses books as platforms to fly or move through the air.

Book Summoning — The user can summon books from their surroundings, using them as tools or weapons as needed.

current_state.summary_text: The Book-Book Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Mushroom-Mushroom Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate, control, and even transform into mushrooms, spores, and fungal matter at will. The user can sprout mushrooms or fungal masses from the ground or their body, creating walls, platforms, spore clouds, or fungal traps. They can release spores to poison, paralyze, or confuse enemies, or use mushrooms as living shields, terrain, or weapons.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Spore Burst — The user releases a cloud of spores that can cause hallucinations or disorientation in opponents.

Mushroom Shield — The user creates a large, durable mushroom to block attacks or protect allies.

Fungal Growth — The user accelerates mushroom growth, creating barriers or platforms for mobility.

Spore Bomb — The user condenses spores into a compact form and detonates them, releasing a toxic cloud.

Mycelium Network — The user extends underground fungal threads to detect movement or create traps.

Hallucinogenic Spores — The user releases spores that induce vivid hallucinations in those who inhale them.

Mushroom Mimicry — The user transforms parts of their body into various types of mushrooms for camouflage or utility.

Spore Infestation — The user infects an area with spores that can grow into aggressive mushrooms, attacking intruders.

Mushroom Clones — The user creates duplicates of themselves using mushrooms to confuse enemies.

Fungal Explosion — The user causes a cluster of mushrooms to explode, releasing a burst of spores and force.

current_state.summary_text: The Mushroom-Mushroom Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Assemble-Assemble Fruit
items.description:
Allows the user to create constructs by assembling random objects, forming functional machines or tools.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Union Armada — The user disassembles objects and reassembles them into a giant mecha suit, increasing their physical strength and combat capabilities.

Supreme Faust — The user assimilates a massive arm and coats it with Armament Haki to deliver a powerful punch capable of causing massive destruction.

Det Sterkeste Strike — The user enters an energized state and delivers rapid, powerful punches, overwhelming opponents with sheer force.

Awakened Assimilation — The user extends their disassembly and reassembly abilities to a greater range, allowing them to manipulate the environment and create complex structures.

Haki-Reinforced Constructs — The user infuses their reassembled creations with Armament Haki, making them more durable and capable of delivering powerful attacks.

Mechanical Minions — The user assembles small mechanical creatures or drones to assist in combat or reconnaissance.

Adaptive Armor — The user reconfigures their mecha suit or body armor to adapt to different combat situations, improving defense and mobility.

Structural Reconfiguration — The user disassembles and reassembles parts of the environment to create barriers, traps, or escape routes as needed.

Fusion Strike — The user combines several reassembled weapons or limbs into a single devastating attack.

Reconstruction Surge — The user rapidly assembles and disassembles parts of their body or surroundings to confuse opponents and gain an advantage in battle.

current_state.summary_text: The Assemble-Assemble Fruit is not recognized in the official One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent within the established work.

items.name: Dice-Dice Fruit
items.description:
This fruit gives the user the ability to transform their body into razor-sharp steel. Their arms, legs, and even skin become as hard and sharp as metal blades. With this power, they can cut through almost anything — wood, stone, ships — and slice through enemies and obstacles with brutal precision. The user can also reshape or reform their body into different sharp forms, blades, spines, edges, to adapt to battle or defense.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.
Moves:

Supa Supa Carving Knife —
The user transforms their arms into long sharp blades and slashes at high speed with precision and force.

Supa Supa Roast Cutter —
The user rapidly spins with their blade arms extended to slash enemies around them in a whirlwind of steel.

Supa Supa Back Cutter —
The user transforms parts of their legs or back into blades to unexpectedly attack opponents behind them.

Supa Supa Crescent Moon Cut —
The user performs a sweeping blade attack that arcs outward in a crescent shape, capable of cutting through solid obstacles or multiple enemies at once.

current_state.summary_text: The Dice-Dice Fruit (Supa Supa no Mi) was consumed by Mr. 1 (Daz Bonez), a former Baroque Works agent. Its current bearer is Daz Bonez, who following the events of Impel Down and Marineford follows Crocodile in the Cross Guild organization.

items.name: Cage-Cage Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create iron shackles and chains from their own body, which can be used to bind, capture, or restrain enemies. The user can form chains quickly and with great force, controlling where they coil, how tightly they hold, and even lifting or throwing opponents. It is a powerful tool for capturing or immobilizing enemies, controlling the battlefield, and supporting allies.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.

Moves:

Ori Ori no Jutsu: Cage Prison – The user instantly binds a target by generating a person-sized small cage around them, immobilizing their entire body.

Ori Ori no Jutsu: Arm Lock – The user generates cage-shaped shackles around an enemy's arms or legs, restricting movement and disarming them.

Ori Ori no Jutsu: Dome Prison – The user generates a full-sized dome-shaped cage to trap multiple enemies or completely block off an area.

Ori Ori no Jutsu: Piercing Prison – The user forms cages with spikes that impale or pin opponents while also locking them in place.

current_state.summary_text: The Cage-Cage Fruit (Ori Ori no Mi) was consumed by Hina, Vice Admiral of the Marines. Hina remains an active Marine officer and is the current bearer of the fruit.

items.name: Spring-Spring Fruit
items.description:
This fruit gives the user a body made of powerful coiled springs. Their limbs become elastic and flexible: they can launch punches or kicks with explosive force, rebound from strikes, or attack at long range. Their coiled body grants them both great offensive power and good resilience. The user can extend or compress their limbs to move with surprising speed or reach across distances.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Spring Shot –
The user launches themselves like a bullet using spring propulsion, delivering a powerful kick or body blow to the enemy.

Spring Hopper –
The user repeatedly leaps across the battlefield, dodging attacks and confusing opponents with erratic movements.

Spring Cannon –
The user tightly compresses their spring legs before releasing an explosive jump or kick with massive force.

Spiral Kick –
The user spins in the air using spring tension to perform a rotating kick, piercing enemies with rapid, spinning force.

current_state.summary_text: The Spring-Spring Fruit (Bane Bane no Mi) was consumed by Bellamy, former captain of the Bellamy Pirates. After his defeat in Dressrosa, Bellamy's whereabouts are not clearly established by canon; he remains the presumed bearer, but his current situation is uncertain.

items.name: Door-Door Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create doors on any surface — walls, air, ground, even empty space — and use them to travel instantly from one place to another. The user can open a door here and another door elsewhere, then pass through it to appear instantly at the destination. They can also open a door on themselves to escape danger, dodge attacks, or ambush enemies.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Air Door –
The user creates a door in the air and passes through it, disappearing from the battlefield or reappearing instantly elsewhere.

Wall Door –
The user opens a portal on any solid surface, such as stone, steel, or wood, to pass through barriers effortlessly.

Floor Door –
The user generates a trapdoor beneath a target, causing them to suddenly fall through.

Human Door –
The user opens a door directly in a person's body to bypass them or potentially trap parts of them.

Dimensional Room –
The user opens a door to a pocket dimension where time apparently stops, allowing them to hide or set an ambush.

current_state.summary_text: The Door-Door Fruit (Doa Doa no Mi) was consumed by Blueno, former CP9 agent. After CP9's defeat at Enies Lobby, Blueno's whereabouts are not confirmed by canon; he remains the presumed bearer, but his current situation is uncertain.

items.name: Bubble-Bubble Fruit
items.description:
This fruit gives the user the power to create and control soap and bubbles from their body. The bubbles are more than just water — they can wash away dirt and cleanse an enemy's strength or powers, leaving them weaker, vulnerable, and slippery. The user can envelop themselves in dense bubbles for protection or cover enemies with slippery foam so they cannot keep their footing or fight properly.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Soap Sheep – The user creates a huge sheep-shaped cluster of soap bubbles that can attack enemies or trap them in a foamy prison.

Bubble Lock – Traps enemies in sticky, elastic soap bubbles that drain their strength and render them immobile or unable to keep their footing properly.

Bubble Master – The user releases and controls multiple bubbles simultaneously for large-scale weakening, sweeping enemies in groups.

Bubble Shower – A rain of bubbles falls over a wide area, causing confusion and gradually draining the strength of anyone caught in it.

current_state.summary_text: The Bubble-Bubble Fruit (Awa Awa no Mi) was consumed by Kalifa, former CP9 agent. After CP9's defeat at Enies Lobby, Kalifa is briefly seen in CP0, but her current situation post-Egghead is not precisely established by canon; she remains the presumed bearer.

items.name: Berry-Berry Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform parts of their body into grape-like balls, effectively becoming a Grape Human. The user can roll, bounce, or stretch these berry balls for movement, attack, or defense. They can attack enemies by launching or hurling parts of their body, and the balls can absorb impacts, allowing the user to withstand blows. With creativity, the user can reshape their body to dodge, trap enemies, or create barriers.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Berry Bomb – The user launches explosive berries that detonate on impact, dealing area damage.

Berry Trap – Creates sticky berry patches that can immobilize or slow enemies when stepped on.

Berry Barrage – Rapid release of multiple berries to overwhelm opponents.

Berry Heal – Certain berries can be used to restore minor wounds or temporarily boost resilience.

current_state.summary_text: The Berry-Berry Fruit (Beri Beri no Mi) was consumed by Very Good, Vice Admiral of the Marines. Very Good remains the confirmed bearer; his active status in the Marines receives no relevant canonical update after Enies Lobby.

items.name: Revive-Revive Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to return to life after their first death. The user's soul returns, reviving their body, even if it has deteriorated, lost parts, or become nothing more than bones. Once revived, the user lives as something between the living and the undead: their body may bear the scars of death, but their soul's powers grant them continued existence.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Restore – The user touches a broken or damaged object to instantly repair it to its original state.

Cell Regeneration – The user can accelerate the healing of wounds by rapidly restoring damaged tissue.

Rebuild – The user can reconstruct destroyed structures or machinery by touching the remains.

Revive Touch – The user's touch can restore non-living things to working order, useful in combat or support situations.

current_state.summary_text: The Revive-Revive Fruit (Yomi Yomi no Mi) is consumed by Brook, musician and swordsman of the Straw Hat Pirates. Brook is with Luffy's crew, which after the events of Egghead is en route to the next destination on their journey through the Grand Line.

items.name: Shadow-Shadow Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to manifest and control shadows — not only their own, but the shadows of other living beings. The user can steal another person's shadow, sever it from their body, and reshape or reassign it at will. They can also make their own shadow take physical form: a dark, semi-solid silhouette that moves, fights, and obeys the user's will.

You must remember to inform them about your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about your moves.
Moves:

Shadow Clutch – The user extracts and controls an opponent's shadow to immobilize or control them.

Shadow Prison – The user traps enemies within shadows, restricting their movements.

Shadow Meld – The user merges with shadows to become intangible and invisible.

Shadow Clone – The user creates shadow duplicates to confuse or attack opponents.

Shadow Step – The user moves instantly between shadows across distances.

current_state.summary_text: The Shadow-Shadow Fruit (Kage Kage no Mi) is consumed by Gecko Moria, former member of the Seven Warlords of the Sea. After his survival at Marineford and appearance in Wano (Onigashima arc), Moria's current whereabouts post-Egghead are not precisely confirmed by canon; he remains the established bearer.

items.name: Non-Devil Fruit User
items.description: A normal human without Devil Fruit powers. They rely purely on physical strength, skill, intelligence, and determination. Through hard training and experience, they have become fighters capable of holding their own in a world full of monsters and abilities.

current_state.summary_text: This item does not correspond to a canonical Devil Fruit or a named weapon/object — it is a generic character category. There is no specific canonical state to record.

items.name: Gomu Gomu Fruit
items.description:
This fruit transforms the user's body into rubber, allowing them to stretch, bend, and move in ways normal people cannot. Their punches and kicks can reach long distances, and their rubber body helps them withstand powerful blows without breaking. As the user trains, they can unlock special battle forms called Gears. Each Gear powers them up in a different way, granting more strength, speed, or flexibility.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Gomu Gomu no Pistol – The user can stretch their arm and launch a punch at high speed from a distance.

Gomu Gomu no Bazooka – The user can stretch both arms back and thrust them forward for a strike of massive force.

Gomu Gomu no Gatling – The user can deliver a rapid barrage of punches, overwhelming enemies in seconds.

Gomu Gomu no Rocket – The user can launch their body by grabbing onto objects and hurling themselves forward.

current_state.summary_text: The Gomu Gomu no Mi — true name Hito Hito no Mi, Model: Nika — is carried by Monkey D. Luffy, captain of the Straw Hat Pirates and current Emperor of the Sea, since his childhood.

items.name: Sube Sube Fruit
items.description:
This fruit makes the user's skin super smooth and slippery. Any attack, punch, sword, bullet, even heavy objects will slide off their body without causing damage, because nothing can grip or penetrate their slippery surface. If the user was overweight, the sliding effect can even cause excess fat to slip away, making their body slender and elegant.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Sube Sube Dash – The user uses their slippery body to glide across surfaces at incredible speeds, evading attacks with ease.

Sube Sube Slip – When struck, the user's body becomes so slippery that weapons and limbs slide off without causing damage, nullifying physical attacks.

Sube Sube Launch – The user launches themselves forward like a projectile, using their slippery body to slide at high speed and strike opponents.

Sube Sube Counter – The user deflects an enemy attack and immediately slides back in for a counter-attack with increased speed and momentum.

current_state.summary_text: The Sube Sube no Mi is carried by Alvida, a former pirate who consumed the fruit and currently belongs to Buggy's alliance as part of Buggy's Delivery/Cross Guild.

items.name: Bomu Bomu Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform any part of their body — arms, legs, breath, even spit or mucus — into a living bomb. They can detonate limbs or exhalations to create powerful explosions, injuring enemies and damaging the environment without harming themselves. The user becomes immune to explosions, even cannonballs, so they can unleash blasts freely while remaining safe.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Bomb Fist – The user throws a punch that makes their hand explode on impact, creating a powerful explosion without harming themselves.

Bomb Charge – The user rushes forward with multiple small explosions propelling their body, knocking down enemies and causing small shockwaves.

Bomb Bullet – The user fires small explosive particles or creates mini bomb projectiles that explode on contact.

Bomb Storm – The user detonates a series of rapid explosions across the battlefield, targeting multiple enemies.

Self-Detonation – The user triggers a massive explosion centered on their own body to clear a wide area, without suffering any damage.

current_state.summary_text: The Bomu Bomu no Mi is carried by Mr. 5 (Gem), a former Baroque Works agent. Following the organization's dissolution, his current whereabouts and activity are not confirmed by post-Egghead canon.

items.name: Hana-Hana Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to sprout copies of their body parts — arms, legs, eyes, even entire limbs — from almost any surface: walls, the ground, other objects, or living beings. The user can control these extra limbs as if they were their own, allowing them to attack, grab, hold, or spy from unexpected angles.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Flower Manipulation: The user can create, control, and manipulate flowers and plant life at will.
Petal Burst: Releases a burst of petals that can be used for attack, defense, or distraction.

Vine Whip: Generates and controls vines to attack or restrain enemies.

Healing Flower: Some produced flowers have mild healing properties to mend wounds or relieve pain.

Floral Armor: Covers the user's body with a layer of durable petals for protection.

Pollination Cloud: Emits a cloud of pollen that can cause drowsiness or disorientation in opponents.

current_state.summary_text: The Hana Hana no Mi is carried by Nico Robin, archaeologist of the Straw Hat Pirates, who continues with the crew following the events of Egghead.

items.name: Kilo-Kilo Fruit
items.description:
The Kilo-Kilo Fruit allows the user to change their weight at any moment, from as light as 1 kilogram to as heavy as 10,000 kilograms — 10 tons — in an instant. They can make their body super light to jump very far, walk on water or thin branches, float on the wind, or dodge attacks with ease. They can also become extremely heavy to break through walls, crush enemies with enormous pressure, create shockwaves by stomping or falling from the sky like a meteor, and leave a large crater.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Weight Manipulation: The user can increase or decrease their body weight from 1 kilogram to 10,000 kilograms without affecting their size or physical shape.

Heavy Impact: By drastically increasing their weight, the user can deliver powerful, crushing blows to opponents or anchor themselves firmly in place.

Light as a Feather: By reducing their weight to near zero, the user gains extreme agility and speed, making them difficult to hit.

Weight-Based Defense: The user can increase their weight to become harder to move or knock down.

current_state.summary_text: The Kilo Kilo no Mi was carried by Miss Valentine, a former Baroque Works agent. Following her defeat in Alabasta and the organization's dissolution, her current whereabouts are not confirmed by post-Egghead canon.

items.name: Doru-Doru Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create and control large quantities of special wax from their body. The wax hardens quickly and can become as strong as steel, allowing the user to form shields, walls, weapons, traps, or complete wax structures. They can also mold the wax into armor or giant figures to fight on their behalf. Although the wax is strong, it can still melt under extreme heat.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Wax Body: The user can transform their body into wax, allowing them to reshape and harden parts of their body at will.

Wax Claws: Creates sharp wax claws for close-combat attacks.

Wax Projectiles: Fires hardened wax bullets or fragments at enemies from a distance.

Wax Armor: Forms durable wax armor around the body for enhanced defense.

Wax Prison: Generates sticky wax to trap or immobilize opponents.

Wax Wings: Creates wax wings to glide or perform limited flight maneuvers.

current_state.summary_text: The Doru Doru no Mi is carried by Mr. 3 (Galdino), a former Baroque Works agent who later joined Buggy's crew. His exact post-Egghead situation is not detailed by canon.

items.name: Baku-Baku Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to eat any object — including things that are not food, such as weapons, buildings, and even living beings — and absorb them into their body. Once eaten, the user can rearrange or transform that material: they could turn swallowed metal into a cannon, wood into armor, or even fuse two eaten items to create new forms.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:
Baku Baku Factory – The user combines multiple consumed objects or beings to create new entities or weapons.

Baku Baku Shock – The user transforms parts of their body into weapons or tools based on what they have eaten, such as turning an arm into a cannon.

Yukigesho (Snow Covered) – The user hides in snowy terrain to ambush opponents by leaping out with their mouth open.

Slim-Up Wapol – The user swallows most of their body to rearrange their bone structure, emerging as a slimmer version of themselves.

Wapol House – After consuming several items, the user transforms into a house-like structure equipped with cannons.

current_state.summary_text: The Baku Baku no Mi is carried by Wapol, former king of Drum Island, who after the events of Alabasta built a new kingdom (Wapol's Toy Kingdom) and rose as a magnate. His post-Egghead position receives no explicit canonical update.

items.name: Bat-Bat Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a bat or a human-bat hybrid at will, granting them bat wings, sharp hearing, echolocation, and agility. As a Human-Bat, the user can fly silently through the air, attack enemies with sharp limbs and fangs, use echolocation to locate hidden enemies or navigate in darkness, and strike from above or behind with surprise. In hybrid or full bat form, they become masters of stealth, infiltration, night attacks, and tracking or reconnaissance — silent flight and heightened senses make them difficult to capture.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Hybrid Form — The user transforms into a half-human, half-bat, gaining bat wings and enhanced agility.

Full Bat Form — The user transforms completely into a bat, enabling rapid flight and stealthy movement.

Blood Drain — By biting an opponent's skin, the user can suck their blood, quickly incapacitating them and putting them to sleep.

Thermal Vision — The user can detect heat signatures, allowing them to locate living beings even in total darkness.

current_state.summary_text: The Bat-Bat Fruit is a non-canon Zoan in the One Piece series; its current user is not established by canon.

items.name: Bird-Bird Fruit, Model: Crane
items.description: This fruit allows the user to transform into a crane or a human-crane hybrid at will. As a Human-Crane, the user gains wings for flight, light and graceful limbs for high agility, long legs and neck typical of a crane for extended reach or powerful kicks/cuts, and enhanced reflexes and balance.

current_state.summary_text: The Bird-Bird Fruit, Model: Crane is an unestablished Zoan in the One Piece canon; no bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Pheasant-Pheasant Fruit
items.description:
Transforms the user into a pheasant and a human-pheasant hybrid, known for its agility and beautiful plumage.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Ice Age — The user instantly freezes a vast area, covering the sea and surroundings with ice.

Ice Saber — The user forms a sharp blade of ice for close-range combat.

Ice Time — The user instantly freezes an opponent upon contact.

Ice Block: Pheasant Beak — The user launches a bird-shaped ice projectile at high speed.

Ice Ball — The user encases targets in large spheres of ice, immobilizing them.

current_state.summary_text: The Pheasant-Pheasant Fruit does not exist in the One Piece canon; the ice powers described do not correspond to any established pheasant Zoan in the work.

items.name: Rhino-Rhino Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a rhinoceros or a human-rhinoceros hybrid, turning them into a Human-Rhinoceros with powerful horns, thick skin, and massive brute strength. With this power, the user can charge like a raging beast, pierce enemies with tusks or horns capable of piercing armor or breaking bones, trample and crush enemies or obstacles, and withstand blows that would fell normal fighters thanks to their dense, resilient skin and heavy constitution.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Rhino Punch — The user transforms into a hybrid woolly rhinoceros and delivers a powerful punch, utilizing enhanced shoulder strength.

Rhino Charge — The user fully transforms into a woolly rhinoceros and charges at the opponent with great force.

Rhino Stab — The user rushes at the opponent and pierces them with their horn.

Sword Game — In hybrid form, the user uses their horn as a sword, which can reach Wazamono grade with training or Armament Haki.

Strong Point — The user's hair and horn shrink in size, but muscular strength increases significantly.

Horn Point — The user's body shrinks, but the horn becomes more sword-like with increased durability.

Fur Point — The user's body hair increases, providing resistance to cold, fire, and electric attacks.

Charge Point — The user gains the ability to run at high speed toward the opponent.

Awakening: Monster Point — The user transforms into a mindless woolly rhinoceros monster with enhanced healing abilities

current_state.summary_text: The Rhino-Rhino Fruit is not established in the One Piece canon; no bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Crocodile-Crocodile Fruit
items.description:
Allows transformation into a crocodile and a human-crocodile hybrid, providing powerful jaws and amphibious characteristics.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Hybrid Form — The user transforms into a half-human, half-crocodile, gaining enhanced strength, durability, and powerful jaws.

Full Crocodile Form — The user fully transforms into a saltwater crocodile, increasing size and physical capabilities.

Tail Whip — The user uses their strong tail to perform sweeping attacks, knocking down multiple opponents.

Steel Jaw — The user's bite can crush most materials, including steel.

Armored Skin — The user's skin becomes resistant to most physical attacks.

Deadly Claws — The user gains sharp claws capable of cutting through various materials.

items.name: Snake-Snake Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a snake or a snake-human hybrid, gaining serpentine characteristics such as a long and flexible body, sharp fangs, venom or the ability to spit or produce poison depending on the snake model, and enhanced stealth, agility, and reflexes. As a Snake-Human, the user can coil around enemies to constrict them, attack with venomous bites, slither silently to infiltrate or ambush, climb or pass through narrow spaces, and move with fluid and unpredictable movements that make them difficult to hit or anticipate.

You must remember to inform their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Hybrid Form — The user transforms into a half-human, half-cobra, gaining strength, agility, and the ability to spit venom.
one-piece-aftermath.fandom.com

Full Cobra Form — The user fully transforms into a cobra, increasing size and physical capabilities.

Venom Spit — The user spits purple venom at opponents for long-range attacks.

Heat Resistance — The user gains resistance to heat and fire in hybrid form.

Full Anaconda Form — The user fully transforms into an anaconda, increasing size and strength.

Constriction — The user uses their powerful coils to immobilize and crush opponents.

Enhanced Durability — The user's muscle density provides greater durability

current_state.summary_text: The Snake-Snake Fruit is not established as a unique fruit in the One Piece canon; no canonical bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Bull-Bull Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a bull or a bull-human hybrid at will, granting them immense strength, bulky mass, thick skin, and powerful natural weapons such as horns, hooves, and brute force. As a Bull-Human, the user can charge like a raging bull with devastating headbutts, trample or gore enemies, destroy walls or defenses, and use sheer mass and weight to overpower enemies in close-range combat.

You must remember to inform their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Full Bull Form — The user fully transforms into a bull, increasing size and physical capabilities.

Horn Charge — The user charges at the opponent with great force, using their horns to deal damage.

Stampede — The user stomps the ground, creating shockwaves that knock enemies down.

Bull Rush — The user advances at high speed, striking opponents with immense power.

Iron Hide — The user's skin becomes resistant to most physical attacks.

items.name: Goat-Goat Fruit
items.description: This fruit allows the user to transform into a goat or a goat-human hybrid at will, granting them the agile balance, sturdy limbs, agility, climbing ability, and natural defenses of a mountain goat. As a Goat-Human, the user gains powerful legs and hooves, ideal for leaping, running, climbing steep surfaces or rough terrain, and delivering heavy kicks or hoof stomps. Curved horns in hybrid or beast form serve as natural weapons for ramming or goring enemies, capable of piercing armor or shields.

current_state.summary_text: The Goat-Goat Fruit is not established in the One Piece canon; no bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Raccoon-Raccoon Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a raccoon or a raccoon-human hybrid at will. As a Raccoon-Human, the user gains all typical raccoon characteristics, sharp claws and teeth, night vision, enhanced senses of smell and hearing, agile hands and fingers for fine manipulation or theft, stealthy movement, agility, climbing and leaping abilities, and excellent reflexes.

You must remember to inform their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Moves:

Hybrid Form — The user transforms into a half-human, half-tanuki, gaining enhanced agility and balance.

Full Tanuki Form — The user fully transforms into a tanuki, allowing for stealthy movement and deception.

Leaf Transformation — The user uses leaves to create simple disguises or small illusions, aiding in stealth and disorientation.

Mischief Maker — The user employs playful tactics to confuse and distract opponents.

Enhanced Senses — The user gains sharp senses of smell and hearing, improving tracking and awareness.

current_state.summary_text: The Raccoon-Raccoon Fruit is not established in the One Piece canon; no bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Penguin-Penguin Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a penguin or a penguin-human hybrid at will. As a Penguin-Human, the user gains an aerodynamic body, flippers or windows for limbs or partial limbs, a beak in full beast or hybrid form, and natural resistance to cold. They can move with speed and agility on ice or slippery surfaces, slide or glide across snow, ice, or slippery ground for surprise attacks or rapid movement, and, if near ice or water, use their natural aquatic adaptation for stealth, diving, or survival in freezing climates.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Flipper of Pain — The user charges at the opponent and strikes them with the left arm.

Combo — After performing the Flipper of Pain, the user slashes the enemy with their ice pick in the right hand.

Army — The user summons an army of penguins to attack opponents. These penguins are capable of using Soru and Haki. They can swim and use weapons such as swords, but will die within a few months.

Haki Flipper Barrage — The user creates portals that summon Haki-imbued flippers to strike the enemy.

current_state.summary_text: The Penguin-Penguin Fruit is not established in One Piece canon; no bearer is confirmed by the work.

items.name: Sky-Sky Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to become a Sky-Human, granting the power to shape and manipulate the sky, including the air, clouds, and atmosphere as though they were a tangible substance. The user can walk or fight in the air, create solid or semi-solid celestial fabric to form shields, platforms, or barriers, whip or bind enemies with sky whips, or shape the air and clouds to create gusts, storms, or sudden aerodynamic effects.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Celestial Crush — The user crushes the opponent into the ground with a blanket, or from both sides with two blankets.

Celestial Shield — The user uses a blanket as a shield to protect themselves.

Flying Whip — The user runs in a straight line to form a thin, long blanket and uses that blanket as a whip.

current_state.summary_text: The Sky-Sky Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Box-Box Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to create magical cubes at will, or compress objects, including gases, liquids, fire, and physical items, into small transparent boxes by touching them. The user can trap items or elements, storing weapons, ammunition, treasures, or dangerous substances and carrying them effortlessly. The boxes can be used as shields, traps, or projectiles; the user can release their contents to surprise enemies, trap parts of the environment, or even rearrange the battlefield.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Box Creation — The user can create boxes at will, which can be solid or semi-permeable.

Touch Activation — By touching an object or individual, the user can instantly encase them in a box.

Box Launch — The user can create boxes in front of their hands and launch them forward.

Indestructible Interior — The interior of the boxes is indestructible, providing a safe containment area.

Permeability Control — The user can adjust the permeability of the boxes to allow air and carbon dioxide to escape while keeping other matter inside.

External Durability — The boxes can be broken if a force greater than the internal weight is applied from the outside.

current_state.summary_text: The Box-Box Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Imitate-Imitate Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to copy the abilities, appearance, or skills of others after touching or observing them, transforming the user into a Mimic-Human. With this power, the user can imitate physical traits, strength, speed, agility, fighting style, and even special powers if conditions allow, being able to take on a target's abilities to fight, climb, sneak, or use unique talents.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Mimicry — The user touches a person's face with the right hand to transform into a perfect physical double, including voice and mannerisms.

Reversion — The user touches their own face with the left hand to return to their original form.

Imitation — The user adopts the appearance and voice of others to deceive or infiltrate.

Mimicry Memory — The user retains the appearance of everyone they have touched, allowing them to transform into any of them at will.

Imitation Blitz — The user rapidly alternates between several copied forms to confuse and overwhelm opponents.

Mimicry Counter — The user transforms into an opponent to anticipate and counter their attacks.

Mimicry Feint — The user transforms into an ally to deceive enemies about their true intentions.

Mimicry Ambush — The user transforms into a trusted figure to gain access to restricted areas or information.

Mimicry Escape — The user transforms into a guard or officer to facilitate an escape from captivity.

current_state.summary_text: The Imitate-Imitate Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Paste-Paste Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to generate an adhesive paste at will, being able to coat objects, surfaces, weapons, or even parts of their body with a sticky paste that they control with their mind. The user can use the paste to bind or immobilize enemies, seal doors or weapons, stick objects together, create sticky traps or barriers, or even immobilize enemies by encasing them in hardened paste.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Dough Form — The user transforms into a malleable, semi-liquid state, allowing them to pass through tight spaces or evade attacks.

Dough Shield — The user solidifies their dough form into a durable barrier to block attacks.

Dough Projectile — The user launches hardened dough projectiles at opponents, dealing impact damage.

Dough Trap — The user spreads dough on the ground to create sticky surfaces that can immobilize enemies.

Dough Mimicry — The user shapes their dough form to replicate objects or structures for camouflage or deception.

Dough Constructs — The user shapes dough into tools or weapons, such as hammers or spikes, to aid in combat.

Dough Healing — The user applies dough to wounds, using its adhesive properties to close and seal injuries.

Dough Expansion — The user increases the volume of their dough form to boost strength or create larger constructs.

Dough Compression — The user compresses their dough form to increase density, improving durability and impact force.

Dough Regeneration — The user regenerates lost dough over time, restoring their abilities and form.

current_state.summary_text: The Paste-Paste Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Demon-Demon Fruit
items.description:
Allows the user to transform into a demonic entity with enhanced strength and a fear-inducing presence.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Demon Form — The user grows wings while transformed, enabling flight and taking on a dark aura.

Life Drain — The user siphons vital energy from an ally, healing themselves but weakening the victim in exchange.

Infernal Body — The user's body is consumed by fire and darkness, causing anyone who touches them to be burned by profane fire and increasing the power of their fire- and darkness-based techniques.

Psychic Presence — The user gains a limited form of telepathy, allowing them to partially read the intentions of others.

Black Matter Manipulation — The user can manipulate and shape 'black matter' to form giant claws, blades, or shields.

Infernal Flame — The user freely summons and manipulates red and black flames that are hotter than lava, choosing who is affected by them.

Void Storage — The user can store inanimate objects within the 'black matter' and retrieve them at will.

Nocturnal Empowerment — The user's attributes and stats are significantly increased at night or in darkness.

Demon Claw — The user extends their nails into sharp claws to attack opponents, more powerful than a regular strike.

Spike Tornado — The user spins, exposing the spikes on their back to wound nearby enemies.

Flying Drop — The user flies upward and then falls in hybrid form with extended claws to attack.

Fury — When wounded enough, the user becomes stronger, using drill-shaped hands more easily and becoming more agile.

Drill — The user spins toward the opponent in an attack form, using drill-shaped hands to strike.

Charge! — The user charges at the opponent and then spins to stab them with the spikes on their back.

current_state.summary_text: The Demon-Demon Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Ball-Ball Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform their body into a ball or take on the properties of any ball they imagine, or, in some versions, create and control extra spherical balls around them. The user can curl into a compact sphere and roll or ricochet at high speed for powerful charge attacks or surprise strikes. They can also perform moves such as transforming into a heavy mass, similar to a cannonball, to destroy walls or enemies, becoming a light and bouncy ball to dodge or bounce across terrain, or transforming into specialized ball forms, heavy for impact, light for agility.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Boru Boru no Cannonball — The user transforms into a cannonball, becoming hard, heavy, and explosive.

Boru Boru no Exercise — The user becomes an exercise ball, providing a large bouncy surface to soften landings.

Boru Boru no Football — The user takes the form of an American football, enabling straight-line movement at high speed.

Boru Boru no Bowling — The user becomes a bowling ball, hard and heavy, capable of rolling in multiple directions and breaking small obstacles.

Boru Boru no Wrecking Ball — The user transforms into a massive iron wrecking ball, requiring external assistance to move, capable of destroying structures.

Boru Boru no Bounce — The user becomes a rubber ball, bouncing off surfaces to attack enemies from multiple angles.

Boru Boru no Slam — The user enlarges into a heavy ball to crush opponents with a powerful strike.

Boru Boru no Ricochet — The user rapidly ricochets between walls or enemies, creating a pinball-like attack.

Boru Boru no Shield — The user transforms into a dense spherical form to protect allies from attacks.

Boru Boru no Crush — The user compresses into a dense ball to increase impact force when colliding with targets.

current_state.summary_text: The Ball-Ball Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Resonance-Resonance Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to tune into the natural resonant frequencies of objects — metal, wood, stone, even the air — and manipulate those vibrations. By touching or striking something, the user can lock onto its frequency and amplify or distort it. The user can shatter armor or weapons by resonating them, collapse walls or structures by hitting the right pitch, or make objects vibrate uncontrollably until they disintegrate.

You must remember to inform them of your moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them of your moves,

Moves:

Resonant Pulse — The user emits a wave that diminishes the strength, speed, or size of objects or energies it contacts.

Echo Diminishment — The user reduces the volume and impact of sound waves, nullifying sonic attacks.

Energy Dampening — The user diminishes the intensity of energy-based attacks, such as electricity or fire.

Mass Reduction — The user shrinks objects to make them easier to store or transport.

Self-Minimization — The user diminishes their own size to escape attacks or infiltrate small spaces.

Conceptual Reduction (Awakened Ability) — The user reduces abstract concepts such as the time it takes for an attack to connect or the distance between two points.

Resonant Shield — The user creates a barrier that diminishes the force of incoming attacks.

Speed Nullification — The user slows down high-speed projectiles, making them easier to dodge or deflect.

Strength Drain — The user weakens opponents' physical strength by reducing their muscular efficiency.

Thermal Drop — The user reduces the temperature of heated substances, such as magma, to neutralize them.

current_state.summary_text: The Resonance-Resonance Fruit does not correspond to any Devil Fruit established in One Piece canon; its current bearer is unknown or nonexistent in official material.

items.name: Explode-Explode Fruit

Makes the user's body explosive, allowing controlled detonations for movement or combat.

You must remember to tell them their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Double Explosive Mucus Cannon — An enhanced version of the Explosive Mucus Cannon, launching two explosive mucus projectiles simultaneously.

Bomb Kick — The user delivers a kick that triggers an explosion upon contact with the target.

Bomu Cannon — The user launches an explosive projectile resembling a cannonball at the target.

current_state.summary_text: The Explode-Explode Fruit appears to be based on the Bomu Bomu no Mi, carried by Mr. 5 (Gem) in canon; following Impel Down and the dissolution of Baroque Works, the current whereabouts of Mr. 5 and therefore of the fruit are not established by post-Egghead canon.

items.name: Ox-Ox Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a powerful bovine beast or beast hybrid at will, granting the strength, endurance, and physical prowess of a bull, bison, or horned cow. The user can charge like a raging bull and pierce enemies with horns.

You must remember to tell them their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Bison Hybrid Form — The user adopts a half-human, half-bison form, balancing strength and agility.

Enhanced Senses — The user gains an intense sense of smell and hearing, as well as an enhanced sense of taste, aiding in tracking and cooking.

items.name: Dog-Dog Fruit
items.description:
This fruit allows the user to transform into a dog, dog hybrid, or a powerful and often mythical canine beast at will. As a Human-Elephant, they obtain strong tusks, a long trunk, thick skin, and enough power to break trees, crush rocks, lift large things, or destroy walls. Great Magma Hound — The user launches an imposing wolf-shaped magma projectile that deals massive damage and creates magma pools on impact.

Volcanic Storm — The user accumulates a large amount of scalding magma in their right hand before hurling it all toward the cursor. The mag

current_state.summary_text: The Magma-Magma Fruit is wielded by Sakazuki (Akainu), Fleet Admiral of the Marines. He remains in the Marines' supreme command position following the events of Egghead.

items.name: Horm-Horm Fruit
items.description:
Grants the user control over hormones, allowing them to alter a person's gender, heal injuries, boost energy, or change appearance.

You must remember to tell them their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves,

Gender Hormones — The user can alter a person's gender by modifying their hormonal structure, permanently or temporarily.

Growth Hormones — Allows the user to manipulate bodily development, such as accelerating muscle gain or bodily adaptation.

Adrenaline Hormones — Provides a short-term power boost by flooding the target with adrenaline to increase strength and speed.

current_state.summary_text: The Horm-Horm Fruit is wielded by Emporio Ivankov, leader of the Okama and member of the Revolutionary Army. Their exact whereabouts post-Egghead are not precisely established by the canon.

items.name: Sing-Sing Fruit
items.description:
Grants the user the ability to trap anyone who hears their singing inside a dream-like dimension, where reality bends to the user's imagination, allowing them to manipulate landscapes, objects, and even the physical or mental states of people. The user can also summon a powerful demon known as Tot Musica, a destructive entity sealed by a previous user of the fruit. This demon can manifest both in the dream dimension and in the real world, causing widespread devastation.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

items.description:
Grants the user the ability to control, disassemble, reassemble, and reshape inanimate objects, transforming them into weapons, armor, or constructions. The user can manipulate multiple objects simultaneously, fuse them into complex machines or defensive structures, or temporarily merge with objects to enhance their attacks and defenses. A technical understanding of materials or machinery increases the fruit's effectiveness, but damage to controlled objects can sometimes be transferred back to the user.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

current_state.summary_text: The Clank-Clank Fruit does not exist in the One Piece canon; it is an invented fruit, therefore there is no canonical bearer to record.

items.name: Return-Return Fruit
items.description:
Gives the user the power to reverse the age of anything they touch. They can make people, animals, or objects younger, or return things to the state they were in before. The user can even reverse anyone's age by twelve years, causing them to act as a younger or renewed version of themselves. Overusing this power can cause the user's own age to shift randomly for a short period.

You must remember to inform them about their moves at all costs. Do not overwrite this, as it is important to inform them about their moves.

Moves:

Youth Surge — The user reduces the age of something, causing it to lose strength or return to a previous state.

current_state.summary_text: The Return-Return Fruit does not exist in the One Piece canon; it is an invented fruit, therefore there is no canonical bearer to record.

items.name: More-More Fruit

items.name: Viso-Viso Fruit

current_state.summary_text: The Viso-Viso Fruit does not exist in One Piece canon; it is an invented fruit, so there is no canonical owner to record.

items.name: Mini Mini Fruit

Tell them their Moves,

Yasakani no Magatama – The user can fire a rapid barrage of light bullets from their body, overwhelming targets with pure speed and precision.

current_state.summary_text: The Dog-Dog Fruit, Model: Nine-Tailed Fox is held by Catarina Devon of the Blackbeard Pirates. She actively uses it as one of Blackbeard's agents, making particular use of the mimicry ability for infiltration and deception operations.

items.name: Pluton
